gmw_00002559.name: NGC 3268
gmw_00002559.overview: NGC 3268 is a dominant giant elliptical galaxy in the Antlia Cluster [arXiv:1506.06143, arXiv:1510.04274] that hosts an extended X-ray halo [arXiv:1506.06143] and a rich globular cluster system [arXiv:1510.04274]. The galaxy contains a rotating molecular gas disk that is coincident with dust and exhibits moderate kinematic warping [arXiv:1707.08229].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 3268 as a dominant Antlia giant elliptical, measuring its GCS distance, possible larger separation, bimodal cluster colors, radial profile, and about 4750 globular clusters.

quotes.quote: We present a deep VLT photometry in the regions surrounding the two dominant galaxies of the Antlia cluster, the giant ellipticals NGC 3258 and NGC 3268. We construct the luminosity functions of their globular cluster systems (GCSs) and determine their distances through the turn-over magnitudes. These distances are in good agreement with those obtained by the SBF method. There is some, but not conclusive, evidence that the distance to NGC 3268 is larger by several Mpc. The GCSs colour distributions are bimodal but the brightest globular clusters (GCs) show a unimodal distribution with an intermediate colour peak.

The radial distributions of both GCSs are well fitted by de Vaucouleurs laws up to 5 arcmin. Red GCs present a steeper radial density profile than the blue GCs, and follow closely the galaxies' brightness profiles. Total GC populations are estimated to be about 6000 150 GCs in NGC 3258 and 4750 150 GCs in NGC 3268.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3268 as Antlia's dominant central elliptical, hosting an extended globular-cluster population and X-ray halo, centered in the oldest substructure and sharing a dark halo with NGC 3258.

quotes.quote: At the center of Antlia are two concentrations of galaxies focused around the massive ellipticals, NGC 3268 and NGC 3258. They have the same systemic velocity, but these ellipticals each host an extended globular cluster population Dirsch03 , and hot X-ray halo Nakazawa00,Pedersen97 , suggesting that the galaxies are the product of past mergers, and they sit at the center of their own subhalo. Antlia itself lacks a central X-ray excess that is typical of poor clusters, but the X-ray emission and globular cluster systems around NGC 3268 and NGC 3258 are extended along the line connecting the two galaxies, suggesting that now the two systems reside within the same dark matter halo.

Figure rosat shows that the ring of \ detections is coincident with the extent of the X-ray halo around NGC 3268. The X-ray intensity shows complex structure. On the large scale, it is elongated in the direction between NGC 3268 and NGC 3258, which has its own extended Xray emission. The \ detections avoid the brightest X-ray regions. We conclude that NGC 3268 and its large X-ray halo are the dominant and central structure of the Antlia Cluster, while NGC 3258 and its globular cluster and dwarf galaxy population are likely to be more recently arrived.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3268 as Antlia's dominant giant elliptical for a small subgroup; its surroundings host the richer UCD-candidate system, with UCDs less centrally concentrated than globular-cluster candidates.

quotes.quote: Antlia posee una estructura compleja, que consiste en dos subgrupos dominados por las galaxias el ipticas gigantes NGC 3258 y NGC 3268. Los resultados obtenidos hasta la fecha (Smith Castelli 2008 y estudios en rayos X all i citados) parecen indicar que las galaxias en torno a NGC 3268 constituir an un umulo peque no, y aquellas situadas en los alrededores de NGC 3258 ser an un grupo.

Distribuci on espacial proyectada En la Figura 2 se observa que el sistema de candidatas en los alrededores de NGC 3268 es el m as numeroso. Esto podr ia aportar algunos indicios acerca del origen de dichos objetos; recordemos que NGC 3268 ser ia, dentro de la estructura del umulo, la galaxia dominante de un umulo peque no de galaxias, en tanto NGC 3258 lo ser ia de un grupo.

Por esto trabajaremos s olo con las poblaciones de candidatos a CGs y UCDs cercanas a NGC 3268 . En la Figura 3 se superponen la distribuci on radial proyectada de candidatas a UCDs y de candidatos a CGs azules que se hallan a menos de 10' de NGC 3268 , en tanto la Figura 4 muestra aquellos objetos rojos. En ambos casos las poblaciones de CGs se encuentran m as concentradas en torno a NGC 3268 que las candidatas a UCDs de colores equivalentes.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3268 as the other dominant Antlia II giant elliptical, with X-ray emission, nearly 5000 globular clusters, and discrepant tracer versus X-ray mass estimates.

quotes.quote: Its morphological appearance is that of two groups concentrated around the giant ellipticals NGC 3258 and NGC 3268 in a projected distance of 220 kpc. Both dominant galaxies are extended X-ray sources nak00,ped97 that exhibit rich globular cluster systems. The globular cluster system of NGC 3258 contains about 6000 members. The system of NGC 3268 is somewhat poorer, but is still typical of a giant elliptical galaxy, with almost 5000 globular clusters bas08 .

In the case of the sample surrounding NGC 3268, the result from the eva03 estimator is M _ Tr = (5 - 8) 10^ 13 M _ . We again applied Eq. eq:xray with the parameters derived by nak00 : kT_e= 2 keV, r_c= 5 arcmin, and =0.38 . Then the mass enclosed within 18' around NGC 3268 is 1.4 10^ 13 M_ . In both cases, the mass derived from X-ray observations is significantly lower than the mass estimated from the V_ R,h measurements.

sources.summary: ALMA shows NGC 3268 has dust-coincident rotating CO with a double-horned velocity profile and moderate kinematic warping.

quotes.quote: The bright CO(2 1) emission seen in NGC 1332 , NGC 1380 , NGC 3258 , NGC 3268 , and NGC 6861 directly coincides with optically thick dust disks as seen in the structure maps of the corresponding optical HST images. Inspecting their image cubes reveals clear rotation about the nuclear continuum sources. To visualize their rotational properties, we integrated the flux densities in each channel over the elliptical regions coinciding with the optical dust disks. These CO velocity profiles (Figure  pvd_velchan ) exhibit nearly symmetric double-horned features characteristic of rotating disks with emission ranging between 300 and 500 km s ^ 1 from v_ sys .

The molecular gas kinematics of the five CO-bright galaxies are dominated by regular, disk-like rotation. We characterize the deviations from coplanar, circular rotation in kin . Visual inspection shows moderate kinematic warping in NGC 3268 ; for the remainder of this subsample the disk kinematic axes generally conform to the larger-scale stellar photometric axes.

sources.summary: The paper compares NGC 3268’s globular cluster system to NGC 3258, finding more red clusters, a higher red fraction, similar blue and red radial extensions, and a distinct merger history.

quotes.quote: In the case of NGC 3268, the elongation is not so evident, but an overdensity of GCs towards NGC 3258 seems to be present. For both galaxies, the spatial distribution of red GCs is nearly circular, though that of NGC 3268 looks slightly elongated in the same direction.

For NGC 3268 we obtained a population of 5200 700 blue GCs and 3000 450 reds. The higher f_ red in NGC 3268 might indicate that it has followed the fiducial ellipticals' evolutionary history. However, for NGC 3268 they are much more alike, 140 kpc and 120 kpc for blues and reds, respectively.

For NGC 3258, we obtain 6600 800 blue GCs and 1400 200 red GCs, and for NGC 3268 5200 700 blue GCs and 3000 450 reds. However, the fraction of red GCs is higher in NGC 3268 which suggests that, although both galaxies belong to the same environment, they should have experienced a different merging history.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3268 as the second dominant Antlia giant elliptical, compares its globular-cluster system with NGC 3258, and places it in the cluster merger picture.

quotes.quote: The projected spatial distribution of UCDs, GCs and X-ray emission points to an ongoing merger between two Antlia groups, dominated by NGC 3258 and NGC 3268. The central part of the cluster consists of two subgroups, each one dominated by a giant elliptical galaxy (NGC 3258 and NGC 3268) of comparable luminosity. Galaxies located in both subgroups present an elongated projected distribution, in the direction that joins the two giant ones.

These images correspond to four fields, two of them are centred on each one of the dominant galaxies, NGC 3258 (Field 1) and NGC 3268 (Field 3), the third one is located in the region between them (Field 2), and the last one is located to the north-west direction (Field 4, see Fig. dss ). These images were originally used to perform the first study of the GCSs of NGC 3258 and NGC 3268 in Antlia dir03b . On the other side, if we compare the GCSs associated to both dominant galaxies in Antlia, NGC 3258 and NGC 3268, the system around NGC 3258 has a smaller fraction of red GCs and it does not extend to redder colours as much as the NGC 3268 one does.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 3268 using published globular-cluster data, fitting a core model and a Nuker-law stellar luminosity profile with specified structural parameters.

quotes.quote: NGC 3268 The GC distribution of this galaxy is discussed by Dirsch, Richtler Bassino (2003). The resulting core model fit parameters have the values: _0=24.4 arcmin ^ 2 , r_c=2.6 arcmin and =1.9 . The stellar luminosity profile, following Capetti Balmaverde (2006), is well represented by a ``Nuker'' law (introduced by Lauer et al. 1995): _s()=2^ ( )/ _b r_b ^ 1+ r_b ^ ^ ( )/ where is the slope of the external region of luminosity profile, r_b is the ``break radius'' (corresponding to a brightness _b ) where the profile flattens to a smaller slope, measured by the parameter ; sets the sharpness of the transition between the inner and outer profile. In the case of NGC 3268: =2.49 , =1.64 , =0.13 and r_b=0.0252 arcmin.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3268 as a dominant Antlia elliptical with several thousand globular clusters, bimodal cluster colors, radial profiles like NGC 3258, and a light profile similar to NGC 1399.

quotes.quote: These galaxies each contain several thousand clusters; NGC 3258 more than NGC 3268. The color distributions of the globular cluster systems are clearly bimodal.

The radial number density profiles of the globular cluster systems are indistinguishable for the two galaxies and no difference in the distribution of red and blue clusters - as observed in other elliptical galaxies - can be seen.
The light profile of NGC 3268 appears to be similar to that of NGC 1399, the central galaxy of the Fornax cluster.

gmw_00002560.name: NGC 3258
gmw_00002560.overview: NGC 3258 is a giant elliptical galaxy that dominates one of the two central subgroups in the Antlia cluster [arXiv:1305.1896, arXiv:1510.04274]. It hosts a LINER/composite nucleus with a rotating, mildly warped circumnuclear molecular gas disk [arXiv:1101.0898, arXiv:1906.06267] and a rich system of approximately 6,000 globular clusters [arXiv:1510.04274, arXiv:1812.09364].

sources.summary: The paper studies Antlia UCDs around NGC 3258, one of the cluster-core giant ellipticals, and links nearby compact objects to its subgroup and globular-cluster system.

quotes.quote: All of them, members and candidates, are located in the proximity of NGC 3258, one of the two brightest elliptical galaxies in the cluster core. The projected spatial distribution of UCDs, GCs and X-ray emission points to an ongoing merger between two Antlia groups, dominated by NGC 3258 and NGC 3268. The central part of the cluster consists of two subgroups, each one dominated by a giant elliptical galaxy (NGC 3258 and NGC 3268) of comparable luminosity.

We present here the first results of the search for UCDs in the Antlia cluster, focusing on the surroundings of NGC 3258. The eleven compact objects presented so far in this paper (UCDs and bright GCs, Table 1) have a weighted mean radial velocity and standard deviation of 2528 102 km s ^ 1 and 339 km s ^ 1 , respectively. smi08a measured for NGC 3258 a radial velocity of 2689 50 km s ^ 1 , while NED This research has made use of the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED) which is operated by the Jet Propulsion Laboratory, California Institute of Technology, under contract with the National Aeronautics and Space Administration. gives 2792 28 km s ^ 1 . If we take into account the more recent measurement of NGC 3258 velocity, the difference with the compact objects mean velocity is within the dispersion range. This points to a physical association of these compact objects with the host galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3258 as a massive Antlia elliptical with its own globular-cluster population and X-ray halo, but argues its companion population is a younger subcluster recently merged with NGC 3268.

quotes.quote: At the center of Antlia are two concentrations of galaxies focused around the massive ellipticals, NGC 3268 and NGC 3258. They have the same systemic velocity, but these ellipticals each host an extended globular cluster population Dirsch03 , and hot X-ray halo Nakazawa00,Pedersen97 , suggesting that the galaxies are the product of past mergers, and they sit at the center of their own subhalo. Antlia itself lacks a central X-ray excess that is typical of poor clusters, but the X-ray emission and globular cluster systems around NGC 3268 and NGC 3258 are extended along the line connecting the two galaxies, suggesting that now the two systems reside within the same dark matter halo.

Meanwhile the system of galaxies around NGC 3258 are kinematically less consistent with the velocity of their central elliptical. As mentioned above, four galaxies within 80 kpc projection of NGC 3258 and within its extended X-ray halo Pedersen97 are detected in . Three of these are blue-shifted with respect to 2800 , consistent with the rest of the NGC 3258 subcluster. We cannot rule out that the \ population may be its own substructure seen in projection against NGC 3258, but we require redshift independent distances to disentangle the \ and non- \ detected galaxies. None the less, the locality of \ in projection to NGC 3258, in addition to its weaker X-ray halo, is further evidence that the NGC 3258 subcluster is a younger system that has merged with the NGC 3268 cluster.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 3258 as a dominant Antlia II giant elliptical with X-ray emission and about 6000 globular clusters, then compares tracer and X-ray mass estimates for its surroundings.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 3258 as a dominant Antlia giant elliptical, measuring its GCS distance, bimodal cluster colors, radial cluster profile, and about 6000 globular clusters.

quotes.quote: We present a deep VLT photometry in the regions surrounding the two dominant galaxies of the Antlia cluster, the giant ellipticals NGC 3258 and NGC 3268. We construct the luminosity functions of their globular cluster systems (GCSs) and determine their distances through the turn-over magnitudes. These distances are in good agreement with those obtained by the SBF method. There is some, but not conclusive, evidence that the distance to NGC 3268 is larger by several Mpc. The GCSs colour distributions are bimodal but the brightest globular clusters (GCs) show a unimodal distribution with an intermediate colour peak.

The radial distributions of both GCSs are well fitted by de Vaucouleurs laws up to 5 arcmin. Red GCs present a steeper radial density profile than the blue GCs, and follow closely the galaxies' brightness profiles. Total GC populations are estimated to be about 6000 150 GCs in NGC 3258 and 4750 150 GCs in NGC 3268.

sources.summary: The paper contrasts NGC 3258 with NGC 5813, describing its young stellar population, LINER/composite nucleus, MIR PAH emission, and evidence for a recent nuclear starburst.

quotes.quote: Notwithstanding the unperturbed morphology revealed by both the deep optical Tal09 and our UV imaging, NGC 3258 and NGC 5813 hide a different recent star formation history. The analysis of the optical line-strength indices performed by A07 shows that NGC 3258 has a ``young'' average stellar population (luminosity-weighted age of 4.5 0.8 Gyr).

The diagram shows that the two galaxies host a LINER/Composite nucleus (A10) as large fraction of ETGs see e.g. Phi86,Sarzi06 . The different star formation history suggested by the optical results is supported by our Spitzer mid-infrared (MIR) observations Panuzzo10 . Despite the remarkable similarity of the shape of their optical spectra, the nuclear MIR emission of NGC 3258 and NGC 5813, shown in the bottom panel of Figure fig2 , is completely different. The Polycyclic Aromatic Hydrocarbons emission (PAH) complexes at 6.2 m, 7.7 m, 11.3 m and 17 m dominate the MIR spectrum of NGC 3258. Their ratios indicate that the galaxy nucleus has undergone a recent starburst see details in Panuzzo10,Vega10 .

sources.summary: The paper reports NGC 3258 as a CO-bright early-type galaxy with dust-coincident rotation, a double-horned profile, and an unambiguous central high-velocity PVD rise.

quotes.quote: The bright CO(2 1) emission seen in NGC 1332 , NGC 1380 , NGC 3258 , NGC 3268 , and NGC 6861 directly coincides with optically thick dust disks as seen in the structure maps of the corresponding optical HST images. Inspecting their image cubes reveals clear rotation about the nuclear continuum sources. To visualize their rotational properties, we integrated the flux densities in each channel over the elliptical regions coinciding with the optical dust disks. These CO velocity profiles (Figure  pvd_velchan ) exhibit nearly symmetric double-horned features characteristic of rotating disks with emission ranging between 300 and 500 km s ^ 1 from v_ sys .

We also constructed a position-velocity diagram (PVD; also shown in Figure  pvd_velchan ) for each galaxy by extracting a slice along the major axis as determined from the average disk position angle (measured in kin ). The extraction widths were set to the projected CLEAN beam FWHM along the disk major axis. A central rise in the PVD envelope to high velocity at small radius, which indicates a massive, compact object at the disk center, is unambiguously observed only in NGC 1332 and NGC 3258 , with a marginal detection in NGC 1380 within the central 0 1 . The PVD for NGC 6861 shows a 2 wide hole in CO emission.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 3258’s globular cluster system, finding a large blue population, compact red population, color gradients, and evidence for a different merger history from NGC 3268.

quotes.quote: We also obtain photometric metallicities for a large number of GC candidates around NGC 3258, by applying multicolour-metallicity relations. The projected distribution of blue GCs around NGC 3258 is elongated, in a direction that roughly matches the one that crosses both galaxy centres.

As a consequence, the blue and red GC populations for NGC 3258 result 6600 800 and 1400 200 members, respectively. The extension of the blue and red GC subpopulations for NGC 3258 are quite different, 170 kpc and 60 kpc for blues and reds, respectively. The red GC subpopulation is much more concentrated in NGC 3258 than in NGC 3268.

Otherwise, the smaller f_ red in NGC 3258 may suggest that major mergers ocurred between galaxies with lower gas mass available for starburst episodes, even possibly including dry mergers. A mixture of wet and dry mergers could be in agreement with the slightly bluer colour profile of NGC 3258 dir03b .

sources.summary: The paper measures NGC 3258's black hole with high-resolution ALMA CO data, modeling a mildly warped circumnuclear disk and deriving an exceptionally precise elliptical-galaxy black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: This paper presents the most precise BH mass measurement to date for an elliptical galaxy, using 0 10 resolution ALMA Cycle 4 CO(2 1) imaging of NGC 3258's arcsecond-scale molecular gas disk. These new ALMA observations reaffirm our previous Cycle 2 findings of a dynamically cold disk with CO emission extending well within \ and nearly to the galaxy center. At high spatial resolution, the disk appears to be mildly warped with a kinematic twist of 20 . Near the disk center, the line emission reaches the same 500 \ rotation speed also detected in the Cycle 2 data set.

In our final gas-dynamical model, we determine the best-fit NGC 3258 BH mass to be 2.249 10^9 \ with sub-percent level modeling systematics that are equivalent to its statistical uncertainty. For an assumed distance, the high accuracy and precision of this BH mass measurement is commensurate with that obtained for the best-case megamaser disk in NGC 4258. Even after accounting for uncertainties in the galaxy distance, which introduces an additional 12 contribution to the full \ error budget, this is the most precisely measured BH mass for any elliptical galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 3258 major-axis spectra to demonstrate a noise-diagnosis method, showing realistic kinematic errors are nearly twice least-squares estimates.

quotes.quote: Observations NGC 3258 is an E1 galaxy and was observed with the ESO-NTT telescope in the nights of 27-28/2/2001 (64.N-192). Spectra of the major axis were taken using the red arm of EMMI, covering the Ca II triplet. Grating 7 was used, having a dispersion of 0.66 /pix. The detector was a Tektronix CCD with 2048 2047 pixels, 24 m 24 m in size and with a pixel scale of 0.27'' /pixel. A slit width of 1.5'' thus yields a spectral resolution of 3.67 FWHM, resulting in an instrumental dispersion of about 54 km/s in the region of the Ca II triplet.
The method was tested on spectroscopic observations of NGC 3258. In this particular case, the realistic errors are almost a factor of 2 larger than the errors based on least squares statistics.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3258 as a dominant giant elliptical in Antlia, likely anchoring a group, with the most numerous globular-cluster system but a diminished corrected UCD-candidate population.

quotes.quote: Antlia posee una estructura compleja, que consiste en dos subgrupos dominados por las galaxias el ipticas gigantes NGC 3258 y NGC 3268. Los resultados obtenidos hasta la fecha (Smith Castelli 2008 y estudios en rayos X all i citados) parecen indicar que las galaxias en torno a NGC 3268 constituir an un umulo peque no, y aquellas situadas en los alrededores de NGC 3258 ser an un grupo.

Esto podr ia aportar algunos indicios acerca del origen de dichos objetos; recordemos que NGC 3268 ser ia, dentro de la estructura del umulo, la galaxia dominante de un umulo peque no de galaxias, en tanto NGC 3258 lo ser ia de un grupo. Puesto que NGC 3258 posee el sistema de CGs m as numeroso, esto podr ia indicar una falta de proporci on entre los sistemas de CGs y UCDs. Distribuci on radial En el caso de las candidatas a UCDs en torno a NGC 3258 , al corregir por contaminaci on de fondo la poblaci on azul se pierde, en tanto la roja se reduce significativamente.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3258 as one of Antlia's two dominant ellipticals, finding a larger globular-cluster system than NGC 3268, bimodal cluster colors, shared radial profiles, a steeper outer light profile, and blueward color gradients.

quotes.quote: These galaxies each contain several thousand clusters; NGC 3258 more than NGC 3268. The color distributions of the globular cluster systems are clearly bimodal.

The radial number density profiles of the globular cluster systems are indistinguishable for the two galaxies and no difference in the distribution of red and blue clusters - as observed in other elliptical galaxies - can be seen.
NGC 3258 has a light profile which is steeper at large radii. Both galaxies exhibit color gradients, becoming bluer outwards.

sources.summary: The paper fits NGC 3258 globular-cluster and stellar luminosity profiles, deriving a modified core model and a Nuker-law representation for the galaxy light.

quotes.quote: NGC 3258 As for NGC 3268 the GCS density profile data for NGC 3258 are taken from Dirsch, Richtler Bassino (2003). The best modified core model fit is given by the values _0=16.9 arcmin ^ 2 , r_c=3.1 arcmin and =2.4 . The analytical fit to the luminosity profile of the galaxy is, again, obtained with the ``Nuker'' law (Eq. ( Nuker )) with =2.10 , =1.51 , =0 and r_b=0.0192 arcmin.

gmw_00002561.name: NGC 1272
gmw_00002561.overview: NGC 1272 is a massive giant elliptical galaxy in the Perseus cluster [arXiv:1405.0314, arXiv:1703.10204] that hosts a central supermassive black hole [arXiv:1906.11867] and a rich globular cluster system [arXiv:1703.10204]. It features bent double radio jets likely distorted by ram pressure [arXiv:1405.0314] and contains a cool X-ray emitting minicorona [arXiv:1906.11867].

sources.summary: The paper reports bent double jets in NGC 1272, attributing their small-radius curvature to ram pressure and using radio/X-ray data to infer jet pressure.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of bent double jets in the elliptical galaxy NGC 1272, a member of the Perseus cluster. The jets have a radius of curvature of 2 kpc, much smaller than the galaxy half-light radius of 11 kpc. This bending is likely a result of ram pressure, and indicates that the intracluster gas enters deep within the galaxy and that the interstellar medium of NGC 1272 has been significantly removed. X-ray observations of the surrounding intracluster medium allow us to constrain the pressure within the jet. We find that the standard assumptions of equipartition often used in interpreting other jets underestimate the pressure in the jets by a factor 30.

We report the serendipitous discovery of bent radio jets associated with the galaxy NGC 1272, a massive elliptical galaxy in the Perseus cluster. The jets of NGC 1272 are bent within 2 kpc of the galaxy centre, and their morphology is consistent with being distorted by ram pressure from the motion of NGC 1272 through the ICM. The bent jets provide compelling evidence that the ICM influence extends deep within the potential of the galaxy, and highlight the strong interactions between cluster galaxies and their gaseous environment.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1272 as a dominant Perseus elliptical with a smooth light profile and a rich GC system, estimating about 12,000 globular clusters and S_N=7.9.

quotes.quote: NGC 1272 ( M_V^T = -22.96 ) and NGC 1275 ( M_V^T = -22.61 ) are the two dominant member galaxies of Perseus and have similar visual luminosities. In this respect Perseus is analogous to several other rich clusters where two major E galaxies of roughly equal luminosity dominate (for example, NGC 4472 and 4486 in Virgo, or NGC 4874 and 4889 in Coma). However, NGC 1275 appears to be the closest to the dynamical center of Perseus, based on its projected location virtually at the center of the hot X-ray halo, its radial velocity (5264 km s ^ 1 ) close to the cluster mean, and the dramatic presence of large gas inflow revealed in its extensive gas and dust network of filaments. Although NGC 1272 is also projected fairly close to cluster center, its radial velocity (3815 km s ^ 1 ) is much further from the cluster mean, and its integrated-light profile is smooth and featureless at all radii.

The placement of the ACS fields allows a good opportunity to determine the spatial distribution of the NGC 1272 GC system in particular. ACS-3 contains the galaxy, while ACS-4 and ACS-6 are immediately adjacent and (helpfully) sit on the opposite side from NGC 1275. (ACS-5 is equidistant from both NGC 1272 and 1275 and its GC population might be expected to comprise a roughly equal mixture of the two; see again Fig. fig:radial .) In Figure fig:n1272 (upper panel), the number density _ cl (objects per arcsec ^ 2 ) is plotted for ACS-3,4,6 relative to the center of NGC 1272. Here _ cl decreases smoothly outward, converging to a near-constant level at the largest radii sampled.
We estimate that NGC 1272 contains 12000 GCs with S_N = 7.9 0.9 , while for the central giant NGC 1275 we estimate more crudely N_ GC 13500 and S_N 12 , very similar to M87.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 1272 with Chandra, finding a cool X-ray minicorona whose thermal emission dominates its core while transport processes require suppression, likely by magnetic fields.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The coordinates of NGC 1270 and NGC 1272 are RA (J2000): 49.7419 ; DEC (J2000): +41.4702 and RA (J2000): 49.8385 ; DEC (J2000): +41.4908 , respectively.

quotes.quote: We study the X-ray properties of two elliptical galaxies, NGC 1270 and NGC 1272, in the core of the Perseus cluster with deep Chandra observations. Both galaxies have central supermassive black holes, the mass of which is 6.0 10^ 9 \ M _ and 2.0 10^ 9 \ M _ respectively. Our aim is to examine relatively cool soft X-ray emitting gas within the central region of these massive early-type galaxies.

With thermal emission from the minicorona dominating over any power-law radiation components, we find that both NGC 1270 and NGC 1272 encompass minicoronae, the temperature and radius of which are 0.99 keV and 0.63 keV; 1.4 kpc and 1.2 kpc respectively. For NGC 1272, the thermal coronal component dominates the core emission by a factor of over 10. We show that the depletion time scale of minicoronal gas via viscous stripping is shorter by a factor of 100 than the replenishment time scale due to stellar mass loss.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1272 as NGC 1275’s bright Perseus companion, one magnitude fainter, with a compact globular-cluster distribution extending only to about 100 kpc.

quotes.quote: The GC surface density contours of NGC 1272 and NGC 1275 were fit iteratively, allowing us to separate the GCs of these two giant elliptical galaxies. These galaxies only differ by 1 mag in luminosity, yet the radial distributions of their GCs, plotted in Fig. fig:GC_1d , are remarkably different. Both profiles reveal a flattening towards their cores, consistent with Penny2012 and , and likely due to incompleteness. However, the central 10--20 kpc region of NGC 1275 contains over twice the GC density of NGC 1272. The most obvious difference between the profiles of NGC 1275 and NGC 1272 is the extremely wide distribution of GCs around NGC 1275, extending out to 600 kpc.
On the other hand, NGC 1272 presents a relatively compact distribution that only extends out to 100 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 1272 as the second-brightest Perseus galaxy, a normal giant elliptical whose globular-cluster system has red and blue subpopulations and nearby UCDs.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: The giant elliptical NGC 1272, located at = 03:19:21.3, = +41:29:26, with M_ B = -22.45 refcat is the second brightest galaxy in the Perseus Cluster.

quotes.quote: The giant elliptical NGC 1272, located at = 03:19:21.3, = +41:29:26, with M_ B = -22.45 refcat is the second brightest galaxy in the Perseus Cluster. This galaxy is a normal elliptical, and unlike NGC 1275, is relatively easy to fit and remove from our imaging using iraf ellipse . This allows for the identification of compact stellar systems to smaller galactocentric distances.

The GC system of NGC 1272 also exhibits red and blue subpopulations, with the blue subpopulation exhibiting a blue tilt, with the slope of the fit to the red subpopulation exhibiting a slope consistent with zero within the errors. Similar to NGC 1275, the UCDs in the vicinity of NGC 1272 occupy the bright end of the colour magnitude relation, falling on both the blue and red subpopulations.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1272 as one of Perseus's largest, massive core cD galaxies; including or excluding it and the other brightest cDs tests luminosity-function robustness.

quotes.quote: Only the two largest galaxies in the cluster, NGC 1272 and NGC 1275, required a tile size of 8000 8000 VIS pixels, as illustrated in fig:AP .

Robustness of the derived parameters The three cD galaxies in the core of the cluster (NGC 1275, NGC 1272, and NGC 1265) introduce a discontinuity in the LF that cannot be traced by our simple parametric function given in Eq. ( EqLF ). We thus fit the data including all galaxies with an absolute magnitude M( ) 11.33 , or excluding either NGC 1275, or the three brightest objects NGC 1275, NGC 1272, and NGC 1265. The best fit to the data derived using these three different samples are given in Table table:TabLF . pdfLF shows the probability distribution functions of the output parameters derived when the three cD galaxies are excluded. As expected, the output parameters of the Gaussian function strongly depend on the adopted sample, i.e., on the inclusion or exclusion of the brightest cD galaxies.

gmw_00002562.name: host galaxy of OGLE16aaa
gmw_00002562.overview: The host galaxy of OGLE16aaa is located at a redshift of z=0.1655 [arXiv:2006.11179]. Line ratios from its optical spectrum indicate that the galaxy exhibits both weak active galactic nucleus activity and recent star formation [arXiv:2006.11179].

sources.summary: The paper places OGLE16aaa at its host-galaxy center, notes weak AGN activity and recent star formation, and measures host optical/UV magnitudes for subtraction.

quotes.quote: OGLE16aaa is a TDE candidate, whose discovery was reported by WLZ2017 . It was detected on January 2, 2016 by the OGLE survey at the center of its host galaxy WKU2016ATel . The PESSTO transient survey took an optical spectrum of it on January 17, 2016 Firth2016ATel, WLZ2017 , showing a blue continuum with narrow optical emission lines of H , H , N II, and O III at a common redshift of z=0.1655 WLZ2017 . The optical spectrum resembles the TDE in SDSS J074820.66+471214.6 YCW2013 , and the line ratios suggested that both weak AGN activity and recent star formation were present in the galaxy WLZ2017,OCJ2019 .

We also clearly detected the host galaxy of OGLE16aaa in the u and b filters and used the color correction terms (from the SWIFT-UVOT-CALDB-03-R02 calibration document) to derive the Johnson B magnitude of B = 18.91 0.14 mag. This magnitude is, in fact, slightly higher than the USNO-B1.0 catalog value of B = 18.38 mag (with a typical uncertainty about 0.3 mag), suggesting that we were indeed measuring only the host galaxy contribution. We therefore subtracted these Feb. 9, 2020 host galaxy magnitudes from all photometry taken in 2016--2017 (and propagated the errors), such that only the TDE contribution is shown for the UV filters in Fig. fig:LC (similarly to the host-galaxy subtracted OGLE I and V filter data). The /UVOT magnitudes are consistent with the host galaxy from about MJD 57700 (November 2016) onward, apart from the uvw2 filter data, which is slightly above the host galaxy level.

gmw_00002563.name: PGC 19996
gmw_00002563.overview: PGC 19996 is a galaxy also designated as ESO 491-015 [arXiv:0902.3670]. Its neutral hydrogen profile has been observed and analyzed using both the Green Bank Telescope and the Green Bank 140-foot telescope [arXiv:0902.3670].

sources.summary: The paper uses PGC 19996 as an example EDD entry, showing its LEDA image and comparing GBT and Green Bank 140-foot H I profiles processed through the same pipeline.

quotes.quote: The galaxy seen in this case is PGC 19996 = ESO 491-015. The image of the galaxy is drawn from the LEDA website and displayed with a field of 10 arcmin, roughly the beam size of the GBT, hence a scale reasonable for an inspection for contamination from any near neighbors. The left profile was acquired by the authors with observations using GBT. The right profile is based on observations with the Green Bank 140-foot telescope and was obtained from the Cornell archive.

gmw_00002564.name: PGC 71392
gmw_00002564.overview: PGC 71392 is a massive, fast-rotating hybrid S0-Sa galaxy dominated by its bulge component [arXiv:2203.02885, arXiv:2207.02906]. It is relatively gas-poor [arXiv:2207.02906], exhibits an extremely low star formation rate [arXiv:2203.02885], and hosts a currently quiescent supermassive black hole [arXiv:2203.02885].

sources.summary: The paper presents UGC 12591 as a massive fast-rotating quiescent S0-Sa galaxy with bulge dominance, cold dust, a quiescent SMBH, and severe baryon deficiency.

quotes.quote: We present Hubble Space Telescope (HST) observations of the nearby (z= 0.023), very massive, highly rotating (488.4 12.5 ) hybrid (S0-Sa) galaxy UGC 12591, along with observations taken at other wavelengths from UV to FIR. Deep HST data of the galaxy in V, I and H bands is used to disentangle its bulge and disk components. The surface brightness distributions show a dominance of the bulge component over the disk with H-band bulge-to-disc luminosity ratio 69 . From spectral energy distribution (SED) fitting, we find an extremely low global star formation rate (SFR) of 0.1 - 0.2 y^ 1 , which is unusually low for this galaxy's huge stellar mass ( M_ 1.6 10^ 11 ), that implies a strong quenching of it's SFR.

UGC 12591 is found host to a super massive black hole (SMBH) of mass 6.18 10^ 8 M_ which at the present moment is quiescent, i.e., neither we see large ( > 1 kpc ) radio jets nor is the black hole contributing significantly to the mid-IR SED, ruling out the presence of strong radiative feedback from a bright AGN. We have obtained a detailed census of all observable baryons in this galaxy. The star formation efficiency is very low ( 0.02-0.06 ) and within a virial radius the galaxy has a total observed baryonic mass of 6.87 10^ 11 , amounting to a baryonic deficiency of 80 relative to the cosmological mean. A small fraction of these cosmic baryons reside in a warm/hot circum-galactic halo phase, while the majority of baryons are still not observable.

sources.summary: The paper describes UGC12591 as a gas-poor, massive S0/Sa and fastest-rotating disc whose noisy data still place it on the Tully-Fisher relation.

quotes.quote: Notable exceptions are NGC5635 and UGC12591. The former has an asymmetric rotation curve and, on the approaching side, shows a significant amount of lagging extra-planar gas, which might indicate a recent accretion event Saglia+1988 , also supported by diffuse stellar tails in the optical images. The latter is an extremely massive, lenticular galaxy with relatively little gas, and it is barely detected in our new VLA \ data ( SNR 3 in most channels). Although we decided to model these two galaxies and we keep them in our sample, we note that their kinematics, and thus the following dynamical analysis, may be very uncertain, in particular for UGC12591.

Our sample includes the S0/Sa galaxy UGC12591, which has been known for decades as the fastest rotating disc in the local Universe Giovanelli+1986 . Contrary to recent results by Ray+2022 , we find that this galaxy is consistent with the Tully-Fisher relation within the uncertainties. This discrepancy is ascribable to the fact that Ray+2022 estimated a significantly lower stellar mass ( / 11.2 vs our 11.7) and they used the \ maximum rotation velocity from Giovanelli+1986 , which exceeds 500 in the inner regions, while we use the flat part of the extended \ rotation curve ( 450 ).

sources.summary: The catalog paper identifies PGC 71392 as the widest reliable single-target H I profile in All Digital HI and notes possible central-continuum absorption affecting its spectrum.

quotes.quote: Of reliably measured profiles for apparently single targets, that for PGC 71392 (UGC 12591) is by far the widest, with W_ m50 = 989 . The line profile and an image of the galaxy (typed SO/Sa) are shown in Figure p0071392 . 1986ApJ...301L...7G have drawn attention to this unusual galaxy. That reference notes that there is probably absorption from a central continuum source affecting the spectrum.

gmw_00002565.name: PGC 10314
gmw_00002565.overview: PGC 10314 is a nearly face-on Sc spiral galaxy [arXiv:1509.07094, arXiv:2209.13967] containing a pseudobulge that is actively forming stars [arXiv:1004.5393]. The galaxy features a knotty structure not organized into spiral arms [arXiv:1509.07094, arXiv:astro-ph/0004203], with a flocculent appearance in its innermost disk regions [arXiv:1509.07094].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1058 as a nearly face-on spiral with flocculent inner structure, and reports vertical-velocity changes around its corotation radius and spiral arms.

quotes.quote: NGC 1058 is a nearly face-on spiral galaxy with an inclination angle in the range 4^ to 11^ 2007AJ....134.1952P . The distance to this galaxy is uncertain, ranging from 8.4 Mpc 2000A&A...357..437B to 14.5 Mpc 1974ApJ...194..559S . We adopt a distance of 9.8 Mpc, given by a more recent work 2006ApJS..164...81M , for which 1 \ corresponds to 47.5 pc. It has a clear knotty structure not organized into spiral arms 2000A&A...357..437B , with a complete absence of any well-defined arms in \ emission, and a flocculent appearance of the disk's innermost regions 1995IAUS..164..425F . These features are also observed in the HST image (Figure fig1 ).

In NGC 1058, for PA = 32.5^ and around the corotation radius at 1.5 kpc, there is another example of V_Z across the spiral arm. In the opposite side of the slit, between 1.5 and 2 kpc, just before the spiral arm there is an important change of V_Z=-10 to 15 . Another example of the pattern we are describing as similar to a galactic bore is found at PA = 125^ , between 1 and 1.5 kpc, just before the arm (around 1 kpc) where there is a large vertical deviation.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1058 as a nearby face-on Sc field galaxy with knotty non-spiral structure, metallicity and reddening gradients, and 71 identified young star groupings with compact sizes.

quotes.quote: NGC 1058 is a nearly face-on field Sc galaxy with B=11.89 mag and D _ 25 =3.0 at a distance of 8.4 Mpc. The physical scale of our CCD images is 11 pc/pixel. This galaxy shows a clear knotty structure not organized into spiral arms. Some recent studies by Ferguson et al. (1998a,b) of its H regions indicate the presence of a radial metallicity and reddening gradient in the sense that inner regions are more metal rich and more reddened, a trend often observed in spirals.

As for NGC 7217, Table 2 gives all the relevant data for the set of 71 YSGs identified in this galaxy, whose map is shown in Fig. 6b. The size distribution of Fig. 3 peaks at 50 pc with average value and standard deviation of 104 pc and 109 pc, respectively. There is no clear correlation between the size and the galactocentric distance. In Figure 4 we show the YSG B-band luminosity function; the decreasing tail corresponds to a slope of =-1.33 0.26 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1058 as a nearby Sc galaxy with specified inclination, position angle, optical radius, distance, and stellar mass.

quotes.quote: NGC 1058 is a Sc galaxy (morphological type code T = 5.1 0.9). The inclination angle of NGC 1058 is i = 15 , the position angle of the major axis PA = 145 GarciaGomez2004 . The optical radius is 1.51 arcmin, or 90.6 arcsec RC3 . At the distance of d = 10.6 Mpc Schmidt1994 , the physical optical radius is R_ 25 = 4.66 kpc. The stellar mass (rescaled to the adopted distance) is M _ = 2.51 10 ^ 9 M _ Leroy2019 .

sources.summary: The paper presents PGC 10314 as the narrowest-profile extreme in the database and notes prior work identifying it as an almost face-on galaxy.

quotes.quote: At the other extreme, PGC 10314 (NGC 1058) is the most anorexic of galaxies in the current database. The profile is shown in Figure p0010314 . This galaxy has been identified by 1975A&A....44..147L and 1984A&A...134..258V as an example of a galaxy seen almost face on.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 1058 as a pseudobulge galaxy, citing There is a prominent, nearly face-on spiral, that extends to the central lesssim1 ; n_b=1.1 , and the bulges is actively forming stars with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =0.8 mag.

quotes.quote: NGC 1058 .SAT5../Sc(s) Pseudobulge. There is a prominent, nearly face-on spiral, that extends to the central lesssim1 . The decomposition yields n_b=1.1 , and the bulges is actively forming stars with M_ 3.6 M_ 8.0 =0.8 mag.

gmw_00002566.name: NGC 7814
gmw_00002566.overview: NGC 7814 is an early-type, bulge-dominated spiral galaxy [arXiv:1606.09251, arXiv:1802.09967] viewed almost perfectly edge-on [arXiv:1206.6231]. It features a faint stellar disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0702486], a straight dust layer [arXiv:astro-ph/0702486], and a massive stellar halo with a bright outer envelope [arXiv:1611.05448, arXiv:0904.2777].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 7814 as a bulge-dominated edge-on disk galaxy whose Spitzer photometry and rotation curve provide a strong MOND test with improved bulge-disk modeling.

quotes.quote: The bulge dominated galaxy NGC 7814 provides one of the strongest dynamical tests possible for Modified Newtonian Dynamics (MOND). Spitzer 3.6 m photometry fixes the bulge parameterisation and strongly constrains the properties of the subdominant stellar disk. Furthermore, the distance is known to better than 5 , virtually eliminating it as a free parameter. The rotation curve is easily measured, since the H I (and stellar) disks are edge on, and both the receding and approaching sides agree very well.

The bulge dominated galaxy NGC 7814 has an edge on stellar disk and a regular H I disk. The bulge-disk decomposition performed by FSK11 suggested that an impossibly large mass-to-light ratio for the stellar disk was required in order to fit the rotation curve of the galaxy. Here we used two S e rsic profiles to model the projected luminosity of the bulge such that one profile is used below 0.5 kpc and the other is used beyond 0.5 kpc. This allows a superior fit to the observed surface brightness, especially below 0.3 kpc and between 2 and 4.5 kpc.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7814 as the most distant GHOSTS galaxy, warns about shallow/crowded data, derives steep halo profiles, and finds the largest stellar halo in the sample.

quotes.quote: NGC 7814 NGC 7814 is the most distant galaxy in our sample; as can be seen in Fig. \ CMDs , the CMDs are relatively shallow and are limited in their colour coverage towards the red fainter than F814W 27.2 by a relatively shallow F606W limit. Accordingly, we caution that our stellar counts may be somewhat less reliable than for our other galaxies, and may represent a lower limit to the true value. Furthermore, the innermost fields in GHOSTS suffer from significant crowding.

NGC 7814 has power slopes of 3.71^ +0.99 _ 0.09 0.2 along its minor axis, and 5.33^ +3.34 _ 0.57 0.2 along its major axis. The implied axis ratio is 0.59^ +0.14 _ 0.05 0.1 . The implied stellar mass between 10 and 40kpc is 2.05^ +0.43 _ 0.26 0.6 10^9 M_ , or a estimated total stellar halo mass of 6.41^ +1.34 _ 0.81 10^9 M_ , corresponding to 14 6 of its total stellar mass. This is the largest stellar halo in our sample .

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 7814 as a bulge-dominated high-surface-brightness spiral where the automated maximum-disc fit uses a prior to break bulge-disc degeneracy.

quotes.quote: NGC 7814 fig:bulgeexamples (left) shows the classic bulge-dominated HSB spiral NGC 7814. Our automated algorithm gives = 0.76 0.05 and = 0.76 0.06 , which are acceptable in terms of stellar populations. The bulge component nicely reproduces the inner declining portion of the rotation curve and dominates over the disc out to the last measured point. In fig:ngc_7814 (top panels), we show the key role of the > prior to achieve sensible fits.

Without this prior (left), the algorithm reproduces at 3 R 6 kpc but fails to reproduce the inner points at R 3 kpc. This happens because the declining portion of the rotation curve is not well sampled, so the weighting function w(R) prioritizes the disc over the bulge to achieve baryonic maximality out to the largest possible radii. The bulge has no room left to reproduce the inner declining portion of the rotation curve: increasing V_ bul would overshoot V_ obs at intermediate radii.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 7814 as a bulge-dominated Sab example with central light enhancement, a steep inner rotation rise, outer flattening, and baryons explaining only the inner curve.

quotes.quote: The Choice of the Characteristic Radius Figure fig:massmodels shows mass models for two galaxies with diametrically opposed properties: a bulge-dominated spiral galaxy (NGC 7814) and a gas-dominated dwarf galaxy (UGCA 442).

NGC 7814 (left) is an early-type spiral (Sab). The surface brightness profile shows a central light enanchement due to the bulge. The rotation curve displays a very steep rise in the inner parts, a decline of 50 km s ^ 1 , and a flat outer portion.

For _ =0.5 M_ / L_ baryons explain the inner parts of the rotation curve, while DM is needed at large radii. Note that R_ eff occurs along the declining part of the rotation curve, while R_ 2.2 occurs along the flat part: R_ bar is the only radius that captures the concept of baryonic maximality.

sources.summary: NGC 7814 is paired with NGC 891 as an edge-on spiral with a similar rotation curve but opposite morphology; RCFM fits both and captures differing outer inflections.

quotes.quote: NGC 7814 and NGC 891 The spiral galaxies NGC 7814 and NGC 891 present an interesting challenge to dark matter and MOND models. Both galaxies are presented edge-on on the sky, and both have essentially identical RCs, but are extreme opposites in morphologies. NGC 7814 is a bulge dominated galaxy and NGC 891 is almost entirely a disk galaxy.

Frat1 asks ``why are these RCs so identical if their dark matter halos are necessarily different to accommodate the differences in the luminous mass?'' In the RCFM paradigm the two RCs are very similar in magnitude, but differ in their inflections at large radii; NGC 7814 inflects up, whereas NGC 891 inflects down. RCFM fits to these galaxies are successful, see Fig. fitCompare7814_891 and Tables  tab:taleOftwoGalaxies7814 and tab:taleOftwoGalaxies .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7814 as an early-type, spheroid-dominated galaxy with a straight dust layer, faint disk, no main truncation, and possible faint extraplanar H I.

quotes.quote: NGC 7814 is bulge dominated and no truncation in the surface brightness distribution exists, although there is some evidence that the faint disk displays one, which becomes visible after subtraction of the exyended R^ 1/4 spheroid in the surface brightness maps. For NGC 5023 it is smaller than 1.1, because the truncation becomes noticable only at very fainter levels and in NGC 7814 because the light is dominated by the spheroid. The cases of NGC 5023 and NGC 7814 show that disks without evidence for truncation or bulge-dominated galaxies will have this latter ratio close to 1.0.

The early-type galaxy NGC 7814 is very much spheroid-dominated. Yet, there is a straight dust-layer and a relatively faint disk. Recent deep Westerbrok observations (Kamphuis et al., 2006) have shown evidence for extra-planar \ at faint levels.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 7814 as one of four lenticular or early-type spiral galaxies with detailed kinematics, finding its bulge-plus-disc dynamics fit without requiring an inner dark halo.

quotes.quote: The model is successfully applied to reproduce the stellar kinematic and photometry of the bulge of the 4 lenticular and early-type spiral galaxies NGC 4565, NGC 7814, NGC 5866 and NGC 4594. For these galaxies detailed stellar kinematical data are available at different positions across the galaxy. The application of our models shows that: 1) For all the galaxies considered in this work, an isotropic model is able to reproduce the whole dynamical data.

2) We do not need a dark mass halo to reproduce these data. This do not mean that a dark halo is not present, but just that its dynamical effects in these inner regions of the galaxies are negligible.

sources.summary: NGC 7814 is the bulge-dominated member of the edge-on pair; its new rotation curve shows mass and light more centrally concentrated than in NGC 891.

quotes.quote: The two edge-on galaxies NGC 891 and NGC 7814 are representative of two extreme morphologies: the former is disk-dominated while the latter is almost entirely bulge-dominated.

We have derived new rotation curves from recent, high-sensitivity \ observations and have found that the shapes of the rotation curves are significantly different for the two galaxies. They indicate that in NGC 7814 the mass is more concentrated to the centre as compared to NGC 891. This reflects the distribution of light which is more centrally concentrated in NGC 7814 than in NGC 891.

sources.summary: The review cites NGC 7814 as a stringent MOND test: an edge-on disk galaxy with accurate distance, high-quality infrared photometry, and model rotation-curve mass-to-light ratios matching expectations.

quotes.quote: Among these, an interesting case is NGC 7814: this galaxy is almost perfectly edge-on and the uncertainties on the rotation curve deriving from the disk inclination are negligible; in addition, the distance to the galaxy is accurate to 5 and is basically not a free parameter any longer. This galaxy provides a stringent test for MOND: high-quality infrared photometric observations enable, for the first time, the construction, from an accurate bulge-disk decomposition, of a three-dimensional model of the galaxy whose gravitational potential is inferred by numerically solving the MONDian Poisson equation. The comparison of the model rotation curve with the observed one allows the derivation of the mass-to-light ratios for both the disk and the bulge components. Both ratios are found to be in excellent agreement with the expected values .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 7814 as a farther GHOSTS disk galaxy, bins its fields for comparable spatial scales, and finds a stellar-halo colour gradient.

quotes.quote: However, NGC 0891, NGC 4565 and NGC 7814 are at further distances, indicated in Table table:trgb . Therefore, the FoVs of the fields located around these galaxies cover larger linear extensions, having side lengths from 9 to 14 kpc. In order to obtain colour measurements that represent the properties of stars from similar spatial regions, we divide each field of the more distant galaxies in either three or four radial bins, such that each region for which a colour measurement is made covers 3 kpc on a side.

Half of the galaxies (NGC 4565, NGC 0891, and NGC 7814) show fits consistent with stellar halo colour gradients whereas the remaining three galaxies (NGC 0253, NGC 3031, and NGC 4945) have rather flat colour profile.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 7814 as a cautionary example where Arecibo beam size loses flux from the galaxy outskirts and changes the measured profile width.

quotes.quote: The galaxy NGC 7814 has a diameter at the B band isophot of 25 mag as ^ 2 of 5.5^ , larger than the Arecibo beam but much smaller than the 140-foot beam. Flux is lost in a single beam pointing with Arecibo Telescope but not with a pointing involving the smaller telescope. The lost flux from the extremities of the galaxy with the Arecibo observation cause a pronounced reduction in the `horns' and affects the measurement of the profile width.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 7814 as a massive, bulge-dominated GHOSTS galaxy with a bright outer envelope, a -2.5 profile at 20 kpc, and high stellar-envelope metallicity.

quotes.quote: At 20 kpc NGC 891, NGC 4565, and NGC 7814 have power law profiles with a slope of about -2.5.

At first sight, the envelope luminosity at 20 kpc seems correlated with Hubble type and bulge-to-disk ratio, with the bulge dominated NGC 7814 being the brightest and the late-type spiral NGC 5236 showing only an uncertain hint of an envelope beyond 30 kpc.
The more massive galaxies (NGC 891, NGC 3031, NGC 4565, NGC 7814) have significantly higher metallicities than the lower mass galaxies (IC5052, NGC4244, NGC7793) as has been seen before e.g., Mou07 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 7814 as an inclination outlier: its edge-on thin disk and prominent bulge make optical b/a underestimate the kinematic inclination, a case handled by the fiducial q prior.

quotes.quote: The optical inclination of NGC 7814 is a severe underestimate of the kinematic value. The SDSS image reveals it to be an edge-on thin disk with an extremely prominent bulge, resulting in a high b/a . The flat prior on q in our fiducial model accounts for this, as q=1 is appropriate for a bulge.

gmw_00002567.name: IC 2511
gmw_00002567.overview: IC 2511 is a galaxy characterized by an asymmetric neutral hydrogen profile that features a wing on its short-wavelength side [arXiv:0902.3670]. Despite this profile irregularity, the galaxy's optical image displays no visible abnormalities [arXiv:0902.3670].

sources.summary: The paper classifies IC 2511 as an ugly but usable H I profile, with a short-wavelength wing yet no abnormality visible in the galaxy image.

quotes.quote: With IC 2511 seen in Figure p0028246 the profile is merely ugly. There is a wing on the short wavelength side. The linewidth is sensitive to the choice of measurement flux level, but not sufficiently to cause us to reject the profile by assigning an error greater than 20 . There is no evidence of an abnormality in the image of the galaxy.

gmw_00002568.name: host galaxy of GRB 111117A
gmw_00002568.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 111117A is a late-type galaxy located at a spectroscopic redshift of z = 2.211 [arXiv:1707.01452]. It contains a population of relatively young stars and exhibits unusually high rest-frame X-ray derived hydrogen column density [arXiv:1707.01452].

sources.summary: The paper measures the GRB 111117A host at z = 2.211, superseding a lower photometric redshift, and describes it as a young, late-type sGRB host with unusually high X-ray absorption.

quotes.quote: Here, we present spectroscopy of the host galaxy of GRB 111117A and measure its redshift to be z = 2.211 . This makes GRB 111117A the most distant high-confidence short duration GRB detected to date. Our spectroscopic redshift supersedes a lower, previously estimated photometric redshift value for this burst. We use the spectroscopic redshift, as well as new imaging data to constrain the nature of the host galaxy and the physical parameters of the GRB. The rest-frame X-ray derived hydrogen column density, for example, is the highest compared to a complete sample of sGRBs and seems to follow the evolution with redshift as traced by the hosts of long GRBs (lGRBs).

From the clear host association, GRB 111117A does not belong to the hostless class of GRBs Berger2010a and because the host exhibits emission lines this is indicative of a population of relatively young stars. As the majority of sGRBs Fong2013b , the host of GRB 111117A is therefore a late-type galaxy and is entirely consistent in terms of stellar mass and stellar age with the general population of sGRB hosts ( M _* = 10^ 10.1 M_ and _* = 0.3 Gyr) Leibler2010 . Being a late-type host, both the stellar mass and sSFR are entirely within the range expected for the hosts of sGRBs Behroozi2014 .

gmw_00002569.name: 2MASX J11443212+2006238
gmw_00002569.overview: 2MASX J11443212+2006238 is a red galaxy located in the Abell 1367 cluster [arXiv:1709.01735, arXiv:1703.10301]. It exhibits an 85-kiloparsec ionized-gas tail [arXiv:1709.01735] and a morphology that indicates it is undergoing strong ram-pressure stripping [arXiv:1703.10301].

sources.summary: The paper maps 2MASX J11443212+2006238 in Abell 1367, detecting the full 85 kpc H emission tail and interpreting the visible wake timescale as ram-pressure stripping during cluster passage.

quotes.quote: We report the detection of an H trail of 85 kpc projected length behind galaxy 2MASX J11443212+2006238 in the nearby cluster of galaxies Abell 1367. This galaxy was discovered to possess an extended component in earlier, deeper H observations carried out with the Subaru telescope. However, lying at the border of the Subaru field, the extended H tail was cut out, preventing the determination of its full extent. We fully map this extent here, albeit the shallower exposure.

Fig. NET shows the continuum subtracted H image of our field smoothed by 2.8 arcsec, from which we measured the extent of the diffuse flux associated to 2MASX J11443212+2006238. Assuming a distance from Abell 1367 of 95 Mpc, the tail extends in the NE direction for 85 kpc . Such a distance can be covered in 85 Myr by a galaxy travelling at approximately 1000 km s^ 1 , which is the typical velocity of galaxies in clusters. Given that this length is typical in Abell 1367 (the tail length ranges from 15 to 230 kpc in this cluster, Yagi et al. 2017), 85-100 Myr can be assumed as the typical time during which the tails remain visible.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2MASX J11443212+2006238 as an Abell 1367 galaxy with a detected ionized-gas extension, red color, residual central star formation, and morphology implying strong ram-pressure stripping.

quotes.quote: The EIG of 2MASX J11443212+2006238 was detected at the very edge of our field fig:FC1 . The EIG may have a much longer extension.

Interestingly, the parent galaxy is red, as shown in Section sec:CMD . We checked g-i color in Consolandi2016 and confirmed that g-i is also red ( g-i = 1.03 ). In the R image, the central 15'' region shows an excess, which may be the remaining star formation, though the R color is unreliable for \ estimator in the galactic disk.

In the \ image, the southern half of the galaxy shows a sign of negative R, while positive in the northern half. This morphology resembles IC 4040 in the Coma cluster Yoshida2008 and implies that 2MASX J11443212+2006238 suffers strong ram-pressure stripping from the south direction.

gmw_00002570.name: LEDA 1051269
gmw_00002570.overview: LEDA 1051269 is a spiral galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.0338 [arXiv:1911.12083]. It is a moderately star-forming galaxy with a host-wide star formation rate of approximately 3 solar masses per year [arXiv:1911.12083].

sources.summary: The paper identifies LEDA 1051269 as the z=0.0338 spiral host of DES17X1boj, with a nuclear transient position and host-wide SFR near 3 solar masses per year.

quotes.quote: The location of DES17X1boj is consistent with the nucleus of a spiral galaxy LEDA 1051269, Brouty2003 at z=0.0338 , while DES16E2bjy appears to be associated with a red elliptical galaxy at z=0.1305 with a separation of 5 (11.6 kpc).

We estimated the Star-Formation Rate (SFR) for the host of DES17X1boj within the 2 aperture to be 2.00 0.03 10^ 2 M _ yr ^ 1 based on the observed H flux using the formula of Kennicutt1998 . Extrapolating this over the whole galaxy results in SFR 3 M _ yr ^ 1 , corresponding to specific star-formation rate log(sSFR) =-9.3 . While the given values are likely underestimated due to the central location of the aperture, they demonstrates that the host is moderately star-forming.

gmw_00002571.name: host galaxy of DES16E2bjy
gmw_00002571.overview: The host galaxy of DES16E2bjy is a passive elliptical galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.1305 [arXiv:1911.12083]. Although it lacks optical signatures of recent star formation, it is relatively bright in the ultraviolet, indicating a low level of residual star formation [arXiv:1911.12083].

sources.summary: The paper describes the host galaxy of DES16E2bjy as a z=0.1305 red elliptical/passive galaxy 5 arcsec from the transient, UV-bright for a passive system but with low residual star formation.

quotes.quote: The location of DES17X1boj is consistent with the nucleus of a spiral galaxy LEDA 1051269, Brouty2003 at z=0.0338 , while DES16E2bjy appears to be associated with a red elliptical galaxy at z=0.1305 with a separation of 5 (11.6 kpc).

Based on the Figure fig:host_spectra , it is clear that the hosts of these two transients are different: DES17X1boj happened in a star-forming galaxy characterised by numerous strong nebular emissino lines, while the host of DES16E2bjy is a passive galaxy with no obvious signatures of recent star formation in the optical spectrum (e.g. H , [ OIII ] , [ NII ] ).

Apart from DES-SN optical photometry, the host galaxy of DES16E2bjy has also been detected in the ultraviolet (UV) regime by The Galaxy Evolution Explorer GALEX; Martin2005 in both far UV (FUV, 1350-1750 Å in observer frame) and near UV (NUV, and 1750-2750 Å). The UV magnitudes ( FUV = 22.75 0.07 and NUV =22.56 0.05 ) correspond to colors FUV =3.99 and NUV =3.79 indicating that the host is rather bright in UV for a such passive galaxy in the optical see e.g. Petty2013 . Assuming that all the UV emission of the galaxy comes from some residual star-formation still occurring in the galaxy we find SFR =0.14 M _ yr ^ 1 using the formula from Kennicutt1998 and corresponding specific star-formation rate of log(sSFR) =-11.3 . While these values demonstrate that some star-formation could still be happening in the host, its level is fairly low.

gmw_00002572.name: XLEO J0332 2744
gmw_00002572.overview: XLEO J0332 2744 is an X-ray line-emitting active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0502091]. Optical spectroscopy of the source reveals emission lines at a redshift of 0.665, while X-ray observations show flux variability [arXiv:astro-ph/0502091].

sources.summary: The paper presents XLEO J0332 2744 as an X-ray line-emitting AGN in the CDFS, with an XMM line redshift validated by optical emission-line spectroscopy and Chandra/XMM variability.

quotes.quote: XLEO J0332 2744 was detected in the analysis of observation n. 7 (see Table 1). The discovery scan (a box of 40'' 40'' 200 eV was used) is shown in Figure 1. An excess in the count distribution is clearly visible at 3.8 keV, where 9 counts are recorded.

The optical spectrum (from http://cencosw.oamp.fr/VVDS/CDFS.html) is reproduced in Figure 2; it shows two prominent emission lines identified as [ OII ] 3727 and [ OIII ] 5007 \ from which a redshift z_o = 0.665 is derived (Szokoly et al., 2004). This is in excellent agreement with the X-ray determination, and thus validates our XLEO detection. We have also inspected the 1Ms Chandra ACIS-I observation. The resulting 2-10 keV flux is 2.6 times higher than the XMM-Newton flux, further implying source variability.

gmw_00002573.name: 2WHSP J035257.4 683117
gmw_00002573.overview: 2WHSP J035257.4 683117 is a known blazar that exhibits hard X-ray and gamma-ray detections [arXiv:1609.05808]. Although it has not yet been detected at TeV energies, it is considered a promising target for next-generation TeV telescopes [arXiv:1609.05808].

sources.summary: The paper describes 2WHSP J035257.4 683117 as a known blazar with hard X-ray and other high-energy detections, no TeV detection, and promise for next-generation TeV telescopes.

quotes.quote: 2WHSP J035257.4 683117 . This is a known blazar with 18.1 . It has hard X-ray and ray detections but no TeV detection yet. This source might be a good target for next generation TeV telescopes. This source is not in 5BZCat yet.

gmw_00002574.name: 2WHSP J215305.2 004229
gmw_00002574.overview: 2WHSP J215305.2 004229 is a blazar and an X-ray active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1609.05808]. The source exhibits a very hard X-ray spectrum with compact emission, making it a promising TeV candidate [arXiv:1609.05808].

sources.summary: The paper presents 2WHSP J215305.2 004229 as a hard-X-ray HSP candidate whose compact X-ray emission is unlikely to be cluster-related and whose high-energy data make it a promising TeV candidate.

quotes.quote: 2WHSP J215305.2 004229 (5BZBJ2153 0042) . This source has a very hard X-ray spectrum and the SED in the X-ray band keeps increasing up to the highest energies, implying a larger that 10^ 18 Hz. The X-ray emission is not likely to be related to a cluster of galaxy as it is compact. It has ray data and may be a good TeV candidate source.

gmw_00002575.name: GOODS-S ID 31759
gmw_00002575.overview: GOODS-S ID 31759 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate with broad-band photometry that strongly favors a redshift between 6.02 and 6.33 [arXiv:1808.01847]. It is identified as a weak-Balmer-break outlier, though a spectral feature may depress this measurement [arXiv:2309.02504].

sources.summary: The paper reports two emission lines in GOODS-S ID 31759, weighing a low-redshift [O II] interpretation against a photometry-favored high-redshift line solution.

quotes.quote: In one further galaxy, GOODS-S ID 31759 we detect two emission lines that are separated by 6 \ and are consistent with the [ OII ] emission doublet (3726/3729 ) from a galaxy at z=1.393. This would be a low luminosity [ OII ] emitter with Log(L( [ OII ] ))=41.2. However there is a strong sky-line exactly between the two components: therefore it could be also possible that the line is actually \ at z=6.34, which appears split into two components because of the strong sky-line. In this latter case the line would be intrinsically very bright since the measured flux (with no correction) is already 1.7 10^ 17 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 . The broad band photometry of this object actually strongly favors the high redshift solution, with the photometric redshift constrained between 6.02 and 6.33 at 68 confidence.

sources.summary: The paper treats JADES-18846 as a weak-Balmer-break outlier, but cautions that a spectral feature may depress the measurement and place the true value near simulation limits.

quotes.quote: However, we find three clear candidates (JADES-18846 and 10058975, MACS0647-3754) which show Balmer breaks that deviate significantly from the simulated data such that they are consistently lower than those predicted by the simulations, even when considering the 2.2-97.8th percentile variations from the median in FLARES and the measurement uncertainties of the break strength.

JADES-18846 (fig. fig_spec_18846 ) also reveals a scenario where a spectral feature complicates the break measurement. The estimated errors are very small due to a relatively clean spectrum in the break region, but the spectral feature pushes the break strength down slightly. The true Balmer break strength could potentially be closer to 1 , which would bring this galaxy right to the very edge of the 97.8th percentile of the simulated FLARES distribution.

gmw_00002576.name: NGC 4490
gmw_00002576.overview: NGC 4490 is a highly inclined barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:2405.06016, arXiv:2108.10909] that is dynamically interacting with its smaller companion, NGC 4485 [arXiv:2405.06016, arXiv:0904.0337]. This interaction has triggered active star formation throughout the disk [arXiv:1606.01699, arXiv:1009.4396] and produced an unusually flat metallicity gradient [arXiv:1308.5545].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4490 as the main disk of the Cocoon pair, a young star-forming interacting system with giant H I tails and radio emission from its disk.

quotes.quote: NGC 4490 is a very special case of an interacting system. Despite the small distance between member galaxies of the pair and the ``cocoon'' outlook, tidal forces have not yet disrupted its disk, as the interaction is just starting. The aforementioned stream of intergalactic star-forming regions between the galaxies, accompanied by a bridge of radio emission is the single hint of this process. Two other phenomena that are usually explained on the basis of tidal interactions -- giant neutral gas tails, and generally rapid star formation -- are not triggered by the merging process. clemens_hi suggested that NGC 4490 is a very young galaxy that rapidly forms stars at a constant rate and the giant tails result from the wind caused by frequent supernovae explosions.

Among the most distinctive features of the NGC 4490 85 system are the giant neutral hydrogen tails that extend north and south from NGC 4490. The unusual H i morphology of this pair was first reported by dd , who used the Jodrell Bank Mark II telescope to create square grids of pointings for a selected list of nearby galaxies. For NGC 4490, they noticed that the gaseous halo is much larger in diameter than the optical counterpart.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4490 as an interacting barred spiral host with a flattened, velocity-coherent satellite system that is rare among IllustrisTNG analogs.

quotes.quote: Recently, 2024MNRAS.528.2805K presented the NGC 4490 galaxy and its surrounding objects as another example of a flattened and kinematically coherent structure similar to the known planes of satellite galaxies. They show that the on-sky distribution of the objects associated with the NGC 4490 group is flattened along the Supergalactic plane, and exhibits a strong velocity coherence consistent with rotation that is also traced by the extended \ structure surrounding the host. They furthermore report the discovery of an additional stellar system, a diffuse Plume, that is aligned with the flattened structure.

The barred spiral galaxy NGC 4490 and its very nearby companion NGC 4485 are an interacting pair of low-mass galaxies. However, the secondary galaxy, NGC 4485, has only about 5 \ of the K band luminosity of the major galaxy. The pair can thus be considered as a hierarchically merging system, in contrast to a major merger that would require a more equal mass ratio.
The NGC 4490 group is a 3 outlier based on our analysis employing Illustris TNG-50. This is consistent with estimates found for other galaxies in the Local Volume.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes Chandra observations of NGC 4490, deriving hot-ISM properties in its disk and halo and connecting X-ray morphology to interaction-driven star formation and outflows.

quotes.quote: We present an analysis of the hot interstellar medium (ISM) in the spiral galaxy NGC 4490, which is interacting with the irregular galaxy NGC 4485, using 100 ks of Chandra ACIS-S observations. The high angular resolution of Chandra enables us to remove discrete sources and perform spatially resolved spectroscopy for the star forming regions and associated outflows, allowing us to look at how the physical properties of the hot ISM such as temperature, hydrogen column density and metal abundances vary throughout these galaxies. We find temperatures of >0.41 keV and 0.85_ 0.12 ^ +0.59 keV, electron densities of >1.87 ^ 1/2 10^ 3 cm ^ 3 and 0.21_ 0.04 ^ +0.03 ^ 1/2 10^ 3 cm ^ 3 , and hot gas masses of >1.1 ^ 1/2 10^ 7 M _ and 3.7 ^ 1/2 10^ 7 M _ in the plane and halo of NGC 4490 respectively, where is the filling factor of the hot gas.

NGC 4490 is a type SB(s)d spiral galaxy, and its companion, NGC 4485, is a type IB(s)m irregular galaxy devaucouleurs . The optical isophotes of NGC 4490 indicate that its disk has a high inclination of 60^ bertola , which allows us to separate emission from the hot gaseous medium in the plane and the halo of the galaxy. These galaxies are embedded in an unusually large envelope of HI gas viallefond , which clemens98 suggest may have been formed by star formation driven outflows. clemens99 studied the thermal and synchrotron components of the radio continuum emission from these galaxies and concluded that active star formation has been progressing throughout the disk of NGC 4490 at an approximately constant rate of 4.7 M _ yr ^ 1 for at least 10^ 8 years.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4490 as a highly inclined interacting SBm galaxy with measured radio fluxes, offset radio and 24 micron peaks, and halo emission tracing cosmic-ray propagation.

quotes.quote: (v) NGC 4490: This is a SBm type of galaxy, seen highly inclined. This galaxy was observed in L-band using VLA Condon1987ApJS , and measured flux density was 774 mJy. This galaxy has also been observed by GMRT at 0.61 GHz by Nikiel-Wroczy2016MNRAS and measured its total flux density to be 1426 116 mJy. Their measured flux density of this interacting galaxy pair in L-band is 800 41 mJy. Our measured flux densities for this galaxy are 1990 100 and 850 20 mJy from our 0.33 GHz data and 1.4 GHz VLA archival data respectively, which yields galaxy integrated spectrum of 0.59 0.07 .

Significant halo emission is seen in its 0.33 GHz map, which has no counterpart in the 24 m image, indicating propagation of CRe from the sites of production. It is interacting with its smaller companion NGC 4485 seen towards North West.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4490 as the brighter member of a disrupted interacting pair with NGC 4485, classifies it as SB(s)d, notes multiwavelength study, and adopts a 9.6 Mpc distance.

quotes.quote: NGC 4490 is the brighter member of a closely interacting pair of galaxies, variously classified as a spiral or irregular system. In RC3 rc3 NGC 4490 is classified as type SB(s)d, and the smaller companion NGC 4485 as type IB(s)m. Both galaxies show signs of tidal disruption (see Fig. fig:sn08axseq ). NGC 4485/90 is well studied over a wide range of wavelengths (from radio to X-rays).

The distance is the dominant source of uncertainty in the calibration of the luminosity of SN 2008ax, as there is some discrepancy among published distances of NGC 4490. Here we adopt a distance of 9.6 1.3 Mpc ( =29.92 0.29 mag), derived by P08 averaging several different estimates, including Tully-Fisher, Sosies and kinematic distances.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 4490 as the barred spiral host of SN 2008ax, describing its interaction with NGC 4485, H I envelope, high star-formation rate, and distance estimates.

quotes.quote: SN 2008ax (see Fig. fig1 ) was discovered independently by on March 3.45 UT mos08 and on March 4.62 UT nak08 in the nearby barred spiral galaxy (type SBcd) NGC 4490.

NGC 4490 and NGC 4485 are a well known pair of late-type, interacting galaxies embedded in an extended and asymmetric H I envelope huc80 , which is elongated perpendicularly to the plane of NGC 4490. cle98 claim that the configuration of the neutral hydrogen envelope might result from a bipolar outflow of H I driven by starburst activity / SN explosions, and not from the interaction between the two companion galaxies. This is in agreement with the particularly high star formation rate estimated in NGC 4490 via80,thr89,cle99 . The distance modulus ( ) for NGC 4490 is not well constrained.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4490 as an edge-on SBm galaxy with a knotty center, several disk ULX candidates, and compact FIRST radio emission near the nucleus.

quotes.quote: ULX 1 in NGC 4490 NGC 4490 is a galaxy classified as SBm, a system close to be edge-on. It presents a knotty center but does not exhibit apparent nucleus or bulge. Sw04 listed 5 potential ULXs associated to this galaxy. After close inspection we found that 4 of them are within the galaxy disk. The FIRST radio emission is limited to a smaller region close to the nucleus rather than covering the whole disk.

sources.summary: NGC 4490 is described as a nearby interacting galaxy with subsolar oxygen abundances and an unusually flat metallicity gradient likely linked to interaction with NGC 4485.

quotes.quote: NGC 4490 (see Fig. sn82F ) is a nearby, interacting galaxy that hosted SN 1982F and also the Type IIb SN 2008ax taubenberger11 . pilyugin07 published some metallicity measurements that we have complemented with our N2 estimates. In this case we placed the slit through a small H ii region close to the location of SN 1982F and through a bright H ii region closer to the center.
