gmw_00002601.overview: SUMSS J113032 780105 is a BL Lacertae object and blazar located at a redshift of z = 0.3171 [arXiv:2401.07911]. Its active galactic nucleus is hosted by a luminous galaxy with an absolute magnitude of M_R = -23.2 [arXiv:2401.07911].

sources.summary: VLT/FORS spectroscopy of SUMSS J113032 780105 detects multiple host-galaxy absorption features, measures z=0.3171, and finds a luminous host.

quotes.quote: SUMSS J113032 780105 A moderate S/N (72) spectrum obtained with CTIO/COSMOS for 3400 s has been taken by Des19 . We performed two observations of the source with VLT/FORS for a total exposure of 4790 s. An high S/N = 119 spectrum (Fig. fig_spec2 , second row panel, on the left) allows us to detect several absorption features (Ca II HK, CaIG, Mg _ b , Ca Fe, NaID), leading to a measured z = 0.3171 0.0002, compatible with the value presented in Marais van Soelen (in prep.). The host galaxy is quite luminous at M_ R = 23.2.

gmw_00002602.name: RBS 1424
gmw_00002602.overview: RBS 1424 is classified as a BL Lacertae object and a blazar [arXiv:2401.07911]. High signal-to-noise ratio spectra of this active galactic nucleus are featureless, meaning its redshift remains undetermined [arXiv:2401.07911].

sources.summary: SALT/RSS observations of RBS 1424 produce another high-S/N featureless spectrum, so its redshift remains undetermined.

quotes.quote: RBS 1424 Two featureless high S/N spectra obtained with VLT and GTC have been reported by Sbar06b and Pai20 , respectively. We observed the source with SALT/RSS for 2390 s. Our high S/N ( 130) spectrum (Fig. fig_spec2 , third row panel, on the left) is featureless too. The redshift of the source remains undetermined.

gmw_00002603.name: RBS 1457
gmw_00002603.overview: RBS 1457 is classified as a BL Lacertae object and a blazar [arXiv:2401.07911]. Optical spectra obtained for this active galactic nucleus are featureless, leaving its suggested redshift lower limit unconfirmed [arXiv:2401.07911].

sources.summary: VLT/FORS observations of RBS 1457 remain featureless and cannot confirm the previously suggested Mg II absorber redshift lower limit.

quotes.quote: RBS 1457 The spectrum obtained with the Hale Telescope at Mt. Palomar and presented in Shaw13 claims the detection of an MgII absorber at 3380 , corresponding to a redshift lower limit of z 0.21. Pir07 and Pai20 find featureless spectra, obtained with the ESO 3.6 m and the GTC, but their range does not cover the possible absorber. We observed the source with VLT/FORS for 1950 s. The high S/N ( 120) spectrum obtained (Fig. fig_spec2 , third row panel, on the right), which starts at 5100 , is featureless too. As in the previous observations, due to the lack of coverage for the wavelength at which the MgII absorber should be detected, with our spectrum we cannot confirm or revise the redshift lower limit suggested by Shaw13 .

gmw_00002604.name: PMN J1539 1128
gmw_00002604.overview: PMN J1539 1128 is a BL Lacertae object and blazar [arXiv:2012.05176, arXiv:2401.07911]. Although early observations showed a featureless spectrum [arXiv:2012.05176, arXiv:2401.07911], subsequent spectroscopy detected Ca II H&K and [O II] lines, revealing a luminous host galaxy at a redshift of z = 0.4420 [arXiv:2401.07911].

sources.summary: The paper observes PMN J1539 1128 with EFOSC2 but finds no spectral features, leaving its redshift unknown.

quotes.quote: A spectrum of PMN J1539 1128 was reported by Pen17 . Using the Goodman spectrograph at the SOAR telescope they obtained a featureless spectrum with S/N around 100. We observed the source with EFOSC2 using Gr 6 for 5400 sec obtaining a spectrum with median S/N = 80 (see Fig. fig_spec2 , first row, left). With the exception of the Galactic NaID, no spectral feature was detected and the redshift of PMN J1539 1128 remains unknown.

sources.summary: SALT/RSS spectra of PMN J1539 1128 detect Ca II H K and [O II], determining z=0.4420 and a luminous host galaxy.

quotes.quote: PMN J1539 1128 Two intermediate S/N spectra are presented in Pen17,Gol21 , both of them featureless. We observed this BL Lac two times at separation of one day with SALT/RSS for a total exposure of 4380 s. In our high S/N ( 150) SALT/RSS spectrum (Fig. fig_spec2 , bottom panel, on the left), we are able to detect Ca II HK and [ OII ] . We thus were able to determine the redshift of the source as z = 0.4420 0.0002. The host galaxy is luminous with M_ R = 23.5.

gmw_00002605.name: 1RXS J165655.0 201049
gmw_00002605.overview: 1RXS J165655.0 201049 is a BL Lacertae object and blazar [arXiv:2401.07911]. High signal-to-noise spectroscopy of the active galactic nucleus host galaxy has detected Ca II HK, Mg b, CaFe, and NaID absorption features, establishing a redshift of z = 0.3405 [arXiv:2401.07911].

sources.summary: VLT/FORS spectroscopy of 1RXS J165655.0 201049 detects several absorption features and determines a redshift of z=0.3405.

quotes.quote: 1RXS J165655.0 201049 Jon09 reported a low S/N 6dF spectrum for this source, which has turned out to be featureless. A moderate S/N featureless spectrum obtained by SOAR/Goodman was reported in Pen17 . We observed it with VLT/FORS, obtaining a high S/N ( 130) spectrum (Fig. fig_spec2 , bottom panel, on the right). With our spectrum we were able to detect the Ca II HK, Mg _ b , CaFe, and NaID absorption features and determine a redshift z = 0.3405 0.0002.

gmw_00002606.name: TXS 1742 078
gmw_00002606.overview: TXS 1742 078 is a highly absorbed BL Lacertae object located near the Galactic plane [arXiv:2401.07911]. Observations with the Very Large Telescope detected [O II] and [O III] emission lines, establishing a redshift of z = 0.4210 [arXiv:2401.07911].

sources.summary: TXS 1742 078 is a highly absorbed BL Lac near the Galactic plane; VLT/FORS detects [O II] and [O III] emission at z=0.4210.

quotes.quote: TXS 1742 078 TXS 1742 078 is a very absorbed source ( E_ B-V = 0.802) near the Galactic plane (b = +10.8). A low S/N featureless spectrum obtained with the Hobby-Eberly Telescope has been presented in Shaw13 . The source has been observed with Swift UVOT in the six UVOT filters and with SARA in SDSS g',',i', and z' filters on 2018 August 28 (i'=17.97) but no photometric redshift or upper limit has been obtained by the SED fitting of the ten filters obtained by SARA+UVOT Raj20 .

We observed the source twice within a week with VLT/FORS for a total exposure time of 5200 s. In the resulting moderate S/N =50 VLT/FORS spectrum (Fig. fig_spec3 , top panel, on the left), we detected (with high S/N) the [ OIII ] b emission feature and with moderate S/N the [ OIII ] a and [ OII ] emission features, obtaining a redshift of z = 0.4210 0.0002.

gmw_00002607.name: 1RXS J184230.6 584202
gmw_00002607.overview: 1RXS J184230.6 584202 is a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2012.05176, arXiv:2401.07911] and active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2401.07911]. Its redshift is confirmed at z = 0.4226 based on multiple absorption features, including CaHK, CaIG, Mgb, and CaFe [arXiv:2401.07911].

sources.summary: The paper checks prior redshift evidence and finds a weak tentative CaHK detection at z=0.421 while the source was fainter than in earlier observations.

quotes.quote: Two spectra of 1RXS J184230.6 584202 have been reported recently. One spectrum Des19 has been taken on June 1 2018 with the KOSMOS spectrograph at the 4-m Mayall telescope at CTIO. The source had magnitude V=17.5 and the spectrum was featureless with signal to noise 30. The other has been taken with the Goodman spectrograph on the SOAR telescope at CTIO Marchesini19 on May 9 2017. The signal to noise is similar to the first one, but in this case the authors report the detection of the CaHK and CaIG features at a redshift z =0.421.

After careful examination, we possibly detect at the 3.6 level the CaHK feature at z =0.421 the same redshift as in Marchesini19 . We note that, at magnitude V = 18.8, the source was much fainter than in Des19 which may explain our weak detection and their non-detection.

sources.summary: New high-S/N VLT/FORS spectroscopy confirms the previously tentative redshift of 1RXS J184230.6 584202 at z=0.4226 via multiple absorption features.

quotes.quote: 1RXS J184230.6 584202 A moderate S/N spectrum (S/N = 33) obtained using the 4 m telescope at Cerro Tololo InterAmerican Observatory in Chile with 3600 s resulted in a featureless spectrum Des19 . Observations with SOAR for 2200 s, with a S/N of 27 comparable to the spectrum obtain by Des19 , resulted to the detection of the Ca II HK absorption corresponding to a redshift z = 0.421 Marchesini19 . Based on a moderate S/N spectrum obtained with NTT/EFOSC2 for 4500 s, in which the Ca II HK absorption has been detected, a tentative redshift of z = 0.421 has been reported in Paper I. We took two VLT/FORS observations for a total exposure time of 4100 s in order to confirm or disprove the result. In the resulting high S/N = 77 spectrum (Fig. fig_spec3 , top panel, on the right), we determined a redshift z = 0.4226 0.0003, based on the detection of the CaHK, CaIG, Mg _ b , and CaFe absorption features.

gmw_00002608.name: 1RXS J203650.9 332817
gmw_00002608.overview: 1RXS J203650.9 332817 is classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2012.05176] and a blazar [arXiv:2012.05176, arXiv:2401.07911]. Although a redshift of z = 0.237 was previously suggested [arXiv:2012.05176, arXiv:2401.07911], subsequent high signal-to-noise spectra are featureless, leaving its redshift undetermined [arXiv:2401.07911].

sources.summary: The paper observes 1RXS J203650.9 332817 to test a weak proposed z=0.237 feature but obtains only a low-S/N featureless spectrum.

quotes.quote: 1RXS J203650.9 332817 was observed by Alv16b who took a 20 minutes spectrum with the Goodman spectrograph on the SOAR telescope. The spectrum has a signal to noise of about 50 and a general power law shape consistent with the classification as a BL Lac. A weak feature around 5000 , if interpreted as CaHK, sets a redshift value of z = 0.237. Due to the weakness of the feature we observe the source with EFOSC2 in order to confirm or disprove this result with a higher signal to noise spectrum.
However, due to bad weather, we were only able to obtain a low signal to noise, featureless spectrum (Fig. fig_spec3 , first row, left). The redshift of 1RXS J203650.9 332817 remains undetermined.

sources.summary: The paper tests an uncertain z=0.237 claim for 1RXS J203650.9 332817; new high-S/N VLT/FORS spectra are featureless and do not confirm it.

quotes.quote: 1RXS J203650.9 332817 The redshift of the source is uncertain. A value of z = 0.237 has been suggested by Alv16b on the basis of a SOAR spectrum and the detection of the Ca II HK absorption. A low S/N NTT/EFOSC2 spectrum reported in Paper I did not offer the capability to confirm this detection. We observed it two times at a separation of two weeks with VLT/FORS for a total exposure time of 7200 s. The resulting high S/N spectrum (S/N = 143; Fig. fig_spec3 , second row panel, on the left) is featureless, not confirming the redshift measurement of z = 0.237.

gmw_00002609.name: RBS 1751
gmw_00002609.overview: RBS 1751 is classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2012.05176] and a blazar [arXiv:2401.07911]. Observations of the source have detected an Mg II absorber at a redshift of 0.6185 [arXiv:2401.07911], establishing a lower redshift limit of z > 0.6185 for the blazar [arXiv:2401.07911].

sources.summary: The paper observes RBS 1751 in difficult conditions, finds a weak possible MgII absorption feature, and assigns a tentative z>0.618 redshift.

quotes.quote: RBS 1751 has been classified as a BL Lac on the basis of a noisy spectrum from the 6dF survey Jon04, Jon09 . We observed the source with EFOSC2 for one hour in difficult atmospheric conditions. The resulting spectrum has a signal to noise ratio 40, higher than the 6dF one, and has a power law shape (Fig. fig_spec3 , first row, right). Only after normalisation, a weak absorption feature with EW = 2.3 0.5 centred around 4530 can be found. If we interpret this feature as due to an unresolved MgII system, then the source would be located at z 0.618.
We conclude that RBS 1751 is tentatively at a redshift z 0.618.

sources.summary: A short VLT/FORS observation confirms an Mg II absorber toward RBS 1751 at z=0.6185, establishing the blazar lower limit z>0.6185.

quotes.quote: RBS 1751 A possible MgII absorber at z 0.681 was detected in a moderate S/N NTT/EFOSC2 spectrum presented in Gol21 . To confirm this redshift, we performed a short (350 s, S/N 20) VLT/FORS observation using Grism 1200B+97, confirming the absorber but at z = 0.6185 0.0001 (Fig. fig_spec3 , second row panel, on the right). We conclude that the blazar is at z> 0.6185. The total EW of the system is 1.70 0.14 , and the two components have EW = 1.1 0.1 and 0.6 0.1 . The ratio of the two components is 1.8 0.3, which indicates a saturated system see e.g. Spitzer78 .

gmw_00002610.name: RX J2156.0 + + 1818
gmw_00002610.overview: RX J2156.0 + + 1818 is a blazar and BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2401.07911]. Deep imaging observations of the source indicate that it resides in a very bright host galaxy [arXiv:2401.07911].

sources.summary: Keck/ESI spectra of RX J2156.0 + + 1818 detect a weak Mg II absorber at z=0.6347, and imaging suggests a very bright host galaxy.

quotes.quote: RX J2156.0 + + 1818 Two spectra of the source obtained with the Observatorio Astronómico Nacional San Pedro Mártir (OAN) for 5400 s Alv16 and SDSS Ahu20 are featureless. We took an high S/N ( 90) spectra with Keck/ESI for 6000 s (Fig. fig_spec3 , third row panel, on the left) and we clearly detected a weak MgII absorber at z = 0.6347 0.0001 with a total EW of 0.5 0.04 . The two components' EWs are 0.3 0.03 and 0.2 0.03 , respectively, and their ratio is 1.5 0.3, indicating mild saturation. Deep imaging observations detected the host galaxy of this object (Nilsson et al., in prep.), which suggests a very bright host.

gmw_00002611.name: PMN J2221 5224
gmw_00002611.overview: PMN J2221 5224 is classified as a BL Lacertae object and a blazar [arXiv:2401.07911]. High signal-to-noise spectroscopy of the active galactic nucleus reveals a featureless spectrum, leaving its redshift undetermined [arXiv:2401.07911].

sources.summary: VLT/FORS spectroscopy of PMN J2221 5224 is high-S/N but featureless, leaving the source redshift undetermined.

quotes.quote: PMN J2221 5224 A spectrum of PMN J2221 5224 was reported by Shaw13 . Using NTT/EFOSC2 they obtained a featureless spectrum with low S/N. We observed the source with VLT/FORS for 900 s, obtaining an high S/N ( 150) spectrum (Fig. fig_spec3 , third row panel, on the right) that is featureless. The redshift of the source remains undetermined.

gmw_00002612.name: SUMMS J224017 524111
gmw_00002612.overview: SUMMS J224017 524111 is an active galactic nucleus classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:2401.07911]. High signal-to-noise spectroscopy of the source reveals both absorption and emission lines from its host galaxy at a redshift of z = 0.2223 [arXiv:2401.07911].

sources.summary: VLT/FORS spectroscopy of SUMMS J224017 524111 detects host-galaxy absorption and emission lines at z=0.2223.

quotes.quote: SUMMS J224017 524111 A low S/N spectrum of the source obtained by CTIO with 1950 s is reported by Des19 , resulting in a featureless spectrum. We observed it with VLT/FORS spectrum for 2600 s, obtaining a high S/N ( 90) spectrum (Fig. fig_spec3 , bottom panel, on the left). We detected several absorption (CaIG, Mgb, CaFe) and emission lines (see Table SUMSStab ) of the host galaxy at a common redshift of z = 0.2223 0.0001.

gmw_00002613.name: RBS 1888
gmw_00002613.overview: RBS 1888 is a blazar host galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.2265 [arXiv:2401.07911]. Spectroscopic observations of the galaxy reveal strong hydrogen absorption, which suggests that its host stellar population is younger than what is typically observed in blazar hosts [arXiv:2401.07911].

sources.summary: VLT/FORS spectra validate the earlier RBS 1888 redshift with z=0.2265 and show strong H absorption suggesting a younger host stellar population.

quotes.quote: RBS 1888 For this source, Fis98 propose a redshift z = 0.226 on the basis of a low S/N spectrum. To investigate this result we took an intermediate S/N ( 60) spectrum with VLT/FORS with an integration time of 3900 s (Fig. fig_spec3 , bottom panel, on the right). We detected several absorption features (CaIG, Mgb, CaFe, and NaID) and the fit of these features gives a redshift z = 0.2265 0.0002, thus validating the result presented in Fis98 with higher precision. The detection of strong H absorption (EW = 2.8 0.1 ) hints at a stellar population younger than what usually is observed in the blazar hosts see e.g. Paper I and II and references therein Falo14 .

gmw_00002614.name: 3C 332
gmw_00002614.overview: The active galaxy 3C 332 is classified as a quasar [arXiv:1203.6071] and a Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:1508.03287]. Its host galaxy is well-fit by a single bulge [arXiv:1710.02194], and its active nucleus contains a large supermassive black hole [arXiv:1203.6071] surrounded by multiple dust components [arXiv:1508.03287].

sources.summary: The paper finds a single-bulge host while discussing literature disk claims and fitting-method dependence, leaving a disk component possible but unconfirmed.

quotes.quote: 3C 332 (Fig. 11.5) --- \ \ The host galaxy is well-fit with a single bulge. Donzelli et al. (2007) claim that the host is fit with a bulge and a disk. As in 3C 287.1, the fit from Donzelli et al. can be affected by the bright nucleus. In addition, Floyd et al. (2008) show that the \ index of the host ranges from 1.7 to 4.5 depending on the fitting method (1D or 2D). We cannot completely rule out the existence of the disk in this object.

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSSJ161742.53 + 322234.3 as quasar 3C 332, emphasizing its double-peaked line, large black-hole mass, and long-term profile variability.

quotes.quote: SDSSJ161742.53 + 322234.3 . This is the strong ratio quasar 3C 332. The \ line is relatively weak, but the \ line exhibits a very prominent double peak structure with a FWHM of 19,600 \ measured by str06 and 23,200 \ measured by wux04 . The black hole mass is large, (M_ BH /M_ ) =9.334 wux04 . The \ line also displays long-term profile variability which can be explained by a low, smooth, secular change in disk illumination gez07 .

sources.summary: B2 1615+32/3C 332 is described as a Seyfert 1 whose continuum is probably AGN-dominated, with MIR/FIR dust components.

quotes.quote: B2 1615+32 (3C 332). This is a Seyfert 1, with strong broad emission lines. The continuum is probably due to the AGN, as discussed in Sect. XUVIR . MIR and FIR data are available, which can be fitted by a mix of dust with temperatures of 40 K ( M_d = 3.0 10^6 M_ ), 200 K ( M_d = 1.0 10^3 M_ ), and 800 K ( M_d = 2.1 M_ ).

sources.summary: The paper says 3C 332 has extended monitoring, measurable H peaks, strong deviations from binary-orbit models, and peak-separation behavior that weakens the SMBH-binary interpretation.

quotes.quote: Those three objects, Arp 102B, 3C 390.3, and 3C 332, are included in our present collection but their time series are now 2, 1.5, and 1.3 times longer, respectively.

To reinforce our point, we note that in 3C 332 and 3C 390.3, the peaks of the H line are fairly easy to discern and measure (see Figure specs ), yet the radial velocity curves show very significant deviations from the orbital model (See Figure vcurve ).

Lacking clear spectroscopic evidence for orbital motion in the velocity curves, the three objects chosen for study by , 3C 390.3, Arp 102B, and 3C 332, were found to require masses >10^ 10 M_ under the binary BL region hypothesis, and our extension of that monitoring has only increased those limits.

gmw_00002615.name: Q B0841+129, z _ DLA =2.375
aliases_external: DLA at z=2.375 toward Q B0841+129
DLA at z=2.476 toward Q B0841+129
gmw_00002615.overview: The damped Lyman-alpha system Q B0841+129 at redshift z = 2.375 is a high-redshift galaxy with low dust corrections [arXiv:0705.1650]. Its observed abundance patterns are best reproduced by a dwarf irregular bursting model characterized by episodic star formation [arXiv:0705.1650].

sources.summary: The paper models this DLA galaxy from abundance ratios, finding low dust corrections and several viable star-formation histories, with a dwarf irregular bursting model selected as the favored fit.

quotes.quote: Q B0841+129, z _ DLA =2.375 The abundance ratios of interest (see above) available in this DLA system are [ Si/Fe ] , [ S/Fe ] , [ S/Zn ] , [ Mg/Fe ] , [ Ni/Fe ] , [ N/Si ] , and [ N/S ] . For the refractory elements we consider the dust-corrected values given in Table abundances , although in this DLA the dust corrections are small, since [ Zn/Fe ] = +0.24 0.02 . The spiral model which best reproduces the observed abundance patterns is the spiral outer disk model (see the thick dashed-dotted curve in Fig. Q0841-2p375-SFH ).

We thus have a degeneracy between these three models which all well reproduce the abundance patterns observed in this DLA galaxy. However, in terms of derived ages and star formation rates, this degeneracy mainly concerns the bursting versus continuous star formation regimes. The values given in Table SFH , indeed, show that the spiral outer disk model and the dwarf irregular bursting model, characterized by an episodic bursting and a single burst star formation history, respectively (see Sect. models ), lead to very similar results (age and SFR), while those of the dwarf irregular continuous model are different. The only way to break this degeneracy is to impose that all the abundance patterns have to be within 1 of the model curve (i.e. have the minimum ^2 ). The resulting favored model then is the dwarf irregular bursting model characterized by = 0.3 Gyr ^ 1 and t = 0.2 Gyr.

sources.summary: This dust-free DLA galaxy is best reproduced by a short, efficient dwarf irregular bursting model, though oxygen diagnostics leave possible measurement or yield uncertainties.

quotes.quote: Q B0841+129, z _ DLA =2.476 We have at disposal the following abundance ratios in this DLA system: [ Si/Fe ] , [ S/Fe ] , [ Mg/Fe ] , [ Ni/Fe ] , [ N/Si ] , [ N/S ] , and the rare [ O/Fe ] and [ N/O ] . With [ Zn/Fe ] = 0.04 0.11 , the system is likely free from dust and thus unaffected by dust depletion effects. No dust corrections have been computed.

The model with = 2 Gyr ^ 1 and a very short burst duration t = 0.02 Gyr best reproduces the observed abundance patterns. Indeed, the model fits all the data points within 1 error, except the [ O/Fe ] and [ N/O ] ratios (thin solid curve in Fig. Q0841-2p476-SFH ). O is the most reliable diagnostic in the SFH studies, thus the fact that the [ O/Fe ] and [ N/O ] ratios are not well reproduced by the model may suggest either that the O abundance measurement is underestimated due to a possible O i line saturation (see Paper II) or that the adopted O yields are not reliable enough.

sources.summary: For the z=2.375 system toward Q0841+12, the paper adopts HI=20.95, reports good-SNR profiles and accurate column densities, but substitutes Fe from Cr because FeII lines are sky-blended.

quotes.quote: Q0841+12, z = 2.375 z = 2.476 The damped systems toward this BL Lac object were first identified by C. \ Hazard and were subsequently analyzed by Pettini et al. \ (1997). We take HI = 20.95 0.087 for the system at z = 2.375 and HI = 20.78 0.097 for the system at z = 2.476 ( ). Figures vpQ0841A and vpQ0841B plot the metal-line profiles for these systems and Tables Q0841A-clm and Q0841B-clm list the ionic column densities.

The profiles for the lower redshift system have good SNR and the derived column densities are accurate. Unfortunately, its FeII 1608 and FeII 1611 transitions are blended with sky lines. In the abundance analysis, then, we will adopt an Fe abundance, [ Fe/H ] = [ Cr/H ] , motivated by the fact that [ Cr/Fe ] 0 in the damped systems.

sources.summary: For the z=2.476 system toward Q0841+12, the paper adopts HI=20.78, lists metal-line measurements, treats SiII 1808 as blended, and improves the Zn upper-limit measurement.

quotes.quote: Q0841+12, z = 2.375 z = 2.476 The damped systems toward this BL Lac object were first identified by C. \ Hazard and were subsequently analyzed by Pettini et al. \ (1997). We take HI = 20.95 0.087 for the system at z = 2.375 and HI = 20.78 0.097 for the system at z = 2.476 ( ). Figures vpQ0841A and vpQ0841B plot the metal-line profiles for these systems and Tables Q0841A-clm and Q0841B-clm list the ionic column densities.

For the system at z = 2.476 , SiII 1808 is blended with the AlIII 1862 transition from the z=2.375 system and also may be contaminated by a sky line. Therefore, we adopt a lower limit to Si^+ from the saturated SiII 1526 profile. Finally, we obtain an upper limit measurement for Zn^+ for this system which is a significant improvement over previous efforts ( ).

sources.summary: The paper presents VLT/UVES spectroscopy of quasar Q0841+129, whose spectrum contains proximate absorbers, and derives its Lyman-limit luminosity and ionizing flux incident on the foreground PDLA.

quotes.quote: We present VLT/UVES spectroscopy of the quasar Q0841+129, whose spectrum shows a proximate damped \ (PDLA) absorber at z = \ and a proximate sub-DLA at z = , both lying close in redshift to the QSO itself at z_ em =2.49510 0.00003.

The method uses the QSO's observed V magnitude (18.5) and emission-line redshift , assumes an intrinsic QSO spectral slope of 0.5 (where F_ ^ ), and corrects for Galactic extinction using the value E(B-V) =0.048 found toward \ from the maps of Sc98 . This gives L_ 912 =8.86 10^ 30 erg s ^ 1 Hz ^ 1 . Assuming the QSO-PDLA distance is Hubble-Flow dominated (6.5 Mpc), we derive a specific flux of QSO radiation at 912 \ at the PDLA of F_ 912 ^ QSO 1.7 10^ 21 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 Hz ^ 1 , a factor of 6 lower than the calculated flux of the UV background (UVB) at this redshift F_ 912 ^ UVB 10^ 20 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 Hz ^ 1 HM01, FG09 A recent measurement of the UVB at z =2.73 using the proximity effect is F_ 912 ^ UVB 4 10^ 21 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 Hz ^ 1 Da08 , several times lower than the Haardt Madau (2001) value, but still higher than the flux incident on the PDLA from the background QSO.

gmw_00002616.name: Q B1210+175, z _ DLA =1.892
aliases_external: DLA at z=1.892 toward Q B1210+175
gmw_00002616.overview: The damped Lyman-alpha system Q B1210+175, z_DLA=1.892 is a high-redshift galaxy with a relatively low dust content [arXiv:0705.1650]. Its observed abundance ratios are best reproduced by a dwarf irregular bursting model, indicating a formation redshift of 2.2 to 2.6 and an age of 0.5 to 1 Gyr [arXiv:0705.1650].

sources.summary: This DLA galaxy has low dust content and abundance ratios best matched by a dwarf irregular bursting model, yielding a formation redshift of 2.2-2.6 and age of 0.5-1 Gyr.

quotes.quote: Q B1210+175, z _ DLA =1.892 We have at disposal the following abundance ratios in this DLA system: [ Si/Fe ] , [ S/Fe ] , [ S/Zn ] , [ Ni/Fe ] , [ N/Si ] , [ N/S ] , and an upper limit to [ Mg/Fe ] . We consider the dust-corrected values given in Table abundances for the abundance ratios including a refractory element, although the dust content in this system is relatively low, [ Zn/Fe ] = +0.24 0.07 . The spiral model which best reproduces the abundance patterns is the spiral inner disk model.

The favored model is the dwarf irregular bursting model with an efficiency = 1.2 Gyr ^ 1 and a duration t = 0.2 Gyr (thin solid curve in Fig. Q1210-SFH ). Indeed, this model reproduces all the abundance patterns within 1 error and is in agreement with the upper limit to [ Mg/Fe ] as well as the lower limit to [ Mg/Fe ] obtained from the Mg ii 2796,2803 doublet which dust-corrected value is [ Mg/Fe ] _ cor > +0.37 (see Paper II). The analysis of the abundance ratios as a function of redshift gives for the favored model a redshift of formation z_f = 2.2-2.6 of this DLA galaxy. This corresponds to an age of 0.5-1 Gyr. The star formation rate is 1.7 10^ 2 M _ yr ^ 1 kpc ^ 2 .

gmw_00002617.name: Q B2230+02, z _ DLA =1.864
aliases_external: DLA at z=1.864 toward Q B2230+02
gmw_00002617.overview: Q B2230+02, z _ DLA =1.864 is a high-metallicity damped Lyman-alpha system [arXiv:0705.1650, arXiv:astro-ph/9810381] with a high dust depletion correction [arXiv:0705.1650]. Its abundance patterns are best fit by a weak continuous dwarf irregular model, suggesting a very old age and a formation redshift above 10 [arXiv:0705.1650].

sources.summary: This high-metallicity DLA galaxy is best fit by a weak continuous dwarf irregular model and inferred to be very old, with a formation redshift above 10.

quotes.quote: Q B2230+02, z _ DLA =1.864 This DLA galaxy has a very high metallicity, [ Zn/H ] = -0.67 0.09 , higher than 1/5 solar, and relatively important dust depletion corrections that may bring an uncertainty in the study of the star formation history of this object (see Table abundances ). The abundance ratios at disposal are [ Si/Fe ] , [ S/Fe ] , [ S/Zn ] , [ Ni/Fe ] , [ N/Si ] , [ N/S ] , and an upper limit to [ Mg/Fe ] . Only the spiral inner disk model manages to reproduce the observed high metallicity of this DLA galaxy.

We found that the dwarf irregular continuous model with = 0.1 Gyr ^ 1 best reproduces the abundance patterns of this DLA galaxy (thin dotted curve in Fig. Q2230-SFH ). It is in agreement with the upper limit to [ Mg/Fe ] and fits all the data points within 1 error, except the [ N/S ] ratio at 2.3 . The oversolar [ Ni/Fe ] measurement can hardly be explained by any chemical evolution model, since Ni should closely trace Fe. We used the abundance ratios versus redshift to estimate the redshift of formation and the age of this DLA galaxy for the favored model identified. The redshift of formation found is very high, z_f > 10 , implying a very old age, higher than 3.2 Gyr, for this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper reports extensive measurements for the LBQS damped system toward Q2230 + 02, including accurate columns, Ti detection, and low-ion profiles indicating a narrow unresolved component.

quotes.quote: Q2230 + 02, z = 1.864 Figure vpQ2230 presents the velocity plots for the damped system toward Q2230 + 02 and Table Q2230-clm lists the measured ionic column densities. We adopt HI = 20.85 0.084 ( ) for this LBQS damped system. We have wavelength coverage of 28 metal-line transitions and have determined accurate column densities for the majority of them. This system is notable for a 4 detection of Ti^+ .

The profile for the low-ion transitions is comprised of three primary features whose relative optical depth apparently varies from transition to transition. For instance, the central feature is the strongest in the SiII 1808, CrII 2062, CrII 2066, and ZnII 2026 profiles and the weakest in the FeII 2260 and CrII 2056 transitions. While differences in the level of dust depletion could account for some of the variations, it would be impossible to explain the differences for a single ion (e.g. \ the CrII transitions). The most likely explanation is that this central feature is a very narrow component -- not fully resolved at our resolution -- whose relative optical depth varies significantly with the strength of the transition.

gmw_00002618.name: Q B2348--1444, z _ DLA =2.279
aliases_external: DLA at z=2.279 toward Q B2348-1444
gmw_00002618.overview: The high-redshift galaxy Q B2348--1444 is a very metal-poor damped Lyman-alpha system [arXiv:astro-ph/9810381, arXiv:0705.1650]. It exhibits a highly disturbed morphological structure that suggests gravitational interaction or merging [arXiv:0709.2568], and its low metallicity shows weak effects from dust depletion [arXiv:0705.1650].

sources.summary: The paper detects the host of HE 2348 1444 in both bands, finding low extended flux and a strongly asymmetric residual interpreted as disturbed morphology from interaction or merging.

quotes.quote: HE 2348 1444: The situation with this QSO is similar to the previous object: We have two bright stars close to quasar which show very similar surface brightness profiles. We again find positive PSF-subtraction residuals in both bands which were confirmed by the same test procedure as described for HE 2355 5457. However, the fraction of an extended flux component is even lower, only 1-2 , or 6 and 5 in \ and H , respectively, after correction for oversubtraction.

In this object the residual flux is strongly asymmetric (see Fig. \ qso_im ). This feature becomes particularly evident after subtracting the azimuthally symmetric two-dimensional model fit. At the same time, the lacking symmetry makes the extended nature of the object unconspicuous in the radial profiles (it is in fact not visible in the H band profile). Nevertheless, we believe that since the feature appears as highly consistent in both bands, its reality can be safely assumed. We conclude that also in this QSO we have detected the host galaxy, and that there is evidence for a highly disturbed morphological structure, most likely due to gravitational interaction or merging.

sources.summary: The paper presents Q2348 14 as a very metal-poor damped system, discusses its HI fit uncertainty, compares abundances with prior work, and uses S/Fe to argue against dust depletion.

quotes.quote: Q2348 14, z = 2.279 This very metal-poor system was first discussed by Pettini et al. \ (1994) and has been previously observed at high resolution by Pettini et al. \ (1995). In Figure Q2348-lya we plot the transition for this system. Overplotted are Voigt profiles centered at z = 2.2794 with HI = 20.56 0.075 corresponding to the measurements made by Pettini et al. \ (1994). While the profiles provide a reasonable fit to the left wing of the damped profile, the fit for v > 750 is clearly inconsistent for all of the assumed HI values.

The system is exceptional for a number of reasons. First, it is one of the few systems where we have a measurement of S^+ based on the SII 1259 transition. As discussed for the damped system toward Q0347 38, S/Fe is an excellent diagnostic of dust depletion. Here we find [ S/Fe ] = 0.17 dex which argues strongly against dust depletion if the system has an underlying Type II SN pattern.

sources.summary: The paper describes this low-metallicity DLA galaxy as weakly dust depleted and degenerate between continuous and bursting dwarf irregular star-formation histories.

quotes.quote: Q B2348--1444, z _ DLA =2.279 This DLA, on the contrary to the DLA toward Q B2230+02, has a low metallicity 1/180 solar ( [ Fe/H ] = -2.25 0.09 ). The derived upper limit to the Zn abundance indicates weak effects due to dust depletion, since [ Zn/Fe ] < +0.28 . The abundance ratios at disposal for the study of the star formation history of this DLA galaxy are [ Si/Fe ] , [ S/Fe ] , [ N/Si ] , [ N/S ] , and the upper limits to [ Ni/Fe ] and [ S/Zn ] .

The best dwarf irregular continuous model is characterized by an efficiency = 0.1 Gyr ^ 1 (see the thin dotted curve in Fig. Q2348-SFH ). It reproduces all the abundance patterns within 1 , except the [ Si/Fe ] ratio which is at 1.1 . Similarly, the dwarf irregular bursting model, characterized by an efficiency = 0.1 Gyr ^ 1 and a burst duration t = 0.15 Gyr, also reproduces all the data points within 1 , except the [ N/Si ] ratio which is at 1.4 (see the thin solid curve in Fig. Q2348-SFH ). We thus have for this DLA galaxy a high degeneracy between these two dwarf irregular models tracing two different star formation histories.

gmw_00002619.name: IRAS 13342+3932
gmw_00002619.overview: IRAS 13342+3932 is an ultraluminous infrared spiral galaxy [arXiv:2105.13460] hosting a radio-quiet Seyfert 1 nucleus [arXiv:2105.13460] and a starburst-dominated mid-infrared spectrum [arXiv:0908.0280]. The galaxy features two spiral arms [arXiv:2105.13460], compact ionized and neutral outflows [arXiv:2105.13460], and a nearby velocity companion [arXiv:2105.13460].

sources.summary: F13342+3932 is a ULIRG radio-quiet Seyfert with spiral arms, compact ionized/neutral outflows, and a nearby velocity companion; NOEMA resolves a triple-peaked CO line structure.

quotes.quote: F13342+3932 is a ULIRG kim98 with a radio-quiet Seyfert 1 nucleus zheng02,nagar03 . HST WFPC2/F814W (wide I band) imaging shows two prominent spiral arms in the host galaxy borne00,rupke17 . The host galaxy environment was initially mis-classified as an interacting group borne00 , but redshift analysis shows that only the nearest companion (upper left corner in Figure  fig:sdss ) with z=0.180 is nearby in velocity space veilleux02 . Gemini IFS reveals the stellar velocity field and relatively compact ionized and neutral outflows in all the strong optical emission lines (e.g., [ N  ii ]   6583 ) and Na  i D rupke17 . The velocity field of the gas traced by optical emission lines is rotation dominated with a major axis position angle 55^ measured East of North (i.e., oriented roughly NE to SW).

The \ observations from NOEMA show a triple-peaked line profile in the integrated spectrum of Figure  fig:intspec . The middle and red peaks are what blend together in the single-dish observations to create what appears to be a broad, redshifted base to the line. The peaks in the line profile separate themselves spatially to some degree.

sources.summary: The survey reports S(0) and S(1) detections for IRAS 13342+3932, but warns that non-detections of higher lines bias its derived temperature and mass.

quotes.quote: Besides NGC 6240, we detect the S(0) line in only two other sources, IRAS 12112+0305 and IRAS 13342+3932.

In IRAS 13342+3932 the fit is heavily weighted towards a low temperature, as only the S(0) and S(1) lines are detected. The resulting gas mass is an order of magnitude larger than the masses calculated for NGC 6240 and IRAS 12112+0305. A similar decrease of 50-100 K in the temperature and increase in the mass by a factor of ten is obtained for both NGC 6240 and IRAS 12112+0305, if only the S(0) and S(1) lines are used in the second temperature fit. This suggests that our result for both the mass and temperature in IRAS 13342+3932 is strongly biased by our non-detection of the S(2) and S(3) lines.

sources.summary: IRAS 13342+3932 is described as a ULIRG and the only BLS1 with strong 6.2 micron PAH emission, with a starburst-dominated mid-infrared spectrum.

quotes.quote: The third peculiar source in the archival sample is IRAS 13342+3932, another ULIRG galaxy, which is the only BLS1 showing strong 6.2 PAH emission. Even if its optical spectrum is typical of a pure type 1 Seyfert, the mid-IR one (Fig. fig11 ) is Starburst dominated with an AGN infrared contribution 15 (Weedman Houck 2008). The fact that only one BLS1 is found with such a starburst dominated spectrum does not change our overall result about an enhanced star formation in NLS1 galaxies.

gmw_00002620.name: host galaxy of GRB 071028B
gmw_00002620.overview: The host galaxy of GRB 071028B is a dwarf galaxy located at a photometric redshift of approximately 0.94 [arXiv:1103.6130]. Spectral energy distribution modeling of its optical to near-infrared photometry indicates that it is a starburst galaxy [arXiv:1103.6130].

sources.summary: The paper identifies the nearby constant source as the unabsorbed GRB 071028B host, fits its optical-near-infrared SED, derives z about 0.94, and classifies it as a dwarf galaxy.

quotes.quote: Based on accurate seven-channel photometry covering the optical to near-infrared wavelength range, we derive a photometric redshift of z = 0.94 ^ +0.05 _ 0.10 for the unabsorbed host galaxy of GRB 071028B.

The photometry of the host galaxy (H) yields the apparent brightnesses presented in Table tab_mags and Fig. fig_mags . We utilised HyperZ for fitting the model of a starburst (SB), as well as models of elliptical (E), irregular (I), and spiral (Sx) galaxies to the optical and near-infrared SED of the host galaxy bru03 . Besides the simplest case of an unabsorbed host galaxy, we examined scenarios based on dust models of the MW sea79 , the LMC fit86 , the SMC pre84,bou85 , and a SB cal00 .

By applying the absolute brightness of M_K 21.5 mag to the equation above, we calculate a stellar mass of M_ 7.0 9 for the host galaxy. This agrees with the stellar mass of M_ 31 9 derived from the model fitting, since both procedures are affected by a large uncertainty. We therefore conclude that the host galaxy belongs to the category of dwarf galaxies.

gmw_00002621.name: foreground galaxy (G)
gmw_00002621.overview: The foreground galaxy (G) is an extended irregular galaxy located at a photometric redshift of approximately 0.54 [arXiv:1103.6130]. Its structure consists of a clearly visible bulge and an extended southern structure [arXiv:1103.6130].

sources.summary: Object G is a prominent foreground irregular galaxy with z about 0.54, a large projected offset from the afterglow, optical-near-infrared SED fitting, and dust-favored photometry.

quotes.quote: Southwest of the afterglow position, there is a prominent and extended object (G) located at the central coordinates of = 23 36 38 49 , 31 37 16 1 with an uncertainty of 0 3 . The angular offset of 6 4 0 4 between the afterglow and this constant source at a photometric redshift of z = 0.54 ^ +0.09 _ 0.10 corresponds to a projected distance of (41 2) kpc . Since this is 31 times larger than the median projected distance of GRB hosts, we conclude that this object is not associated with GRB 071028B and instead represents a foreground galaxy (see Sect. sec_res_galaxy ).

We assume for the photometry of the foreground galaxy (G) that the clearly visible bulge and the extended structure south of it constitute a single irregular galaxy. In this way, we estimate the apparent brightnesses presented in Table tab_mags . We applied the same methods as already used for analysing the host galaxy SED on the optical and near-infrared SED of the foreground galaxy, and calculate a minimum goodness-of-fit of 4.00/4 for the scenarios without dust.

gmw_00002622.name: extended object (D)
gmw_00002622.overview: The extended object (D) is a galaxy located northwest of the afterglow position of GRB 071028B [arXiv:1103.6130]. Based on its detection in only specific bands, it is likely a very blue and young dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1103.6130].

sources.summary: Object D is treated as a possible host galaxy at unknown redshift, but its minimum projected distance makes association with GRB 071028B implausible; it is likely a blue young dwarf.

quotes.quote: The extended object (D) northwest of the afterglow position is centred on the coordinates of = 23 36 38 70 , 31 37 10 5 with an error of 0 3 . We now assume that this object represents a host galaxy at an unknown redshift z . In this way, we estimate a lower limit of z 0.7 by adopting the lowest redshift in the sample of hosts of long-duration GRB , which are fainter than this constant source with an apparent brightness of m_ 24.4 mag sav09 . We then calculate a minimum projected distance of 24 kpc between the afterglow and this object, thus making an association of this source with GRB 071028B very implausible. Given the detection in only and band images, we suppose this is a very blue and young dwarf galaxy.

gmw_00002623.name: Sp1149
aliases_external: Sp1149 largest lensed image behind MACS J1149.5+2223
gmw_00002623.overview: Sp1149 is a high-redshift galaxy that has been observed using laser guide star adaptive optics [arXiv:1302.6232]. It represents a rare case of strong gravitational lensing, where the galaxy is stretched almost equally by a factor of five on both image axes [arXiv:1302.6232].

sources.summary: Sp1149 is the high-redshift lensed test galaxy, observed with Keck OSIRIS at 170 pc source-plane resolution and stretched nearly equally by lensing on both image axes.

quotes.quote: Sp1149 was observed using the Laser Guide Star Adaptive Optics aided integral field spectrograph (OSIRIS; Larkin06 ) on KECK II in 2011. We achieved an image-plane angular resolution of 0 1 , corresponding to a source-plane resolution of 0.02 ^ (170 pc). The pixel scale we use for Sp1149 is 0 1 , with a field of view (FOV) of 4.8 ^ by 6.4 ^ and a spectral resolution of R 3400. Sp1149 is a rare lensing case as it is stretched almost equally by 5 times on each side of the two-dimensional image. As a result, the source-plane morphology looks almost identical to the image-plane morphology.

gmw_00002624.name: IRAS F17222 5953
gmw_00002624.overview: IRAS F17222 5953 is an isolated, relatively face-on Sbc spiral galaxy [arXiv:1302.6232] classified as a luminous infrared and strongly starbursting system [arXiv:1302.6232, arXiv:1509.08468]. Although located near other galaxies, it is currently non-interacting [arXiv:1509.08468, arXiv:1204.5520] and exhibits star formation concentrated primarily at its nucleus [arXiv:1302.6232].

sources.summary: IRAS F17222 5953 is the local isolated luminous infrared spiral used for high-redshift IFS simulations, with nuclear star formation, Sbc morphology, and moderate H-alpha SFR.

quotes.quote: In order to simulate an IFS observation at high- z , we choose a local galaxy that has star formation concentrated at the galactic nucleus instead of in the spiral arms. We use the isolated luminous infrared galaxy IRAS F17222 5953 (Sp17222) at z=0.021 . Sp17222 is relatively face-on, has a morphological type of Sbc, and a moderate SFR (H ) of 11 M _ yr ^ 1 . The optical IFU data of Sp17222 is adopted from the Wide Field Spectrograph (WiFeS) Integral Field Unit (IFU) Great Observatory All-Sky LIRG Survey (GOALS) Sample (WIGS) Rich12 .

To make realistic comparisons with observed IFU data, we fix Sp17222 at the redshift of Sp1149 ( z=1.49 ). We derive the size and surface brightness distribution for each redshifted wavelength slice using basic equations of angular diameter distance and luminosity distance, and based on the conservation of total luminosity. Due to pure cosmology effects, the angular size and total flux of Sp17222 are reduced by 0.05 and 2.9 10^ 5 respectively at z=1.49 .

sources.summary: The paper describes this system as a non-interacting starburst spiral near other galaxies, with star-forming BPT behavior and an interpreted nitrogen enhancement.

quotes.quote: IRAS F17222-5953 (ESO 138-G027). This system is more akin to a typical, non-interacting, strongly starbursting spiral galaxy in our sample. It is in the vicinity of a few other galaxies, including the similarly bright ESO 138-G026, but even the nearest galaxy is at a projected distance of over 100 kpc and IRAS F17222-5953 is not yet interacting with any of these systems. Our IFU data applied to the / v / BPT diagram show a clean curve following the shape of the SDSS sequence of local star forming galaxies (e.g. Kewley06 ), but with a slight apparent shift in total / .

sources.summary: IRAS F17222-5953 is treated as an isolated, non-interacting, strongly starbursting spiral whose IFU line ratios suggest nitrogen enhancement rather than merger-driven disturbance.

quotes.quote: IRAS F17222-5953 (ESO 138-G027). This system is more akin to a typical, non-interacting, strongly starbursting spiral galaxy in our sample. It is in the vicinity of a few other galaxies, including the similarly bright ESO 138-G026, but even the nearest galaxy is at a projected distance of over 100 kpc and IRAS F17222-5953 is not yet interacting with any of these systems. Our IFU data applied to the / v / BPT diagram show a clean curve following the shape of the SDSS sequence of local star forming galaxies (e.g. Kewley06 ), but with a slight apparent shift in total / . We interpret this shift as an overall Nitrogen enhancement Perez-Montero09 .

gmw_00002625.name: Draco II
gmw_00002625.overview: Draco II is a compact satellite of the Milky Way [arXiv:1510.01326] that is classified as either an ultra-faint dwarf galaxy [arXiv:1804.10230] or a star cluster [arXiv:2308.07394]. It features an old stellar population [arXiv:1605.04820] with a low average metallicity and a potential metallicity spread [arXiv:1510.01326].

sources.summary: Draco 2 is a tiny nearby system whose dwarf-versus-cluster nature remains debated; the algorithm recovers known spectroscopic members and identifies candidates to large elliptical radii.

quotes.quote: Draco 2 Dra2 is a very small and nearby collection of stars whose estimated distance and brightness is 21 kpc and L_ V 180 L _ ( M_ V 0.8 mag; longeard2018 ) respectively. With a half-light radius of a mere 3 (0.05 ^ , or 20 pc) and a sufficiently low (marginally resolved) velocity dispersion ( 6 km s ^ 1 at the 95 confidence interval), it has been a subject of controversy as to whether it is a true, dynamically cold UFD, or alternatively, a star cluster. Though Dra2 is very nearby, spectroscopic follow-up of this intriguing system has been fairly limited due to a lack of bright (g < 19 mag) stars. First discovered in the Pan-STARRS 1 (PS1) 3 survey ( chambers2016 ) by laevens2015 , only two works have published further results on the system. martin2016 and longeard2018 obtained a total of 14 spectroscopic members reported for Dra2. Of these stars bright enough to be included in Gaia eDR3, we report that we successfully identify all 12 spectroscopic members successfully via our 2-component algorithm.

Currently, the most distant spectroscopic member of Dra2 is located at 2.6 r_ h ( 8 ; longeard2018 ). In our work presented here, we identify members up to 9.15 r_ h , and contrary to past works, we identify an additional, bright (G-band = 17.5 mag) RGB at this large elliptical distance. This particular star was not identified in our 1-component runs, suggesting that the spatial likelihood without the free parameters of an outer profile was too restrictive to obtain a complete sample.

sources.summary: The paper presents first spectroscopy of Draco II, finding member-star kinematics, weak calcium triplet lines, metallicity spread, and combined evidence favoring an extremely faint dwarf galaxy interpretation.

quotes.quote: We present the first spectroscopic analysis of the faint and compact stellar system Draco II (Dra II, M_V=-2.9 0.8 , r_h=19^ +8 _ 6 ), recently discovered in the Pan-STARRS1 3 survey. The observations, conducted with DEIMOS on the Keck II telescope, establish some of its basic characteristics: the velocity data reveal a narrow peak with 9 member stars at a systemic heliocentric velocity v_r =-347.6^ +1.7 _ 1.8 , thereby confirming Dra II is a satellite of the Milky Way; we infer a velocity dispersion with _ vr =2.9 2.1 ( <8.4 at the 95 confidence level), which implies _ 10 M_ 1/2 =5.5^ +0.4 _ 0.6 and _ 10 (M/L)_ 1/2 =2.7^ +0.5 _ 0.8 , in Solar units; furthermore, very weak Calcium triplet lines in the spectra of the high signal-to-noise member stars imply <-2.1 , whilst variations in the line strengths of two stars with similar colours and magnitudes suggest a metallicity spread in Dra II. These new data cannot clearly discriminate whether Draco II is a star cluster or amongst the faintest, most compact, and closest dwarf galaxies. However, the sum of the three --- individually inconclusive --- pieces of evidence presented here, seems to favour the dwarf galaxy interpretation.

Therefore, the low metallicity and the metallicity dispersion implied by our analysis lead us to conclude that, independently of the kinematics, Dra II is likely a dwarf galaxy and not a globular cluster. In this letter, we performed a first spectroscopic study of the Dra II stellar system recently discovered in the PS1 3 survey, which establishes some of its basic properties: A systemic velocity of v_ , gsr 180 confirms it is indeed a satellite of the Milky Way. The inferred velocity dispersion of Dra II is _ vr = 2.9 2.1 .

sources.summary: Draco II is described as a poorly studied nearby ultra-faint dwarf with significant disk-rotation motion, inferred early infall, near-pericenter location, and four bright members.

quotes.quote: Only Draco II and Tucana III have any significant motion in the direction of the rotation of the Milky Way disk. Among the galaxies for which the binding energy is well-behaved, the rocha12 relation suggests infall times of 7  Gyr for Tri II, Segue 2, and Willman 1, 8  Gyr for Draco II and Tuc III, and >12  Gyr for Ret II. More than half of the sample (Hyi I, Hor I, Ret II, Car III, UMa II, UMa I, Com Ber, Boo II, and Dra II) is within 4 kpc of the derived pericenter, and most of the satellites are no more than 100 Myr away from passing or reaching pericenter.

Draco II (Dra II) ranks with Boo II as one of the most poorly-studied of the nearby ultra-faint dwarfs. martin16b obtained Keck/DEIMOS spectroscopy of Dra II, identifying 9 member stars and 25 non-members. Only four of the members are brighter than g=20 , with the brightest at g=19.4 . No high-resolution chemical abundance studies are available. The online table in martin16b containing the list of spectroscopic non-members is not currently available, so our sample for the present work includes only the 4 bright members.

sources.summary: The paper uses Draco II as a newly discovered ultra-faint dwarf satellite to constrain wave dark matter, deriving a soliton core radius and particle-mass estimate.

quotes.quote: We have considered in this work data from recently discovered Draco II and Triangulum II Milky Way dwarf satellites. The measurements of the luminosity of these objects suggest that they are systems with a mass-to-light ratio of 10^7 in the outer regions and therefore completely DM dominated. With such extreme DM environments, these dwarfs provide a great laboratory to study dark matter physics despite the contribution of baryonic feedback.
We find: r_c 105 pc ; m_ 22 5.6 for Draco II and r_c 160 pc ; m_ 22 3.8 for Triangulum II. The corresponding core masses, using Eq. eq:mc , are M_c^ DraII 1.5 10^7 and M_c^ TriII 2.3 10^7 .

sources.summary: The paper treats Draco II as the observed ultra-faint dwarf most similar to its compact N-body model, comparing its size, velocity dispersion, luminosity, age, inferred stellar mass, and predicted unresolved stellar cusp.

quotes.quote: The best UFD that is the most similar to my N body model ( R_ h 20 pc , _ h =1.5 km s^ 1 and (M_ /M_ )=3.4 ) would be Draco II, which has R_ h 19^ +8 _ 6 pc , _ h =2.9 2.1 km s^ 1 , the total luminosity (L_ V /L_ )=3.1 0.3 and an age 12 Gyr l:15,m:16 . Although the most probable value of _ h observed is nearly twice higher than that in my model, it is within the error range. If I adopt a stellar mass-to-luminosity ratio M/L_ V =2 - 3 M_ /L_ for a metal-poor system of 12 Gyr from a simple stellar population model of m:05 , the stellar mass of Draco II is approximately (M_ /M_ )=3.1 - 3.9 . At the Heliocentric distance of Draco II, 20 3 kpc l:15 , the size of a stellar cusp expected from my N body simulations, 2 pc , corresponds to 0.3 arcmin . Unfortunately, the cusp region is smaller than the size of the innermost bin of a stellar surface density profile shown in fig. 3 of l:15 , therefore it might be still challenging for the current observations to detect a stellar cusp in Draco II even if it is present.

sources.summary: The paper treats Draco II as a compact dSph with high phase-space density, difficult stellar-dispersion measurements, and the strongest single-object sterile-neutrino constraints in its sample.

quotes.quote: If we extrapolate this relationship, the predicted stellar velocity dispersion values for Cetus II and Tucana V are about 3-4 km/s, which is comparable to other ultra-faint dSphs with similar r_h such as Segue 1 and Draco II. The expected velocity uncertainties are estimated to be comparable or worse than Reticulum II or Segue 1 because it is often difficult to get substantial stellar kinematic sample from very faint objects. However, we note that for Draco II the velocity uncertainty is particularly large due to its high velocity measurement systematics ( 2.25 km/s for DEIMOS) and small sample (9 member stars) that make resolving the intrinsic stellar dispersion difficult.

We show the constraints for combining dSphs (in yellow) that currently have stellar velocity measurements and from two dSph (Draco II in red dotted line and Segue 1 in blue dash line) that possess the largest Q values. We note that here we only show results from our predictions using NFW profiles (4th column in Table tb:Q_value ). The combined limits exclude sterile neutrino mass m_ _s 1.9--2.8 keV for mixing angle sin^2 2 _V 1.4 10^ 8 -4.4 10^ 11 . The constraining power comes mainly from two dSphs: Draco II and Segue 1. The limits from Draco II, which provide the best single object limits, exclude sterile neutrino mass m_ _s 1.8--2.7 keV for mixing angle sin^2 2 _V 1.8 10^ 8 -5.7 10^ 11 .

sources.summary: The paper treats Draco II as the most extreme confirmed ultra-faint dSph, with 19 pc half-light radius, 3 km/s dispersion, and a dynamical-friction timescale comparable to its old stellar population age.

quotes.quote: We now go to the most extreme confirmed case, the Draco II ultra faint dSph recently reported in Laevens et al. (2015) and Martin et al. (2016). For this system a half light radius of only 19 pc was reported, with an observed velocity dispersion of 3 km/s . Keeping an ln =15 value, the resulting dynamical friction timescales for Draco II is of _ DF = 10.2 Gyr . We see that for Draco II, about one dynamical friction timescale has elapsed over the typical age of a dSph stellar population of 10 Gyr , suggesting significant dynamical evolution should be expected, something which is not evident from the appearance of the system in question, which shows no distinct features when compared to the other ultra faint dSphs.

sources.summary: The paper treats Dra2 as uncertain but possible, noting a matching orbital pole and sparse simulated particles at its location while not ruling out LMC association.

quotes.quote: Hya2 and Dra2 could be associated with the LMC and merit further attention.

Dra2, Tuc2, Hya2 and Gru1 are also consistent within their measured 1 errors, where the errors in the poles are calculated as standard deviation of 1000 Monte Carlo drawings over the measurement errors in Galactocentric X , Y , Z and V_X , V_Y and V_Z .

In the case of Dra2, we find almost no particles at its location in the simulation. However, had LMCa been more massive and extended, this area could be more populated with LMC debris even on a first passage, and therefore we cannot rule out the association of Dra2.

sources.summary: The paper uses Martin spectroscopy for Draco II, finds six stars satisfying the selection, and reports good agreement with Simon plus supporting photometric-member motions.

quotes.quote: Draco II We use the catalog of Martin_16 and find 6 stars matching all our criteria. The derived PM agrees very well with that by Simon_18 . There are a few faint stars w/o spectra whose motion agrees with the spectroscopic selected members Kallivayalil_18 .

gmw_00002626.overview: The galaxy 12011428 is characterized as an inflow system with an extended infalling stream [arXiv:1206.5552]. This stream is structurally connected to one side of the galaxy's disk, where a star-forming knot is located approximately 9.6 kiloparsecs away [arXiv:1206.5552].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 12011428 as an inflow galaxy whose redshifted absorption matches offset line emission, suggesting an extended infalling stream connected to one side of its disk.

quotes.quote: 12011428: Along the slit that crosses 12011428, the line emission is not continuous. To the north of 12011428, a second blob of [ O 2 ] emission is detected 1 14 (9.6 kpc) away along the slit. The image shows a hint of faint, extended R band emission in this direction. An [ OII ] emission-line spectrum extracted at this location exhibits a redshift of 125 15 . The emission redshift on this side of the galaxy is remarkable because resonance absorption is seen at a similar velocity, 154 32 , in the galaxy spectrum (extracted from an aperture centered on the galaxy continuum).

This coincidence in velocity space may indicate that the infalling stream crossing the center of the galaxy reaches the galaxy near the star-forming knot 9.6 kpc away. Further evidence supporting a structural connection between the redshifted emission and absorption is found in the grism (blue) spectrum extracted on the northern side of the galaxy (aperture marked in green in   fig:inflow_2d ). The \ resonance absorption remains redshifted in the off-center spectrum, and we find no spatial gradient in the absorption velocity across the 12011428 slit.

gmw_00002627.overview: The galaxy 22005270 exhibits a weak redshifted [O III] emission component in its primary spectrum [arXiv:1206.5552]. This emission is linked to a nearby, spatially extended [O III] emission blob and a strong redshifted absorption feature, suggesting an extended infalling structure [arXiv:1206.5552].

sources.summary: The paper finds the strongest redshifted absorption in 22005270 and links it to nearby redshifted [O III] emission, implying an extended infalling structure around the galaxy.

quotes.quote: 22005270: Along the slit that crosses 22005270, the line emission is not continuous in the two-dimensional spectrum shown in Figure  fig:inflow_2d . Roughly 3 5 southwest of 22005270, where the slit passes near galaxy 22005066, we detect a spatially extended blob of [ O 3 ] emission (1 6 or 12.2 kpc across). Further along this slit, we extracted another spectrum 4 3 (33 kpc) southwest of 22005270 because the [ O 3 ] emission-line profile is double-peaked; the stronger, longer wavelength maximum at 346 \ and a weaker component at 158 . The large emission redshift near 22005066 is remarkable because \ resonance absorption is seen at a similar velocity in the spectrum of 22005270. In fact, the spectrum of 22005270 shows the largest \ redshift at 401 43 .

Within the uncertainties, this absorption velocity is marginally consistent with the emission component at 346 19 . Further evidence for a connection between the redshifted [ O 3 ] emission near 22005066 and the redshifted absorption in the 22005270 spectrum is apparent upon re-inspection of the primary spectrum towards 22005270. In both   fig:inflows_4 and   fig:inflow_2d , the bright [ O 3 ] emission in the 22005270 spectrum falls at zero velocity by definition, but a weak redshifted emission component is detected at 341 , a redshift similar to the centroid velocity of the \ absorption.

gmw_00002628.overview: The galaxy 12019996 is characterized as a rotating-disk galaxy [arXiv:1206.5552]. Its redshifted resonance absorption suggests that infalling gas may extend across the entirety of the galaxy's disk [arXiv:1206.5552].

sources.summary: The paper describes 12019996 as a rotating-disk galaxy where redshifted absorption nearly matches the receding-side velocity gradient, suggesting infalling gas may span the disk.

quotes.quote: 12019996: The tilt of the [ O 2 ] emission in the 12019996 spectrogram shows the usual signature of a rotating disk. The emission extends 0 68 (5.6 kpc) to the south where the Doppler shift reaches 102 10 . The velocity gradient between the central aperture and the receding side is nearly as large as the 117 25 \ redshift of the \ absorption in the central aperture. The infalling gas marked by the redshifted resonance absorption may therefore extend all the way across the 12019996 disk.

gmw_00002629.overview: The galaxy 42006875 features a rotating disk, as indicated by the tilt of its oxygen emission [arXiv:1206.5552]. It exhibits central redshifted absorption and spatially extended emission of comparable velocities, suggesting that an infalling gas structure meets the disk approximately 10 kpc from the galactic center [arXiv:1206.5552].

sources.summary: The paper reports that 42006875 has rotating-disk emission and central redshifted absorption with comparable velocities, consistent with an extended infalling structure meeting the disk.

quotes.quote: 42006875: In the 42006875 spectrum, the tilt of the [ O 2 ] emission shows the usual signature of a rotating disk. Across the 42006875 slit, the relative redshift of the emission reaches 68 \ 1 4 (10 kpc) to the northwest of the center of the galaxy. The velocity gradient between the center of the galaxy and the receding side is as large as the 51 14 \ redshift of the \ absorption in the galaxy spectrum. The comparable redshifts of the spatially extended emission and the \ absorption toward the center of the galaxy suggest to us that the infalling gas is part of a spatially extended structure, and this structure meets the disk roughly 10 kpc southeast of the galactic center. The \ absorption trough exhibits a red wing in the 2803 consistent with the infalling structure contributing to the line profile.

gmw_00002630.name: 2MASX J22434289--1659083
gmw_00002630.overview: 2MASX J22434289--1659083 is a compact host galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.1192 [arXiv:1910.01142]. Its nucleus hosted the luminous optical, ultraviolet, and X-ray transient ASASSN-18jd [arXiv:1910.01142].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 2MASX J22434289--1659083 as the compact host galaxy of nuclear transient ASASSN-18jd and models its archival photometry to estimate stellar mass, age, and star formation rate.

quotes.quote: We present the discovery of ASASSN-18jd (AT 2018bcb), a luminous optical/UV/X-ray transient located in the nucleus of the galaxy 2MASX J22434289--1659083 at z=0.1192 .

The available UV, optical, and IR photometry of the host galaxy 2MASX J22434289--1659083 is summarized in Table tab:host_mags . The data come from the Galaxy Evolution Explorer (GALEX, martin05,bianchi11 ), Data Release 1 of Pan-STARRS (Pan-STARRS1, chambers16,flewelling16 ), the Two Micron All-Sky Survey (2MASS, skrutskie06 ) Extended Survey Catalog, and the Wide Infrared Survey Explorer (WISE, wright10 ). Due to the compactness of the galaxy, we used a region of 5'' to derive the SED of the host, as this captures most of the emission of the galaxy in the relevant wavelengths.

We used the archival GALEX, Pan-STARRS1, and WISE fluxes to model the host galaxy's SED with the code Fitting and Assessment of Synthetic Templates (FAST; kriek09 ). In doing the fits, we assumed minimum errors of 0.1 mag to account for potential systematic errors, except for the GALEX data, where we used the reported errors of 0.2 mag. We included Galactic extinction, an exponentially declining star-formation history, a Salpeter initial mass function, and the bruzual03 stellar population models. Our best fit had a stellar mass of M_* = 1.7^ +0.1 _ 0.9 11  M_ , age = 8.9^ +0.6 _ 5.9  Gyr, and a star formation rate of SFR = 0.6^ +0.1 _ 0.3  M_  yr^ 1 .

gmw_00002631.name: Haro 3
gmw_00002631.overview: Haro 3 is a nearby blue compact dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2012.10054, arXiv:1002.0991] that is also classified as a dwarf irregular starburst [arXiv:astro-ph/0406493]. Its ongoing star formation is concentrated in several bright compact regions that form a bar-like structure [arXiv:1808.08525], containing a known population of Wolf-Rayet stars [arXiv:astro-ph/0406493, arXiv:astro-ph/0610405].

sources.summary: The paper studies Haro 3, a nearby dwarf galaxy, using optical and far-infrared spectra of Region A to test abundance discrepancies and infer obscured gas in its star-forming environment.

quotes.quote: We conduct a comprehensive analysis of O ^ ++ and N ^+ ion abundances using optical and far-infrared spectra of a star-forming region within the nearby dwarf galaxy Haro 3, which exhibits a typical ADF. Assuming homogeneous conditions, the far-IR emission indicates an O abundance which is higher than the T_e method and consistent with the RL value, as would be expected from temperature fluctuations, whereas the N abundance is too large to be explained by temperature fluctuations. Instead a component of highly obscured gas is likely required to explain the high far-IR to optical flux ratios. Accounting for this obscured component reduces both the IR-based metallicities and the inferred magnitude of temperature fluctuations, such that they cannot fully explain the ADF in Haro 3.

Here we focus on a star-forming region within the dwarf galaxy Haro 3 (also known as NGC 3353 or Mrk 35; RA=10:45:22.4; Dec= + 55:57:37; z=0.003208 ). Based on the fiber spectrum centered on the host galaxy from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey data release 8 (SDSS DR8), the host galaxy has a stellar mass (M_*/M_ ) 8.5 , star formation rate SFR 0.1 M_ / yr , and gas-phase metallicity 12 + ( O / H ) 8.7 kauffmann03, brinchmann04, tremonti04 , although we note that metallicities adopted from SDSS are systematically higher than from the T_e method e.g., kewley08 . In this work, we focus on the luminous \ region 4 2 ( 300 pc) northwest of Haro 3's nucleus, often labeled as ``Region A'' steel96 . This region dominates the host galaxy's total star formation and ionizing photon production johnson04 .

sources.summary: The paper describes Haro 3 as a nearby Wolf-Rayet dwarf irregular starburst whose current star formation is dominated by region A, with evidence for very young and partly obscured clusters.

quotes.quote: The Wolf-Rayet galaxy Haro 3 (Mrk 35, NGC 3353) was observed at the near-IR and radio wavelengths as part of ongoing program to study the earliest stages of starbursts. These observations confirm that the current episode of star formation is dominated by a single region (region A). While there are knots of recent ( 10 Myr) star formation outside of region A, the sources of ionizing radiation as observed in both radio and Br observations are almost exclusively associated with region A. The derived ionizing flux implies a star formation rate of 0.6 M_ yr ^ 1 localized within a radius of 0.1 kpc. A comparison with observations from HST indicates that one or more of the star clusters in region A are optically obscured.

Haro 3 is a dwarf irregular starburst galaxy that was selected for infrared and radio study based on its relatively close proximity of 13.1 Mpc and known Wolf-Rayet (WR) stellar content steel96 . Haro 3 has an optical diameter of 3.8 kpc and consists of two main sites of star formation, A and B steel96 , as indicated in Figure XcontV a. Its classification as a WR-galaxy indicates that the system has undergone a starburst episode within the last 3-6 Myr, making it an excellent candidate for hosting sites of ongoing star formation less than a few Myr old.

sources.summary: Haro 3 is presented as a blue compact dwarf with two young starburst regions; its N/O diagnostics differ from simple N3O3 but agree better after density correction.

quotes.quote: Haro 3 is a Blue Compact Dwarf galaxy (BCD) with two very young starburst regions. One of the two regions hosts a starburst younger than 5 Myr, and the other is 8 10 Myr old 2004AJ....128..610J . The stellar mass in Haro 3 is 10^6 \ M_ , and SFR 0.8 \ M_ / yr measured by combining FUV and MIR photometry 2014A&A...568A..62D . It is included in the Herschel Dwarf Galaxy Survey sample DGS; 2015A&A...578A..53C .

Comparing the N3O3 in Column (2), the most simplistic estimation of N/O using only two spectral lines, to the value in Column (5) which is the most precise and reliable diagnostic utilizing 5 lines, the value changes by < 0.15 dex for all targets except Haro 3 and II Zw 40. This small change is because the effect of density correction which would lift N/O ratio result, is offset by the model calibration for the [ Ne iii ] / [ Ne ii ] in the -0.5 to 1.5 range which would decrease the N/O estimate. For the exceptions Haro 3 and II Zw 40, they have moderately high electron densities as well as hard ionization conditions, resulting in noticeable differences between N3O3 and the model calibration, but N3O3_ n_e parameter instead shows great agreement with the model calibrated results.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 3353/Mrk 35 as the most metal-rich BCD in its sample, giving its oxygen abundance, distance, luminosity, Wolf-Rayet population, and suitability for early-stage star formation studies.

quotes.quote: We present \ observations of the blue compact dwarf galaxy (BCD) , with an oxygen abundance of 12 + log(O/H) = 8.32.

In this first paper of a series, we discuss the most metal-rich object in our sample, \ = NGC 3353 = Mrk 35. \ has a heliocentric radial velocity of 969 km s ^ 1 thuanizotov05 which gives a distance of 16.3 Mpc, adopting a Hubble constant of 70 km s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 and correcting for the Virgocentric flow. The present distance is about 25 larger than the old distance of 13.1 Mpc used by previous investigators who did not correct for Virgocentric flow. The blue apparent magnitude is of \ is 13.2 (RC3), which corresponds to a blue absolute magnitude of 17.9 . With its known population of Wolf-Rayet stars steel96,thuanizotov05 , \ is an ideal object for studying star formation in its relatively early stages.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 35 as a blue compact galaxy whose compact star-forming regions form a bar-like structure, with ionized-gas velocity dispersion peaking between central star-forming regions.

quotes.quote: Mrk 35 is another example of a blue compact galaxy. The ongoing star formation here is concentrated in several bright compact regions. Star-forming regions near the optical center of the galaxy form a bar-like structure, where the population of Wolf--Rayet stars is observed Cairos2007 . The radial velocity dispersion of the ionized gas in the galaxy reaches about 70 , whereas in the central regions it lies within the range of 20--35 . The highest dispersion of radial velocities is observed in the gas located between three central regions of star formation.

sources.summary: The paper identifies Haro 3 as the sample's highest-metallicity BCD, notes its extinction correction, and links it to PAH-band behavior and steep mid-infrared continuum strength.

quotes.quote: This is the third in a series of papers; the first two papers have focused on two particular BCDs, Haro 3, the highest metallicity object in our sample hunt06 , and Mrk 996, a BCD with an unusually dense and compact nuclear \ region thuan08 .
Such corrections would be applicable only to two sources, Haro 3 and Mrk 996. Haro 3, with (9.7) = 0.39, would have a correction 25 for wavelengths longer than 15 ; Mrk 996 would have a 2 correction.
The four BCDs with the steepest continua and most enhanced PAH strength are also the most metal-rich galaxies in our sample (Haro 3, Mrk 450, Mrk 1315, and UM 311).

sources.summary: The paper reports compatible WR bump estimates for NGC 3353, high-significance WR apertures, and a nonphysical O-star estimate for R1.

quotes.quote: Only in one case (NGC 3353) can we consider both values compatible within the uncertainties.
Note that this does not affect the WR regions with the highest detection significance since their aperture is similar to that of the associated clump (e.g., in NGC 3353 , NGC 3773 , NGC 3991 ).
In two cases (R1 in NGC 3353 and NGC 3381 ), the computed number of O stars is negative, a non-physical result.

gmw_00002632.overview: The radio source 1928+738 is a core-dominated flat-spectrum radio quasar [arXiv:2405.03365, arXiv:1412.4909] associated with a bright optical blazar [arXiv:1412.4909]. It features a precessing, superluminal jet [arXiv:2308.00153, arXiv:2405.03365] that exhibits a distinct turn [arXiv:1412.4909] and curves toward the south and southeast [arXiv:1101.5822, arXiv:1712.06314].

sources.summary: The paper studies the FSRQ 1928+738 with VLBI, finding parabolic-to-conical jet geometry, outward acceleration, and an acceleration/collimation zone tied to black-hole gravity.

quotes.quote: Using time-resolved multifrequency Very Long Baseline Array data and new KaVA (KVN and VERA Array) observations, we study the structure and kinematics of the jet of the flat spectrum radio quasar (FSRQ) 1928+738. We find two distinct jet geometries as function of distance from the central black hole, with the inner jet having a parabolic shape, indicating collimation, and the outer jet having a conical shape, indicating free expansion of the jet plasma. Jet component speeds display a gradual outward acceleration up to a bulk Lorentz factor _ max 10 , followed by a deceleration further downstream. The location of the acceleration zone matches the region where the jet collimation occurs; this is the first direct observation of an acceleration and collimation zone (ACZ) in an FSRQ.

FSRQ 1928+738 (a.k.a. 4C +73.18) is located at a redshift of 0.302 Lawrence1986 and hosts a SMBH with a mass of M_ 3.7 10^ 8 M_ in its center see and the references therein Park2017 . It is noteworthy that 1928+738 is one of very few `misaligned' FSRQs with an unusually large viewing angle of _ v 10^ - 15^ between the jet and the line of sight Lahteenmaki1999, Hovatta2009, Liodakis2018 . Therefore, 1928+738 is one of the best FSRQ targets to directly investigate its putative jet ACZ with (sub)-mas VLBI observations. At parsec scales, it has long been known that the 1928+738 jet shows apparently superluminal motion.

sources.summary: The paper describes S5 1928+738 as a core-dominated quasar and optical blazar with a turning VLBI jet, interpreting the turn as possible evidence for multiple black holes.

quotes.quote: The radio source S5 1928+738 is a core dominated quasar at a redshift of 0.302 LaPe+:86 . It is associated with a bright optical blazar which magnitude is m_ R 15 to 16 HeRo+:08 . The jet morphology is two-sided on kpc scales, the southern part being more pronounced MuBr+:93 . On pc scales the source is one-sided.

The map of 1928+738 (Fig. fig:1_1928+738_03_08_Bis_3 ) shows that the VLBI jet turns after about 10 mas LiHo:05 . Close to the nucleus the ejection direction is characterized by 163 and after about 10 mas, the VLBI jet turns and has an asymptotic direction characterized by 182 which corresponds to the large scale jet direction observed with the VLA MuBr+:93 . As mentioned in Sec. sec:intro , the VLBI jet turn could be an indication that the nucleus of 1928+738 contains either three black holes or two BBH systems.

sources.summary: The paper treats 1928+738 as one of five AGN targets observed by KVN at 22-130 GHz for multi-frequency VLBI astrometry, map registration, position shifts, and jet-related alignment tests.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:source_pos shows the distribution in the sky of the five selected sources (1803+784, 1807+698, 1842+681, 1928+738 and 2007+777) along with their angular separations, which range between 3.6^o and 11^o . None of the sources had been observed previously with VLBI at 130 GHz. The observations consisted of 3 minutes long scans alternating between the sources in each of the two triangles shown in Figure fig:source_pos , and between the triangles ca. every hour and a half, at the four bands simultaneously. Alternating between multiple sources allowed us to develop a strategy to decompose the relative astrometric measurements into single source position shifts that allow, for example, the registration of the images at different frequencies.

Figure fig:sc shows self-calibration images for the five AGN sources in the multi-frequency KVN observations, including the first images of these sources at 130 GHz.
