The visibility datasets were modelfitted and imaged and show little divergence from point-source core dominated images; in some cases there appears to be some elongation aligned with the jet direction as seen in higher resolution maps from MOJAVE mojave . Table tab:flux lists the total flux values as measured from the maps, at all frequency bands.

sources.summary: The paper models S5 1928+738 as a quasar with a precessing VLBI jet, supporting a supermassive black-hole binary through periodic and monotonic jet-orientation changes.

quotes.quote: We repeated the fit of the projected helical jet to the (x,y) positions of the inner components of the 15 GHz VLBI jet of S5 1928+738 employing new 15 GHz VLBA observations in 29 additional epochs between 2013.34 and 2020.89. We present the best-fit results for _0 in Table table:fluxden_incli . We show the phenomenological fit to the inclination angle change of the symmetry axis of the 15 GHz inner jet of S5 1928+738 with a periodic and a monotonic term see in Kun2014 described as _0 (t)=A_0 + A_1 2 T t + A_2 t in Fig. fig:phenofit , where ^2 =37.8 , and the degrees of freedom D=67 .

Based on the periodic modulation and the monotonic trend in the inclination angle of the symmetry axis of the inner jet, we further supported the existence of the SMBH binary in the heart of the quasar S5 1928+738. We made a more precise estimation of the velocity of the spin precession, from (-0.05 0.02) yr ^ 1 to (-0.04 0.01) yr ^ 1 . Using this rate, we estimate the symmetry axis of the jet goes through our line of sight sometime between 2180 and 2200, which is far from the present. Finally we conclude that the precession of the 15 GHz VLBI inner jet of S5 1928+738 is still ongoing, showing a similar behaviour over 25 years between 1995.96 and 2020.89.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes long-term VLBI data for quasar S5 1928+738, modeling its helical jet and linking periodic jet-axis changes to a binary SMBH plus spin-orbit precession.

quotes.quote: We perform an analysis of long-term VLBI data of the quasar S5 1928+738 in terms of a geometric model of a helical structure projected onto the plane of the sky. We monitor the direction of the jet axis through its inclination and position angles. We decompose the variation of the inclination of the inner 2 milliarcseconds of the jet of S5 1928+738 into a periodic term with amplitude of 0.89^ and a linear decreasing trend with rate of 0.05^ yr ^ 1 .

We also decompose the variation of the position angle into a periodic term with amplitude of 3.39^ and a linear increasing trend with rate of 0.24^ yr ^ 1 . We interpret the periodic components as arising from the orbital motion of a binary black hole inspiraling at the jet base and derive corrected values of the mass ratio and separation from the accumulated 18 years of VLBI data. Then we identify the linear trends in the variations as due to the slow reorientation of the spin of the jet emitter black hole induced by the spin-orbit precession and we determine the precession period T_ SO =4852 646 yr of the more massive black hole, acting as the jet emitter.

sources.summary: The paper describes non-radial VLBA knot motions, a curved VLA jet, strongest X-ray detection along the inner jet, and marginal detection over much of the remaining jet.

quotes.quote: 1928 + 738 (4C +73.18) This source was in the sample and is included in our analysis for completeness. The VLBA data show many knots moving non-radially, at 2-8 on an arc that extends over PAs 150-160 \ toward due south vlba,2009AJ....138.1874L , while the VLA data show knots in a jet that curves from PA -170 \ to the east between 5 and 10 \ from the core. In the X-ray image, the jet is detected most strongly at a PA of -170 \ out to 3.5 \ and is marginally detected for much of the remainder of the jet. The readout streak is visible in the image shown in Fig.  fig:images .

sources.summary: The paper describes 1928 + 738/4C + 73.18 as a z=0.302 low-spectral-peaked AGN with VLA-scale jets, diffuse emission, a hotspot, and bent southern components.

quotes.quote: 1928 + 738 (J1927 + 7358) 1928 + 738 (4C + 73.18) is a low-spectral peaked AGN at a redshift of z = 0.302 1996ApJS..107..541L . The source was not detected by EGRET or Fermi /LAT. The VLA image shows a core with jets and diffuse emission. A series of at least four bright components extends 18 to the south and the jet gradually bends from the south to southeast 1993MNRAS.264..298M . To the north of the core, there is one bright hotspot about 1 from the core and a large patch of diffuse emission beyond the hotspot 1987ApJ...313L..85J,1990AJ....100.1057K,1986CaJPh..64..440R .

sources.summary: The paper uses QSO 1928+738 as a VLBA kinematic example, aligning eight epochs to the brightness peak and finding jet components moving southward with superluminal, slightly curved trajectories.

quotes.quote: An Example: 1928+738 As an example of our analysis, we show in Fig. fig:ros1_1928 the results on the QSO 1928+738 (4C 73.18) from our image database, based on 8 epochs of observations. The images in the top panel have been aligned relative to the brightness peak, although it is known from astrometric results (see Ros et al. \ 1999 and references therein) that the central engine is somewhere to the north of the brightest component at frequencies lower than 15 GHz. The bottom panels show the estimated positions of the relative peaks of brightness in the images with respect to the main one (associated tentatively with the core). The kinematical analysis based on the procedure described above shows that the components move southwards with superluminal speeds in slightly curved trajectories.

sources.summary: The paper reports a 15 GHz core circular-polarization measurement for 1928 + 738 and compares it with multiple HW99 detections and limits from 1996.

quotes.quote: 1928 + 738: We measure m_c = +0.19 0.09 in the core at 15 GHz in June of 2002. HW99 also measured circular polarization in the core 15 GHz at the limit of their sensitivity. They found m_c =+0.3 0.1 in two epochs, m_c =+0.2 0.1 in one epoch, and limits of 0.1 and 0.2 in their other two epochs during 1996.

gmw_00002633.name: HCG16c
gmw_00002633.overview: HCG16c is an S0-a luminous infrared galaxy [arXiv:1910.03420] and the brightest, most extended member of its group [arXiv:astro-ph/0211350]. It is an active starburst galaxy that may also host a composite active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1910.03420], driving a bipolar galactic wind [arXiv:1910.03420].

sources.summary: The paper presents HCG 16c as the group's most actively star-forming LIRG, with possible composite AGN activity, a starburst wind, tidal tails, and disturbed but still rotating H I.

quotes.quote: HCG 16c HCG 16c is classified as an S0-a galaxy and a LIRG (Luminous Infrared Galaxy). It is also the most actively star-forming galaxy in the group. It has been classified as both a pure starburst based on X-ray observations Turner+2001 and a composite object, with both AGN and SF activity, using optical line ratios Martinez+2010 . The overall morphology of the galaxy is very reminiscent of M82, with the nuclear starburst driving a bipolar galactic wind that was studied in detail by Vogt+2013 .

HCG 16c is apparently involved in a number of ongoing interactions: the NW tail extends between HCG 16c towards HCG 16a, the NE tail extends from HCG 16c away from the rest of the group, and there is an \ bridge connecting HCG 16c and d, all of which can be seen in Figure fig:mom0 , the http://amiga.iaa.es/FCKeditor/UserFiles/X3D/HCG16/HCG16.html X3D plot , and in the channel maps (available in the electronic version). All three of these features contain more than 10^ 9 \ of , that is, they are comparable in \ mass to the galaxy itself, yet only considering the \ emission in the region we identified as the \ disc of HCG 16c the galaxy is not found to be \ deficient.

sources.summary: The paper treats HCG16c as the brightest, most extended group galaxy and uses EMOS and Chandra surface-brightness profiles to test PSF corrections for diffuse X-ray emission.

quotes.quote: We applied this test to HCG16c, the brightest and most extended galaxy, and thus the most extreme case. We obtained a surface brightness (SB) profile of this galaxy from the EMOS 1 image in the energy range 0.2-1.4 keV and we fitted it with the model of a point source convolved with the EMOS PSF. We fit this profile up to 2 after which the profile becomes dominated by the diffuse emission. The fit is not good, but if we extrapolate the model up to 5 we obtain a F = 0.063 using a cutoff radius of 1 .2 for this galaxy. We then obtained a SB profile of galaxy HCG16c from the Chandra image in the same energy range as above.

gmw_00002634.name: J0943+5417
gmw_00002634.overview: J0943+5417 is a bright iron low-ionization broad absorption line quasar [arXiv:1011.4327]. It is a radio-intermediate source with a suppressed ultraviolet continuum and a high inferred X-ray absorbing column density [arXiv:1011.4327].

sources.summary: J0943+5417 is presented as a bright FeLoBAL quasar; the paper notes its radio-intermediate FIRST detection, suppressed UV continuum, and high inferred X-ray absorbing column density.

quotes.quote: In this paper we present new \ observations of three FeLoBALs, SDSS objects J094317.59+541705.1, J135246.37+423923.5, and J172341.08+555340.5 (hereafter J0943+5417, J1352+4239, and J1723+5553, respectively). The targets are classified as FeLoBALs by b14 , and are the brightest ( K_s < 14.4 mag) of the 2 Micron All Sky Survey ( ) selected BALQSOs b2 . J0943+5417 is a radio-intermediate quasar detected with a peak flux of 1.71 mJy/beam and RMS noise of 0.145 mJy/beam by Faint Images of the Radio Sky at Twenty-Centimeters (FIRST). None of these objects are detected at the flux limit in the NRAO/VLA Sky Survey c98 . J0943+5317 and J1352+4239 are typical FeLoBALs with extremely complex rest frame UV spectra, whereas J0943+5417 has absorption troughs so wide that they overlap and completely suppress the UV continuum.

Therefore, combining all constraints, J1723+5553 is consistent with normal AGN for column densities 6 10^ 23 N_H 3 10^ 24 , while \ for J0943+5417 and J1352+4239 are consistent with normal AGN for N_H 7 10^ 23 \ and N_H 2 10^ 24 , respectively. Column Densities and Intrinsic X-ray Luminosities We find significantly high column densities for all three FeLoBALs studied, 6 10^ 23 N_H 3 10^ 24 \ for J1723+5553, 7 10^ 23 \ for J0943+5417, and 2 10^ 24 \ for J1352+4239.

gmw_00002635.name: J1352+4239
gmw_00002635.overview: J1352+4239 is an overlapping-trough iron low-ionization broad absorption line quasar located at a redshift of z=2.26 [arXiv:2001.07347]. The active galactic nucleus exhibits anomalous reddening [arXiv:2001.07347], high intrinsic X-ray absorption [arXiv:1011.4327], and an exceptionally fast, energetic outflow [arXiv:2001.07347].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J135246.37+423923.5 as an overlapping-trough FeLoBAL quasar with z=2.26, anomalous reddening, scattered light, and an exceptionally fast, luminous BAL outflow capable of quasar feedback.

quotes.quote: We analyzed the rest-UV spectrum of an ``overlapping trough'' iron low-ionization broad absorption line quasar (FeLoBAL) SDSS J135246.37+423923.5 using the novel spectral synthesis code SimBAL leighly18a and discovered an extraordinarily fast and energetic BAL outflow. Our analysis revealed outflow velocities reaching 38000 km s^ 1 with a velocity width of 10000 km s^ 1 which is the largest FeLoBAL outflow velocity measured to date. The column density of the outflow gas is log N_H 23.2 [ cm^ 1 ] with the log kinetic luminosity L_ KE 48.1 [ erg s^ 1 ] which exceeds the bolometric luminosity of the quasar and is energetic enough to effectively drive quasar feedback. The energy estimate for the outflow is far greater than the estimates from any BAL object previously reported. The object also shows ``anomalous reddening'' and a significant scattered component that we were able to model with SimBAL .

SDSS J135246.37+423923.5, hereafter referred to as SDSS 1352 + 4239, is an overlapping trough object that was initially observed by the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS). This object has all the fascinating BAL characteristics in its spectrum, including a wide overlapping trough, anomalous reddening and a substantial scattered light signature. With new near-infrared observations of SDSS 1352 + 4239, we measured an accurate redshift, z=2.26, from the Balmer emission lines.

sources.summary: J1352+4239 is one of the bright FeLoBAL quasars observed with Suzaku; its X-ray limits are consistent with a normal AGN SED only for high intrinsic absorption.

quotes.quote: In this paper we present new \ observations of three FeLoBALs, SDSS objects J094317.59+541705.1, J135246.37+423923.5, and J172341.08+555340.5 (hereafter J0943+5417, J1352+4239, and J1723+5553, respectively). The targets are classified as FeLoBALs by b14 , and are the brightest ( K_s < 14.4 mag) of the 2 Micron All Sky Survey ( ) selected BALQSOs b2 .

For column densities N_H 7 10^ 23 \ for J0943+5417 and N_H 2 10^ 24 \ for J1352+4239, \ is consistent with the normal AGN sample. The upper limits given by the soft spectrum fall within (and above) the scatter in the high column density region, where the hard spectrum gives the better constraint. Our 3 detection of J1723+5553 indicates that for column densities N_H 8 10^ 24 , J1723+5553 is consistent with the SEDs of normal AGNs. Therefore, combining all constraints, J1723+5553 is consistent with normal AGN for column densities 6 10^ 23 N_H 3 10^ 24 , while \ for J0943+5417 and J1352+4239 are consistent with normal AGN for N_H 7 10^ 23 \ and N_H 2 10^ 24 , respectively. Column Densities and Intrinsic X-ray Luminosities We find significantly high column densities for all three FeLoBALs studied, 6 10^ 23 N_H 3 10^ 24 \ for J1723+5553, 7 10^ 23 \ for J0943+5417, and 2 10^ 24 \ for J1352+4239.

gmw_00002636.name: J1723+5553
gmw_00002636.overview: J1723+5553 is a broad-absorption line quasar that has been detected in the hard X-ray band [arXiv:1011.4327]. Its X-ray hardness ratio indicates that the active nucleus is heavily obscured by a high intrinsic column density [arXiv:1011.4327].

sources.summary: J1723+5553 is detected by Suzaku in the hard band; the paper uses the hardness ratio to infer heavy absorption and place it within normal quasar SED expectations.

quotes.quote: We marginally detect SDSS J1723+5553 ( 2.6 and 1.6 ) in both of the FI CCDs in the observed 2--10 keV band. Combining the data from the two FI CCDs, we detect J1723+5553 at the 3 significant level (Table cntrate ). The FI CCDs have comparable effective areas, and lower background rates at 2--10 keV than the BI CCD, which means they are more efficient at detecting faint sources in this energy band. Thus the non-detection of the source in the BI CCD (XIS1) is expected, given the low X-ray flux. Therefore, we conclude that the detection of J1723+5553 in the 2--10 keV band is real.

J1723+5553 is a different story: only at N_H 6 10^ 23 \ are the upper limits from the soft X-ray spectrum consistent with the 3 detection in the hard X-ray spectrum. The 3 detection and its uncertainties for \ calculated from the hard X-rays are 0.3 above the upper limits given by the soft X-ray energy range for column densities < 6 10 ^ 23 . Thus, the X-ray hardness ratio of J1723+5553 constrains the intrinsic column density to N_H 6 10^ 23 .

gmw_00002637.name: NGC 3021
gmw_00002637.overview: NGC 3021 is a spiral galaxy classified as SA(rs)bc: [arXiv:1702.01118]. It hosted the Type Ia supernova SN 1995al and has a derived distance modulus of 32.178 [arXiv:1702.01118].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3021 as the SN 1995al host and uses HST resolved-star photometry plus TRGB calibration to derive a distance modulus of 32.178.

quotes.quote: We report TRGB distances to three SN Ia host galaxies, NGC 3021, NGC 3370, and NGC 1309. We obtain F555W and F814W photometry of resolved stars from the archival Hubble Space Telescope data. Luminosity functions of red giant stars in the outer regions of these galaxies show the TRGB to be at I QT=28.2 28.5 mag. From these TRGB magnitudes and the revised TRGB calibration based on two distance anchors (NGC 4258 and the LMC) in jan16 , we derive the distances: (m-M)_0=32.178 0.033 for NGC 3021, 32.253 0.041 for NGC 3370, and 32.471 0.040 for NGC 1309.

In this paper, we present the TRGB distances to three additional galaxies hosting three SNe Ia: NGC 3021 (SA(rs)bc:) hosting SN 1995al, NGC 3370 (SA(s)) hosting SN 1994ae, and NGC 1309 (SA(s)bc:) hosting SN 2002fk.

Measured TRGB values are: F814W _ TRGB =28.179 0.049, 28.252 0.035 and 28.457 0.044 mag for NGC 3021, NGC 3370, and NGC 1309, respectively. Similarly, QT_ RGB =28.146 0.033 mag for NGC 3021, 28.212 0.041 mag for NGC 3370, and 28.398 0.040 mag for NGC 1309 were obtained.

gmw_00002638.name: NGC 3370
gmw_00002638.overview: NGC 3370 is a moderately inclined, late-type spiral galaxy classified as SA(s) [arXiv:1702.01118, arXiv:2208.02824]. It hosted the Type Ia supernova SN 1994ae [arXiv:1702.01118, arXiv:2208.02824] and contains a multimodal star cluster system spanning old, intermediate, and very young ages [arXiv:0906.3432].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3370 as the SN 1994ae host and uses HST resolved-star photometry plus TRGB calibration to derive a distance modulus of 32.253.

quotes.quote: We report TRGB distances to three SN Ia host galaxies, NGC 3021, NGC 3370, and NGC 1309. We obtain F555W and F814W photometry of resolved stars from the archival Hubble Space Telescope data. Luminosity functions of red giant stars in the outer regions of these galaxies show the TRGB to be at I QT=28.2 28.5 mag. From these TRGB magnitudes and the revised TRGB calibration based on two distance anchors (NGC 4258 and the LMC) in jan16 , we derive the distances: (m-M)_0=32.178 0.033 for NGC 3021, 32.253 0.041 for NGC 3370, and 32.471 0.040 for NGC 1309.

In this paper, we present the TRGB distances to three additional galaxies hosting three SNe Ia: NGC 3021 (SA(rs)bc:) hosting SN 1995al, NGC 3370 (SA(s)) hosting SN 1994ae, and NGC 1309 (SA(s)bc:) hosting SN 2002fk.

Measured TRGB values are: F814W _ TRGB =28.179 0.049, 28.252 0.035 and 28.457 0.044 mag for NGC 3021, NGC 3370, and NGC 1309, respectively. Similarly, QT_ RGB =28.146 0.033 mag for NGC 3021, 28.212 0.041 mag for NGC 3370, and 28.398 0.040 mag for NGC 1309 were obtained.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 3370 with HST BVI data, finding 277 star-cluster candidates, multimodal colors, and subpopulations consistent with old, intermediate-age, and very young clusters.

quotes.quote: We study the photometric and structural properties of the star cluster system in the late type Sc spiral NGC 3370 . BVI observations from the Advanced Camera for Surveys on board of HST are used to analyse in detail the colours, magnitudes and spatial properties of cluster candidates. The final catalogue of sources used for the study is composed by 277 objects. The colour distributions of cluster candidates appear multi-modal. Although firm age constraints need the use of more age-sensitive indicators, the comparison of cluster candidate colours with the colours of Galactic and Magellanic Clouds star clusters, suggests an age difference between the various sub-peaks, with a red old sub-system, a rich population of intermediate age ( 1 Gyr), and a blue tail of very young (below 100 Myr) clusters.

sources.summary: The paper uses deep ACS observations of NGC 3370 to test PSF photometry, noting the galaxy is a late-type disk, SN 1994ae host, and distance-indicator field.

quotes.quote: Deep ACS observations of NGC 3370 were reduced using four distinct approaches. These reductions explore a number of methodological differences: software packages (DAOPHOT and DOLPHOT), input images (individual and stacked frames), PSF models (synthetic and empirical), and aperture correction methods (automatic and manual).

In this paper, we now present detailed photometry tests applied to deep observations of NGC 3370 taken with ACS/WFC on board the Hubble Space Telescope ( HST ). NGC 3370 is a moderately inclined late-type disk galaxy (Figure fig1 ) and has been host to a Type Ia Supernova, SN 1994ae. The ACS observations for this galaxy provide a unique opportunity for this study since two compelling stellar distance indicators have been applied to the same dataset and resulted in accurate distances: Cepheids (m-M)_0 = 32.29 0.06 rie05 and the Tip of the Red Giant Branch (TRGB) (m-M)_0 = 32.25 0.04 jan17b .

gmw_00002639.name: NGC 1309
gmw_00002639.overview: NGC 1309 is a spiral galaxy classified as SA(s)bc: [arXiv:1702.01118]. It hosted the Type Ia supernova SN 2002fk and has a derived distance modulus of 32.471 based on tip of the red giant branch photometry [arXiv:1702.01118].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 1309 as the SN 2002fk host and uses HST resolved-star photometry plus TRGB calibration to derive a distance modulus of 32.471.

quotes.quote: We report TRGB distances to three SN Ia host galaxies, NGC 3021, NGC 3370, and NGC 1309. We obtain F555W and F814W photometry of resolved stars from the archival Hubble Space Telescope data. Luminosity functions of red giant stars in the outer regions of these galaxies show the TRGB to be at I QT=28.2 28.5 mag. From these TRGB magnitudes and the revised TRGB calibration based on two distance anchors (NGC 4258 and the LMC) in jan16 , we derive the distances: (m-M)_0=32.178 0.033 for NGC 3021, 32.253 0.041 for NGC 3370, and 32.471 0.040 for NGC 1309.

In this paper, we present the TRGB distances to three additional galaxies hosting three SNe Ia: NGC 3021 (SA(rs)bc:) hosting SN 1995al, NGC 3370 (SA(s)) hosting SN 1994ae, and NGC 1309 (SA(s)bc:) hosting SN 2002fk.

Measured TRGB values are: F814W _ TRGB =28.179 0.049, 28.252 0.035 and 28.457 0.044 mag for NGC 3021, NGC 3370, and NGC 1309, respectively. Similarly, QT_ RGB =28.146 0.033 mag for NGC 3021, 28.212 0.041 mag for NGC 3370, and 28.398 0.040 mag for NGC 1309 were obtained.

gmw_00002640.name: KKR 25
gmw_00002640.overview: KKR 25 is an isolated dwarf spheroidal galaxy located just outside the Local Group [arXiv:2311.16397, arXiv:2106.02672]. It features a very low surface brightness [arXiv:0710.0711], hosts predominantly old to intermediate stellar populations [arXiv:2311.16397], and has a low neutral gas content [arXiv:2311.16397] despite a detected H I halo [arXiv:2106.02672].

sources.summary: The paper derives an HB-based age for isolated dwarf KKR 25, arguing its low gas content and low star formation likely arose through internal processes rather than recent environment.

quotes.quote: In this section, we provide a first application of our new method by deriving stellar ages for the isolated galaxies VV 124 D 1.37 0.07 Mpc, Tully13 and KKR 25 D 1.93 0.06 Mpc, Makarov12 . VV 124 and KKR 25 are two relatively bright ( 10^6 L/L_ 10^7 ), metal-poor ( 1.9 Fe/H 1.5 ) dwarf galaxies located just outside the LG e.g., Karachentsev01,Kopylov08,Bellazzini11a,Makarov12, Kirby12, Kirby13b . Both galaxies host predominantly old/intermediate stellar populations with a small amount of recent ( 1 Gyr) star formation Kopylov08,Bellazzini11b,Makarov12,Neeley21 , have relatively low H_ I content Begum05,Bellazzini11a , and (in the case of VV 124) pressure-supported kinematics Kirby12 . Many of these observations have led to suggestions that these galaxies are transition dwarfs in a late stage of their evolution.

We use this calibration to measure the mass-weighted age of the distant, isolated galaxies KKR 25 and VV 124. For those two galaxies, we measure t^* values of ( 11.21^ +0.70 _ 0.65 Gyr) and ( 11.03^ +0.73 _ 0.68 Gyr) for KKR 25 and VV 124, respectively. These values indicate that early star formation in these targets may have proceeded for an extended period of several Gyr. Combined with the current isolated position of these galaxies, this suggests that KKR 25 and VV 124 reached their low specific star formation rate and neutral gas content in isolation, strongly suggesting that internal processes played a major role in their transformation from gas-rich to gas-poor dwarf galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes KKR 25 as an isolated, very-low-surface-brightness dwarf spheroidal with uncertain H I detection and a faint northern H-alpha knot needing spectroscopy.

quotes.quote: KKR 25 . This is an isolated dSph galaxy of very low surface brightness, near which a bright star is projected. KKR 25 is located between the CVnI cloud and the Local Group at a distance of 1.86 Mpc. Its radial velocity of +68 km ^ 1 measured by Huchtmeier et al. (2003), has not been corroborated by deeper observations with GMRT (Begum Chengalur, 2005) who estimated only the upper limit of the HI flux. On the northern side of KKR 25 one can see in H a faint knot, the nature of which can be established by spectral observations.

sources.summary: The paper treats KKR 25 as a nearby isolated dwarf, noting its distance from M31, dwarf-spheroidal appearance, lack of obvious dust, and H I halo.

quotes.quote: The nearest large galaxy to the dwarf KKR 25 is M 31, 1.4 Mpc from KKR 25; the closest known dwarf is KK 230 at 1.0 Mpc away . The nearest large galaxy to KKH 98 is IC 10, 1.8 Mpc away; the closest known dwarfs are IC 5152, GR 8, and KKR 3, at 1.0 Mpc . I think the nearest large galaxy to KKH 18 is M 81, 1.3 Mpc away . The images of these three objects look to me like dwarf spheroidals, with no obvious signs of dust revealed by obscuration or scattered starlight. All three have detected HI halos, suggesting isolated evolution appropriate for their isolated present positions , though I don't know whether the HI is contributing to star formation.

gmw_00002641.name: VV 124
gmw_00002641.overview: VV 124 is an isolated dwarf irregular and dwarf spheroidal transition-type galaxy located at the edge of the Local Group [arXiv:1203.4561, arXiv:1107.2556]. It features pressure-supported kinematics [arXiv:2311.16397], low neutral gas content [arXiv:2311.16397], and extended stellar wings that may represent a disk or merger remnant [arXiv:2101.03189].

sources.summary: The paper presents VV124 as an isolated dIrr/dSph transition galaxy whose stellar dynamics, metallicity distribution, gas content, and weakly rotating wings imply dark matter and possible transformation toward a dSph.

quotes.quote: VV124 (UGC 4879) is an isolated, dwarf irregular/dwarf spheroidal (dIrr/dSph) transition-type galaxy at a distance of 1.36 Mpc. Previous low-resolution spectroscopy yielded inconsistent radial velocities for different components of the galaxy, and photometry hinted at the presence of a stellar disk. In order to quantify the stellar dynamics, we observed individual red giants in VV124 with the Keck/DEIMOS spectrograph.

Since its recent relocation to the edge of the Local Group, the dwarf galaxy VV124 has received considerable scrutiny. However, measurements of its radial velocity have been inconsistent. The galaxy also seemed to possess a disk of stars. We observed VV124 with Keck/DEIMOS, and we determined that its radial velocity agrees with H 1 measurements. The stellar velocities we measured show no evidence of a disk rotating any faster than  km s ^ 1 , but larger samples will be necessary to place stricter limits on rotation.

sources.summary: The paper highlights UGC 4879 as an isolated dwarf with possible disk-like wings, a preferred two-component profile, and structural features unlikely to be caused by massive-galaxy tides.

quotes.quote: UGC 4879 UGC 4879 (also known as VV 124) was first studied in detail by . performed a comprehensive analysis using HST ACS, the Large Binocular Telescope, and HI observations, and identify 2 young star clusters. and noted that it is a very isolated dwarf, and so likely not a backsplash system. Its structure is somewhat unusual, insofar as there are two ``wings" on either side of an otherwise elliptical system, perhaps suggestive of a stellar disk. placed an upper limit on the possible stellar disk rotation of 8.6 km s ^ 1 .

An exception to both of the above generalisations is UGC 4879. Here, the stellar distribution falls off faster than an exponential ( n 1.3 ), and the galaxy does present evidence of extended "wings" along its major axis. This latter feature has previously been interpreted as an edge-on disk.

The tentative evidence of two components in Figure 16 also suggests that a multi-component interpretation could be correct for this galaxy, where both components are dominated by old stars (potentially in contrast to DDO 210 and WLM; see discussion for WLM below). The extreme isolation of this system makes it extremely unlikely that such signatures are the results of a tidal interaction with a more massive galaxy (M31 or the MW). However, it is possible that this type of structure - especially the surface brightness map - could also be the result of a merger in this galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper derives an HB-based age for isolated dwarf VV 124, noting old/intermediate populations, low H I content, pressure support, and likely internally driven quenching.

quotes.quote: In this section, we provide a first application of our new method by deriving stellar ages for the isolated galaxies VV 124 D 1.37 0.07 Mpc, Tully13 and KKR 25 D 1.93 0.06 Mpc, Makarov12 . VV 124 and KKR 25 are two relatively bright ( 10^6 L/L_ 10^7 ), metal-poor ( 1.9 Fe/H 1.5 ) dwarf galaxies located just outside the LG e.g., Karachentsev01,Kopylov08,Bellazzini11a,Makarov12, Kirby12, Kirby13b . Both galaxies host predominantly old/intermediate stellar populations with a small amount of recent ( 1 Gyr) star formation Kopylov08,Bellazzini11b,Makarov12,Neeley21 , have relatively low H_ I content Begum05,Bellazzini11a , and (in the case of VV 124) pressure-supported kinematics Kirby12 . Many of these observations have led to suggestions that these galaxies are transition dwarfs in a late stage of their evolution.

We use this calibration to measure the mass-weighted age of the distant, isolated galaxies KKR 25 and VV 124. For those two galaxies, we measure t^* values of ( 11.21^ +0.70 _ 0.65 Gyr) and ( 11.03^ +0.73 _ 0.68 Gyr) for KKR 25 and VV 124, respectively. These values indicate that early star formation in these targets may have proceeded for an extended period of several Gyr. Combined with the current isolated position of these galaxies, this suggests that KKR 25 and VV 124 reached their low specific star formation rate and neutral gas content in isolation, strongly suggesting that internal processes played a major role in their transformation from gas-rich to gas-poor dwarf galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper presents HST photometry of the isolated dwarf VV124=UGC4879, finding an extended old horizontal branch, metal-poor outskirts, gas, and young central clusters.

quotes.quote: We present deep V and I photometry of the isolated dwarf galaxy VV124=UGC4879, obtained from archival images taken with the Hubble Space Telescope - Advanced Camera for Surveys . In the color-magnitude diagrams of stars at distances larger than 40 from the center of the galaxy, we clearly identify for the first time a well-populated old Horizontal Branch (HB). We show that the distribution of these stars is more extended than that of Red Clump stars. This implies that very old and metal poor populations becomes more and more dominant in the outskirts of VV124.

The dwarf galaxy VV124=UGC4879 has been recently recognized to lie at the outer fringes of the Local Group (LG), near the turn-around radius k08,tik . It is extremely isolated, being at 1.3 Mpc from the nearest giant galaxy (the Milky Way) and at 0.7 Mpc from the nearest dwarf (Leo A).

sources.summary: The paper illustrates UGC 4879 as having high proper-motion uncertainties, finds all orbits indicate a first approach toward the Milky Way, and groups it with low-mass early-forming dwarfs.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:errors shows an illustration of orbital uncertainties with low (Leo I), intermediate (NGC 6822), and high (UGC 4879) PM uncertainties. All orbits are plotted with respect to the MW. The magenta lines indicate the same direct orbital histories as in Figure fig:orbits , while the gray lines indicate the 1,000 orbital histories resulting from the error propagation of the measured uncertainties. For UGC 4879, all orbits indicate a first approach towards the MW but orbits can vary by hundreds of kiloparsecs even in the recent past ( < 2 Gyr).

Shallower CMDs (as available for IC10, NGC 6822, Pegasus, Sag DIG, and UGC 4879) are generally less reliable, especially for constraining the exact amount of oldest star formation. Leo A, Leo T, Pegasus dIrr, Phoenix, Sag DIG, UGC 4879) we see a different pattern with much of the stellar mass being accumulated in the early universe, prior to the epoch of reionization, and low levels of star formation in more recent times Jacobs_2011,Weisz_2012,Weisz2014,Gallart2015 .

sources.summary: The paper treats VV 124 as one of seven isolated Local Group dwarf galaxies, measuring red-giant velocities and dispersions and comparing isolated dwarfs with Milky Way and M31 satellites.

quotes.quote: We measured velocities of \ individual red giant stars in seven isolated dwarf galaxies in the Local Group: NGC 6822, IC 1613, VV 124 (UGC 4879), the Pegasus dwarf irregular galaxy (DDO 216), Leo A, Cetus, and Aquarius (DDO 210). We also computed velocity dispersions, taking into account the measurement uncertainties on individual stars. None of the isolated galaxies is denser than the densest Local Group satellite galaxy. Furthermore, the isolated dwarf galaxies have no obvious distinction in the velocity dispersion--half-light radius plane from the satellite galaxies of the Milky Way and M31. The similarity of the isolated and satellite galaxies' dynamics and structural parameters imposes limitations on environmental solutions to the too-big-to-fail problem, wherein there are fewer dense dwarf satellite galaxies than would be expected from cold dark matter simulations.

sources.summary: The paper identifies UGC 4879 as a notable dwarf with low H I-to-stellar mass, evidence for intermediate-age quiescence, young and old stars, and a need for deeper CMD confirmation.

quotes.quote: UGC 4879 presents an interesting case. Despite its low M _ HI /M _* ratio, there is evidence to suggest that it has little to no star formation at intermediate times, but significant populations of both young and old stars jacobs2011star . However, it should be noted that the CMDs used to construct the SFHs that support this conclusion do not reach the oldest main sequence turn-off. A deeper CMD is really needed to confirm these findings.

gmw_00002642.name: Q 1321+058
gmw_00002642.overview: Q 1321+058 is an ultraluminous infrared type II quasar [arXiv:astro-ph/0702334] that may harbor a Compton-thick active galactic nucleus [arXiv:astro-ph/0508557, arXiv:astro-ph/0702334]. The system is in a late-stage merger, displaying an elongated tidal tail [arXiv:astro-ph/0702334] and a surrounding starburst [arXiv:astro-ph/0508557].

sources.summary: The paper describes Q 1321+058 as an ultraluminous infrared type II quasar with a tidal-tail merger signature, stellar optical continuum, dust-heated infrared emission, and outflowing gas.

quotes.quote: We present here only a brief account of the results from an analysis of the optical/ultraviolet spectra and the broad band properties of the ULIRG Q 1321+058. Detailed analysis and discussion will be presented elsewhere (Wang et al. 2007, in preparation). The object was classified as a quasar based on a low resolution spectrum taken in an attempt of optical identification of HEAO-A2 X-ray sources (Remillard et al. 1993). However, it was not detected by XMM Newton (Bianchi et al. 2005), which led those authors to propose that it is a Compton-thick type II object. Its HST images show an elongated tidal tail and a bright nucleus, indicating a merger at a later stage. Its SDSS spectrum and HST FOS spectrum were extracted from the archives and were corrected for the Galactic reddening.

The broad band spectral energy distribution (SED) of Q 1321+058 as shown in Fig fig2 is consistent with that of a Type II quasar: the steep rise from near to mid-infrared and no apparent absorption or emission features around 10 m suggest that the mid-infrared emission is dominated by an active nucleus.
Q 1321+058 would appear as a BAL QSO provided that the line of sight intersected the outflow.

sources.summary: The paper notes that IRAS FSC 13218 + 0552 had only an XMM upper limit, but prior work suggested a Compton-thick AGN with strong starburst and warm infrared color.

quotes.quote: One OHM was targeted by \ (IRAS FSC 13218 + 0552 at z=0.205 , with net exposures of  8.6--9.7 ks in the EPIC MOS/pn cameras) but only an upper limit, consistent with the values we obtained for our sources (see 4 and Table 2), was derived (Bianchi et al. 2005). Recently, it has been suggested that this source, characterized by an extremely red infrared continuum (Low et al. 1989) and a blueshifted component in the \ line (e.g., Zheng et al. 2002), might harbour a Compton-thick AGN (i.e., with N_ H 10^ 24  cm ^ 2 ), surrounded by a strong starburst (see 4.4 and Fig. 6 of Bianchi et al. 2005). We also note that IRAS FSC 13218 + 0552 has the warmest f_ 100 /f_ 60 colour in Fig. 2 (i.e., it is the left-most object in the plot), suggestive of an AGN contribution at IR wavelengths.

gmw_00002643.name: NGC 6762
gmw_00002643.overview: NGC 6762 is an early-type galaxy containing a weak LINER nucleus and extended LINER-like gas emission [arXiv:1202.0511]. Its ionized gas and stellar emission are kinematically and morphologically aligned, with the gas likely ionized by post-asymptotic giant branch stars [arXiv:1202.0511, arXiv:1408.5888].

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 6762's warm ionized gas, finding gas and stellar emission aligned, weak LINER-like emission, and pAGB stars sufficient to power the Balmer emission.

quotes.quote: In NGC 6762, the ionized gas and stellar emission display similar morphologies, while the emission line morphology is elongated in NGC 5966, spanning 6 kpc, and is oriented roughly orthogonal to the major axis of the stellar continuum ellipsoid. Whereas gas and stars are kinematically aligned in NGC 6762, the gas is kinematically decoupled from the stars in NGC 5966.

Both galaxies have weak emission-lines relative to the continuum [ EW(H ) 3 ] and have very low excitation, log( [ O iii ] 5007/H ) 0.5. Based on optical diagnostic ratios ( [ O iii ] 5007/H , [ N ii ] 6584/H , [ S ii ] 6717,6731/H , [ O i ] 6300/H ), both objects contain a LINER nucleus and an extended LINER-like gas emission. The emission line ratios do not vary significantly with radius or aperture, which indicates that the nebular properties are spatially homogeneous. The gas emission in NGC 6762 can be best explained by photoionization by pAGB stars without the need of invoking any other excitation mechanism.

Typical values of H _ pred. /H _ obs. are close to 1 for NGC 6762, suggesting that pAGB stars can produce enough ionizing photons to explain the Balmer line fluxes. In the case of NGC 6762, the morphology of gas and stellar emission following each other is an additional and strong argument supporting the hypothesis that pAGB stars are responsible for ionizing the gas.

sources.summary: NGC 6762 is a LINER-classified CALIFA galaxy whose line emission is mostly consistent with pAGB-star ionization rather than young stars, making its properties distinct from the rest of the sample.

quotes.quote: In contrast, 90 percent of the spaxels in NGC 6762 have EW_ H < 3 ; indicating that pAGB stars are likely to be responsible for a significant fraction of its line emission. Kehrig12 compare the spatially resolved emission line ratios of NGC 6762 with AGN, pAGB and fast shock models, and conclude that the pAGB scenario is most consistent with the observations.
It is interesting to note that NGC 6762 appears to have distinctly different properties from the rest of the galaxies in our sample.
The absence of a correlation between the \ and \ ratios in NGC 6762 suggests that young stars are not a significant source of ionization; consistent with the low EW_ H values observed in this object.

gmw_00002644.name: NGC 5966
gmw_00002644.overview: NGC 5966 is an early-type galaxy that hosts a nuclear radio source, suggesting the presence of an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1202.0511]. It features a 6-kiloparsec elongated structure of warm ionized gas that is kinematically decoupled from and oriented roughly orthogonal to its stars [arXiv:1202.0511].

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 5966's warm ionized gas, finding a 6 kpc structure orthogonal to the stellar body, kinematic decoupling, and likely AGN-related ionization.

quotes.quote: In NGC 6762, the ionized gas and stellar emission display similar morphologies, while the emission line morphology is elongated in NGC 5966, spanning 6 kpc, and is oriented roughly orthogonal to the major axis of the stellar continuum ellipsoid. Whereas gas and stars are kinematically aligned in NGC 6762, the gas is kinematically decoupled from the stars in NGC 5966. A decoupled rotating disk or an ``ionization cone'' are two possible interpretations of the elongated ionized gas structure in NGC 5966.

In the case of NGC 5966, the differing gas and stellar morphologies, and the energetic balance indicate that an additional ionization source other than pAGB stars is needed. The existence of a nuclear radio source in NGC 5966 suggests that an AGN might be present in this galaxy, and may be responsible for the extended ( 6 kpc) elongated ionized gas emission. Shock-ionization cannot be ruled out in either galaxy. An ionization cone is a possible interpretation for the elongated gas feature in NGC 5966, which would be the first ionization cone associated with a weak emission-line galaxy.

gmw_00002645.name: 4C+00.03
gmw_00002645.overview: 4C+00.03 is a late-type galaxy [arXiv:2405.17552] hosting a Type 2 active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2405.17552] and a compact radio source [arXiv:2201.06612]. It displays post-starburst spectral features [arXiv:2201.06612] and exhibits extended, low-surface-brightness structures likely caused by a tidal interaction [arXiv:2311.15009].

sources.summary: The paper presents 4C+00.03 as an intermediate-age compact radio source with a red continuum, strong emission lines, elevated metallicity, and post-starburst/E+A spectral features.

quotes.quote: The top panel of Fig. fig:fit2 illustrates the results for 4C+00.03. The optical spectrum of this object is dominated by a stellar population of intermediate age. It has a red continuum and strong emission lines. We find the source being older and of higher metallicity than J1430+1339. The internal reddening is similar in both sources.

It is noteworthy that 4C+00.03 displays spectral features typical of transitioning post-starburst or E+A galaxies. These objects have high-order, deep Balmer absorption lines and measurable nebular emission lines. Such features are indicative of either an ongoing starburst with the youngest stellar population obscured by dust or that the star formation has not been fully quenched in these galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSS J002833.42+005510.9 as a low-mass PSS-CSO galaxy with extended low-surface-brightness structures, likely from tidal interaction with another galaxy.

quotes.quote: SDSS J002833.42+005510.9 is a relatively small galaxy with the lowest stellar mass (log M _ / M _ = 10.58 ) in our sample. The contrast-enhanced -band image reveals an extended structure of low surface brightness to the west of the main galaxy. This feature is barely visible on the image from the SDSS legacy survey, but is similarly well seen on the _ deep co-adds from the IAC Stripe 82 Legacy Project Fliri_2016 . The structure seems to be connected with the main body and extends up to at least 45 kpc from the nucleus. A fainter and shorter structure is visible on the opposite side.
The most likely interpretation of the extended structure is a low-surface brightness galaxy in tidal interaction with SDSS J002833.42+005510.9 .

sources.summary: The paper decomposes the host of SDSS J002833.42+005510.9 and classifies the LLC Type 2 AGN as a late-type galaxy with a pseudo-bulge.

quotes.quote: We performed the host galaxy photometric decomposition of y-band image of J002833.42+005510.9 <4C +00.03>, ( z = 0.104, Type 2 AGN), to determine its morphological type. As explained in the previous section, since this is a Type 2 AGN, a PSF model was not needed to remove the AGN contamination. The best fit for this galaxy was achieved with two Sérsic functions, one for the bulge and one for the disc (S1 and S2, respectively).

For this source, we applied GALFITM to the g_ P1 , i_ P1 (containing H ) and y_ P1 bands. We left the total magnitude, the effective radius R_ e and the Sérsic index free to vary for both Sérsic functions, and fit them with a 2nd-order Chebyshev polynomial to give the freedom to interpolate data precisely. We found consistent results for S1 and S2 to the one obtained for the y-band, confirming that this AGN is hosted in a late-type galaxy with a pseudo-bulge.

gmw_00002646.name: 2MASXJ09240529+1410217
gmw_00002646.overview: 2MASXJ09240529+1410217 is an elliptical brightest cluster galaxy [arXiv:2103.08682] dominated by an old stellar population [arXiv:2201.06612]. It hosts a radio-loud active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2103.08682] and exhibits a small fraction of young and intermediate-age stars that indicate residual star formation [arXiv:2201.06612].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J092405.30+14 as the elliptical BCG in A795 hosting a radio-loud FR0/LERG AGN, with low-efficiency accretion, compact radio morphology, and a small MERLIN core-jet elongation.

quotes.quote: The elliptical BCG J092405.30+14, located at RA, DEC = 09:24:05.3, +14:10:21.5 (J2000) hosts a radio loud AGN. 2018MNRAS.476.5535T classified it as a FR0 radio galaxy, and concluded that the X-ray incoming photons are produced in the jet and that an inefficient, advection-dominated accretion flow powers the central engine.

2012MNRAS.421.1569B derived the information on the stellar mass of the BCG (4.2 10^ 11 M _ ), the 4000 break (1.76) and the [ OIII ] line luminosity of the AGN ( 5.4 10^ 40 erg s ^ 1 ), arguing that J092405.30+14 is a non-star forming, low-excitation line radio galaxy (LERG). We exploited the values reported by 2012MNRAS.421.1569B and the velocity dispersion measurement of the SDSS DR12 2015ApJS..219...12A to compute the accretion parameters of the central engine in J092405.30+14 (see Tab. tab:laccledd ): we used the definition of 2004ApJ...613..109H of L_ acc =3500 L_ [ OIII ] to obtain the accretion luminosity (second column); from the M _ BH _ relation of 2002ApJ...574..740T we derived the mass of the black hole (third column), which we used to compute the Eddington luminosity L_ Edd (fourth column). We find a ratio L_ acc / L_ Edd of 3.8 10^ 3 , typical of LERG sources in which the central engine is powered by an advection-dominated accretion flow (e.g., 2018MNRAS.476.5535T ).

Radio observations of J092405.30+14 in the GHz band disclosed its compact radio morphology: it is unresolved in the FIRST images, thus implying an upper limit on its size at 1.4 GHz of 5'' ( 12 kpc, see Fig. fig:bcgfirst ); 2015MNRAS.453.1201H did not resolve the source at 4.8 GHz with the Very Large Array (VLA) in configuration C (resolution of 3.4'' 8.3 kpc), but resolved it at 8.4 GHz with the VLA in configuration A (resolution of 0.2'' 0.48 kpc). This source has also been observed by 2010MNRAS.408.2261K with MERLIN at 5 GHz (sub-arcsec resolution), which revealed an elongation in the north-east direction with a largest linear size of 0.30'' ( 0.73 kpc), suggesting a core-jet morphology.

sources.summary: The paper describes 2MASXJ09240529+1410217 as an old, red early-type compact radio source with a strong 4000 A break and a small residual young/intermediate component.

quotes.quote: We present the results for 2MASXJ09240529+1410217 in the bottom panel of Fig. fig:fit2 . This object is dominated by old stars, has a red continuum and a prominent 4000 \ break. Notice the presence of absorption lines produced in the atmospheres of giant and cold stars such as Ca k 3933 , Mg i 5175 and NaD 5892 . Although the spectrum of this source displays characteristics of early-type galaxies, there is a small fraction of young and intermediate age stars (less than 10 ) indicating that there is a residual star formation in the galaxy.

A similar result was found by Diniz for an early-type galaxy hosting an AGN. Their findings indicated that while the old-aged (13 Gyr) stellar population is confined to the central region, the intermediate-aged (900 Myr) one surrounds the galaxy nucleus. Unfortunately, we do not have enough spectral and spatial resolution to state whether this is the case of the 2MASXJ09240529+1410217 source.

gmw_00002647.name: CDFS-309
aliases_external: Luo et al. CDF-S Chandra source 309
gmw_00002647.overview: CDFS-309 is a Compton-thick quasar [arXiv:1504.03329, arXiv:2003.08631] located at a redshift of approximately z = 2.6 [arXiv:1107.3777, arXiv:1504.03329]. The active nucleus exhibits a strong, redshifted iron K emission line [arXiv:1101.3478, arXiv:1504.03329], while its host galaxy shows a double-peaked line profile that may indicate a merging system [arXiv:2003.08631].

sources.summary: The paper treats CDFS-309 as a z~2.6 low-Lx/L6 Compton-thick AGN; prior work found a high-EW Fe K line, and this analysis confirms Fe K improvement.

quotes.quote: Four low- 6 sources could be classified as Compton-thick: CDFS-309, CDFS-95, CDFS-197, and CDFS-398. CDFS-309 has been discussed in detail in Feruglio2011 and was found to present a Compton-thick source on the basis of an Fe K line with a high EW. Due to the limited photon-statistics we cannot tell whether the source is flat 0.6 or obscured with N_H 3 10^ 23 . However, the addition of an FeK line improves the statistic by C 20 yielding an EW of 3.6 1.8 keV.

One of these (CDFS-309 at a redshift of z 2.6) has been already reported as a Compton-thick source by Feruglio2011 while the other one (CDFS-398 at a redshift of z=1.222) is reported here for the first time.

sources.summary: XID 490S is described as a z=2.579 quasar with a strong redshifted Fe K line and heavy Compton-thick column densities supported by multiple spectral models.

quotes.quote: XID 490S: This z=2.579 quasar shows evidence for a strong emission line, consistent with the redshifted Fe K line at E=6.4  keV, with an equivalent width of EW>1  keV (although the limited counting statistics prevent us from obtaining better constraints on the EW ). The spectral fitting results from model 2 constrained the intrinsic column density largely in the CT regime (see Table tab1 ). As in the previous case, fitting the spectrum with PLCABS or Torus models constrains the column density to lower values than that obtained from model 2 (  (4-10) 10^ 24  cm ^ 2 ), but still consistent with heavy CT columns. Similar results were obtained by feruglio2011 and brightman2012 , who identified this source as CT. As for XID 435S, the intrinsic X-ray luminosity estimated by correcting for the high \ obtained from the spectral fit would place this source on the L_ X L_ 6 m relation (see Fig. fig.l6lx ).

sources.summary: The paper identifies XID 666 (BzK 4892) as a Compton-thick QSO with detected ALMA line emission; its double-peaked profile suggests rotation but may instead indicate merging systems.

quotes.quote: XID 666 (BzK 4892) is a Compton-thick QSO studied by .

We detect XID 42, XID 539 and XID 666 with a signal-to-noise ratio (S/N) equal to 6.2, 4.7 and 4.0/4.9, respectively. The S/N is defined as the ratio between the value of the brightest pixel and the root mean square (rms) derived averaging the rms measured in four regions placed at 10" from any source. Since for XID 666 we detected a double peaked line, the two values refer to the blue- and red-shifted peaks.

Indications of a rotating system for XID 666 come from the observed velocity gradient that is clearly distributed along a defined direction (moments 1, Fig. fig:moments ), and from the line that shows a double--peaked feature (Fig. fig:spectra ), with the blue and red--shifted peaks that actually correspond to two different emitting regions. However, the very asymmetric distribution of the two peaks suggests the possibility that XID 666 could actually be a blend of two merging systems.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 4892 as a z=2.578 galaxy with X-ray detections, strong Iron K emission indicating Compton-thick obscuration, and quasar-range intrinsic 2-10 keV luminosity.

quotes.quote: 4892 is a source with spectroscopic redshift z = 2.578 (Szokoly et al. 2004, Vanzella et al. 2006; based on multiple narrow emission lines), detected in the X-rays already in the 1 Ms Chandra data (Giacconi et al. 2001) as well as in the 2 Ms data (Luo et al. 2010). In the 4 Ms dataset it has 75 net counts in the 0.5-7 keV band and hardness ratio of 0.58 (the hardness ratio is defined as HR = hard-soft/hard+soft, where S and H are the soft and hard band net counts detected by Chandra ).

The rest frame equivalent widths of the Iron lines are EW =2.3_ 0.6 ^ +1.0 keV for 4892 and EW =1.2 0.4 keV for 8608 (for a reflection model, see Table 1), implying absorbing columns exceeding 10^ 24 cm ^ 2 .
All in all, it appears that the 2-10 keV luminosity of 4892 is in the QSO range, well in excess of 10^ 44 erg s ^ 1 , and more likely of 10^ 44.5-45 erg s ^ 1 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies source 309 as one of three Compton-thick AGN candidates, confirming the earlier Feruglio et al. classification with XMM-Newton data and noting that Brightman et al. also classified it as CT.

quotes.quote: However, only three of these can be considered as CT AGN: sources 95, 230, and 309 ( Chandra ids), although source 95 is only marginally CT.
Source 309 was already classified as a CT source in feruglio11 . Here we also confirm its CT nature using XMM-Newton data.
As for our three CT candidates, only source 309 is classified as such in brightman14 .

gmw_00002648.name: CDFS-398
gmw_00002648.overview: CDFS-398 is an active galactic nucleus located at a redshift of z=1.222 [arXiv:1107.3777, arXiv:1303.5556]. The source exhibits a flat X-ray spectrum and a high iron K equivalent width, which are characteristic of a reflection-dominated, Compton-thick active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1107.3777, arXiv:1303.5556].

sources.summary: The paper argues CDFS-398 is a newly reported z=1.222 Compton-thick AGN; its flat X-ray spectrum, reflection-dominated fit, and Fe K equivalent width support that classification.

quotes.quote: From Table xmm we see that the spectrum of CDFS-398 is flat ( 0.8 0.3 ). Since the source is detected by , there are good photon statistics available allowing a more complex fit to the spectrum. We consider two spectral models to fit its X-ray spectrum. (1) an absorbed power-law spectrum with the addition of an FeK line, (plcabs+zga), and (2) a reflection dominated model with an FeK line, pexrav+zga.

This source displays a very flat spectrum < 1.2 at the 90 confidence level. This flat spectrum is characteristic of reflection-dominated Compton-thick sources Matt2004 . The rest-frame EW of the Fe K line is 0.68^ +0.66 _ 0.44 keV, which is again consistent with Compton-thick emission Fukazawa2011 .

One of these (CDFS-309 at a redshift of z 2.6) has been already reported as a Compton-thick source by Feruglio2011 while the other one (CDFS-398 at a redshift of z=1.222) is reported here for the first time.

sources.summary: The paper treats PID-324 as one of four secure Compton-thick AGN candidates, with high FeK equivalent width, joint spectral-fit support, and a likely reflection-dominated spectrum.

quotes.quote: Four out of nine sources (PID-66, 144, 147, 324) present high rest-frame FeK EW (see table 1). These are suggestive of high absorbing column densities likely corresponding to Compton-thick AGN.

In agreement with the spectral analysis, presented in the previous section, four sources (PID-66, 144, 147 and 324) present FeK lines with high (rest-frame) EW ( > 0.4 keV). Therefore, the joint and analysis corroborates that these four sources have a good probability for being Compton-thick.

Two of the sources are most likely transmission dominated (PID-66 and PID-144) while the other two are reflection dominated (PID-147 and PID-324) . Although our sample is by no means statistically complete, it represents the most well studied examples of Compton-thick AGN at moderate to high redshifts. In particular, the redshifts of the four most secure candidate Compton-thick sources are z=1.185, 1.222, 1.53 and 3.7.

sources.summary: S472 is a compact VLBI source classified as an AGN by q_24, with a preferred spectroscopic redshift of 0.55 and hard absorbed X-ray spectrum.

quotes.quote: S472 This is a very compact source since 93 of the ATCA flux density are seen in the VLBI image. Based on its q_ 24 =-0.31 this is an AGN. There is conflicting information about its redshift: give a photometric redshift of 1.22, but measured a spectroscopic redshift of 0.55, which we deem more reliable. Its X-ray spectrum is hard between 0.5 keV -- 8 keV and indicates an absorbed type 2 AGN ( Lehmer2005 , Szokoly2004 ).

gmw_00002649.name: NRAO 530
gmw_00002649.overview: NRAO 530 is an optically violently variable flat-spectrum radio quasar located at a redshift of 0.902 [arXiv:1202.3873, arXiv:2206.13665]. The blazar exhibits intense, multiwavelength variability [arXiv:0708.4306, arXiv:1006.4777] and hosts a double-sided kiloparsec-scale radio jet that is misaligned from its parsec-scale jet by 90 degrees [arXiv:1006.4777, arXiv:2206.13665].

sources.summary: The paper uses NRAO 530 as a case study of hard X-ray blazar microvariability, describing its rapid INTEGRAL flare, Swift confirmation, multiband variability, and expanding radio jet.

quotes.quote: Since we will adopt NRAO 530 as a case study, we briefly summarize the properties of its rapid variability event and we refer to Foschini et al. (2006b) for more details. This event occurred on February 17 , 2004 , when INTEGRAL was performing a deep exposure of the Galactic Centre. NRAO 530 is usually below the sensitivity limit of the imager IBIS, but within about 1 hour the blazar reached a flux of about 2 10^ 10 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 in the 20-40 keV energy band ( 5 detection in the fully-coded field of view) and then faded again below the detection limit.

This flare was so short and intense to cast some doubts on its origin (it might have been produced by a Galactic source within the 3' error circle of IBIS/ISGRI instead of the blazar). However, four observations with Swift /XRT performed in 2006 and 2007 have shown that the only source present inside the IBIS error circle is NRAO 530 . Moreover, observations at other wavelengths supported the fact that this strong variability could be due to the blazar. Indeed, NRAO 530 is known to display intense variability at almost all frequencies: e.g. optical (Webb et al. 1988), radio and rays (Bower et al. 1997). Radio observations at 2 cm with the VLBA (The MOJAVE Project, Lister Homan 2005) carried out a few days before the event (February 11 , 2004 ), revealed a moderate increase of the polarization and after roughly 1 year radio maps showed a clear expansion of the radio jet.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NRAO 530 with multi-frequency VLBI, identifying the core, measuring jet position shifts, variability, component speeds, and a swinging non-ballistic jet.

quotes.quote: NRAO 530 was monitored with the Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) at three frequencies (22, 43 and 86 GHz) on 10 consecutive days in 2007 May during observations of the Galactic center (Sgr A*). Furthermore, analysis of archival data of NRAO 530 at 15 GHz over the last 10 years allows us to study its detailed jet kinematics. We identified the compact component located at the southern-end of the jet as the VLBI core, consistent with previous studies. The 10-d monitoring data at the three high frequencies were shown to produce high-quality and self-consistent measurements of the component positions, from which we detected for the first time a two-dimensional frequency-dependent position shift. In addition, the repeated measurements also permit us to investigate the interday flux density and structure variability of NRAO 530.

We find that it is more variable for the inner jet components than those further out. We obtained apparent velocities for eight jet components with _ app ranging from 2 to 26c . The changes in the morphology of NRAO 530 were related to the motion of separate jet components with the most pronounced changes occurring in the regions close to the core.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NRAO 530 as an optically violently variable blazar at redshift 0.902, finding multiple harmonic quasi-periodicities in its long-term radio light curves.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we search for periodicity in the radio light curves of the blazar NRAO 530, making use of the PDM technique. The blazar NRAO 530 (also named PKS 1730-130) is a well-known Optically Violently Variable (OVV) quasar at a redshift of 0.902 (Junkkarinen 1984). It exhibits prominent variability across the whole electromagnetic spectrum covering the radio, optical, UltraViolet, X-ray and ray bands (Hong et al. 2008). NRAO 530 is one of the few blazars showing persistent quasi-periodicity on time scale of a few years (Hovatta et al. 2008; An et al. 2012) and is a good candidate for investigating long-term periodic variabilities and the associated physical processes.

Different methods arrive at a consistent conclusion that there are multiple periodicities in the radio light curves of NRAO 530. Moreover there periodicities are found to have a harmonic frequency relation, n f_1 , where n=1,2,3,... (An et al. 2012). However, the fundamental oscillation frequency f_1 1 20 year ^ 1 is not detected over the total 35-year time span.

sources.summary: The paper presents multi-frequency VLBA polarimetry of blazar NRAO 530, identifying component A as the radio core and mapping component B polarization plus spine-sheath jet structure.

quotes.quote: We report on VLBA polarimetric observations of NRAO 530 at 5, 8, 15, 22, and 43 GHz within an interval of one week in February 1997. Total intensity, fractional polarization and electric vector position angle (EVPA) distributions at all these frequencies are presented. Model fitting has been performed to the full polarization visibility data, from which the fitted southmost component A is confirmed as the core of the radio structure with relatively high brightness temperature and hard spectrum between 15 and 43 GHz in comparison with the central component B of dominant flux.

As a contrast, the component B shows a well fitted power-law spectrum with the spectral index of about 0.5 ( f ^ ), and a linear correlation between EVPAs and wavelength square with the observed rotation measure (RM) of about 1062 rad m^ 2 , indicating its structural singleness. Assuming that the component B has a comparable degree of polarization without depolarization at these frequencies, the decrease in fractional polarization with wavelength mainly results from opacity and Faraday rotation, in which the opacity plays quite a large part of role. A spine-sheath like structure in fractional polarization ( m ) is detected covering almost the whole emission region at 5 and 8 GHz, with degree of polarization relatively low along the jet spine, and getting higher toward two sides of the jet.

sources.summary: The paper describes NRAO 530 as a blazar with a short INTEGRAL-detected hard-X-ray flare, Swift confirmation as the only high-energy source, and high-amplitude multiwavelength variability.

quotes.quote: NRAO 530 (z=0.902) The importance of having a large FOV is again underlined in this case: NRAO 530 had a strong and short flare that was serendipitously detected by INTEGRAL during the normal operations of the Galactic Centre Deep Exposure on February 17 , 2004 . The 20-40 keV flux emitted by the blazar increased up to 2 10^ 10 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 and then dropped below the instrument detection limit in less than 1 hour (see Fig. fig:NRAO530 ) . Given such extraordinary behaviour, never observed in a flat-spectrum radio quasar, there was the doubt of a contaminating source within the 3' error circle of IBIS. However, a couple of snapshots with the X-Ray Telescope (XRT) onboard Swift revealed that NRAO 530 is the only high-energy source present in the IBIS error circle. In addition, this blazar is known to display high-amplitude variability at almost all the wavelengths and therefore, the flux measured by INTEGRAL is not at odds with historical observations.

sources.summary: The paper images NRAO 530 as a distant blazar whose radio structure includes a curved western jet ending in a hotspot and plume, abrupt inner bending, and a faint broad counter-jet.

quotes.quote: Here, we apply this procedure to observations of a distant blazar, NRAO 530, revealing the radio structures surrounding the core in unprecedented detail. Our wideband JVLA image of this source at 5.5 GHz (C band) shows that the kiloparsec radio structure of NRAO 530 is prominently characterized by a moderately curved western jet terminating at a hot spot where the radiation is stretched further out into a diffuse radio lobe or plume. Close to the radio core, an abrupt bending of the jet is revealed in the high-resolution (< 100 milliarcsec) images at 33 GHz (Ka band), showing an evolution of the position angle of the jet from the north at the VLBI scale (50 mas, or 400 pc black projected ), increasing toward the west at the larger VLA scales (1 arcsec, or 10 kpc). The continuation of the jet axis drift forms the curved western jet extending out to 200 kpc. In contrast to the main radio structure, a faint and broad counter-jet is present on the eastern side with a curvature antisymmetric to the western jet.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NRAO 530 as a z=0.902 OVV blazar and reports an INTEGRAL hard X-ray flare lasting under 2000 seconds, alongside radio polarization and past multiwavelength outbursts.

quotes.quote: We report about a short flare from the blazar NRAO 530 occurred on 17 February 2004 and detected serendipitously by the IBIS/ISGRI detector on board INTEGRAL . In the 20-40 keV energy range, the source, that is otherwise below the detection limit, is detected at a level of 2 10^ 10 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 during a time interval of less than 2000 s, which is about a factor 2 above the detection threshold. At other wavelengths, only nearly-simultaneous radio data are available (1 observation at 2 cm on 11 February 2004 ), indicating a moderate increase of the polarization.

NRAO 530 ( z=0.902 ) is a blazar belonging to the optically violent variable (OVV) quasar subclass, with emission lines in the optical spectrum, particularly H with a rest-frame equivalent width of 16 , smaller than usually observed in OVVs (Junkkarinen 1984; cf also Veron-Cetty Veron 2000). It exhibits strong outbursts in radio (Bower et al. 1997), optical (up to 3 magnitudes in 1977 , Pollock et al. 1979, Webb et al. 1988), X-ray ( HEAO-1 , Marscher et al. 1979), and ray bands (EGRET/CGRO, Mukherjee et al. 1997), with significant emission above 1 GeV (Lamb Macomb 1997).

sources.summary: The paper describes NRAO 530 as a highly polarized radio quasar with a double-sided kiloparsec jet and variability from radio through gamma-ray bands.

quotes.quote: B1730 130 NRAO 530 is a high-polarized radio quasar which hosts a double-sided kilo-parsec scale jet. This source is actively variable in the radio marscher81, feng06 , optical pollock79 , X-ray foschini06 , and ray hartman99 wavebands. We used the Swift data of NRAO 530 observed in June 2009 with an integration time of 5 ks (obsid 00035387012).

sources.summary: The paper treats J1733-130 (NRAO 530) as a nearby blazar observed with Sgr A*, using it for calibration checks and reporting 5 percent 230 GHz intensity variability.

quotes.quote: The pipeline software applied system temperatures obtained for J1733-130 (NRAO 530) to , which introduces an error because of the different atmospheric optical depth toward these two sources.

Data were obtained during short ( 20 min), self-contained observation sets, in which observations of \ alternated with those of the relatively nearby blazar J1733-130, and were bookended with observations of a flux standard (typically Neptune).
J1733-130, J1700-261, and J1427-421 show 5 , 9 , and 14 variability, respectively, in the fitted intensity at 230 GHz assuming a power-law spectrum for these sources.

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1733 1304 with PKS 1730 130 and notes a nearby Gaia source that could affect its polarization, leading to fixed-aperture nuclear photometry.

quotes.quote: J1733 1304: PKS 1730 130 has a nearby source located 1.8 arcsec from the core according to Gaia data. It could potentially affect the polarization. We measured the nucleus with a fixed 5 arcsec aperture.

sources.summary: The paper describes PKS 1730 130 as a highly variable FSRQ at redshift 0.9, with no detected extended X-ray or optical emission and a 90-degree pc-to-kpc jet misalignment.

quotes.quote: PKS 1730-130 PKS 1730 130 is a highly variable FSRQ at a redshift of 0.9. We did not detect X-ray or optical emission from the extended jet regions of this source. The pc-scale Pushkarev2017 and the kiloparsec scale jets are misaligned from each other by an angle of 90 .

gmw_00002650.name: IC 4182
gmw_00002650.overview: IC 4182 is a nearby disk galaxy located within 15 megaparsecs [arXiv:1205.5072]. Its outer disk contains marginally resolved star clusters that are spatially correlated with neutral hydrogen gas [arXiv:1205.5072].

sources.summary: The paper includes IC 4182 in its six-galaxy LBT outer-disk sample, finding marginally resolved star clusters beyond R_25 that trace H I gas and imply low-level cluster formation.

quotes.quote: Expanding on the pilot project of , we present deep ( 27.5 mag V band point-source limiting magnitude) U \ and V band imaging of six galaxies: IC 4182, NGC 3351, NGC 4736, NGC 4826, NGC 5474, and NGC 6503. We find that the outer disk of each galaxy is populated with marginally-resolved star clusters with masses 10^3 M_ and ages up to 1 Gyr (masses and ages are limited by the depth of our imaging and uncertainties are large given how photometry can be strongly affected by the presence or absence of a few stars in such low mass systems), and that they are typically found out to at least 2 R_ 25 but sometimes as far as 3 to 4 R_ 25 - even beyond the apparent H 1 disk. The mean rate of cluster formation for 1 R_ 25 R 1.5R_ 25 is at least one every 2.5 Myr and the clusters are spatially correlated with the H 1 , most strongly with higher density gas near the periphery of the optical disk and with lower density neutral gas at the H 1 disk periphery.

We present new observations of six galaxies (IC 4182, NGC 3351, NGC 4736, NGC 4826, NGC 5474, NGC 6503) with the LBC-Blue imaging camera on the LBT on the dates listed in Table tab:gals . These systems comprise a representative sample of nearby disk galaxies by spanning a range of physical disk sizes. All are nearby ( D 15 Mpc) and only two, NGC 4826 and NGC 6503, are seen at high inclination ( > 60^ ).

gmw_00002651.name: NGC 1052-DF2
gmw_00002651.overview: NGC 1052-DF2 is a low-mass, diffuse dwarf galaxy [arXiv:2202.05836, arXiv:1910.07529] that is notably deficient in dark matter [arXiv:2202.05836, arXiv:2103.08610]. It is associated with the NGC 1052 group [arXiv:2202.05836] and hosts an unusually luminous population of massive globular clusters [arXiv:2103.08610, arXiv:1910.07529].

sources.summary: The paper argues that DF2 formed with DF4 in a single bullet-dwarf collision, leaving it dark-matter deficient, unbound, far behind the NGC1052 group, and aligned with a trail of galaxies.

quotes.quote: The situation at that point, a 300 \ encounter between two galaxies in the gas-rich environment of a young group, is a close match to the initial conditions of mini-bullet-cluster scenarios that have previously been proposed for the formation of DF2 and DF4. In a near head-on collision between two gas-rich galaxies, the collisional gas can be shocked and separated from the collisionless dark matter and pre-existing stars. The accompanying star formation favours massive clumps in highly compressed gas, producing the unusual globular clusters as well as the lack of dark matter. Galaxies that form this way are initially compact, in apparent conflict with the large half-light radii of DF2 and DF4. However, intense feedback accompanying the formation of the globular clusters is expected to increase the sizes of the newly formed galaxies, an effect that is particularly efficient when the stars are not bound by a dark matter halo.

The collision produced DF2 and DF4, with DF2's velocity and orbit similar to progenitor 1 and DF4's similar to progenitor 2. DF2 is currently unbound and about 3 Mpc behind the group, whereas DF4 remained bound and has begun falling back.

The joint formation of DF2 and DF4 in a single bullet dwarf event explains their lack of dark matter, large sizes, luminous and large globular clusters, striking similarity, large distance between them, large radial velocity difference, and the presence of a trail of other galaxies on the DF2 -- DF4 axis.

sources.summary: The paper frames DF2 as a dark-matter-deficient low-mass galaxy near NGC 1052, comparing it with simulations and favoring past close encounters as an origin.

quotes.quote: For this reason, the detection of DF2 and DF4, two low-mass galaxies devoid of dark matter, was not anticipated . The two observed galaxies lacking dark matter, DF2 and DF4 , are at close projected separations (80 kpc and 165 kpc, which set a lower limit to the 3D separations) from NGC 1052 (stellar mass M_ =1.05 10^ 11 M_ ). With this procedure, we also obtain S ersic indices ( n_ S e rsic ), which range from 0.60-0.77 , in excellent agreement with observations ( n_ S e rsic =0.60 and 0.79 for DF2 and DF4, respectively).

Our results support a scenario where DF2 and DF4 became dark-matter deficient due to past close encounters with NGC 1052, the only massive galaxy in their neighbourhood (note that our simulation allows hosts to have multiple dark-matter deficient satellites). Their projected (80 kpc and 165 kpc) and line-of-sight separations ( 2.1 0.5 Mpc) - as well as our estimates of the virial diameter of the NGC 1052 halo -- suggest that DF2 and DF4 could be satellites of the NGC 1052 group. Future investment in characterizing accurate 3D separations between DF2 and DF4 relative to NGC 1052 will provide an important test of this interaction-based scenario.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 1052-DF2 as a dark-matter-deficient diffuse galaxy whose globular clusters require extended birth radii, making the collision scenario vulnerable to tidal stripping by NGC 1052.

quotes.quote: Since the high pressures in the compressed gas are conducive to the formation of massive globular clusters (GCs), this scenario can explain the formation of DMDGs with large populations of massive GCs, consistent with the observations of NGC 1052-DF2 (DF2) and NGC 1052-DF4. A potential difficulty with this `mini bullet cluster' scenario is that the observed spatial distributions of GCs in DMDGs are extended. GCs experience dynamical friction causing their orbits to decay with time. Consequently, their distribution at formation should have been even more extended than that observed at present.

Using a semi-analytic model, we show that the observed positions and velocities of the GCs in DF2 imply that they must have formed at a radial distance of 5-10 kpc from the center of DF2. However, as we demonstrate, the scenario is difficult to reconcile with the fact that the strong tidal forces from NGC 1052 strip the extendedly distributed GCs from DF2, requiring 33-59 massive GCs to form at the collision to explain observations.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 1052-DF2 as a dark-matter-deficient satellite of NGC 1052, with low velocity dispersion and dark-to-stellar mass ratio motivating tidal-interaction simulations.

quotes.quote: Such a prediction has been challenged by the discovery of two galaxies (NGC 1052-DF2 and NGC 1052-DF4: DF2 and DF4 hereafter) that seem to lack dark matter VanDokkum2018, VanDokkum2019 in their central regions. These galaxies have a stellar mass of 10^8 and are satellites of NGC 1052 but see for a different interpretation for DF4 Montes2020 , a massive ( M_* 10^ 11 ) elliptical galaxy Muller2019 . Given their stellar mass, these objects are predicted to form in a dark matter halo of a mass 5 10^ 10 Moster2013 ; this mass translates into an expected (stellar) velocity dispersion of about 25 . Quite surprisingly, using the line-of-sight velocity of 9 globular clusters up to a distance of 10 kpc, van Dokkum and collaborators have estimated a maximum velocity dispersion below 10 . When translated back into a mass, such a low velocity dispersion suggests a dark matter to stellar mass ratio of two to three, quite far from values of 30-40 at that radius expected from galaxy formation theory for a halo velocity dispersion of 25 e.g. Behroozi2013 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC1052-DF2 as a dark-matter-deficient UDG whose stellar mass, globular clusters, dynamical constraints, and possible feedback-driven core formation challenge standard galaxy formation models.

quotes.quote: vanDokkum18b and vanDokkum19 found two UDGs in a nearby galaxy group, NGC1052-DF2 and DF4, hosting populations of 10 unusually massive GCs ( M_ GC 9 10^5 ). Both galaxies have stellar masses 10^8 and seem to contain very little DM, with stars providing nearly the entire dynamical mass in the central 8 vanDokkum18a,vanDokkum19 . For an NFW DM halo density profile Navarro97 , this would imply a baryon fraction / 50 per cent, in contradiction with the 3 per cent expected in galaxies of the same mass in Papastergis12 .

The case of NGC1052-DF2 and DF4 The predictions for a galaxy with the stellar mass of DF2/DF4, 2 10^8 , are presented in Figure  fig:DF2 . The top row shows how the formation of the core impacts the DM density profiles of galaxies when measured at a galactocentric radius =8 , which approximately corresponds to the outermost dynamical constraints for DF2 and DF4.

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 1052-DF2 alongside DF4 as an unusual diffuse dwarf in the NGC 1052 field, with large size and luminous globular clusters.

quotes.quote: Follow-up HST /ACS observations ( 2018ApJ...868...96C ) revealed that two of these galaxies, NGC 1052-DF2 and NGC 1052-DF4, in the field of the elliptical galaxy NGC 1052, have unusual properties with respect to the rest of the sample. At the distance of NGC 1052 ( D_ 1052 =19.4-21.4 Mpc; 2001ApJ...546..681T ; 2002MNRAS.330..443B ), NGC 1052-DF2 and NGC 1052-DF4 have the stellar masses of dwarf galaxies ( 2 10^8 \ M _ and 1.5 10^8 \ M _ , respectively) but large sizes ( R_e=2.2 kpc and 1.6 kpc).
