gmw_00002669.name: NGC 4262
gmw_00002669.overview: NGC 4262 is a barred lenticular galaxy in the Virgo cluster [arXiv:2404.10059, arXiv:0908.3564] that hosts a supermassive black hole [arXiv:2311.13747]. It features a large, stable polar ring of neutral hydrogen and young stars [arXiv:1401.2562, arXiv:1107.1966], which likely originated from external gas accretion [arXiv:0908.3564, arXiv:1201.2762].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4262 as a gas-rich Virgo S0 and polar-ring candidate, measures its 38 kpc globular-cluster system, and finds GCS properties resembling peculiar merger/accretion systems.

quotes.quote: NGC 4262 is a lenticular type (S0) galaxy exhibiting peculiar features located in the Virgo cluster region with a redshift of 0.0045 whitmore1990 . The exploration of the gas-rich lenticular galaxy NGC 4262 in the Virgo cluster region began with 1983Giovanardi . Subsequent studies by 1985krum and 1987vanDriel delved further into the galaxy's properties and detected a noticeable amount of atomic hydrogen surrounding NGC 4262. From these observations, whitmore1990 proposed that NGC 4262 could potentially be classified as a kinematically possible Polar Ring Galaxy (PRG).
From the surface density profile, we have estimated that the GCS extent of galaxy NGC 4262 is 38 5 kpc. Here, the selected 112 GCs are defined as the final sample GCs of NGC 4262 from CFHT.

Based on the GCS properties of various morphological types of galaxies, we observed NGC 4262 possesses a similar orientation to the GCS properties of peculiars. Also, NGC 4262 and peculiar galaxies are located in a region between spiral and elliptical galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC4262 as a barred Virgo S0 with no inner star formation but a large UV star-forming H I ring, probably externally acquired and reminiscent of Hoag-type systems.

quotes.quote: NGC4262 is a barred S0 galaxy belonging to the Virgo main cloud A. No star formation is observed within the optical radius. However, UV images reveal the presence of a UV star-forming ring extended up to 2.5 optical radii. Although faint UV emission is marginally visible all across the ring, great part of the star formation is taking place in the south-west. This roughly coincides with the peak in distribution as revealed in the maps presented by and .

Moreover, all the is segregated into a ring and no low surface density disk is detected. and suggested that the ring has probably an external origin and more recently tentatively proposed that the could have been accreted during an interaction between NGC4262 and NGC4254.

sources.summary: NGC 4262 is the lone dense-environment H I detection, with cold gas in a large rotating barred-galaxy ring likely produced by bar resonance.

quotes.quote: NGC 4262 This strongly barred galaxy is the only object in a dense environment in which we detect in . The cold gas is distributed in a large ring. This ring shows regular rotation, albeit with signs of non-circular orbits.
The observed structure is most likely a resonance ring due to the bar. The ionised gas rotates in the same sense as the whereas the stellar rotation is decoupled from that. No CO was detected by .

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 4262 as a classic polar ring galaxy, using stellar and HI kinematics to infer a radius-dependent dark matter halo and a stable polar ring.

quotes.quote: With the aim to determine the spatial distribution of dark matter, we investigate the polar ring galaxy NGC 4262. We used the stellar kinematics data for the central galaxy obtained from optical spectroscopy together with information about the kinematics of the neutral hydrogen for polar component. It was shown that NGC 4262 is the classic polar ring galaxy case with the relative angle of 88^ between components. From simulations of the central galaxy and ring kinematics we found that the shape of the dark matter distribution varies strongly with the radius. Namely, the dark matter halo is flattened towards the galactic disk plane /a=0.4 , however it is prolate to the orthogonal (polar) plane far beyond the central galaxy /a=1.7 .
Also, the simulations of the ring evolution let us to confirm the stability of the ring and the formation of quasi-spiral structures within it.

sources.summary: The paper measures NGC 4262 with Gemini/NIFS, treats its bar by restricting the MGE model, finds a 3-sigma SMBH detection, and notes unusually tight inclination constraints.

quotes.quote: One galaxy, NGC 4262, has a prominent bar. This results in one of its MGE components having a very low axial ratio ( q=0.55 ), all other components being close to circular ( q>0.94 ). To avoid an unphysical limit on the inclination of this face-on galaxy, we refit requiring q>0.94 for all MGE components.

Despite the degeneracy between parameters, the models for the galaxies NGC 4262, 4379, 4458, and 4550 exclude the no black hole case at 3 , thus these galaxies have SMBH detections with 99.7 certainty.

The inclination of NGC 4262 is more tightly constrained than for the other galaxies (see Table tab:ownbh and Fig fig_JAM4262 ). This is because the corresponding physical JAM parameter q , the deprojected axial ratio, see equation 14 of JAMs varies very rapidly with inclination when inclination is low, such as for face-on galaxies like NGC 4262.

sources.summary: NGC 4262 is proposed as the S0 perturber of NGC 4254; its offset, velocity, mass-scale H i ring, and peculiar ring geometry are discussed.

quotes.quote: A possible candidate is the SB0 galaxy NGC 4262, which is located 51 kpc east and 137 kpc north of NGC 4254. Its radial velocity is 1050 km s ^ 1 with respect to the systemic velocity of NGC 4254. NGC 4262 has a ring of H i around it with twice the diameter D_ 25 of the optical disk and an inclination at least 50 ^ with respect to the plane of the optical disk (Krumm et al. 1985). With an H i rotation velocity of 220 km s ^ 1 at 2 ' radius its total mass is near the 10^ 11 M _ used in our model. Since NGC 4262 is an S0 galaxy and therefore much more concentrated than a spiral galaxy, we do not expect to observe an impact of the interaction on its stellar content (see also NGC 4435 in the NGC 4438/NGC4435 system, Combes et al. 1988).
For NGC 4262 we can only speculate that the interaction with NGC 4254 is at least partly responsible for the peculiar ring structure.

sources.summary: The catalogue includes SPRC-33 because its gas ring shows H I and young stars; prior work found inclined ionized and neutral gas rotation consistent with a polar ring.

quotes.quote: The polar ring of SPRC-33 is not noticeable in the SDSS images, however, we included the galaxy in the list, since its outer ring reveals both HI and a young stellar population (see Section sect_known ).

SPRC-33 (NGC 4262) . In a recent paper by Bettoni2010 it was shown that both the ionized gas in the inner region and the neutral hydrogen at large distances from the centre, rotate in the plane, that is strongly inclined to the stellar disc of this galaxy with a central bar. In addition, the UV images demonstrate the presence of a young stellar population in the ring of neutral hydrogen, having the outer diameter of about 18-20 kpc. Bettoni2010 chose to speak of an inclined ring, but a formal calculation of the mutual inclination angle see formula (1) in Moiseev2008 yields two solutions: i=39 and 90 . The latter corresponds to the polar ring, as noted in their subsequent work Buson2011 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4262 as a spiral near VIRGOHI 21 but rules it out as part of the interaction because no bridge connects it and the velocity difference is too large.

quotes.quote: A faint bridge can be seen stretching down to the prominent spiral NGC 4254 (2400 km s ^ 1 ) while the other two sources, NGC 4262 (1500 km s ^ 1 , upper left) and the faint galaxy `C' (1750 km s ^ 1 , immediately to the left of VIRGOHI 21) appear unconnected.

Far more detail can be seen on an animation which is available at http://www.astro.cf.ac.uk/groups/galaxies, for instance the bridge is clear and obvious, as is the lack of any connection between VIRGOHI 21 and either NGC 4262 or C. The apparent alignment of NGC 4262 with VIRGOHI 21 and NGC 4254 in Fig. 1b is a consequence of the particular projection shown

() NGC 4262, the spiral to the North-East, is not involved. There is no bridge to it (see animation) and the radial velocity difference between NGC 4254 and NGC 4262 is too large (900 km s ^ 1 ) to generate one.

sources.summary: NGC4262 is treated as an HRS outlier with unusually low dust-to-H I ratio; the paper reports reliable excess atomic gas, peculiar H I morphology, and possible recent cold-gas accretion.

quotes.quote: In addition to deficient galaxies showing a higher M_ dust /M_ HI ratio than normal, it is interesting to note the presence of a few outliers having M_ dust /M_ HI significantly lower ( 10^ 3 ) than the average value for normal systems ( 10^ 2.1 ), namely: NGC3657, NGC4203, NGC4262, NGC4636 and UGC8045.

Conversely, we can exclude a measurement problem for NGC3657, NGC4203 and NGC4262 and conclude that these systems have significantly higher amounts of atomic hydrogen compared to their dust content. This is confirmed when we look in detail at the properties of these systems. NGC4203 and NGC4262 have peculiar \ morphologies krumm85,vandriel88 and several independent analyses have proposed that these galaxies might have recently accreted cold gas (see cortese09 and references therein).

Thus, we speculate that the unusually low M_ dust /M_ HI ratio observed in these systems may be due to the presence of a considerable amount of pristine gas not yet enriched by star formation, either because it has been recently accreted or because it has too low column density to form stars at high rate.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4262 as an example HI-rich early-type galaxy whose HI disc extends beyond the optical body and whose UV ring affects its color classification.

quotes.quote: As an example, Figure fig:rp_log shows the resulting , UV and optical radial profiles for NGC 4262. In this figure, surface brightness values are converted to AB-mag arcsec ^ 2 and given on the left axis. The \ surface density is given on the right axis. We provide a figure like Fig. fig:rp_log for all rich galaxies in appendix App:AppendixA . As done in previous studies e.g., 2007ApJS..173..538T,2011ApJ...733...74L,2012ApJ...745...34M , we use the radius of the 27.35 mag arcsec ^ 2 NUV isophote as the star forming disc radius (R _ SFTH ). Having calculated the R _ SFTH values for all the rich galaxies, we find that all the \ profiles extend well beyond this radius (e.g., see the black-vertical dashed line in Fig. fig:rp_log ). For NGC 4262, it is clear that the \ disc not only extends much further out than the optical body but also that the highest \ column density is found beyond this threshold.

While the gas consumption time in the inner regions is relatively short, it increases dramatically in the second aperture (e.g., 6 Gyr and 79 Gyr for NGC 4262). On the one hand, if we integrate the total SF over 1 Gyr, it is not enough to create a disc-dominated system. On the other hand, outer regions of these galaxies can stay in the green valley region for a long time, while the inner regions stay in the red-sequence. However, 2009MNRAS.400.1225C note that if the flux is integrated up to the UV ring ( 13 R _ eff ), NGC 4262 moves to the transition region (i.e., green valley) in NUV-K colour.

gmw_00002670.name: COLA1
gmw_00002670.overview: COLA1 is a luminous, compact double-peaked Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy at a redshift of approximately 6.59 [arXiv:1805.11621, arXiv:2404.10040]. It exhibits intense, dust-free star formation [arXiv:2404.10040] and a high ionizing photon escape fraction [arXiv:1911.04774, arXiv:2404.10040], allowing it to actively contribute to the reionization of its surrounding intergalactic medium [arXiv:1805.11621, arXiv:1911.04774].

sources.summary: The paper confirms COLA1 as a real z=6.593 double-peaked LAE, measuring luminous compact Ly emission, weak inferred nebular lines, low H i column density, and likely ionising photon escape.

quotes.quote: We present deep high-resolution ( R=5000 ) VLT/X-SHOOTER observations, along with an extensive collection of photometric data of `COLA1', a proposed double peaked LAE at z=6.6 Hu2016 . We rule out that COLA1's emission line is an [ O ii ] doublet at z=1.475 on the basis of i) the asymmetric red line-profile and flux ratio of the peaks (blue/red= 0.31 0.03 ) and ii) an unphysical [ O ii ] /H ratio ( [ O ii ] /H > 22 ). We show that COLA1's observed B band flux is explained by a faint extended foreground LAE, for which we detect Ly and [ O iii ] at z=2.142 . We thus conclude that COLA1 is a real double-peaked LAE at z=6.593 , the first discovered at z>6 , confirming the result from .

COLA1 is UV luminous (M _ 1500 =-21.6 0.3 ), has a high equivalent width (EW _ 0, Ly =120^ +50 _ 40 ) and very compact Ly emission ( r_ 50, Ly = 0.33^ +0.07 _ 0.04 kpc). Relatively weak inferred H + [ O iii ] line-emission from Spitzer /IRAC indicates an extremely low metallicity of Z<1/20 Z_ or reduced strength of nebular lines due to high escape of ionising photons. The small Ly peak separation of 220 20 km s ^ 1 implies a low H i column density and an ionising photon escape fraction of 15-30 , providing the first direct evidence that such galaxies contribute actively to the reionisation of the Universe at z>6 . Based on simple estimates, we find that COLA1 could have provided just enough photons to reionise its own 0.3 pMpc (2.3 cMpc) bubble, allowing the blue Ly line to be observed.

sources.summary: The paper identifies ID 593625 as a foreground z=2.142 LAE near COLA1, with Ly and [O iii] lines, faint F814W emission, and contamination of COLA1 photometry.

quotes.quote: This object has ID 593625 in the catalogue (with photometric redshift p_z=1.9^ +0.2 _ 0.1 ) and we will refer to it with that ID from now on. COLA1 is detected at 3 , with F814W = 26.6^ +0.4 _ 0.3 measured in a 0.6 '' diameter aperture and corrected for aperture losses using tabulated encircled fluxes from . ID 593625 has a F814W magnitude of F814W = 26.5^ +0.3 _ 0.3 .

This corresponds roughly to the foreground source ID 593625 as can be seen in the B band thumbnail image (Fig. fig:thumbnails ), which shows that outskirts of this galaxy fall in the X-SHOOTER slit. We identify the two lines as Ly and [ O iii ] _ 5007 at z=2.142 , as illustrated in Fig. fig:foreground_spectrum , where we have optimised the centroid of spatial extraction aperture for these lines. Ly is redshifted by 130 km s ^ 1 with respect to the systemic redshift, similar to other LAEs at z 2 Trainor2015,Sobral2018b . Without correcting for slit losses, we measure a Ly luminosity of 1.0 0.1 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 , well below the typical Ly luminosity at z 2.2 ( 0.4 L^ ; Sobral2016 ).

sources.summary: The paper confirms COLA1 as a z=6.5917 double-peaked Lyman-alpha emitter in an ionized bubble, with high LyC escape, blue UV slope, and compact intense star formation.

quotes.quote: One luminous galaxy, COLA1, stands out because of its unique double-peaked Ly line at z=6.6 , unseen in any simulation of reionization. Here we present JWST/NIRCam wide-field slitless spectroscopy in a 21 arcmin ^ 2 field centered on COLA1. We find 141 galaxies spectroscopically-selected through the \ doublet at 5.35<z<6.95 , with 40 of these sources showing H . For COLA1 we additionally detect _ 4363 as well as H . We measure a systemic redshift of z=6.5917 for COLA1, confirming the classical double-peak nature of the Ly profile.

COLA1 shows all the signs of a prolific ionizer with a Ly escape fraction of 81 5 , Balmer decrement indicating no dust, a steep UV slope ( _ UV =-3.2 0.4 ), and a star-formation surface density 10 that of typical galaxies at similar redshift.

COLA1 also presents a compact morphology ( R_ UV <0.26 kpc) and eminently higher star-formation rate surface density than the average at similar redshifts, in line with known LyC leakers in the literature e.g., Vanzella16, Vanzella18 (Sects. sec:escape_frac and sec:star_formation , Fig. fig:Sigma_SFR , Table tab:fesc_LyC ).

sources.summary: The paper confirms COLA1 as a z=6.59 double-peaked Lyman-alpha emitter, links its narrow Ly peak separation to high escape fraction, and argues it can ionize its own reionization bubble.

quotes.quote: Therefore, COLA1 is confirmed as a double peaked LAE at z=6.59 . How is it possible that double peaked Ly emission is seen in COLA1? The answer likely lies in the narrowness of the Ly peak separation ( 220 km s ^ 1 ).
Following the trend from ), the peak separation in COLA1 corresponds to a high f _ esc,LyC 30 .

Assuming an ionising efficiency _ ion = 10^ 25.4 Hz erg ^ 1 , an escape fraction of 15 (conservative, compared to inferred 30 ) and a SFR of 30 M _ yr ^ 1 (based on the UV luminosity), COLA1 can plausibly ionise the required region on its own in 10 ^7 yr. COLA1 is the first galaxy that can be used as both a tracer and an agent of reionisation.

sources.summary: COLA1 is used as the proof-of-concept blue-peaked z=6.6 galaxy; the paper infers a >0.7 pMpc highly ionized region, low residual neutral fraction, high escape fraction, steep UV slope, and underdense sightline.

quotes.quote: In a proof-of-concept, we demonstrate the z 6.6 galaxy COLA1 -- with a blue Ly peak -- likely resides in an ionised region >0.7 pMpc, with residual neutral fraction <10^ 5.5 . To ionise its own proximity zone we infer COLA1 has a high ionising photon escape fraction ( f_ esc >0.50 ), relatively steep UV slope ( < -1.79 ), and low line-of-sight gas density ( 0.5 the cosmic mean), suggesting it is a rare, underdense line-of-sight.

COLA1 is a z 6.6 galaxy with a blue peak with flux up to 250 from systemic Hu2016,Matthee2018b . From Figure fig:lya_transmission_minDV we see that this requires it to reside in an ionised region at least 0.7 pMpc to the nearest neutral patch, with a residual neutral fraction < 10^ 6 . Our estimate of the extent of the ionised region is roughly double than that of Matthee2018b , who estimated 0.3 pMpc (2.3 cMpc). This is due to their assumption that the entire bubble is optically thin. As discussed in Section sec:results_opticallythin , with just the minimum observed blue flux velocity we can only calculate R_ (Equation eqn:opticallythin_DV ), but the total ionised region is much larger.

sources.summary: The paper models COLA-1's double-peaked Ly-alpha profile as potentially powered by an obscured AGN, deriving its near-zone size, ionizing emissivity, UV magnitude, and X-ray luminosity.

quotes.quote: One of the most studied double-peaked LAEs at z 6.5 is the galaxy labeled COLA-1, located in the COSMOS field hu2016, matthee2018 at z 6.593 .

In the case of COLA-1, the observed v_ = -250 km/s leads to R_ , COLA-1 = 0.31 pMpc. Equating the right-hand side of rmax to R_ , COLA-1 , we can estimate the quasar ionizing emissivity that is required for the observation of the double peak. Using _ = -1.70 in rmax , we find that the required quasar emissivity corresponding to R_ , max, COLA-1 = 0.31 pMpc is N_ ion , COLA-1 = 2.60 10^ 55 \ s ^ 1 This ionizing emissivity can be used to normalize the spectral shape, by using the relation: N_ ion = _ _ H ^ L_ h d , with L_ given by In practice, the contribution from the X-ray section of lnudoublepower is several orders of magnitude lower than that of the 600 - 912 \ portion. Using nionlnu with the value of COLA-1's ionizing emissivity to normalize lnudoublepower , we can plot the flux spectrum of the AGN in the UV to IR regime, shown as the blue line in Fig. fig:cola1 . We find that this spectrum corresponds to a flux density at 4500 \ of log L_ , 4500 = 45.08 ergs/s/Hz, or a UV magnitude of M_ 1500 = -23.2 for the quasar.

sources.summary: The paper uses COLA1 as the comparison blue-wing LAE: it is a z=6.6 luminous source with a complex red-and-blue profile and strong blue side.

quotes.quote: What is even more intriguing is that the most luminous LAE---COLA1 at z=6.6 with L( Ly ) 44  erg s ^ 1 hu16 --is the first source near the EoR to show a complex LAE profile with a stronger red and weaker blue component (though there has been debate over whether it might be a lower redshift emission line; matthee17b ). We shall loosely refer to such sources as blue-wing LAEs; however, without an accurate measure of the systemic velocities, we do not know if the blue component is at a negative velocity with respect to the galaxy.
For the complex profile LAEs (i.e., NEPLA4 and COLA1), we measure the peak-to-peak velocity separations between the main and blue wing components to be 300 and 270  km \ s ^ 1 , respectively.

Both NEPLA4 and COLA1 have Ly profiles with strong blue sides: the ratios of the peak of the blue wing component to the peak of the red tail component are 0.6 and 0.5, respectively. These are stronger than those generally seen in low-redshift samples.

sources.summary: The paper reports COLA1 as the most luminous known high-redshift LAE at z=6.593, with a blue-wing Ly profile suggesting a highly ionized region and possible infall.

quotes.quote: We report the detection of the most luminous high-redshift Lyman Alpha Emitting galaxy (LAE) yet seen, with L( Ly ) = 43.9 ergs \ s^ 1 . The galaxy -- COSMOS Ly 1, or COLA1 -- was detected in a search for ultraluminous LAEs with Hyper Suprime-Cam on the Subaru telescope. It was confirmed to lie at z=6.593 based on a Ly line detection obtained from followup spectroscopy with the DEIMOS spectrograph on Keck2. COLA1 is the first very high-redshift LAE to show a multi-component Ly \ line profile with a blue wing, which suggests that it could lie in a highly ionized region of the intergalactic medium and could have significant infall.

sources.summary: The paper compares COLA1 with simulated blue Ly-alpha transmission, describing it as a luminous, compact z=6.59 emitter in COSMOS with bright line and UV-continuum emission.

quotes.quote: `COLA1' is a luminous emitter at z=6.59 located in the well-studied COSMOS field. Besides being exceptionally bright in (L _ Ly = 4 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 ) it appears reasonably bright in the UV continuum with M _ UV =-21.6 0.3 . Contrarily to other galaxies with similar UV luminosity, COLA1 appears particularly compact Matthee2018 .

Unlike COLA1 and NEPLA4, the velocity separation between the red and blue peak of the line is large in Aerith B ( v = (580 80) ), indicating the galaxy is unlikely a significant Lyman continuum leaker 2016ApJ...828...71D,2016MNRAS.461.3683I .

sources.summary: The paper describes COLA1 as a z=6.6 high-redshift Lyman-alpha emitter with a double-peaked profile and blue bump used to infer ionized-bubble properties.

quotes.quote: These include Aerith B at z=5.8 Bosman.2020 , NEPLA4 at z=6.5 Songaila.2018 and COLA1 at z=6.6 Hu.2016 , which all show impressive \ double-peaks, strongly resemblant of the z 0 compact starbursts Henry.2015,Jaskot.2017,Izotov.2020 . According to simplified, homogeneous prescriptions for the neutral fraction of the universe at these redshifts, these profiles should not exist, and even in modern radiation hydrodynamic simulations of the EoR, blue peaks at z 6 are rare Gronke.2020 . Clearly the reionization progression is patchy, and Aerith B is a perfect example, being discovered inside the proximity zone of a luminous quasar. Bagley.2017 recently calculated the bubble sizes necessary to explain the existence of two very luminous emitter at z 6.4 , finding they must exist on Mpc scales. Matthee.2018 performed a similar study using the spectral profile of COLA1 including its blue \ bump, and found comparable if somewhat smaller values; their calculation was revised upwards very recently by MasonGronke.2020 whose Bayesian inference places additional new constraints on the ionizing output and neutral fraction within the \ bubble, as well as the bubble radius itself.

gmw_00002671.name: MASOSA
gmw_00002671.overview: MASOSA is a luminous, compact Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 6.5 to 6.6 [arXiv:1903.08171, arXiv:1606.03526]. It exhibits a very high Lyman-alpha equivalent width [arXiv:1903.08171] but remains undetected in both dust continuum and [C II] emission, suggesting a low metallicity and minimal obscured star formation [arXiv:1903.08171].

sources.summary: The paper presents MASOSA as a luminous z=6.5 Ly-alpha emitter with high equivalent width, no detected dust continuum or [C II], and very low inferred metallicity.

quotes.quote: We present new deep ALMA and HST /WFC3 observations of MASOSA and VR7, two luminous Ly emitters (LAEs) at z=6.5 , for which the UV continuum level differ by a factor four. No IR dust continuum emission is detected in either, indicating little amounts of obscured star formation and/or high dust temperatures. MASOSA, with a UV luminosity M _ 1500 =-20.9 , compact size and very high Ly EW _ 0 145 , is undetected in [ C ii ] to a limit of L _ CII <2.2 10^7 L _ implying a metallicity Z 0.07 Z_ . Intriguingly, our HST data indicates a red UV slope =-1.1 0.7 , at odds with the low dust content.

MASOSA is not detected in [ C ii ] or FIR continuum emission. This may be explained by a higher dust temperature combined with a low dust mass and a relatively low metallicity. Such properties are indeed expected given MASOSA's high-redshift and very high Ly EW (typically found in young, metal poor and dust-free galaxies; e.g. Sobral2015,Nakajima2016,Trainor2016,Sobral2018 ). As illustrated in Fig. fig:SFRUV_LCII , the [ C ii ] luminosity limits implies that MASOSA is significantly [ C ii ] deficient given its SFR, compared to the typical [ C ii ] luminosity found for star-forming galaxies in the local Universe e.g. DeLooze2014 .

sources.summary: The paper identifies MASOSA as a compact bright z=6.6 LAE candidate/confirmation, undetected in near-infrared stacks and morphologically different from CR7 and Himiko.

quotes.quote: MASOSA (with a Ly luminosity of at least 3 10^ 43 erg s ^ 1 , both in a 2" aperture and MAG-AUTO ) is not detected in any of the near-infrared bands, and also not in the stacked image (meaning YJHK > 26.7 ). MASOSA is undetected in the z band, meaning that the luminosity is a lower limit.

It is only brighter than Himiko when measured in 2" apertures, but this could also be due to our fainter narrow-band imaging in COSMOS than in UDS. It is not extended (diameter 0.9" ), while CR7 and Himiko show an extent of 3" in diameter. This means that the sources are of a different nature and therefore interesting targets for follow-up study with e.g. HST .

sources.summary: The paper treats MASOSA as one of three z=6.6 LAE candidates in the COSMOS field, noting it was previously confirmed, fainter than COLA1, and compact.

quotes.quote: Two of these, MASOSA, and CR7, were previously known and spectroscopically confirmed by sobral15 and matthee15 .
For detailed comparison with COLA1, we also observed CR7 in the same configuration but we did not re-observe the fainter MASOSA.
Both MASOSA and COLA1 are quite spatially compact.

gmw_00002672.name: QSO B0241+62
gmw_00002672.overview: QSO B0241+62 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar [arXiv:2005.08569] that is classified as a radio-loud active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0809.0255]. The source is compact at high radio frequencies [arXiv:2005.08569], exhibits possible variability [arXiv:1901.04188], and contains a jet aligned close to the line of sight [arXiv:2005.08569].

sources.summary: The paper classifies QSO B0241+62 as a radio-loud AGN candidate, describes compact radio structure, uses Chandra data, and reports weak reflection evidence.

quotes.quote: QSO B0241+62 and IGR J13109-5552 have been poorly studied at radio frequencies and their classification is uncertain; both are Seyfert 1 according to NED and b59 . QSO B0241+62, shows a compact unresolved core in the NVSS map, although high resolution radio imaging indicates a possible double-lobe morphology within this core structure b27 . QSO B0241+62 is reported as a relatively strong source in various radio surveys, has a flat radio spectrum with a 0.1 S_ ^ b62 and so almost certainly qualifies as a radio loud AGN.

B3 0309+411 and 4C 74.26 require reflection at more than 99 confidence level, while 3C 390.3 and QSO B0241+62 at a lower confidence level (98 and 92 respectively). For QSO B0241+62, B3 0309+411 and IGR J13109-5552 no previous work is available and thus the analysis presented here is the first broad-band study for those 3 AGN.

sources.summary: The paper finds QSO B0241+62 among sources with high cross-calibration constants, attributes this partly to non-perfect simultaneity or variability, and notes NuSTAR did not improve its cut-off constraint.

quotes.quote: Although most points cluster below 1.2 for both detectors, there are a few objects, i.e. QSO B0241+62, Mrk 6, IGR J12415-5750 and IGR J16482-3036, that display higher constant values albeit with larger errors. In the cases of QSO B0241+62 and Mrk 6 the non perfect simultaneity of the data (see Table 1) and possibly some variability can explain values of the cross-calibration constants around 1.4-1.5.
It is worth noting that NuSTAR data employed in this work allow to put better constraints than those reported in Ricci_2017 on the high energy cut-off in almost all cases, except for QSO B0241+62.

sources.summary: The paper finds QSOB0241+62 remains compact at 22 and 45 GHz, with flat high flux densities interpreted as a line-of-sight jet in a flat-spectrum radio quasar.

quotes.quote: QSOB0241+62 It has been the subject of many VLA and VLBI studies, which revealed a flat-spectrum core plus a jet-like feature in the SE direction lobe morphology Preston1985,Lister1994,Lister2018 , and the morphology is also reported at 22 and 43 GHz Charlot2010 . In our VLA maps at 22 and 45 GHz we do not observe hint of jet-like structure at either frequencies, although the source is slightly resolved (but still compact) in both cases. The high measured flux densities are in the range 700 - 900 mJy, with a spectral index which is compatible with flat, which can be interpreted in the light of a jet closely aligned with the line-of-sight, see the MOJAVE project page Lister2019 , in agreement with the Flat-Spectrum Radio Quasar (FSRQ) classification Hervet2016 .

gmw_00002673.name: IGR J16482-3036
gmw_00002673.overview: IGR J16482-3036 is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1901.04188]. It exhibits strong variability in the 2-10 keV X-ray band [arXiv:1901.04188].

sources.summary: The paper flags IGR J16482-3036 as having high cross-calibration constants, strong 2-10 keV variability, and only loose NuSTAR constraints on the high-energy cut-off.

quotes.quote: Although most points cluster below 1.2 for both detectors, there are a few objects, i.e. QSO B0241+62, Mrk 6, IGR J12415-5750 and IGR J16482-3036, that display higher constant values albeit with larger errors.

As far as IGR J12415-5750 and IGR J16482-3036 are concerned, from the inspection of the light curves, both sources are found to be extremely variable in the 2-10 keV band, therefore producing also in these cases constants greater than 1.4 in the broad-band fits.

Finally we note that in some cases NuSTAR data allow to put more stringent constraints on the cut-off energy, while in other cases, such as those of Fairall 1146 and IGR J16482-3036, only loose constraints could be placed on the parameter, referring to Malizia:2014 for a more accurate estimate of the high energy cut-off.

gmw_00002674.name: B2 1520+31
gmw_00002674.overview: B2 1520+31 is a high-redshift flat-spectrum radio quasar [arXiv:1810.12607, arXiv:2006.03991] whose optical-UV emission is dominated by thermal emission [arXiv:1202.1602]. The active nucleus exhibits large gamma-ray flux variations [arXiv:1709.07904] with a lognormal distribution [arXiv:2003.01339, arXiv:2006.03991] and multiple candidate quasi-periodic oscillations [arXiv:2204.13051].

sources.summary: The paper includes B2 1520+31 in a four-AGN Fermi LAT variability analysis with near-continuous detections, large gamma-ray flux variations, linear rms-flux behavior, and shot-noise-like PSDs.

quotes.quote: However, for detection and scientific analysis, only data points which satisfy the significance of 3 , corresponding to a TS value of 9 are considered. The 0.1-300 GeV ray light curves of the four AGNs: the FRI radio galaxy NGC 1275, BLL Mrk 421, FSRQ B2 1520+31, and FSRQ PKS 1510-089 between August 05, 2008 (MJD 54683) to October 05, 2015 (MJD 57300) over a uniform integration time of 3-days with detection significance above/equal 3 are shown in Figure fig:latLc . All the sources are detected over 97 e.g.

A different variability of PKS 1510-089 is due the fact that its flux distribution is bi-lognormal with equal variability in both 2016ApJ...822L..13K and consistent with values inferred for others here. Though the histograms are skewed/lognormal, the rms-flux relation is linear for all the sources with large scatters and a flux offset different from zero at the zero rms.

It should be noted that a Doppler boost ( ) of a few to ten can make the NGC 1275 flux ( ^3/distance^2 ) similar to the BL Lacs and FSRQs studied in this work, though the energetics and dynamics details may be different for each source. In this work, we explored the ray variability characteristics of four LAT AGNs: FR I NGC 1275, BLL Mrk 421, FSRQs B2 1520+31 and PKS 1510-089 with all having a near continuous detection in Fermi LAT over an integration time of 3-days.

sources.summary: The paper identifies three month-long QPO candidates in the distant FSRQ B2 1520+31, noting high-significance periods near 174, 71, and 39 days.

quotes.quote: B2 1520+31 Three possible month-long QPOs are identified by the CWT of the light curves of this distant (z=1.489) FSRQ, as shown in Fig. fig:CWT1520 (A), (C) and (E). The CWT maps of this source are a good example of the inherent difficulty of this analysis technique when dealing with blazar light curves: rapid flares result in vertical structures in the map that overlap with the horizontal bands we are interested into, and require visual inspection of all peaks that appear in the global wavelet spectrum. The first QPO candidate is found in both light curves at around 174 48 d ( 179 42 d) for the monthly (weekly) binned light curve with a significance above 5 in both cases. This QPO candidate is of particular interest because its period seems to increase with time.

The second and third candidate QPOs can be better identified in the CWT map of the weekly binned light curve, with periods of 71 15 d and 39 11 d, both exceeding 5 significance. A zoomed-in CWT is shown in Fig. fig:CWT1520 (E), which allows us to visualize better the signal of the two very short period QPO candidates. The 71 15 d period is compatible with the one reported by with a period of 71 d, by analysing the first 4 years of Fermi LAT data of B2 1520+31 using the LSP and weighted wavelet z-transform (WWZ) techniques.

sources.summary: The paper reports a 71-day gamma-ray QPO in the high-redshift flat-spectrum radio quasar B2 1520+31 and uses it to estimate the central black-hole mass.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage We detected a possible quasi-periodic oscillation (QPO) of 71 days in the 0.1 -- 300 GeV ray Fermi-LAT light curve of the high redshift flat spectrum radio quasar B2 1520+31. We identify and confirm that quasi-period by Lomb Scargle periodogram (LSP), and weighted wavelet z-transform (WWZ) analyses. Using this QPO period, and assuming it originates from accretion-disc fluctuations at the innermost stable circular orbit, we estimate the central supermassive black hole mass to range between 5.4 10^ 9 M _ for a non-rotating black hole and 6.0 10^ 10 M _ for a maximally rotating black hole.

Here we report the first probably QPO detection in the blazar B2 1520+31 with a period of 71 days in 0.1 -- 300 GeV ray energies. This is also the first QPO detection in the blazar B2 1520+31 in any EM band at any timescale.

sources.summary: The paper says the standard accretion-disk model does not fit B2 1520+31's UV emission, while its thermal optical-UV emission and external photon density affect the modeled blob.

quotes.quote: For the source B2 1520+31, it seems that the standard accretion disk model we used here does not adapt to describe its UV emission.

It should be noted that the thermal emission of PKS 1510-089, 3C 273 and B2 1520+31 are so significant that the optical-UV emission are purely from the thermal emission. Meanwhile, they have the smallest value of ^ _ b and ^ _ max . This indicates that the environment around the blob have a significant effect on the distribution of electrons in blob and the cooling of the electrons are dominated by EC process for these sources.

From Fig. Fig9 , B2 1520+31 has a largest value of ^ int1 due to its high energy density of external photon field, U^ max _ BLR (r_ b ^ min )=7.9 10^ 3 \ erg \ cm^ 3 , which becomes significant for photons with >10^ 25 \ Hz.

sources.summary: The paper reports the shortest flux-doubling time in B2 1520+31, compact flare regions, possible expansion, and inconclusive spectral-cutoff and cooling-timescale tests.

quotes.quote: B2 1520+31 B2 1520+31 shows a fastest flux doubling time of 0.65 0.11 hours from the July 2009 flare, the shortest variability timescale obtained from all the flares studied in this investigation and implying an extremely compact emission region of size (0.70 0.12) 10^ 13 m for this particular flare. The November 2009 flare has a variability timescale of 3.03 0.84 hours corresponding to an emission region of size (3.27 0.91) 10^ 13 m. These two timescales were observed in flux measurements 100 days apart and if the two flares had their origin in a single event, this would suggest the emission region is expanding with a velocity of (2.97 0.97) 10^ 6 ms ^ 1 0.01 . The spectra of the flares studied show no strong preference for either a power law or a log parabolic model, making the search for a spectral cut-off inconclusive. The study of energy dependence in cooling timescales was also found to be inconclusive, due to a lack of photon statistics preventing analysis using LCCFs.

sources.summary: The paper studies B2 1520+31 as one of three blazars, finding blazar-like flux-histogram behavior and PSD/rms-flux properties in gamma rays.

quotes.quote: Using a continuous gamma-ray light curves over 3-days cadence from August 2008 – October 2015, we present the first systematic study of these features in four sources: the FR I radio galaxy NGC 1275 and three blazars- Mrk 421, B2 1520+31 and PKS 1510-089. For all, except Mrk 421, the flux spans 2 orders of magnitude. For blazars, a log-normal profile describes the flux histograms better compared to a Gaussian, while none is favored for NGC 1275, the only non blazar source, suggesting a complex distribution. Regardless of flux histogram profile, the rms-flux relation is linear for all with PSDs being consistent with a power-law shot noise spectrum despite hints of breaks.

sources.summary: The paper describes B2 1520+31 as a high-redshift FSRQ detected by Fermi LAT, with lognormal light-curve behavior, accretion-powered PSD hints, and a reported 71-day QPO.

quotes.quote: B2 1520+31 B2 1520+31 ( =15^ h 22^ m 09 99, = 31 44 14 38, J2000 epoch) is a high-redshift z=1.489 ; Shaw12 FSRQ with a BH mass of 8.3 10^ 8 M_ Sbar12 , detected in HE rays by Fermi LAT in the three months sky survey Abdo09 . Kushwaha17 noticed a lognormal distribution in 7-years-long Fermi LAT LCs, while its PSD is typical for an accretion-powered compact source with a PL profile showing hints of a break. A QPO in a 4-years-long and 3-days-binned Fermi LAT LC with a period of 71 days was reported by gupta19 .

gmw_00002675.name: B2 1633+38
gmw_00002675.overview: B2 1633+38 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar and optically violent variable blazar located at a redshift of z = 1.814 [arXiv:2108.04723, arXiv:2006.03991]. It exhibits multi-wavelength variability from radio to gamma-ray bands [arXiv:1711.10120, arXiv:2006.03991], featuring a compact radio jet with a transverse Faraday rotation measure gradient [arXiv:1410.6653] and possible quasi-periodic oscillations [arXiv:2211.00588, arXiv:2001.06014].

sources.summary: The paper monitors the flat-spectrum radio quasar 1633+382 across radio through gamma rays, finding long-lived multi-band activity, correlated radio spectra, short inter-band lags, and parsec-scale emission regions.

quotes.quote: We present multi--frequency simultaneous VLBI radio observations of the flat spectrum radio quasar 1633+382 (4C 38.41) as part of the interferometric monitoring of gamma-ray bright active galactic nuclei (iMOGABA) program combined with additional observations in radio, optical, X--rays and rays carried out between the period 2012 March -- 2015 August. The monitoring of this source reveals a significant long-lived increase in its activity since approximately two years in the radio bands, which correlates with a similar increase on all other bands from sub--millimeter to rays. A significant correlation is also found between radio fluxes and simultaneous spectral indices during this period. The study of the discrete correlation function (DCF) indicates time lags smaller than the 40 days uncertainties among both radio bands and also high-energy bands, and a time lag of 70 days, with rays leading radio. We interpret that the high-energy and radio fluxes are arising from different emitting regions, located at 1 12 and 40 13 pc from the central engine respectively.

The source 1633+382 (4C 38.41) is a flat spectrum radio quasar (FSRQ) at a redshift z=1.813 Hewett10 . Very Large Array (VLA) observations have found that its kilo--parsec scale morphology shows a core--dominated triple structure in the north-south direction with an extension of about 12 arcseconds Murphy93 , whereas parsec scales show a misalignment of about 90 with a single jetted structure detected by the Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) towards the west. Superluminal motion with jet velocities up to 373 16 as yr ^ 1 ( 29.2 1.3 ) has been detected based on 10 moving features Lister13 .

sources.summary: The paper studies the blazar 4C 38.41 through multifrequency monitoring, describing its 2011 outburst, redshift, previous gamma-ray variability, and bent-jet interpretation.

quotes.quote: After years of modest optical activity, the quasar-type blazar 4C 38.41 ( B3 1633+382 ) experienced a large outburst in 2011, which was detected throughout the entire electromagnetic spectrum, renewing interest in this source. We present the results of low-energy multifrequency monitoring by the GASP project of the WEBT consortium and collaborators, as well as those of spectropolarimetric/spectrophotometric monitoring at the Steward Observatory. We also analyse high-energy observations of the Swift and Fermi satellites.

In this paper, we study the multifrequency behaviour of one blazar, namely the optically violent variable (OVV) FSRQ 4C 38.41 (1633+382) at redshift z=1.814 , which belongs to the target list of both the GASP and the Steward Observatory monitoring programmes. The period of observations presented here includes a big optical outburst in 2011 rai11_atel that was also detected at ray energies by Fermi szo11 , and in the X-ray and UV bands by the Swift satellite, providing an excellent opportunity to study the source variability over most of the electromagnetic spectrum. 4C 38.41 had already been observed in rays by the Energetic Gamma Ray Experiment Telescope (EGRET) instrument onboard CGRO several times, and from these data its emission was found to vary significantly on a day timescale mat93 .

As suggested for other blazars, the long-term variability of 4C 38.41 can be interpreted in terms of an inhomogeneous bent jet, where different emitting regions can change their alignment with respect to the line of sight, leading to variations in the Doppler factor .

sources.summary: The paper finds 1633+382 has an extreme core RM and a possible external Faraday-rotation gradient in a jet knot, but treats that gradient cautiously.

quotes.quote: 1633+382 Shows a very high (RM=22 krad/m ^2 ), featureless RM in the core, but displays an interesting gradient in the knot located at about 4 mas from the core. We discuss reliability of this gradient in section 4.3.2.
In general, we find rotation measures to be on the order of several krad/m ^2 , the core of 1633+382 being the most extreme case.

The quasar 1633+382 shows a rotation measure at about 4 mas from the core. If we take into account the errors, the RM runs from around a thousand rad/m ^2 to zero (or possibly negative values). Unlike the case of 0906+430, this RM is located in a knot of the source, where both total intensity and degree of polarization are enhanced. The range of the RM indicates that, as in 0906+430, it is dominated by external Faraday rotation.

sources.summary: The paper describes 1633+382 as compact, with ambiguous EVPA orientations but a clear transverse RM gradient through the core and inner jet.

quotes.quote: 1633+382 Figure fig:pmaps2 b shows our 7.9 GHz polarization map for 1633+382. The source structure is quite compact. The jet EVPAs are offset toward the southern edge of the jet, but their orientation relative to the jet direction is not entirely clear; the jet appears to bend toward the north near the location of this polarization, and it is possible that the detected polarization corresponds to a longitudinal magnetic field at the southern edge of the jet near this bend. The orientation of the core EVPAs relative to the jet direction is likewise not entirely clear.

This map shows a clear transverse RM gradient all along the core and inner jet region. Figure fig:1633RM also shows two slices taken roughly perpendicular to the jet direction plotted together with their errors. The transverse RM gradients are monotonic, and have significances of approximately 7 in the core region and 3 in the jet.

sources.summary: The paper reports low polarization and uncertain RM for 1633+38, contrasting its small KVN core RM with earlier very high RM measurements and inferring substantial temporal variability.

quotes.quote: 1633+38 This source is relatively faint and shows a low degree of linear polarization ( 2 ) which leads to relatively large EVPA errors. Thus, the RM at 43/86 GHz is comparable to its error and the obtained a=1.91 1.96 has also a large error. When fitting a single linear function to the EVPAs at the three frequencies available, we obtained RM = 974 509 \ rad/m^2 . This is surprisingly low because a very high RM, 2.2 10^4 \ rad/m^2 , was reported previously for the core of this source using six different frequencies from 12 GHz to optical wavelength algaba2012 .

The observations in were performed in 2008 November which indicates that there is substantial temporal variability of the EVPA rotation in 1633+38. Four years before their observations, core EVPAs could not be fitted with a single ^2 law and the obtained RM value was much smaller, RM = -570 430 \ rad/m^2 algaba2011 . This also suggests substantial temporal RM variability in 1633+38.

sources.summary: The paper analyzes B2 1633+382 as a flat-spectrum radio quasar, linking multiwavelength correlations to jet-region locations and variation mechanisms.

quotes.quote: In this work, we make a comprehensive study on the variation phenomena of the spectral index and polarization degree (PD) to deeply understand the variation mechanism of B2 1633+382 (4C 38.41). We use the local cross-correlation function (LCCF) to perform the correlation analysis between multi-wavelength light curves. We find that both ray and optical V band are correlated with the radio 15 GHz at the beyond 3 confidence level. Based on the lag analysis, the emitting regions of ray and optical locate at 14.2_ 2.4 ^ +0 pc and 14.2_ 8.3 ^ +8.3 pc upstream of the core region of radio 15 GHz, and are far away from the broad-line region (BLR).

B2 1633+382, also known as 4C 38.41, is a typical flat spectrum radio quasar (FSRQ) target Pauliny:1978,Colla:1973,Pilkington:1965 . Its redshift is 1.814 Paris:2017 . As one of the brightest ray blazars, it is an aimed target for many monitoring campaigns.

sources.summary: The paper treats B3 1633+382 as a z=1.814 quasar with a compact radio jet, using 22 GHz VLBI epochs to compare linear and helical component motions.

quotes.quote: We present a study of the motion of compact jet components in quasar B3 1633+382. Through analyzing 14 epochs of VLBI observations of three components (B1, B2, and B3) at 22 GHz, we find two different possibilities of component classification. Thus two corresponding kinematical models can be adopted to explain the evolutionary track of components.

Associated with a redshift of z = 1.814 Stri1974 , the radio source B3 1633+382 (4C 38.41) was identified as a quasar Paul73 and has been frequently observed at different wavelengths across the electromagnetic spectrum during the past decades. A nearly flat spectrum has been derived from 1.5 to 90 GHz contemporaneous measurements Land86 . Strong variability at both long and short radio wavelengths has also been detected Span81, Alle92, Seie85, Kuhr81 .

sources.summary: The paper studies 4C +38.41 as an FSRQ blazar and reports multiple activity and quiescent phases with transient gamma-ray QPO-like behavior on several timescales.

quotes.quote: The blazar 4C +38.41 ( _ 2000.0 = 16h \ 35m \ 15.4929s , _ 2000.0 = + 38 ^ \ 08 ^ ' \ 04.5 ^ '' ) https://www.lsw.uni-heidelberg.de/projects/extragalactic/charts/1633+382.html is also an FSRQ, located at redshift, z = 1.814 Hewett2010Jul,Paris2018May . Ciprini2009Jul first reported high state activity of this source with gamma-ray flux (Fermi-LAT) of (1.38 0.32) 10 ^ 6 photons cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 . It is also observed in different wavebands, from radio to hard X-rays, with the different ground and space-based telescopes, e.g., Effelsberg 100-m radio telescope, Guillermo Haro Observatory, Swift-XRT/UVOT ( Myserlis2012Oct , Raiteri2011Jul , Ghisellini2015Sep ).

For 4C+38.41, we similarly identified four activity (AP-1, AP-2, AP-3, AP-4) and two quiescent (QP-1, QP-2) phases. AP-1 phase of PKS 0244-470 shows QPO of 225 days persisting for 8 cycles ( 4.1 ). In 4C+38.41, AP-1 and AP-2 phases show QPO-like behavior of 110 days and 60 days, respectively, persisting for 5 cycles. In AP-3, we identified three sub-phases, and all show a week scale possible recurrent rise with five complete cycles, while in QP-1, we could identify 2 sub-phases (Q1, and Q2).

sources.summary: The paper describes 4C 38.41 as an EGRET-detected bright quasar with a complex wavelet plot and a 3.4-year timescale seen at both monitored frequencies.

quotes.quote: 4C 38.41 ( 1633+382 ) : This is another EGRET-detected ray bright quasar which has been a target of many different observing programs e.g. katajainen00, jorstad01, lister05, bach07 . In Mets ahovi it has been monitored for over 20 years at 22 and 37 GHz. It is also included in the wavelet study of which used the UMRAO data at 4.8, 8 and 14.5 GHz. In their analysis no timescale was found for the source. We found a timescale of 3.4 years in both frequency bands but the wavelet plot has a complex structure and the variability timescale is weaker in the late 1980s and early 1990s.

sources.summary: The paper studies B2 1633+38 as a flat-spectrum radio quasar, finding first optical quasi-periodicity evidence near 1.9 years plus weaker polarized and gamma-ray hints.

quotes.quote: We find evidences of possible quasi-periodic variability in the optical photometric data of both sources with periods of 3 years for 3C 66A and 1.9 years for B2 1633+38, with significances between 3 and 5 . Only B2 1633+38 shows evidence of this behaviour in the optical polarized data set at a confidence level of 2 4 . This is the first reported evidence of quasi-periodic behaviour in the optical light curve of B2 1633+38. Also a hint of quasi-periodic behaviour is found in the ray light curve of B2 1633+38 with a confidence level 2 , while no periodicity is observed for 3C 66A in this energy range.
The other selected source, B2 1633+38, is a low synchrotron peaked source classified as a FSRQ, located at redshift z=1.814 paris2017 .

sources.summary: The paper reports 147 GHz transatlantic VLBI detections of 1633+382, treats it as one of two analyzed quasars with zero-consistent closure phases, and derives a 17 microarcsecond lower source-size limit.

quotes.quote: We note that we have detected 3 sources (NRAO150, 3C279, 1633+382) on the transatlantic baselines from Arizona (HHT, KP) to Pico Veleta at a fringe spacing of 4.2G (corresponding to 24 as resolution). This is, as far as we know, the highest angular resolution, ever achieved in astronomical radio interferometry.

So far we analyzed only the data for the two sources, which are detected with SNR > 7 on all of the 3 baselines (3C 279, 1633+382) and for which closure phases could be measured (see Fig. 2). For both sources the closure phases are consistent with zero, indicating either a point-like, or a symmetrical source structure (within the limitations given by the uv-coverage).

With the usual assumption of the brightness temperature being limited by the inverse Compton effect ( T_B 10^ 12 K), an interesting lower limit to the source size of 17 as for 1633+382 and 27 as for 3C 279 is obtained (from _ mas 1.22 S_ Jy / _ GHz ^2 ^ 0.5 ).

sources.summary: The paper reports correlated gamma-ray and 43 GHz variability for PKS 1633+382 with no clear lag, consistent red-noise PSDs, and a gamma-ray harder-when-brighter relation.

quotes.quote: PKS 1633+382. The flux variations between ray and 43 GHz are correlated at a 2 confidence level, as illustrated by the long-term light curves and the DCF result. The derived PSD slopes in the two energy bands are consistent within the errors and correspond to the red-noise process. The DCF curve of this source displays a broad bump approximately peaking at 0, likely indicating that the flux variations between ray and 43 GHz are coincident without time-lag. _ is correlated with L_ with =0.53 and p=4.8 10^ 9 .

sources.summary: The paper summarizes B2 1633+38 as a high-redshift FSRQ, optically violent variable blazar with radio-to-gamma variability, PSD modeling, and possible optical and Fermi-LAT QPOs.

quotes.quote: B2 1633+38 B2 1633+38 ( =16^ h 35^ m 15 49, = 38 08 04 5, J2000 epoch) is an FSRQ type blazar, located at z=1.814 Stri74 and with a BH mass of 2.3 10^ 9 M_ Sbar12 , first detected in HE range by the EGRET instrument Compton observatory; Matt93 . This blazar is highly variable at radio and optical frequencies (classified as an optically violent variable blazar) as well as X-ray and HE ray energies e.g., Bart95,Rait12 . Alga18 analyzed, among others, its ray LC and fitted its PSD with a PL with =1.7 . Recently, Oter20 reported possible QPOs in optical R and V bands, with periods between 657 and 705 days, at a significance of 2-5 , polarized optical data at 654--701 days ( 2-4 ) and, finally, in a 10-years-long Fermi LAT data at 581--646 days ( 2-4 ).

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS1633+382 as one of four discrepant FSRQs, using RXTE/VLBA data and deriving an inverse Compton Doppler factor of 24.7 rather than the previous 3.52.

quotes.quote: X-ray data for 1633+382 were obtained by RXTE whereas Radio data were obtained by VLBA. Observations in the X-ray band were performed between 13 of May 2008 and 22 of July 2010, and the Radio observations between 16 of August 2009 and 19 of May 2010 Jorstad2011 .

In the case of PKS1510-089 Marscher2010 , our estimate for the Doppler factor is 14.7 whereas the previous value was 26.26, and for the fourth source, PKS1633+382 Jorstad2011 , the Doppler factor is equal to 24.7 whereas the previous value was 3.52.

gmw_00002676.name: J081740
gmw_00002676.overview: J081740 is a high-redshift star-forming galaxy [arXiv:2302.03049, arXiv:2405.00103] modeled as a rotating gas disk [arXiv:2302.03049, arXiv:2403.00904] with a low velocity dispersion [arXiv:2302.03049]. It contains a large reservoir of neutral gas [arXiv:1703.07797] and exhibits gas turbulence driven primarily by stellar feedback [arXiv:2405.00103].

sources.summary: J0817+1351 is a z=4.2584 DLA host detected in [C II] and continuum, with a velocity gradient and weak evidence for disk-like rotation.

quotes.quote: J0817+1351 ALMA J081740.85+135138.2 (J0817+1351) is the host galaxy of a high metallicity damped Lyman-alpha absorber (DLA) at z = 4.2584 . The DLA at z =4.2584 was originally detected in Ly- absorption towards the background quasar at a redshift of z = 4.398 . These observations yielded an H 1 column density of (N ( H 1 )/cm ^ 2 ) = 21.3 0.2 and a metallicity of [ M/H ] = -1.15 0.15 rafe12 . The [ C 2 ] 158 m observations come from neel17 , who observed J0817+1351 on December 30, 2015 with ALMA in cycle 3 (project 2015.1.01564.S). The total on-source time was 46 minutes with a spatial resolution of 1'' and spectral resolution after smoothing of 50 km s ^ 1 .

J0817+1351 There is slight evidence for a flat rotation curve, and the model fits the general trend of the velocity field well. While a standard rotating galaxy shows a `butterfly'-like velocity field, with isovelocity lines radiating from its center, J0817+1351 simply shows a velocity gradient. The possible ripple in this field could be caused by a warp, but we lack the resolution to confirm this. While the systemic velocity, position angle, and inclination found through RC1 are more applicable than the GAUS estimates, the position from GAUS (only 2 pixels different) returns a better fit. The slight bend of the rotation curve at large radii may be interpreted as weak evidence for detection of disk-like rotation, but is more likely due to the non-uniform appearance of the northern and southern portions of the input velocity field.

sources.summary: The paper presents J081740 as the sample's normal star-forming galaxy, previously identified through a damped Ly-alpha system and modeled here as a comparatively low-dispersion rotating disc with possible extraplanar gas.

quotes.quote: J081740 A galaxy first identified as a damped Lyman alpha system in the spectrum of a background quasar neeleman17 . Because this selection method is not biased towards objects brighter in the submillimetre, it is the only non-starburst galaxy in this sample. With a SFR of only 177 M _ yr ^ 1 , which is likely representative of more typical star-forming galaxies at z 4. The high-resolution [ CII ] dataset of J081740, used here, was previously analysed by neeleman20 with QubeFit, a parametric kinematic modelling tool for 3D data neeleman20qubefit .

J081740 The velocity field and major axis p-v of J081740 show that the emission is less broad in velocity than the previous two cases, indicating that it has lower velocity dispersion. The model contours generally agree with the data contours and reproduces most of the emission without any obvious features. The velocity rises in the centre and flattens out quickly. There is also a tail of emission in the upper left quadrant at lower velocity that may indicate the presence of extraplanar gas e.g., marasco19, fraternali02 .

sources.summary: The paper compares DLA0817g ALMA kinematic analyses, noting rotating-disk modeling, reported velocity dispersion and Vmax/sigma values, and its own higher maximum rotation from adding a bulge component.

quotes.quote: Another galaxy that was observed with ALMA and was recently analyzed by Neeleman:2020 and Jones:2021 is DLA0817g. Neeleman:2020 exploited the higher-resolution observations ( 0.2'' as used in this work) and used an approach similar to ours, in which the flux was fit assuming a rotating thin disk with an exponential brightness profile. They compared it with the results from ^ 3D Barolo . Jones:2021 studied the kinematics by exploiting lower-resolution data ( 1'' ) using ^ 3D Barolo . Their results agree with those by Neeleman:2020 , who they found _ gas = 80 ^ +13 _ 11 km s ^ 1 and V_ max / _ gas = 3.4 ^ +1.1 _ 0.3 .

We note that we obtain a higher value for the maximum rotational velocity because we also considered the bulge component in our fitting because this model better reproduced the kinematic maps. When we use the exponential disk alone, we obtain a V_ max / _ gas = 4.01^ +0.21 _ 0.73 , while the velocity dispersion that we obtain is the same using the bulge+exponential disk or the exponential disk model.

sources.summary: The paper includes J81740 as a high-redshift star-forming galaxy with [CII] kinematics, discrepant inferred gas scale heights, and gas turbulence apparently driven mainly by stellar feedback.

quotes.quote: The other four galaxies in this sub-sample were taken from and consist of a star-forming galaxy (J81740) and three starbursts (BRI1335-0417, SGP38326-1, and SGP38326-2). The gas kinematics was derived by using on [ CII ] emission line observations. These data have spatial resolution of about 1-2 kpc and velocity resolution of 15 for BRI1335-0417 and 26 for the other three galaxies.

performed some experiments to obtain a tentative estimate of the gas disc scale height in J81740, SGP38326-1, SGP38326-2, and BRI1135-0417 using the Python routine Cannubi https://www.filippofraternali.com/cannubi . This was designed to estimate geometrical parameters of a rotating disc performing an iterative 3D modelling of the observations using . report scale heights of the order of 1 kpc, while we obtain z_ gas 0.2 kpc using Galpynamics . The only exception is BRI1135-0417: we find z_ gas 1-1.5 kpc, which is close to the values reported by . The origin of the discrepancy between the scale heights for J81740, SGP38326-1, and SGP38326-2 is not easy to understand, as the methods used are not fully comparable: Cannubi assumes that the thickness is constant within the disc, while Galpynamics derives a radial profile using a dynamical modelling based on the vertical hydrostatic equilibrium.

Similarly, show that the gas turbulence in J81740, SGP38326-1, and SGP38326-2 appears to be primarily driven by stellar feedback rather than LGI. Overall, these results potentially suggest that LGI and stellar feedback dominate in driving turbulence in different regions of the disc.

sources.summary: The paper models J081740 as a high-redshift rotating gas disc with derived SFR, shared dynamical assumptions, and stellar/gas mass constraints from rotation-curve decomposition.

quotes.quote: Star Formation Rates The global star formation rate (SFR) of the galaxies J081740, SGP38326-1 and SGP38326-2 were derived from the total IR luminosity ( 166 M_ yr ^ 1 , 1830 M_ yr ^ 1 , 882 M_ yr ^ 1 , respectively), as reported by neeleman20 and oteo16 .

The procedure employed for the dynamical modelling of the galaxies J081740, SGP38326-1 and SGP38326-2 is the same: a single spherical S ersic profile for the stars with free n , an exponential profile considering a razor-thin disc for the gas following the [ CII ] surface brightness and a spherical NFW CDM halo with the priors described in Table tab:priormm .

The gas mass is constrained for J081740, SGP38326-1 and SGP38326-2, while for BRI1335-0417 we find an upper limit that is driven by the imposition of the cosmological baryon fraction. In general, the values of the gas masses have larger uncertainties than those for the stellar masses. This is because at small radii the stellar component is dominant, while at larger radii the main contribution to the rotation curve comes from both the gas and the CDM halo.

sources.summary: The paper describes ALMA J081740.86+135138.2 as a rotating z~4 DLA host with a large dynamical mass and an extended neutral gas reservoir beyond its [ C ii ] emission.

quotes.quote: For ALMA J081740.86+135138.2, the shape of the emission is indicative of rotation, which is corroborated by the `double-horned' profile seen in the [ C ii ] 158 m emission spectrum (Fig. 2). From the magnitude of the rotation, we estimate a lower limit to the dynamical mass of 6 10^ 10 solar masses (M _ ) for this galaxy .

The observed impact parameters correspond to physical (proper) distances of 42 kpc and 18 kpc at the DLA redshifts for ALMA J081740.86+135138.2 and ALMA J120110.26+211756.2, respectively. These distances are substabtially larger than the extent of the [ C ii ] 158 m line emission, which extends to 5 kpc, and indicate that these galaxies have large reservoirs of H i relatively far away from their star-forming regions.

sources.summary: DLA0817g1 is described as the z~4 Wolfe disk, the sample's only galaxy detected in both CO and [C II], with FIR star formation far exceeding its NUV SFR.

quotes.quote: The only z 4 selected galaxy with a detection of CO emission thus remains DLA0817g1 at z 4.2603 , the Wolfe disk Neeleman20 ; this also has the highest \ line luminosity and FIR luminosity of all the selected galaxies of our sample.

For comparison, we have included high-redshift emission-selected galaxies main-sequence galaxies and Lyman-break galaxies, but excluding sub-mm galaxies; Capak15,Zanella18 ; It is clear that DLA0817g1, the only galaxy of our sample with detections of both \ and CO emission Neeleman20 , has a \ line luminosity and a molecular gas mass consistent with those of the emission-selected galaxies.
This is very unlike the situation in the Wolfe disk (DLA0817g1), where the FIR SFR is nearly fifteen times larger than the NUV SFR this work Neeleman20 .

gmw_00002677.name: SGP38326-1
gmw_00002677.overview: SGP38326-1 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy [arXiv:2302.03049, arXiv:2405.00103] that is the more massive member of an interacting pair [arXiv:2302.03049, arXiv:2403.00904]. It exhibits a clear velocity gradient that flattens at large radii [arXiv:2302.03049, arXiv:2403.00904], with kinematic features suggesting a widespread outflow or tidal interaction with its companion [arXiv:2302.03049].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SGP38326-1 as the more massive member of an interacting starburst pair, with a clear high-velocity gradient and features suggesting outflow or tidal interaction with SGP38326-2.

quotes.quote: SGP38326 A pair of starbursts with a combined SFR of around 4500 M _ yr ^ 1 , here we refer to the more massive galaxy as SGP38326-1 and the companion as SGP38326-2. Detection in ^ 12 CO(J=5-4) showed an elongated emission connecting the two galaxies which could be interpreted as a molecular gas bridge among them suggesting an ongoing interaction oteo16 . However, dust was not detected along this region and there appear to be no signs of stripped material in the [ CII ] emission oteo16 .

SGP38326-1 The velocity field and major axis p-v of SGP38326-1 show a very clear velocity gradient with the highest line-of-sight velocity of the sample reaching 380 km s ^ 1 . It has a rising velocity that flattens in the external regions. The kinematic model reproduces most of the bright emission of the galaxy except for a few interesting features. In the upper left quadrant there is a fainter high receding velocity region that is partially interpreted as high velocity dispersion by the best-fit model, but could be due to a widespread outflow, alike the galactic fountain gas or extraplanar gas seen in local galaxies fraternali06, boomsma08 . In the lower right quadrant there is another similar structure that forms a southeast tail in the velocity field that is in the direction of the companion galaxy SGP38326-2 and could be evidence of a tidal interaction between the two galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper treats SGP38326-1 as a high-redshift starburst with [CII] gas kinematics, discrepant gas scale-height estimates, and turbulence apparently driven mainly by stellar feedback.

quotes.quote: The other four galaxies in this sub-sample were taken from and consist of a star-forming galaxy (J81740) and three starbursts (BRI1335-0417, SGP38326-1, and SGP38326-2). The gas kinematics was derived by using on [ CII ] emission line observations. These data have spatial resolution of about 1-2 kpc and velocity resolution of 15 for BRI1335-0417 and 26 for the other three galaxies.

performed some experiments to obtain a tentative estimate of the gas disc scale height in J81740, SGP38326-1, SGP38326-2, and BRI1135-0417 using the Python routine Cannubi https://www.filippofraternali.com/cannubi . This was designed to estimate geometrical parameters of a rotating disc performing an iterative 3D modelling of the observations using . report scale heights of the order of 1 kpc, while we obtain z_ gas 0.2 kpc using Galpynamics . The only exception is BRI1135-0417: we find z_ gas 1-1.5 kpc, which is close to the values reported by . The origin of the discrepancy between the scale heights for J81740, SGP38326-1, and SGP38326-2 is not easy to understand, as the methods used are not fully comparable: Cannubi assumes that the thickness is constant within the disc, while Galpynamics derives a radial profile using a dynamical modelling based on the vertical hydrostatic equilibrium.

Similarly, show that the gas turbulence in J81740, SGP38326-1, and SGP38326-2 appears to be primarily driven by stellar feedback rather than LGI. Overall, these results potentially suggest that LGI and stellar feedback dominate in driving turbulence in different regions of the disc.

sources.summary: The paper models SGP38326-1 as one member of a high-redshift group, estimating its relative mass, SFR, gas mass constraints, and a rotation curve flattening at large radius.

quotes.quote: This makes the sample heterogeneous in terms of physical properties: one of the galaxies is a quasar host (BRI1335-0417), two are in a group (SGP38326-1 and SGP38326-2) and the remaining galaxy was originally discovered as a damped Lyman alpha system wagg14, oteo16, neeleman19 .

We note that the measurement for SGP38326 is for the whole system and it is estimated that SGP38326-1 is 2.5 times more massive than SGP38326-2 oteo16 . As described in Section sec:dynmod , we determine the total gas mass of the system independently from the gas emission through the mass decomposition of the rotation curves. Hence, we use initial estimates of the gas mass in the Monte Carlo algorithm we use for the mass decomposition by scaling the values in Table tab:colum using a normalisation factor that accounts for conversions between CO luminosities and gas masses (details in Section sec:dynmod ). Star Formation Rates The global star formation rate (SFR) of the galaxies J081740, SGP38326-1 and SGP38326-2 were derived from the total IR luminosity ( 166 M_ yr ^ 1 , 1830 M_ yr ^ 1 , 882 M_ yr ^ 1 , respectively), as reported by neeleman20 and oteo16 .

Similarly to local galaxies, the shapes of the rotation curves of z 4 galaxies are diverse: BRI1335-0417 has a bump in the inner region that resembles the dynamical signature of a bulge; the rotation curves of J081470 and SGP38326-1 seem to flatten at large radii; and the rotation curve of SGP38326-2 has a shallow gradient.

gmw_00002678.name: SGP38326-2
gmw_00002678.overview: SGP38326-2 is a high-redshift starburst galaxy [arXiv:2302.03049, arXiv:2405.00103] that acts as the lower-mass companion in a starburst pair [arXiv:2302.03049, arXiv:2403.00904]. It features a rotating gas disk [arXiv:2302.03049] with a shallow rotation-curve gradient [arXiv:2403.00904] and turbulence likely driven by stellar feedback [arXiv:2405.00103].

sources.summary: The paper treats SGP38326-2 as the companion in the SGP38326 starburst pair, finding a mostly rotating disc but also minor-axis residuals likely related to interaction with SGP38326-1.

quotes.quote: SGP38326 A pair of starbursts with a combined SFR of around 4500 M _ yr ^ 1 , here we refer to the more massive galaxy as SGP38326-1 and the companion as SGP38326-2. Detection in ^ 12 CO(J=5-4) showed an elongated emission connecting the two galaxies which could be interpreted as a molecular gas bridge among them suggesting an ongoing interaction oteo16 . However, dust was not detected along this region and there appear to be no signs of stripped material in the [ CII ] emission oteo16 .

SGP38326-2 The velocity field and major axis p-v of SGP38326-2 show the velocity rising and flattening, like the other galaxies. The kinematic model reproduces most of the emission and no major external features can be seen. Although the major axis p-v seems like a very well behaved rotating disc, it is important to note that this galaxy might be undergoing a tidal interaction with SGP38326-1 which makes the kinematic fit more challenging. In the minor axis, the data contours differ significantly from the model.

sources.summary: The paper treats SGP38326-2 as a high-redshift starburst with [CII] gas kinematics, discrepant gas scale-height estimates, and turbulence apparently driven mainly by stellar feedback.

sources.summary: The paper models SGP38326-2 as the lower-mass member of a high-redshift group, deriving SFR, gas mass constraints, and a shallow rotation-curve gradient.

quotes.quote: This makes the sample heterogeneous in terms of physical properties: one of the galaxies is a quasar host (BRI1335-0417), two are in a group (SGP38326-1 and SGP38326-2) and the remaining galaxy was originally discovered as a damped Lyman alpha system wagg14, oteo16, neeleman19 .

gmw_00002679.name: WISEA J010039.62+394230.3
gmw_00002679.overview: WISEA J010039.62+394230.3 is an active host galaxy located at a redshift of z = 0.083 [arXiv:2305.01694]. Archival spectra of the galaxy reveal pre-flare active galactic nucleus emission and broad Balmer emission lines [arXiv:2305.01694].

sources.summary: The paper identifies WISEA J010039.62+394230.3 as the z=0.083 active host galaxy of AT 2021loi, with pre-flare AGN emission and archival LAMOST spectra showing broad Balmer lines.

quotes.quote: An optical brightening at the center of the z = 0.083 active galaxy WISEA J010039.62+394230.3 was discovered in Zwicky Transient Facility (ZTF) public survey data as ZTF20aanxcpf on 2021 May 7 (UT used throughout) by , using the ALeRCE broker Forster2021 . The brightening was reported with an band reference subtracted Throughout the text, the term ``reference'' in the context of photometry or spectroscopy is used to denote the pre-flare emission from the source, which contains both the stellar light of the host and the pre-flare AGN emission. discovery magnitude of 18.84 0.15 , at R.A. 22:11:21.93 and Dec +39:42:30.31 (J2000).

We retrieved an archival spectrum of WISEA J010039.62+394230.3, the host galaxy of AT 2021loi, from the fifth data release of the Large Sky Area Multi-Object Fiber Spectroscopic Telescope survey LAMOST/DR5; Zhao2012 obtained on 2013 December 22. The LAMOST archive includes a yet earlier spectrum of this source, obtained two years earlier (2011 December 28). That spectrum also shows broad Balmer emission lines, similar to the LAMOST spectrum we use. However, it is significantly affected by spectral calibration issues, including residual (sky) spectral features and uncertain spectrophotometry. We thus decided to only use the latest LAMOST spectrum.

gmw_00002680.name: IC 4970
gmw_00002680.overview: IC 4970 is a lenticular companion galaxy [arXiv:1409.3226, arXiv:astro-ph/0701291] engaged in an ongoing tidal interaction with the larger spiral galaxy NGC 6872 [arXiv:0710.5183, arXiv:0810.3577]. Connected to its neighbor by a stellar bridge [arXiv:0710.5183, arXiv:0810.3577], IC 4970 contains atomic gas [arXiv:astro-ph/0701291] and hosts an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:0710.5183, arXiv:0810.3577] with strong nuclear X-ray variability [arXiv:0710.5183].

sources.summary: The paper focuses on IC 4970 as the lower-mass member of an interacting pair, finding central X-ray point sources, strong nuclear variability, and a heavily obscured active nucleus.

quotes.quote: IC 4970 and NGC 6872 have long been known to be a tidally interacting galaxy pair (Vorontsov-Velyaminov 1959). As shown in the bottom panel of Figure fig:pavogroup , tidal tails extend outward more than 30 kpc ( 2 ) to the east and west from the tip of NGC 6872's spiral arms. A stellar bridge connects the `knee' in NGC 6872's northern arm and tidal tail to the lenticular companion galaxy IC 4970, indicating that tidal interactions between IC 4970 and NGC 6872 are ongoing.

X-ray emission from IC 4970 is concentrated in two point sources within the central 1 kpc ( 4 ) of the galaxy with one point source located at the nucleus ( RA =20^h16^m57^s.56 , DEC =-70^ 44'59 9 ), and one 0.68 kpc ( 2 6 ) to the north ( RA =20^h16^m57^s.6 , DEC =-70^ 44'57 3 ). The analysis regions we use to study these sources and diffuse emission in IC 4970 are listed in Table tab:regions . t Nuclear X-ray Variability The X-ray luminosity of IC 4970 is dominated by emission from the nuclear point source.

Short time scale variability is not expected for circumnuclear starbursts. Thus the observed variability strongly suggests that the nucleus of IC 4970 is an AGN. Nuclear X-ray Spectra and Luminosities t We extracted the spectrum of IC 4970's nucleus in the same elliptical region used to construct the light curves, and used a local background annulus concentric with the nucleus with inner and outer radii of 4 9 and 19 7 , respectively, (denoted `nucleus bkg' in Table tab:regions ) to subtract the background.

sources.summary: The paper identifies IC 4970 as NGC 6872's S0 companion, measures it as Region 16, derives its stellar mass and low recent SFR, and argues its interaction affected the Condor less than expected.

quotes.quote: NGC 6872, hereafter the Condor, is a large spiral galaxy that is interacting with its closest companion, the S0 galaxy IC 4970. The far-ultraviolet (FUV) to mid-infrared (mid-IR) coverage of NGC 6872 and IC 4970 (hereafter the Condor galaxy and its companion, respectively) provides important information on their youngest star forming regions and total stellar mass, rendering this system a perfect laboratory for studying the effects of galaxy interactions on their spectral energy distributions (SEDs) Machacek2009 . The physical extent of its arms is often attributed to the recent interaction of the Condor with its closest companion IC 4970, an S0 galaxy located 1.1 \ north of Condor's center.

Region 16 corresponds to the companion galaxy, IC 4970. The 100 Myr-averaged SFR summed over all Regions in the Condor (0-15) is equal to 5.4 and equal to 0.08 in the companion. We derive a total stellar mass of 1.2^ +0.2 _ 0.1 10^ 11 for Regions 0-15 and 1.0^ +0.1 _ 0.1 10^ 10 for the companion. This corroborates the idea that the interaction with the companion IC 4970 affected the Condor less than previously thought and the idea that the Condor galaxy was already a very massive ( M_ > 10^ 11 ) and extended galaxy before the interaction.

sources.summary: The paper describes IC 4970 as NGC 6872's spheroidal companion in an ongoing tidal interaction, with a stellar bridge, small velocity offset, and previously argued obscured AGN.

quotes.quote: The large spiral galaxy, NGC 6872, and its spheroidal companion galaxy, IC 4970, located 1 12 to the north near a break in NGC 6872's northern tidal arm, have long been known to be a tidally interacting galaxy pair (Vorontsov-Velyaminov 1959), and are well-studied in the optical (Mihos 1993; Bastian 2005) and radio (Horellou Booth 1997; Horellou Koribalski 2003; 2007) wavebands.
