The stellar bridge pointing from the northern tidal arm of NGC 6872 to its less massive companion IC 4970 indicates the tidal interactions between these two galaxies are ongoing. The 74 radial velocity difference Line-of-sight velocities and redshifts are taken from the CfA Redshift Survey by Martimbeau Huchra. Online versions of the catalog, supporting software, and documentation are available at http://cfa-www.harvard.edu/ huchra/zcat. between NGC 6872 and IC 4970 suggest that these galaxies form a spiral dominated subgroup.

In a previous paper, we used Spitzer mid-infrared and Chandra X-ray images to analyze the role of the IC 4970/NGC 6872 collision on the nuclear activity in the less massive galaxy, IC 4970, arguing that it hosts a highly-obscured active galactic nucleus (AGN) fueled by cold gas driven into the nucleus by the interaction (Machacek 2008a).

sources.summary: The paper treats IC 4970 as NGC 6872's nearby small lenticular companion, detected in atomic gas and implicated in forming the larger galaxy's tidal structure.

quotes.quote: The dynamical evolution of the large ( >100 kpc), barred spiral galaxy NGC 6872 and its small companion IC 4970 in the southern group Pavo is investigated.
NGC 6872 is likely to be affected by tidal perturbations from the nearby companion IC 4970, a small lenticular galaxy located 1 1 to the north.
The companion galaxy, IC 4970, is clearly detected in (Fig. fig3hicompa ). It contains 1.3 10^9 M_ of atomic gas.

sources.summary: The paper treats IC 4970 as the smaller S0 companion interacting with NGC 6872, with models giving a 5:1 mass ratio and a 145 Myr time since periapse.

quotes.quote: NGC 6872 is a barred spiral galaxy that is interacting with IC 4970, a smaller S0 galaxy (de Vaucouleurs et al. 1991), which is the elliptical structure in the center left of Fig. image .

The NGC 6872/IC 4970 interaction has been modeled by Mihos et al. (1993) and Horellou Koribalski (2003) who both find a mass ratio between NGC 6872 and IC 4970 of 5:1, and a time since periapse of 145 Myr, i.e. the event that triggered the formation of the tails.
We find clusters with masses up to 10^ 6.5 10^ 7 M_ forming due to the interaction between NGC 6872 and IC 4970.

gmw_00002681.name: NGC 6872
gmw_00002681.overview: NGC 6872 is a large, gas-rich barred spiral galaxy that is tidally interacting with the smaller companion galaxy IC 4970 [arXiv:astro-ph/0502561, arXiv:0710.5183]. This ongoing interaction has produced extended tidal tails and a stellar bridge, with most star formation occurring in these outer arms and tails rather than the central region [arXiv:0710.5183, arXiv:1409.3226].

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 6872 as an interacting barred spiral whose tidal tails host young massive clusters, asymmetric star formation, and material likely produced by its encounter with IC 4970.

quotes.quote: We present a photometric analysis of the rich star cluster population in the tidal tails of NGC 6872. We find star clusters with ages between 1 - 100 Myr distributed in the tidal tails, while the tails themselves have an age of less than 150 Myr. Most of the young massive ( 10^ 4 M/M_ 10^ 7 ) clusters are found in the outer regions of the galactic disk or the tidal tails. The mass distribution of the cluster population can be well described by a power-law of the form N(m) m^ , where = 1.85 0.11 , in very good agreement with other young cluster populations found in a variety of different environments. We estimate the star formation rate for three separate regions of the galaxy, and find that the eastern tail is forming stars at 2 times the rate of the western tail and 5 times the rate of the main body of the galaxy.
NGC 6872 is a barred spiral galaxy that is interacting with IC 4970, a smaller S0 galaxy (de Vaucouleurs et al. 1991), which is the elliptical structure in the center left of Fig. image .

The NGC 6872/IC 4970 interaction has been modeled by Mihos et al. (1993) and Horellou Koribalski (2003) who both find a mass ratio between NGC 6872 and IC 4970 of 5:1, and a time since periapse of 145 Myr, i.e. the event that triggered the formation of the tails.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6872 as a gas-rich spiral interacting with IC 4970, with extended tidal tails, a stellar bridge, and star-forming outer arms.

quotes.quote: IC 4970 and NGC 6872 have long been known to be a tidally interacting galaxy pair (Vorontsov-Velyaminov 1959). As shown in the bottom panel of Figure fig:pavogroup , tidal tails extend outward more than 30 kpc ( 2 ) to the east and west from the tip of NGC 6872's spiral arms. A stellar bridge connects the `knee' in NGC 6872's northern arm and tidal tail to the lenticular companion galaxy IC 4970, indicating that tidal interactions between IC 4970 and NGC 6872 are ongoing.

The spiral galaxy NGC 6872 is gas rich. From CO J=1-0 line emission, Horellou Booth (1997) infer 9.6 10^8 of molecular hydrogen in NGC 6872's central region. Although atomic hydrogen is not observed near the center of the spiral galaxy, 1.41 10^ 10 of HI gas is distributed along the full extent of NGC 6872's spiral arms and associated tidal tails (Horellou Booth 1997; Horellou Koribalski 2007) and 1.3 10^9 of HI gas is seen along the direction of IC 4970, that may be associated with the companion galaxy (Horellou Koribalski 2007).

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 6872, the Condor, as a giant interacting spiral whose extended arms host most star formation and whose prior mass and disk size may reduce IC 4970's inferred impact.

quotes.quote: NGC 6872, hereafter the Condor, is a large spiral galaxy that is interacting with its closest companion, the S0 galaxy IC 4970. The extent of the Condor provides an opportunity for detailed investigation of the impact of the interaction on the current star formation rate and its history across the galaxy, on the age and spatial distribution of its stellar population, and on the mechanism that drive the star formation activity. To address these issues we analyzed the far-ultraviolet (FUV) to near-infrared (near-IR) spectral energy distribution (SED) of 17, 10 kpc diameter, regions across the galaxy, and derived their star formation history, current star formation rate, and stellar population and mass.

We find that most of the star formation takes place in the extended arms, with very little star formation in the central 5 kpc of the galaxy, in contrast to what was predicted from previous numerical simulations. Our analysis makes clear the Condor galaxy was already very massive ( M_ >10^ 11 ) prior to the encounter with the companion, which disturbed the morphology of the galaxy, but did not strongly affect its stellar or gas masses. The Condor was possibly more extended and was less affected by the interaction with the companion IC 4970 than previously thought.

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 6872 as a very large barred spiral whose extended gas disk, tidal tails, star formation, and morphology are reproduced by interaction simulations.

quotes.quote: The dynamical evolution of the large ( >100 kpc), barred spiral galaxy NGC 6872 and its small companion IC 4970 in the southern group Pavo is investigated.

NGC 6872 contains 1.4 10^ 10 M_ of gas, distributed in an extended rotating disk. Massive concentrations of gas ( 10^9 M_ ) are found at the tip of both tidal tails and towards the break seen in the optical northern arm near the companion.

The southern galaxy NGC 6872 is one of the largest spiral galaxies known. Star formation is traced all along the arms, which extend over more than 100 kpc at our adopted distance of 61 Mpc. The galaxy belongs to a small group, Pavo (see Fig. 1a).

sources.summary: The paper presents multiwavelength observations of NGC 6872, linking PAH emission, luminous X-ray sources, tidal arms, and a nuclear stream to interaction-driven star formation.

quotes.quote: In the spiral galaxy NGC 6872, 8.0 and 5.8 nonstellar emission having colors consistent with polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs) is concentrated primarily in clumps in three regions: in a 5 kpc radius outer ring about the center of the spiral galaxy, in a bridge of emission connecting NGC 6872's northern spiral arm to IC 4970, and along the full extent of both NGC 6872's tidal arms. PAH emission is correlated with young star clusters and dense HI regions. We find no strong differences in the mid-infrared colors of the star-forming regions in the spiral galaxy NGC 6872 as a function of position relative to the tidally interacting companion galaxy IC 4970. We find eleven very luminous X-ray sources ( (0.5 - 5) 10^ 39 ) clustered to the southwest in NGC 6872, near bright star-forming regions. In NGC 6872's tidal features, young star clusters form at the boundaries of diffuse X-ray gas, suggesting that stars form as gas stripped by the interactions cools.

gmw_00002682.name: NGC 6876
gmw_00002682.overview: NGC 6876 is the dominant elliptical galaxy in the Pavo group [arXiv:0810.3577, arXiv:astro-ph/0701291]. It has interacted with the galaxy NGC 6872, creating a long X-ray trail of hot gas between them [arXiv:1409.3226, arXiv:0810.3577], and its core shows evidence of a past merger [arXiv:0810.3577].

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 6876 as the more distant elliptical companion whose interaction formed an X-ray trail and may have helped distort the Condor and trigger its bar instability.

quotes.quote: The Condor, however, has also interacted with an elliptical galaxy in the group, NGC 6876, located 8.7 \ to the SE of Condor's center. This interaction has formed the longest-known X-ray trail, extending from the Condor to NGC 6876 Machacek2005,Machacek2009 . The interaction between the Condor and its companion was first modeled by N-body simulations of , assuming that the further elliptical galaxy NGC 6876 played little or no role in the dynamics of the pair and that the Condor was five times more massive than its closest companion.

The more distant elliptical companion NGC 6876 could have shaped the Condor over a longer period of time, possibly distorting its disk and contributing to the formation of the bar instability much before the interaction with the companion started, in the last 130 Myr. The elliptical galaxy NGC 6876 may have played a larger role in shaping the Condor than previously considered.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 6876 as the dominant Pavo elliptical, linked by hot gas to the NGC 6872/IC 4970 subgroup and showing central evidence for an earlier merger.

quotes.quote: XMM-Newton X-ray observations revealed a 90 kpc long X-ray trail of hot ( 1 keV) gas linking, in projection, NGC 6872/IC 4970 to NGC 6876, the dominant Pavo group elliptical galaxy. The properties of the X-ray gas in the trail, coupled with the large 850 radial velocity difference between NGC 6872 and NGC 6876, provide dramatic evidence for the highly supersonic passage of the NGC 6872/IC 4970 subgroup through the dense 0.5 keV IGM gas in the Pavo group core (Machacek 2005b). Although difficult to identify today, such interactions would likely have been frequent at high redshift, when galaxy groups and clusters were forming at the intersections of large scale filaments. The dominant elliptical galaxy, NGC 6876, also shows evidence for recent interactions. Hubble Space Telescope observations of the inner 1'' of NGC 6876 show a depressed central surface brightness distribution surrounded by a stellar torus, suggesting the presence of a binary black hole from a previous merger (Lauer 2002).

sources.summary: The paper discusses NGC 6876 as the dominant Pavo elliptical near NGC 6872, finding no H I counterpart or strong evidence that it shaped NGC 6872.

quotes.quote: We detect no counterpart to the X-ray trail between NGC 6872 and NGC 6876, the dominant elliptical galaxy in the Pavo group located 8' to the southeast. At the sensitivity and the resolution of the observations, there is no sign in the overall distribution that NGC 6876 has affected the evolution of NGC 6872.
The radial velocity of NGC 6876 is about 800 km s ^ 1 lower than that of NGC 6872 (see Table 1).
Had tidal interactions been at work, their effect would have been seen also in the distribution of the stars in NGC 6876.

gmw_00002683.name: PMN J1451-1512
gmw_00002683.overview: PMN J1451-1512 is a radio-loud quasar located at a high redshift of z=4.763 [arXiv:1805.04317]. It is characterized by strong Lyman-alpha emission and was previously recognized as the brightest known ultraviolet-bright quasar at a redshift greater than 4.5 [arXiv:1805.04317].

sources.summary: The paper compares PMN J1451-1512 as the previous brightest UV-bright high-redshift QSO, noting its radio loudness, emission-line boost, and fainter infrared output.

quotes.quote: The brightest known UV-bright QSO at z>4.5 in the compilation by Wang16 is J145147.05-151220.20 at z=4.763 . Previously labelled PMN J1451-1512, it was identified as a radio-loud QSO by Hook02 with a 1.4 GHz flux density of 28.5 1.0 mJy in NVSS. The object is not contained in the SDSS footprint, but in the SDSS-like filters of VST-ATLAS it has i=17.22 and z=17.14 .

PMN J1451-1512 has strong Ly- emission with 40 nm equivalent width (see Fig. spectrum ), which boosts its i magnitude by 0.3 mag, while in the continuum between the Ly- and CIV lines our object is 0.2 mag brighter than PMN J1451-1512. Our object is consistently brighter than PMN J1451-1512 by 0.5 0.05 mag across the whole infrared range from 1.2 ( J band) to 22 (W4), suggesting a nearly identical spectral slope across the rest-frame range from 200 nm to 4 , and thus will have a larger bolometric energy output (see Figure photometry ).

gmw_00002684.name: SMSS J215728.21-360215.1
gmw_00002684.overview: SMSS J215728.21-360215.1 is an ultra-luminous, radio-quiet quasar located at a redshift of 4.692 [arXiv:2302.10397, arXiv:2005.06868]. It is powered by a supermassive black hole estimated at approximately 34 billion solar masses [arXiv:2005.06868], which drives its high intrinsic UV-optical luminosity [arXiv:1805.04317, arXiv:2005.06868].

sources.summary: The paper models the luminous z=4.692 quasar SMSS J215728.21-360215.1, deriving accretion-disc and single-epoch black-hole masses, luminosity, Eddington ratio, and radiative efficiency.

quotes.quote: We develop an accretion disc (AD) fitting method, utilising thin and slim disc models and Bayesian inference with the Markov-Chain Monte-Carlo approach, testing it on the most luminous known quasar, SMSS J215728.21-360215.1, at redshift z=4.692 . With a spectral energy distribution constructed from near-infrared spectra and broadband photometry, the AD models find a black hole mass of (M_ AD /M_ ) = 10.31^ +0.17 _ 0.14 with an anisotropy-corrected bolometric luminosity of (L_ bol / erg s^ 1 ) = 47.87 0.10 , and derive an Eddington ratio of 0.29^ +0.11 _ 0.10 as well as a radiative efficiency of 0.09^ +0.05 _ 0.03 . Using the near-infrared spectra, we estimate the single-epoch virial black hole mass estimate to be (M_ SE /M_ ) = 10.33 0.08 , with a monochromatic luminosity at 3000 \ of (L( 3000 )/ erg s^ 1 ) = 47.66 0.01 . As an independent approach, AD fitting has the potential to complement the single-epoch virial mass method in obtaining stronger constraints on properties of massive quasar black holes across a wide range of redshifts.

We use spectroscopic observations and publicly available survey photometry to construct the SED of J2157--3602. Photometric Data We crossmatch J2157--3602, positioned at RA = 329.36762 ^ and Dec = -36.03756 ^ (J2000), with AllWISE WISE, ALLWISE , the VISTA Hemisphere Survey VHS; VHS DR6, Two Micron All-Sky Survey 2MASS; 2MASS , SkyMapper Southern Survey SMSS; SMSS_2019 DR3, and NOIRLab Source Catalog NSC; NSC_DR2 DR2 to collect photometry from the infrared to optical passbands. The W1 and W2 magnitudes from CatWISE2020 Marocco_2021_catwise2020 are not appreciably different from that of AllWISE. Properties of J2157--3602, including its photometry, can be found in the discovery paper Wolf_2018 .

sources.summary: The paper reports SMSS J215728.21-360215.1 as an ultra-luminous redshift 4.75 QSO, likely unlensed and radio-quiet, with the highest known intrinsic UV-optical luminosity.

quotes.quote: We report the discovery of the ultra-luminous QSO SMSS J215728.21-360215.1 with magnitude z=16.9 and W4 =7.42 at redshift 4.75. Given absolute magnitudes of M_ 145, AB =-29.3 , M_ 300, AB =-30.12 and L_ bol /L_ bol, = 14.84 , it is the QSO with the highest unlensed UV-optical luminosity currently known in the Universe. It was found by combining proper-motion data from Gaia DR2 with photometry from SkyMapper DR1 and the Wide-field Infrared Survey Explorer (WISE).

By combining data from Gaia, SkyMapper and WISE, we identified SMSS J215728.21-360215.1 as the brightest z 5 QSO known at this point; it has a magnitude of z_ VST =16.93 and H_ 2MASS =14.84 . With a redshift of z=4.75 this translates into an absolute magnitude of M_ 300, AB =-30.12 and a bolometric luminosity of 6.95 10^ 14 solar luminosities.
The derived luminosity is likely intrinsic , making J2157-3602 the QSO with the highest unlensed UV-optical luminosity discovered so far.

sources.summary: The paper refines SMSS J2157 to redshift 4.692, estimates its extremely massive black hole, and explains its record quasar luminosity as the consequence of that black hole mass.

quotes.quote: From near-infrared spectroscopic measurements of the Mg ii emission line doublet, we estimate the black hole (BH) mass of the quasar, SMSS J215728.21 360215.1, as being (3.4 0.6) 10^ 10 M _ and refine the redshift of the quasar to be z=4.692 . SMSS J2157 is the most luminous known quasar, with a 3000 \ luminosity of (4.7 0.5) 10 ^ 47 erg s ^ 1 and an estimated bolometric luminosity of 1.6 10 ^ 48 erg s ^ 1 , yet its Eddington ratio is only 0.4 . Thus, the high luminosity of this quasar is a consequence of its extremely large BH --- one of the most massive BHs at z>4 .

gmw_00002685.name: T1214-277
gmw_00002685.overview: T1214-277 is a starburst and Wolf-Rayet galaxy [arXiv:1510.02542, arXiv:astro-ph/9809096] that exhibits strong Lyman-alpha emission [arXiv:astro-ph/9809096] and indications of leaking Lyman-continuum photons [arXiv:1510.02542]. It hosts massive-star winds with a terminal velocity of 2000 km/s [arXiv:2209.02726] and high-ionization lines consistent with an intermediate-mass black hole [arXiv:2305.02189].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Tololo 1214-277 among the strongest local C III] emitters, classifies it as a Wolf-Rayet galaxy, notes extreme H equivalent width, and cites Lyman-continuum leakage.

quotes.quote: The three galaxies from the z 0 samples with the most extreme C III ] , with equivalent widths exceeding 14 , are Tololo 1214-277 , Markarian 71 (a star-forming region within NGC 2366), Many studies incorrectly name this object; see http://cdsarc.u-strasbg.fr/viz-bin/vizExec/.getpuz?7239 N 202363 and Pox 120. We now explore what is known about these three galaxies, and plot their ultraviolet spectra in Figure fig:spectra . Tololo 1214-277 is classified as a Wolf-Rayet galaxy; Mrk 71 is classified as a star cluster containing Wolf-Rayet stars within the irregular galaxy NGC 2366, and Pox 120 is classified as a Wolf-Rayet galaxy Kunth:1985vk,Schaerer:1999ku . The equivalent width of H is extremely high for Mrk 71 ( <-1000 , Buckalew:2005ch ), and for TOL 1214-277 ( 1700 , Guseva:2011dj ).

Another indication of the extreme star formation occurring in these galaxies is the fact that Tololo 1214-277 is one of very few galaxies in the local universe that show indications of leaking Lyman continuum photons Verhamme:2015ce .

sources.summary: The paper models Tol 1214 with reliable high-ionization lines, finding EUV properties consistent with a 10^4-10^5 solar-mass black hole and noting reported broad H alpha.

quotes.quote: We conduct MCMC calculations for Tol 1214 that has reliably high S/N ratios for high ionization lines including [ Ne v ] 3426 with the new parameter ranges, and obtain the best-fit parameters: log U = -1.93 and log n_ H /cm^ 3 = 2.54 . These parameters do not change the conclusions of the M_ BH value.

Figure fig:K03_and_galaxies indicates that the L_ EUV and _ EUV of SBS 0335-052E (J1205 and Tol 1214) agrees with the models with M_ BH =10^ 3-4 \ M_ ( M_ BH =10^ 4-5 \ M_ ) within the 2 level and therefore the BH mass is estimated to be M_ BH 10^ 3-4 \ M_ ( M_ BH 10^ 4-5 \ M_ ).
We find a report of the broad line of Tol 1214 in . The lower limit of the H FWHM of Tol 1214 is 900 \ km \ s^ 1 and broad line to narrow line flux ratios are 1-2 .

sources.summary: The review reports T1214-277 as a starburst galaxy with strong pure Ly emission, no blueshifted absorption, and an inferred line of sight largely free of neutral gas.

quotes.quote: Observations were subsequently made on additional galaxies by Kunth et al. while Thuan and Isotov have obtained GHRS spectra of two more starburst galaxies, namely Tol65 and T1214-277 with the G140L grating allowing a larger spectral region around \ . Tol65 reveals a broad damped \ absorption while T1214-277 shows a pure \ emission profile with an equivalent width of 70 \ and with no blue absorption.

Thuan and Isotov have detected strong Ly \ emission in T1214-277, with no evidence of blueshifted Ly \ absorption. In this case the detection of the line requires that a significant fraction of the area covered by the slit along the line of sight is essentially free from neutral gas, suggesting a patchy or filamentary structure of the neutral clouds. Such a geometry would be unlikely in galaxies surrounded by enormous H I clouds, as in IZw 18 and similar objects.

sources.summary: The paper cites Tol 1214-277 as a Dwalin-19 galaxy with massive-star wind signatures, a 2000 terminal stellar-wind velocity, and larger inferred outflow/loading rates than SBS 0335-052.

quotes.quote: We stress that some of the galaxies in the Dwalin-19 sample have been studied individually in separate works. ThuanIzotov97 found P-Cygni profiles in the stellar UV absorption lines of SBS 0335-052 and Tol 1214-277, suggesting the presence of a stellar wind from massive stars in these two systems. The terminal velocities measured for the stellar winds in SBS 0335-052 and Tol 1214-277 were 500 and 2000 , respectively. Interestingly, while in our study we do not infer terminal velocities, our estimates for mass outflow rates and loading factors in Tol 1214-277 are about one order of magnitude larger than for SBS 0335-052.

gmw_00002686.name: KiDS 0837
gmw_00002686.overview: KiDS 0837 is a brightest cluster galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.3958 [arXiv:2302.05360]. It exhibits a disturbed morphology with clumpy substructures, ongoing star formation, and no active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:2302.05360].

sources.summary: KiDS 0837 is a z=0.3958 star-forming BCG with disturbed/clumpy morphology, relatively low SFR, no CO detection, and likely depleted or rapidly consumed molecular gas.

quotes.quote: The three BCGs of this work are KiDS 0837 (i.e., G345423 at z=0.3958 ), KiDS 1415 (G497046 at z=0.4098 ), and KiDS 1433 (G343011 at z=0.3546 ). KiDS 1433 is bulge dominated, while KiDS 0837 and KiDS 1415 appear to be have a more disturbed morphology and clumpy substructures. The only exception is KiDS 0837, which has the lowest SFR, a factor of 2 lower than the average SFR of MS galaxies at its redshift. The W1-W2 colors of the three KiDS BCGs of this work lie in the range between (0.36-0.55) mag and are therefore not classified as AGN.

For the other two targeted BCGs, KiDS 0837 and 1415, we did not detect any CO emission in their spectra. Therefore, the differences between the six KiDS BCGs seem to be due to the different molecular gas properties, that is, the CO luminosities, M_ H_2 , and _ dep , as the line-based and SED-based SFRs, stellar masses, and dust masses and luminosities are similar within a factor of 3 among the KiDS BCGs, including those undetected in CO (KiDS 0837 and 1415), and hence also among the BCGs from the parent sample. The other star-forming BCGs (KiDS 0837 and 1415), with only upper limits in CO, may be at a different and possibly later stage of the BCG evolution, where the H_2 gas reservoir has been exhausted or is undergoing rapid consumption at a timescale _ dep (1-2) Gyr, while star formation is still ongoing.

gmw_00002687.name: KiDS 1415
gmw_00002687.overview: KiDS 1415 is a brightest cluster galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.4098 [arXiv:2302.05360]. It exhibits a disturbed morphology with clumpy substructures and is undergoing a recent starburst likely triggered by interactions with nearby companions [arXiv:2302.05360].

sources.summary: KiDS 1415 is a z=0.4098 star-forming BCG with clumpy disturbed morphology, strong [O II]-based star formation, no CO detection, and a recent companion-linked burst.

quotes.quote: The three BCGs of this work are KiDS 0837 (i.e., G345423 at z=0.3958 ), KiDS 1415 (G497046 at z=0.4098 ), and KiDS 1433 (G343011 at z=0.3546 ). KiDS 1433 is bulge dominated, while KiDS 0837 and KiDS 1415 appear to be have a more disturbed morphology and clumpy substructures. The resulting [ O II ] doublet fluxes of KiDS 0837, 1415, and 1433 are F_ O II =(14.8 4.7) , (75.7 25.6) , and (104.2 31.6) , respectively, in units of 10^ 17 erg s ^ 1 cm ^ 2 . With these fluxes, Eq. eq:SFR_OII yields an SFR _ O II =(5.5 1.7) , (21.9 7.4) , and (25.1 7.6) M_ /yr for KiDS 0837, 1415 and 1433, respectively, where we assumed r_ lines =0.5 , following Gilbank2010.

For the other two targeted BCGs, KiDS 0837 and 1415, we did not detect any CO emission in their spectra. For KiDS 1415, in particular, our modeling implies a remarkably recent (300 Myr) burst of star formation. This result, together with the high star formation activity and high infrared luminosity of the BCG, suggests that the source may be interacting with the nearby companion(s), which are clearly visible in Figure fig:BCG_images . The other star-forming BCGs (KiDS 0837 and 1415), with upper limits in CO alone, may be at a different and possibly later stage of the BCG evolution, in which the H_2 gas reservoir has been consumed or is undergoing rapid consumption on a timescale _ dep (1-2) Gyr, while star formation is still ongoing.

gmw_00002688.name: KiDS 1433
gmw_00002688.overview: KiDS 1433 is a bulge-dominated brightest cluster galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.3546 [arXiv:2302.05360]. It hosts a large, extended molecular gas reservoir with a low gas concentration and a high gas-to-stellar mass ratio [arXiv:2302.05360].

sources.summary: KiDS 1433 is a z=0.3546 bulge-dominated BCG with double-horn CO(1-0)/(3-2), a large extended low-concentration molecular reservoir, high gas fraction, and compact-group association.

quotes.quote: We found clear double-horn CO(1 0) and CO(3 2) emission for the KiDS 1433 BCG, yielding a large molecular gas reservoir with M_ H_2 =(5.9 1.2) 10^ 10 M_ and a high gas-to-stellar mass ratio M_ H_2 /M_ =(0.32^ +0.12 _ 0.10 ) . The double-horn emission for the KiDS 1433 BCG implies a low gas concentration, while a modeling of the spectra yields an extended molecular gas reservoir, with a characteristic radius of (5-7) kpc, which is reminiscent of the mature extended-disk phase that is observed in some local BCGs. KiDS 1433 is bulge dominated, while KiDS 0837 and KiDS 1415 appear to be have a more disturbed morphology and clumpy substructures. KiDS 1433 BCG matches the compact group SDSSCGB 22511 McConnachie2009 , as the two have a projected separation of just 3.9 arcsec (i.e., 19 kpc).

We clearly detected double-horn CO(1 0) and CO(3 2) emission lines for one of our targets, that is, the KiDS 1433 BCG ( z=0.3546 ). Our observations and fits of the CO lines imply a large molecular gas reservoir of M_ H_2 =(5.9 1.2) 10^ 10 M_ and a high gas-to-stellar mass ratio M_ H_2 /M_ =(0.32^ +0.12 _ 0.10 ) . The double-horn emission for KiDS 1433 implies a low gas concentration, while a modeling of the spectra yields an extended molecular gas reservoir, with a characteristic radius of (5-7) kpc (Sect sec:double_horn_modeling ), which is reminiscent of the mature extended-disk phase observed in some local BCGs Olivares2022 , as well as in the star-forming KiDS 1220 BCG ( z=0.3886 , C22a). These results suggest that extended molecular gas reservoirs may be common in intermediate- z and star-forming BCGs.

gmw_00002689.name: KiDS 1220
gmw_00002689.overview: KiDS 1220 is a star-forming brightest cluster galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.3886 [arXiv:2207.12073, arXiv:2302.05360]. Its carbon monoxide emission displays a double-horn profile, which indicates an extended, low-concentration molecular gas reservoir [arXiv:2207.12073, arXiv:2302.05360].

sources.summary: The paper identifies KiDS 1220/G172934 as a z=0.3886 BCG whose CO(3-2) spectrum shows a double-horn profile, interpreted as low-concentration molecular gas originating mainly in the BCG.

quotes.quote: The three intermediate- z BCGs of this work are KiDS 0920 (i.e., G198598 at z=0.3216 ), KiDS 1220 (G172934 at z=0.3886 ), and KiDS 1444 (G363753 at z=0.4417 ).

We used instead a combination of two Gaussian curves to fit the CO(3 2) line of KiDS 1220 , as the spectrum shows a clear double-horn profile. Hints of a double-peaked line are also present in the CO(1 0) and CO(2 1) spectra. We believe that the observed double-peaked CO emission is originated from the KiDS 1220 BCG only and we further discuss the underlying structural parameters in Sect. sec:double_horn_modeling .

Structural parameters We now specifically investigate the structural properties of KiDS 1220. As discussed in Sect. sec:IRAM30m_results this BCG shows evidence for a double-horn profile in the CO(3 2) emission. This is indicative of a low concentration of the molecular gas toward the center of the galaxy, which results in the observed flux dimming at small velocities in Fig. fig:BCG_spectra .

sources.summary: KiDS 1220 is compared with KiDS 1433 as a z=0.39 star-forming BCG with a double-horn profile, extended low-concentration gas, and similar stellar/gas properties.

quotes.quote: Interestingly, the star-forming KiDS 1220 BCG at z=0.39 shows a similar double-horn profile. Recently, C22a modeled the spectrum with an extended ( d=9 kpc) and low-concentration molecular gas reservoir, similarly to the KiDS 1433 BCG. The two KiDS 1220 and KiDS 1433 BCGs also have similar stellar masses, M_ (2-3) 10^ 11 M_ , gas-to-stellar mass ratios M_ H_2 /M_ (0.3-0.5) , and effective radii r_ eff 7 kpc.

gmw_00002690.name: PKS 1404-267
gmw_00002690.overview: PKS 1404-267 is the brightest group galaxy in the Abell 3581 cluster [arXiv:1305.0050, arXiv:astro-ph/0410154]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus with an X-ray point source [arXiv:astro-ph/0410154], multiple radio-mode outbursts [arXiv:1305.0050], and dust lanes running north and south through the nucleus [arXiv:1305.0050].

sources.summary: The paper studies IC 4374 as Abell 3581’s brightest group galaxy, linking AGN outbursts, radio bubbles, dust lanes, ionized filaments, molecular gas, and weak star formation.

quotes.quote: This system hosts the most luminous cool core of any nearby group and exhibits active radio mode feedback from the super-massive black hole in its brightest group galaxy, IC 4374. The brightest galaxy has suffered multiple active galactic nucleus outbursts, blowing bubbles into the surrounding hot gas, which have resulted in the uplift of cool ionised gas into the surrounding hot intragroup medium. High velocities, indicative of an outflow, are observed close to the nucleus and coincident with the radio jet. Thin dusty filaments accompany the uplifted, ionised gas.

The BGG IC 4374 in Abell 3581 is one such system where it is clear that the structures in the hot, cool and cold dusty gas are intimately related and are connected with the presence of the radio lobes. IC 4374 exhibits prominent dust lanes running north and south through the galaxy nucleus. Both these dust lanes have extensions to the east which trace the ionised gas filaments (Fig. dust_and_stars ).

sources.summary: The paper treats PKS 1404-267 as Abell 3581's central radio/AGN source, newly associating it with an X-ray point source and little clear ICM heating.

quotes.quote: The central dominant galaxy in Abell 3581 hosts the powerful radio source PKS 1404-267. Since this is a cool cluster with a powerful radio source at its centre, it might be expected that if active galactic nuclei (AGN) are able to heat the ICM in clusters, this source might show the effects more clearly than richer clusters. However, although we will show that the ICM does not have significant amounts of gas below a temperature of kT 0.8 keV, there seems to be no other clear evidence for heating of the ICM by the AGN.

The presence of the radio source PKS 1404-267 at the centre of this cluster has been known since the 1960s, but the image shows, for the first time, that there is an X-ray emitting point source associated with it.
The X-ray point source is located approximately at RA(2000)=14:07:29.8, Dec(2000)= 27 :01:04.

gmw_00002691.name: 3C310
gmw_00002691.overview: The radio galaxy 3C310 is identified as a magnitude 15 elliptical galaxy [arXiv:0903.5408, arXiv:0908.4391] with extended X-ray emission [arXiv:0908.4391, arXiv:astro-ph/9907034]. It features a relaxed double radio structure [arXiv:0903.5408, arXiv:0708.4145] containing old, steep-spectrum lobes [arXiv:0708.4145] with complex filaments and bubbles [arXiv:0903.5408, arXiv:0908.4391].

sources.summary: The paper identifies 3C310 with an elliptical galaxy and analyzes its complex low-brightness lobes, core, and steep-spectrum two-sided parsec-scale structure.

quotes.quote: 1502+26---3C310 It is identified with a 15 mag elliptical galaxy at z = 0.0538. In optical images this galaxy is flattened east-west, on an almost perpendicular direction to the radio jet axis. In the radio band at low resolution it appears as a relaxed double ( ) with a steep radio spectrum ( ^ 26 _ 750 = 0.9; ^ 0.75 _ 10.7 = 1.4).

Low brightness lobes show a complex structure with filaments and bubbles. There is an unresolved radio core with a faint extension from the core to the North, extended 5'', possibly identified as a faint jet ( ).

The spectral index distribution confirms the identification of the core with the faint emission at the center of the pc scale structure which shows a symmetric two-sided extension with a very steep spectral index. The low resolution images suggest that after a few mas there is large change in the source PA from E-W to N-S in agreement with the kpc scale. This peculiar morpholoy could be related to the interaction with a rotating disk as in 3C293 or to the presence of a double nucleus.

sources.summary: The paper presents 3C310 radio imaging, describing a relaxed double source with old steep-spectrum lobes, a flattened optical galaxy, and a peculiar pc-scale structure lacking a dominant core.

quotes.quote: Here we present the radio image at kpc and pc resolution of 3C310.

At kpc scale 3C310 is an extended relaxed double source with a bright core and a short jet in N direction (see Fig. 4). The lobes show a filamentary structure, moreover total flux density measures from the NED database show that 3C310 has a steep radio spectrum ( > 1). These two evidences suggest that the radio lobes are old, but a detailed study is not available in literature.

As noted by for the optical image, this galaxy is flattened east-west, on an almost perpendicular direction to the kpc radio jet axis. VLBI images (see also ) show an extended structure elongated perpendicularly to the kpc scale radio structure and to the short jet visible in the VLA image (see Fig. 5). The pc scale structure is very peculiar since a dominant core structure is not visible even at 5 GHz.

sources.summary: The paper identifies 1502+26 with an elliptical galaxy and describes complex lobes, a weak possible jet, and a peculiar parsec-scale structure nearly perpendicular to the kiloparsec emission.

quotes.quote: 1502+26 ( 3C 310 ) This source is identified with a magnitude 15 elliptical galaxy at z = 0.0538 having clearly extended X-ray emission, first detected with the Einstein satellite by . In the radio band at low resolution, it appears as a relaxed double with a steep radio spectrum ( ^ 26 _ 750 = 0.9; ^ 0.75 _ 10.7 = 1.4). The low brightness lobes exhibit a complex structure with filaments and bubbles. There is an unresolved radio core with a faint extension from the core to the North, extended 5 , possibly identified as a weak jet ( ). The parsec scale images are complex.

sources.summary: The relaxed double radio galaxy 3C 310 has clear extended X-ray emission centered on the radio galaxy and requires a point-source contribution in model fits.

quotes.quote: 3C 310 This relaxed double radio galaxy (van Breugel Fomalont 1984) shows clear extended X-ray emission, first detected with Einstein by Burns, Gregory Holman (1981). The HRI image shows the X-ray emission to be centred on the radio galaxy, with some evidence for east-west extension in the very central regions; because some other sources in the field seem to be extended in a similar direction, we are inclined to treat this as an aspect-induced artefact. When we characterise the emission with a model and point source (Table profile ), is not well constrained but large core radii (between 40 arcsec for = 0.35 and 140 arcsec for =0.9 ) provide the best fit to the data. No model without a point-source contribution is a good fit.

sources.summary: B2 1502+26/3C 310 is singled out for unusually faint 2MASS data caused by point-source catalog treatment, with WISE agreeing with 2MASS.

quotes.quote: one object, B2 1502+26 (3C 310) is quite different, the 2MASS value being as much as two magnitudes fainter than expected from the model fit. In this case, the reason is very simple: 3C 310 is only present in the 2MASS point source catalogue and not in the extended catalogue. Therefore the 2MASS magnitudes were determined using a point source model. Moreover, the WISE data are based on a point source fit in determining the magnitudes, and they therefore agree well with 2MASS;

sources.summary: The paper links a FIRST ring and point source to 3C 310's lobe and core, describing bubble-like edge-brightened rings from weak jets and its FD/FRI-II classification.

quotes.quote: The ring (FCG J150458.8+255940) and nearby point-like source (FIRST 150457.107+260058.30) in the image of FCG J150458.8+255940 (Group 524877) in Figure typical_ring was found coincident with the lobe and core of 3C 310. Van Breugel Fomalont (1984) suggest the rings of this system are edge-brightened boundaries of bubbles of hot gas blown up by weak jets and are surrounded by magnetic fields, while the filamentary structures are remnants of burst bubbles. The system 3C 310 was classified as FD (fat double) and FRI/II by Owen89 .

gmw_00002692.name: NGC 7599
gmw_00002692.overview: NGC 7599 is a star-forming galaxy that exhibits faint radio emission peaking in its western outer disk and spiral arm [arXiv:2104.06236, arXiv:1310.7589]. This emission hotspot lies toward an HI bridge, indicating that NGC 7599 is interacting with NGC 7590 [arXiv:2104.06236].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7599 as a normal star-forming galaxy with anomalous arm radio emission, young UV-bright stars, and a hotspot linked to interaction with NGC 7590.

quotes.quote: NGC 7599: We observe that the emission at both 325 and 610 MHz peaks in one of the spiral arms of the galaxy. This region outshines the rest of the galaxy as not much emission is seen from the other regions in the galaxy. This may indicate some intense star-formation activity happening in that spiral arm of the galaxy. Alternately, it is possible that this is an effect of superposition of emission from an alternate radio source in the sky.

But this hotspot also lies towards the HI bridge linking NGC 7599 and NGC 7590 as seen by . This implies that the two galaxies are interacting and argues against the chance-superposition hypothesis. We also see a high UV flux in the region, which is indicative of young star-formation (see Section UVem ).

sources.summary: The paper detects faint 610 MHz emission from NGC 7599, with peaks in its western outer disk and spiral arm, a measured spectral index, and no polarisation.

quotes.quote: The emission from NGC 7599 is faint, and only the peaks in emission are detected. The peak brightness in NGC 7599 is located towards the west of the outer disk, and arises from a region associated with the spiral arm which emerges in the south and then turns towards the north.
For the brightest emission, _ 610 ^ 1425 was found to be 0.62 0.05 in NGC 7552, 0.62 0.05 in NGC 7582, 0.71 0.24 in NGC 7590, and 0.97 0.35 in NGC 7599.
NGC 7590 and NGC 7599 were undetected in polarisation to 3 upper limits of <0.9 and <1.4 respectively.

gmw_00002693.name: ESO 0353--G036
gmw_00002693.overview: The galaxy ESO 0353--G036 is detected in total intensity with a peak radio brightness of 15.4 mJy/beam [arXiv:1310.7589]. Its radio emission is offset eastward from the near-infrared disk, extending beyond the visible disk, and shows no detected polarization [arXiv:1310.7589].

sources.summary: The paper detects ESO 0353--G036 in total intensity, finding radio emission offset eastward from the near-infrared disk and no detected polarisation.

quotes.quote: The galaxy ESO 0353--G036 was detected and found to have a peak brightness of 15.4 mJy beam ^ 1 (as shown in Fig. SCG1-galaxy ). The peak of radio emission from ESO 0353--G036 appears to be offset from the emission in the near-IR, with the offset directed towards IRAS F01415--3433 and ESO 0353--G039 (towards the east). The offset emission is also extended by two synthesised beams towards the east, and reaches beyond the extent of the disk that is visible at near-IR wavelengths. Further higher resolution observations will be required to identify the cause of this emission.
None of the group galaxies were detected in polarisation. No polarisation was detected from ESO 0353--G036 to a 3 upper limit of <0.4 .

gmw_00002694.name: J2317.9 4213
gmw_00002694.overview: J2317.9 4213 is a Fanaroff-Riley class I radio galaxy that contains an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:1310.7589]. The galaxy features a curved eastern jet and a western jet, displaying asymmetric polarization likely caused by the Laing-Garrington effect [arXiv:1310.7589].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J2317.9 4213 as a background FR-I radio galaxy with AGN-linked X-rays, bent jets and lobes, steep spectra, and asymmetric polarisation.

quotes.quote: J2317.9 4213 The complex radio galaxy J2317.9 4213 is located 1 arcmin to the west of NGC 7590 at 23^ h 17^ m 55 7 , 42^ 13' 29'' . It has possibly been detected as an X-ray source, indicating the presence of an AGN 1980BAAS...12..804E .

It is well-resolved, with the two jets/lobes projecting along an axis that is approximately aligned east--west, as shown in Fig. AGN and AGNpol . The spectrum, _ 610 ^ 1425 , was found to be 0.43 0.15 at the location of the AGN, and 0.84 0.26 and 0.56 0.12 in the brightest emission from the east and west jet respectively. The jet to the east curves away to the north relative to the approximate east--west axis as seen in projection.

The general morphology, in combination with the presence of low brightness regions towards the periphery is indicative of the FR-I class. The 610 MHz polarisation is brighter in the western jet and the contrasting lack of polarised emission to the east is likely a consequence of the Laing--Garrington effect e.g. 1988Natur.331..147G .

gmw_00002695.name: MCG 07 47 031
gmw_00002695.overview: MCG 07 47 031 is a blazar or BL Lacertae object located approximately 228 Mpc away [arXiv:1310.7589]. This active galactic nucleus features a flat-spectrum, linearly polarized nucleus and exhibits two faint, jet-like extensions [arXiv:1310.7589].

sources.summary: The paper identifies MCG 07 47 031 as a blazar or BL Lac with a near-infrared counterpart, flat-spectrum polarised nucleus, and faint jet-like extensions.

quotes.quote: The blazar or BL Lac, MCG 07 47 031, is located 10 arcmin to the north of galaxy NGC 7599 at 23^ h 19^ m 5 8 , 42^ 06' 49'' .
This bright AGN appears to have a near-IR counterpart in the DSS images, which is located at z=0.055 , or 228 Mpc away.

At the faintest levels in the images, there is a 3 detection of two jet-like features leading from the AGN -- one directly north, and another to the east. There is also some indication of a bridge of emission connecting the two jets. The nucleus was found to have a flat spectrum typical of a blazar of _ 610 ^ 843 =0.29 0.25 , and is detected in linear polarisation at this epoch with a fraction of 7.03 0.11 .

gmw_00002696.name: PKS 2316 429
gmw_00002696.overview: PKS 2316 429 is an X-shaped radio galaxy classified as a Fanaroff-Riley type II source [arXiv:1310.7589]. Its radio morphology features an unresolved central core, bright outer hotspots, and active lobes and wings that exhibit a non-thermal spectrum [arXiv:1310.7589].

sources.summary: The paper classifies PKS 2316 429 as an FR-II, X-shaped radio galaxy with an unresolved core, bright hotspots, nonthermal spectrum, and active lobes or wings.

quotes.quote: PKS 2316 429 This previously unclassified radio source is located 25 arcmin south of galaxy NGC 7599 at 23^ h 19^ m 15 9 , 42^ 37' 53'' . The radio morphology has a central unresolved core, with two bright hotspots to the northwest and southeast periphery. Diffuse emission extending along the east--west is also detected, giving the source an X-shaped morphology. The Stokes I , near-IR data, and polarised intensity at 610 MHz are shown in Fig. SE-SCG2 .
The PKS 2316 429 radio source is also detected in the SUMSS, and comparison with the 610 MHz data shows the source to have a non-thermal spectrum with _ 610 ^ 843 =0.57 0.13 .

The non-thermal spectrum, polarisation properties, and radio morphology -- particularly the bright outer hotspots -- is typical of an FR-II type, X-shaped radio galaxy with both a set of active lobes and wings.

gmw_00002697.name: HPC1001.b
gmw_00002697.overview: HPC1001.b is a massive galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 3.7 [arXiv:2209.05895]. It is a candidate quenching galaxy with a low specific star formation rate, depleted dust and gas fractions, and an estimated gas depletion timescale of 200 million years [arXiv:2209.05895].

sources.summary: The paper highlights HPC1001.b as the structure’s massive, low-sSFR galaxy with depleted dust and gas fractions, making it a candidate quenching galaxy at z about 3.7.

quotes.quote: Moving the focus to individual sources, we find that HPC1001.b has a specific star formation rate (sSFR) that places the source 3.0 1.0 below the main sequence at the corresponding redshift (Fig. halo middle). At the same time, as shown in Fig. halo right, its dust to stellar mass ratio f_ dust is lower by a factor of 4-7 with respect to that of main sequence galaxies at z=3.7 Tacconi2020,Kokorev2021 and comparable to that of z 1.5 quiescent galaxies Gobat2018,Magdis2021,Caliendo2021 . Assuming M_ gas =100 M_ dust and accounting for a 20 uncertainty due to unknown metallicity Jin2019alma,Jin2022 , HPC1001.b would have an inferred gas fraction of M_ gas /M_ * =0.2 0.1 and a gas depletion time of 200 Myr.
On the other hand, the remarkably low gas and dust fractions of HPC1001.b suggest that this galaxy is more evolved compared to normal star-forming galaxies at the same epoch Gomez-Guijarro2019 .

A candidate for a galaxy in quenching HPC1001.b is the most massive galaxy in the structure, with a stellar mass comparable to that of the quiescent cluster members at z 3 Kubo2021quiescent,Kalita2021quiescent . Assuming no gas inflow, HPC1001.b would deplete its molecular gas reservoir in 200 Myr and become quiescent at z>3.2 with M_ * 1.2 10^ 11 M_ , forming the first generation of massive quiescent galaxies in dense environments.

gmw_00002698.name: B3 0727+409
gmw_00002698.overview: B3 0727+409 is a distant, radio-loud quasar located at a redshift of 2.5 [arXiv:1404.0248, arXiv:1706.04895]. It hosts a supermassive black hole [arXiv:1404.0248] and features an extended X-ray jet with a radio-bright core and knot, but it lacks a diffuse radio counterpart [arXiv:1610.01580, arXiv:2105.03467].

sources.summary: The paper treats B3 0727+409 as a distant radio-loud quasar projected behind Abell 585; its black-hole mass, luminosity, and VLBA nuclear jet are considered, while extended lobes are judged unlikely to connect to it.

quotes.quote: In this paper we analyze the peculiar radio structure observed across the central region of the galaxy cluster Abell 585 ( z=0.12 ). In the low-resolution radio maps, this structure appears uniform and diffuse on angular scales of 3 , and is seemingly related to the distant ( z=2.5 ) radio quasar B3 0727+409 rather than to the cluster itself. However, after a careful investigation of the unpublished archival radio data with better angular resolution, we resolve the structure into two distinct arcmin-scale features, which resemble typical lobes of cluster radio galaxies with no obvious connection to the background quasar. We support this conclusion by examining the spectral and polarization properties of the features, demonstrating in addition that the analyzed structure can hardly be associated with any sort of a radio mini-halo or relics of the cluster.

B3 0727+409 is a luminous radio source with the 1400 MHz power of P_ 1400 MHz =6.3 10^ 28 W Hz ^ 1 . Its SDSS spectrum reduced through the standard procedure (see k1 ) allows us to estimate the mass of the central black hole M_ BH = (3.33 1.70) 10^ 8 M_ when using the MgII line, and (2.26 0.34) 10^ 8 M_ when using the CIV line. The resulting value is characteristic for radio-loud quasars at high redshifts (e.g., w2,k1 ). Also the bolometric luminosity of the accreting matter in B3 0727+409 estimated from the MgII line is typical for quasars in general, namely L_d 1.5 10^ 45 erg s ^ 1 , implying high accretion rate m _ acc _d^ 1 (L_d/L_ Edd ) 0.4 (in the Eddington units) for the standard _d 10 radiative efficiency of the accretion disk. As mentioned previously, B3 0727+409 is a VLA phase calibrator, but it was also a VLBA phase calibrator. The existing VLBA maps show one-sided nuclear jet oriented toward the extended structure under investigation.

sources.summary: The paper presents B3 0727+490 as a prototype radio-silent extended quasar jet: X-ray bright, radio faint, high redshift, and well described by cooled EC/CMB emission.

quotes.quote: B3 0727+490 This is perhaps the most interesting extended jet discovered so far, as its existence goes against the common paradigm that these objects emit strongly in the radio band. The quasar core and a small knot near it are detected in both radio and X--rays, but the large scale X--ray jet, detected up to a projected distance of 80 \ kpc from the core, lacks a radio counterpart. Both the inner radio--bright knot and the extended jet are resolved by Chandra . Simionescu et al. (2016), using a standard EC/CMB model, estimate the jet bulk Lorentz factor ( 10 ), viewing angle ( 7^ ), and deprojected size for the entire jet ( L 600 kpc ). Such estimates are consistent with those obtained for lower redshift sources.

sources.summary: The paper models B3 0727+409 as a high-redshift source whose extended-jet X-ray emission would require proton synchrotron power far above its Eddington luminosity.

quotes.quote: Assuming a simple power law proton spectrum of spectral index 2 in the energy range of 10^ 15 - 10^ 18 eV we have calculated the photon fluxes for the extended jets of 3C 273, PKS 0637-752 and B3 0727+409 to explain the X-ray data with the proton synchrotron model. The emission region is assumed to be of radius 1 kpc and its Doppler factor is fixed at 1 for simplicity.

B3 0727+409 is located at a redshift of 2.5 having a black hole mass of 3.3 10^8 solar mass ( ), Eddington's luminosity 4 10^ 46 ergs/sec. In Figure-7 the butterfly shows the possible X-ray emission from the extended jet of this source. In this case the jet power required to explain the X-ray emission with proton synchrotron model is 7.1 10^ 48 ergs/sec (corresponding 10 mG), which exceeds the Eddington's luminsoity of this source.

sources.summary: The paper treats B3 0727+409 as an X-ray-discovered radio system with a core, knot, diffuse lobe, and questionable terminal hot spot lacking corresponding radio emission.

quotes.quote: B3 0727+ 409 Simionescu2016 discovered this \ in the X-rays, as it has no diffuse radio counterpart (it does have a radio core and knot in the same direction as the extended X-rays). The X-ray structure is consistent with a jet and a diffuse lobe, so we define the lobe as the region outside of the inner jet. There is an X-ray bright region at the end of the lobe that we identify as a hot spot, but with no radio emission this classification is questionable. In the following analysis, we treat the region as a hot spot, but if we instead treat the entire system as a lobe it does not alter our conclusions.

gmw_00002699.name: NGC 1172
gmw_00002699.overview: NGC 1172 is an intermediate-mass lenticular galaxy located in a low-density environment [arXiv:2009.11375]. It hosts a bimodal globular cluster system with a peculiarly high specific frequency, suggesting the galaxy accreted a large fraction of its clusters from low-mass satellites in the past [arXiv:2009.11375].

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 1172 has a narrow but likely bimodal globular-cluster system and an unusually high specific frequency, suggesting substantial past accretion from low-mass satellites.

quotes.quote: We present a wide-field study of the globular cluster system (GCS) of the field lenticular galaxy NGC 1172, based on observations from GMOS/Gemini (optical), FourStar/Magellan (NIR), and archival data from ACS/ HST (optical). This analysis covers the full extension of the GCS, and results in a value of specific frequency ( S_ N =8.6 1.5 ) peculiarly high for an intermediate-mass galaxy in a low-density environment such as this one. We find that the GCS appears to be bimodal, although the colour distribution is narrow and does not allow for an accurate separation of the subpopulations. However, the combination of optical and NIR filters allows us to obtain an estimation of the metallicity distribution based on the photometry, which supports bimodality. We conclude that the presence of a large fraction of metal-poor globular clusters (GCs) and the high specific frequency point to NGC 1172 having accreted a significant amount of GCs from low-mass satellites in the past.

For NGC 1172 we obtain a value of S_ N =8.6 1.5 , which is in agreement with the previous result of S_ N =9.18 4.41 , presented by . To illustrate the peculiarity of this result, we show in the top panel of Figure fig:sntn the specific frequency of a sample of galaxies versus their absolute visual magnitude from . It is easy to see NGC 1172 is placed on a fairly empty section of the plot, with a specific frequency only comparable with much brighter galaxies.

gmw_00002700.name: PSO J127.0558+26.5654
gmw_00002700.overview: PSO J127.0558+26.5654, also known as J0828+2633, is a high-redshift quasar [arXiv:2212.04452]. Template fitting of the quasar indicates a redshift of z=6.14, which differs from the previously reported literature value of z=6.05 [arXiv:2212.04452].

sources.summary: The paper identifies PSO J127.0558+26.5654 as a z=6.14 quasar also known as J0828+2633 and reports that its template redshift differs from the literature value.

quotes.quote: PSO J127.0558+26.5654 ( z=6.14 ) This quasar is also known as J0828+2633 (Warren et al. \ in prep.). It has been reported and studied in other published work (e.g., mortlock2012,ross2020 ) and an actual optical spectrum was first shown in . Our redshift estimate derived from template fitting ( z=6.14 ) is significantly different to the redshift used in the literature of z=6.05 , which is only possible if the emission was completely absorbed. For completeness, the redshift derived using our weak- template is z=6.08 .

gmw_00002701.name: PSO J148.4829+69.1812
gmw_00002701.overview: PSO J148.4829+69.1812 is a high-redshift quasar located at a redshift of z=5.84 [arXiv:2212.04452]. Situated in the outskirts of the galaxy M81, it exhibits line emission that is either weak or heavily absorbed [arXiv:2212.04452].

sources.summary: The paper describes PSO J148.4829+69.1812 as a z=5.84 quasar in M81's outskirts, with weak or absorbed line emission and future SED and environment work planned.

quotes.quote: PSO J148.4829+69.1812 ( z=5.84 ) This quasar is located in the outskirts of M81, and therefore myriad multi-wavelength data in the field already exist. Furthermore, its location is suitable for parallel observations with JWST covering both the quasar field and M81. The line is weak or heavily absorbed. The detailed spectral energy distribution and environment studies of this source will be presented in a separate paper.

gmw_00002702.name: PSO J158.6937--14.4210
gmw_00002702.overview: PSO J158.6937--14.4210 is a young quasar at a redshift of approximately 6.07 [arXiv:1803.01424, arXiv:2212.04452] with a high Eddington ratio [arXiv:2305.00998, arXiv:2002.01811]. The system exhibits strong dust continuum emission [arXiv:2305.00998, arXiv:2002.01811] and a high star formation rate of about 1420 solar masses per year [arXiv:2002.01811].

sources.summary: The paper highlights PSOJ158-14 as a z=6.0687 quasar with a very small proximity zone and possible damping-wing absorption, though the IGM interpretation remains uncertain.

quotes.quote: The quasar PSOJ158-14 is at a redshift of 6.0687 with a magnitude of 27.32 . The proximity zone size of this quasar is 1.95 pMpc. 2020ApJ...900...37E have investigated this quasar and reported that it has a large star formation rate ( 1420 yr ^ 1 ), large bolometric luminosity ( 10^ 47  erg  s ^ 1 ), high Eddington ratio ( _ edd 1), and shows signs of strong internal motions within the broad line region. They also point out the dust continuum emission of this quasar is very strong ( F_ cont 3.46 mJy).

We see that the spectrum blueward of the \ line resembles a damping wing. Additionally, the flux redward of the \ line shows attenuation, as one would expect in the presence of a damping wing. The flux continues to remain attenuated till 1233   . Interestingly, there is no evidence of a compact high-column-density absorber ahead of the quasar.

sources.summary: The paper reports VST-ATLAS J158.6938-14.4211 as a z>6 quasar with z_AB=19.4, confirmed by Keck LRIS, whose spectrum shows strong Ly emission, a Gunn-Peterson trough, and other emission lines.

quotes.quote: We have found two further z>6 quasars, VST-ATLAS J158.6938-14.4211 at z=6.07 and J332.8017-32.1036 at z=6.32 with magnitudes of z_ AB =19.4 and 19.7 mag respectively. J158.6938-14.4211 was confirmed by Keck LRIS observations and J332.8017-32.1036 was confirmed by ESO NTT EFOSC-2 observations.

The LRIS spectrum showed strong Ly emission, cut off sharply towards blue wavelengths due to a strong Gunn-Peterson hydrogen absorption trough, confirming this as a quasar with an approximate redshift of z=6.05 0.03 (but see Section sect:xshooter and Table table:xshooter ). Also detected in emission are Ly , NV(1240 ), OI(1304 ) and SiIV(1398 ). Other details of this quasar, including i , z , W1 and W2 magnitudes are given in Table table:table1 .

sources.summary: PSO J158--14 is a young quasar with a small proximity zone, strong dust continuum, high star formation, high Eddington ratio, and blueshifted emission line.

quotes.quote: PSO J158--14 This quasar, discovered both by Chehade2018 and Ba nados et al. in prep., shows a small proximity zone of R_p=1.91 0.14 pMpc ( R_ p, corr =1.63 0.12 pMpc). Interestingly, the quasar has a very strong dust continuum emission, i.e. F_ cont =3.46 0.02 mJy . The derived star formation rate of approximately 1420 M_ yr^ 1 suggests the presence of a coeval starburst with the SMBH growth. Furthermore, we estimate a large bolometric luminosity of L_ bol / erg s^ 1 = 47.31 and a high Eddington ratio of _ Edd =1.01 0.08 . The \ emission line is highly blueshifted with respect to the systemic redshift of the quasar, i.e. v=-673 49 km s^ 1 , suggesting strong internal motions within the BLR.

sources.summary: The paper notes PSO J158.6937--14.4210 is an independently discovered z=6.0685 young-quasar candidate and uses a median template at the host-galaxy line redshift.

quotes.quote: PSO J158.6937--14.4210 ( z=6.0685 ) This quasar was independently discovered by and it has appeared in a number of other articles (e.g., eilers2020,schindler2020 ). measure a very small proximity zone and classify this source as a ``young quasar''. Our best-fitting template is median- yielding z=6.06 , in good agreement with z=6.0685 0.0001 as measured from the \ line from the host galaxy eilers2020 . To measure the rest-frame magnitudes we use the median- template at the \ redshift.

gmw_00002703.name: PSO J170.8326+20.2082
gmw_00002703.overview: PSO J170.8326+20.2082 is a high-redshift quasar candidate at z ~ 6.41 [arXiv:2212.04452]. It exhibits a sharp flux break and a blue excess that suggest it may be a gravitationally lensed system with a foreground source [arXiv:2212.04452].

sources.summary: The paper flags PSO J170.8326+20.2082 as a peculiar z=6.41 quasar whose sharp break, blue excess, and possible foreground source make it a likely gravitational lens candidate.

quotes.quote: There is an extremely sharp break in flux at 8992 , consistent with a z 6.4 quasar. Such a high redshift is at odds with its blue <0 color (see Section sec:ps1_selection ). An excess of flux blueward of the break explains the color. This excess declines smoothly and is inconsistent with high transmission in the \ forest.

The spectrum shows no clear emission lines, and the most plausible explanation we could find is that this source could be a lensed quasar. In this scenario, the flux blueward of the break corresponds to a foreground source. If confirmed, this would make it only the second gravitationally lensed quasar known at z>5 see fan2019,andika2022 .

gmw_00002704.name: VIK J1152+0055
gmw_00002704.overview: VIK J1152+0055 is a high-redshift quasar located at a redshift of z=6.3643 [arXiv:2212.04452]. The system has been studied for its cold dust properties and hosts a supermassive black hole with a measured mass of 6.3 billion solar masses [arXiv:2212.04452].

sources.summary: The paper reports VIK J1152+0055 as an independently discovered z=6.3643 quasar with ALMA cold-dust studies and a measured black-hole mass from its emission line.

quotes.quote: VIK J1152+0055 ( z=6.3643 ) This quasar was independently discovered by and it has been widely studied since then. and studied the \ and cold dust properties with ALMA while measured the black hole mass from the \ emission line ( M_ BH =6.3 10^8 M_ ). Our best-fitting template is vandenberk2001 , yielding z=6.36 in agreement with the more accurate redshift z=6.3643 from .

gmw_00002705.name: PSO J265.9298+41.4139
gmw_00002705.overview: PSO J265.9298+41.4139 is a broad-absorption line quasar located at a redshift of approximately 6.0263 [arXiv:2212.04452]. The quasar resides in a strong starburst host galaxy with a star-formation rate of approximately 1470 solar masses per year [arXiv:2212.04452, arXiv:2002.01811].

sources.summary: The paper classifies PSO J265.9298+41.4139 as a BAL quasar whose host is a strong starburst, and explains its very red color by BAL absorption in the filter.

quotes.quote: PSO J265.9298+41.4139 ( z=6.0263 ) P265+41 is a BAL quasar. Our best-fit template is the median- , yielding z=6.05 , and the second best fit is z=6.04 with the yang2021 template. However, if we fix the redshift derived from the \ observations ( z_ =6.0263 0.0001 , eilers2020 ), the best-fit template is yang2021 and that is what we used to derive the rest-frame magnitudes. P265+41 is the brightest quasar in \ and dust continuum emission studied by , indicating that the quasar resides in a starburst galaxy with a star-formation rate SFR 1470 M_ yr ^ 1 .
We note that P265+41 has one of the reddest J colors. This is because the strong \ BAL falls exactly in the \ filter.

sources.summary: PSO J265+41 is a BAL quasar excluded from proximity-zone analysis; its bright line and dust continuum suggest a highly star-forming host galaxy.

quotes.quote: PSO J265+41 This object has been discovered by Ba nados et al. in prep. It is a BAL quasar, and has thus been eliminated from our analysis about proximity zones, since its absorption features might contaminate the proximity zone. This quasar shows a very bright \ line ( F_ line = 9.20 0.50 Jy km s^ 1 ), as well as a bright dust continuum emission ( F_ cont = 3.61 0.07 ), suggesting a highly star-forming ( SFR 1470 M_ yr^ 1 ) quasar host galaxy.

gmw_00002706.name: VIK J2211--3206
gmw_00002706.overview: VIK J2211--3206 is a high-redshift quasar [arXiv:1803.01424, arXiv:2212.04452] located at a redshift of approximately z = 6.3394 [arXiv:2212.04452]. The system exhibits strong broad-absorption line features [arXiv:2212.04452] and contains detectable dust and gas within its host galaxy [arXiv:2212.04452].

sources.summary: The paper reports VST-ATLAS J332.8017-32.1036 as a z>6 quasar with z_AB=19.7, confirmed by ESO NTT/EFOSC-2; its spectrum shows Ly/Gunn-Peterson features plus possible NV and Ly emission.

quotes.quote: We have found two further z>6 quasars, VST-ATLAS J158.6938-14.4211 at z=6.07 and J332.8017-32.1036 at z=6.32 with magnitudes of z_ AB =19.4 and 19.7 mag respectively. J158.6938-14.4211 was confirmed by Keck LRIS observations and J332.8017-32.1036 was confirmed by ESO NTT EFOSC-2 observations.

As with VST-ATLAS J158.6938-14.4211, strong Ly emission was seen redwards of a Gunn-Peterson absorption trough, again confirming that this object was correctly identifed as a high redshift quasar with approximate redshift of z=6.37 0.03 (but see Section sect:xshooter and Table table:xshooter ). The NV (1240 ) emission line might also be detected at 9140 . There is also a hint of Ly emission at 6520 . Further discussion of these spectral features is postponed to Section sect:xshooter , in which we report higher resolution X-shooter spectroscopy.

sources.summary: The paper calls VIK J2211--3206 the brightest quasar in the sample, notes strong BAL features, and cites host-galaxy line, dust, and HST environment measurements.

quotes.quote: VIK J2211--3206 ( z=6.3394 ) This is the brightest quasar of the sample ( M_ 1450 =-28 ) and it has already been part of a number of studies even before its discovery publication. J2211--3206 shows very strong BAL features studied by . The \ and dust from the host galaxy was reported by and HST imaging of the quasar and its environment was presented in . Our template redshift fails at finding a good solution for a quasar with such strong BAL absorption. report a redshift z=6.330 while report a redshift z=6.3394 .

sources.summary: The appendix says telluric residuals and a strong blue-ward absorption feature complicated this quasar spectrum, requiring residual masking and single-Gaussian line fitting.

quotes.quote: VIK J2211--3206 In this spectrum the line falls into the wavelength range of 11000-11600 , where telluric absorption features could not be fully corrected. We exclude the worst residuals from the line fit and approximate the line using only a single Gaussian component. We also note that a strong absorption feature blue-ward of the line complicates the modelling. Hence, we have excluded part of this region from the fit for the line.

gmw_00002707.name: PSO J173.4601+48.2420
gmw_00002707.overview: PSO J173.4601+48.2420 is a radio-loud quasar located at a redshift of z=6.233 [arXiv:2212.04452, arXiv:2309.03936]. It is a candidate blazar that exhibits a flat radio spectrum and potential flux variability [arXiv:2212.04452, arXiv:2309.03936].

sources.summary: The paper presents PSO J173.4601+48.2420 as a radio-selected z=6.233 quasar and blazar candidate, with bright radio emission, possible variability, and a flat radio spectrum.

quotes.quote: PSO J173.4601+48.2420 ( z=6.233 ) This quasar would not have been selected due to its S/N( ) <5 . However, we selected this quasar following the more relaxed selection presented in , which also required a radio counterpart in the Faint Images of the Radio Sky at Twenty cm survey (FIRST; becker1995 ).

P173+48 at z=6.233 is the brightest radio source in the sample. It is detected in all radio surveys that cover the quasar. Its two VLASS epochs differ by more than 2 , which is suggestive of variability. The radio spectral =-0.16 and =-0.41 (VLASS epoch 1) are remarkably flat (the slightly steeper slope using VLASS epoch 2 =-0.69 can be due to variability).

Both high variability and a flat radio spectral index ( 0.5< <0.5 ) are indications of a blazar nature, i.e., a quasar with its relativistic jet pointing at a small angle from our line of sight (e.g., caccianiga2019,ighina2019 ). Monitoring the radio variability, simultaneous constraints on the radio spectral energy distribution, and X-ray observations can help to establish whether this radio-loud quasar is a blazar.

sources.summary: The paper treats J1133+4814 as a flat-spectrum, exceptionally radio-loud quasar and blazar candidate, citing its variability and calling for radio and X-ray confirmation.

quotes.quote: The three flat-spectrum sources we find (J0912+6658, J1037+4033, J1133+4814) could potentially be blazars (like J0309+2717), which have their relativistic jets oriented along the line of sight, making them extremely bright sources e.g. Urry1995PASP..107..803U . Because of their specific jet orientation, blazars can be used to efficiently trace the space density of the quasar population over cosmic time, which makes their identification useful e.g. Ghisellini2010MNRAS.402..497G . Blazars are furthermore known to be highly variable in nature on timescales of hours to days e.g. Ulrich1997ARA&A..35..445U , which could again be problematic for constraining their radio spectra in this work as mentioned in Sect.  sec:results .

Especially J1037+4033 and J1133+4814 are exceptionally radio-loud with R values of 1100 300 and 360 100, respectively (see Gloudemans2022A&A...668A..27G ). J1133+4814 (also called P173+48) has also been identified as a blazar candidate by , who find both a flat spectrum and variability. Follow-up radio monitoring and X-ray observations are necessary to confirm the blazar nature of these quasars.

gmw_00002708.name: PSO J193.3992--02.7820
gmw_00002708.overview: PSO J193.3992--02.7820 is a high-redshift quasar that exhibits extreme radio loudness [arXiv:2212.04452]. It is characterized by an ultra-steep radio spectrum and is considered a possible radio transient [arXiv:2212.04452].

sources.summary: The paper treats PSO J193.3992--02.7820 as a possible radio transient: its RACS and FIRST detections imply an extreme radio-loudness and ultra-steep spectrum, but VLASS is undetected.

quotes.quote: PSO J193.3992--02.7820 -- radio transient? P193--02 has a strong RACS detection, but the relatively weak 3.4 FIRST detection implies an ultra-steep radio spectrum with 4.6 . Taking the radio spectral index at face value would make it the radio-loudest quasar in the sample with R_ 2500 1300 . We note, however, that there is a significant difference between the integrated and peak flux densities ( 4.05 0.07 mJy vs. \ 2.86 0.02 mJy). This discrepancy could mean that the source is extended or that multiple sources contribute to the integrated flux density.

Even if we consider the peak flux density, this results in an ultra-steep spectrum with 3.8 and high radio loudness R_ 2500 800 . The steep radio spectrum is in agreement with the non-detection in VLASS.

gmw_00002709.name: SMG
gmw_00002709.overview: The SMG is a high-redshift starburst galaxy at z=2.674 that is dominated by star formation with no major contribution from active galactic nucleus activity [arXiv:2405.00795]. It is surrounded by a filamentary Lyman-alpha nebula stretching 180 kiloparsecs through its circumgalactic medium [arXiv:2405.00795].

sources.summary: The paper studies a z=2.674 submillimeter galaxy whose metal-poor CGM stream is detected as a 180 kpc filamentary Lyman-alpha nebula, likely tied to cool accretion and star formation.

quotes.quote: Previous high-resolution QSO absorption-line spectroscopy has revealed high \ column density, extremely metal-poor ( 1 solar) gas streams in the circumgalactic medium of the SMG. Here we present deep optical integral-field spectroscopy of the system with the Keck Cosmic Web Imager (KCWI). Reaching a 2 surface brightness (SB) limit 10^ 19 \ with 2 hrs of integration time, we detect a filamentary \ nebula stretching 180 kpc from the SMG intercepting both QSO sightlines. This \ filament may correspond to the same cool gas stream penetrating through the hot halo seen in the absorption.

The SMG studied here is dominated by star formation with no major contribution from AGN activity. If we consider the spectral energy distribution (SED) of the SMG and compare with models for QSOs obscured by a dusty torus, as shown by , the QSO+torus models underpredict the far-IR emission. Star formation sits as the dominate contributor to the bolometric luminosity.

gmw_00002710.name: LAE2
gmw_00002710.overview: LAE2 is an extended Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy located at a redshift of z = 2.6918 [arXiv:2405.00795]. The galaxy's Lyman-alpha emission covers a total area of 20 square arcseconds, and its proximity to a nearby line of sight suggests it is the host galaxy of the C2 absorber [arXiv:2405.00795].

sources.summary: The paper discovers LAE2 as an extended Lyman-alpha emitter at z=2.6918 and argues that its proximity and redshift make it the likely host of the C2 absorber.

quotes.quote: We designate the \ emitter as LAE2 and show its \ surface brightness map in Figure fig:comp_b . The total integrated \ Luminosity within the S/N = 2 contour is 3.6 0.1 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 . We show the integrated spectrum in the right panel of Figure fig:comp_b and fit its profile with a Gaussian profile. The best fit places the redshift of the \ line at z = 2.6918 0.0006

The \ emission is clearly extended, it covers a total area of 20 arcsec ^2 ( 1200 kpc ^ 2 ), and the longest extent of 90 kpc along the EW direction. LAE2 is at v = +1500 \ relative to the SMG, making it unlikely to be part of its CGM. However, there is a DLA near the redshift of LAE2 along the line of sight towards \ (designated as subsystem C in Fu21 ; see Figure fig:emission_absorption top left panel). The DLA has two sub-components (C1 and C2) that correspond to the two CO emission redshifts of . The proximity of LAE2 to \ and its redshift establishes it as the most likely host galaxy of the C2 absorber.

gmw_00002711.overview: NGC 5419 is a high-mass early-type galaxy classified as either an elliptical [arXiv:1607.06466, arXiv:2305.03078] or a lenticular galaxy [arXiv:1502.07024]. It features a large depleted stellar core [arXiv:1607.06466], a double nucleus [arXiv:2305.03078, arXiv:2303.01495], and central counter-rotation [arXiv:1607.06466, arXiv:2303.01495].

sources.summary: The paper re-investigates NGC 5419 with Spitzer imaging, arguing that its profile requires extra components beyond a single Sersic model and illustrating how disks enlarge galaxies.

quotes.quote: Example profile: NGC 5419 While information about the surface brightness profile decompositions can be found in the papers mentioned in Table Tab1 , we felt that it may be instructive to include an example of how the outer exponential profile can increase the galaxy size over the bulge size. We have somewhat randomly chosen NGC 5419 from Laurikainen et al. \ (2010). This was chosen because a) the Laurikainen et al. \ paper simply contains the greatest number of compact massive spheroids in our Table Tab1 , and b) this particular galaxy was modelled by Dullo Graham (2014) as an elliptical galaxy while they noted that a disk might be present. Sandage Tammann (1981) did not support the original elliptical galaxy classification in the Second Reference Catalogue of de Vaucouleurs et al. \ (1976), but instead considered NGC 5419 to be a lenticular galaxy, as did Laurikainen et al. \ (2010) based on their near-infrared K band analysis.

Therefore, we re-investigate this galaxy using a Spitzer Space Telescope 3.6 m image (see Figure Fig1 ), which was reduced following the procedures described in Savorgnan et al. \ (2015, in prep.). The light profile was extracted by allowing the ellipticity and position angle to vary about a fixed center using the IRAF task Ellipse (Jedrzejewski et al. \ 1987) and is shown in Figure Fig2 . Our modelling of the 3.6 m light profile within 60 matches the solution to the K band data in Laurikainen et al. \ (2010). We also find that a single S ersic function is inadequate; the curvature of the light profile, and the residual light profile, reveal the presence of additional components. Figure Fig2 shows how the addition of an outer exponential, and a very faint lens (fit with a Ferrers function), provides a better fit.

sources.summary: High-resolution spectroscopy of 5419 finds a large depleted core, counter-rotating central kinematics, a massive black hole, and evidence that its double nucleus may contain two SMBHs.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage We obtained adaptive-optics assisted \ observations of the central regions of the giant elliptical galaxy 5419 with a spatial resolution of 0.2 arcsec ( 55 pc). 5419 has a large depleted stellar core with a radius of 1.58 arcsec (430 pc). \ and \ images show a point source located at the galaxy's photocentre, which is likely associated with the low-luminosity AGN previously detected in 5419.

While 5419 shows low rotation ( v < 50 ), the central regions (inside 4 r_b ) clearly rotate in the opposite direction to the galaxy's outer parts. We use orbit-based dynamical models to measure the black hole mass of 5419 from the kinematical data outside of the double nuclear structure. The models imply =7.2^ +2.7 _ 1.9 10^9 . The enhanced velocity dispersion in the region of the double nucleus suggests that 5419 possibly hosts two supermassive black holes at its centre, separated by only 70 pc.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 5419 as a high-mass elliptical with a cored profile, double nucleus, isotropic center, and likely massive SMBH binary in an intermediate core-formation stage.

quotes.quote: With its cored surface brightness profile, the elliptical galaxy NGC 5419 appears as a typical high-mass early-type galaxy (ETG). However, the galaxy hosts two distinct nuclei in its center. We use high-signal MUSE (Multi-Unit Spectroscopic Explorer Based on observations collected at the European Organisation for Astronomical Research in the Southern Hemisphere under ESO program 099.B-0193(A). ) spectral observations and novel triaxial dynamical orbit models to reveal a surprisingly isotropic central orbit distribution in NGC 5419.

The observed cored density profile, the double nucleus and the isotropic center of NGC 5419 together thus point to an intermediate evolutionary state where the first phase of the core formation has taken place, yet the scouring process is only beginning.
