This implies that the double nucleus is a SMBH binary. Our triaxial dynamical models indicate a total mass of the two SMBHs in the center of NGC 5419 of M_ BH = (1.0 0.08) 10^ 10 M_ .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5419 as a nucleated core-Sersic galaxy with a double nucleus, but argues that the offset nuclei do not affect the break-radius ellipticity measurement.

quotes.quote: Six of our 24 galaxies (NGC 0741, NGC 4278, NGC 4365, N4472, NGC 4552 and NGC 5419) have additional nuclear light components (Dullo Graham 2012). For four of these nucleated galaxies (i.e., except for NGC 4365 and NGC 5419), both _ inner and < _ mid > are lower than 0.20 and their central light excess is due to a point-source AGN (Dullo Graham 2012, their Section 8). For NGC 5419, Lena et al. \ (2014) noted that it has a double nucleus (see also Lauer et al. \ 2005). They found that the galaxy photocenter is displaced by 7.5 pc and 62 pc (i.e., 0.15 R_ b, N5419 ) from these nuclei. Thus, the double nucleus is unlikely to affect our measurement of the ellipticity at this galaxy's core-S ersic break radius R_ b, N5419 =416 pc (Dullo Graham 2014).

sources.summary: The paper finds NGC 5419 has increasing outer wing light and h4, central counter-rotation, a decoupled core, and literature evidence for a double nucleus.

quotes.quote: By eye, we see increasing wing-light towards larger radii in NGC 6861, NGC 1407, NGC 5328, NGC 5419 (see Figs. fig:FRellipses and fig:SRellipses ). In these same galaxies we also find an increase of h_4 (see Figs. fig:FRkinmap and fig:SRkinmap ).

Both NGC 5328 and NGC 5419 have local maxima of around /r_e 0.1 . This is due to the fact, that both galaxies exhibit counter rotation in their central regions (cf. the v_ rot maps in Fig.  fig:SRkinmap ).
The counter-rotation in the center of NGC 5419 was already reported by Mazzalay2016 , who, using both HST and adaptive optics based SINFONI observations found a double nucleus in the galaxy.

gmw_00002712.name: HT radiogalaxy
gmw_00002712.overview: The HT radiogalaxy is a head-tail radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.5399 [arXiv:1805.00473]. It lies along an optical filament, with its radio lobes pointing in the opposite direction of a tentative radio bridge [arXiv:1805.00473].

sources.summary: The paper identifies a z=0.5399 HT radiogalaxy along the filament into MACSJ0717, connects a tentative radio bridge to it, and notes its lobes point opposite the bridge.

quotes.quote: A head-tail radiogalaxy (hereinafter HT radiogalaxy) at z=0.5399 is found along the filament, between the cluster and the X-ray detected group Ebeling14 .

SE of the X-ray bar, hints of a new radio bridge are found, connecting the radio halo to the HT radiogalaxy located along the optical filament 3' from the X-ray centre As no evidence for radio emission from the bridge was found in the VLA or in the GMRT observations Bonafede09b,vanWeeren17,PandeyPommier13 , and as the background noise of the images are not uniform, even a detection at 10 could be partially affected by calibration artefacts.
In fact, the lobes of the HT radiogalaxy are pointing in the opposite direction with respect to the bridge.

gmw_00002713.name: DLA1201g1
gmw_00002713.overview: DLA1201g1 is a high-redshift interacting galaxy system at z = 3.7978 that exhibits rest-frame near-UV emission [arXiv:2107.05108]. The system features a visible [C II] emission bridge between two galaxies [arXiv:2107.05108] and displays complicated gas dynamics that may arise from a merger or an outflow [arXiv:1703.07797].

sources.summary: The paper describes ALMA J120110.26+211756.2 as a z~4 DLA host with extended neutral gas and complicated dynamics possibly reflecting a merger or outflow.

quotes.quote: For ALMA J120110.26+211756.2, the p-v diagram suggests more complicated gas dynamics which is corroborated by the spatial offset between the redshifted and blueshifted components .

The observed impact parameters correspond to physical (proper) distances of 42 kpc and 18 kpc at the DLA redshifts for ALMA J081740.86+135138.2 and ALMA J120110.26+211756.2, respectively. These distances are substabtially larger than the extent of the [ C ii ] 158 m line emission, which extends to 5 kpc, and indicate that these galaxies have large reservoirs of H i relatively far away from their star-forming regions.

For ALMA J120110.26+211756.2, the individual channel maps show that at 65 km s ^ 1 , the emission shifts to the northwest by 0.5'' . This indicates a more complicated velocity profile then a simple rotating disk, such as one arising from a merger or outflow.

sources.summary: DLA1201g1 is treated as an interacting z=3.7978 system with NUV emission, a CO(1--0) molecular-gas upper limit, and a visible [C II] bridge between two galaxies.

quotes.quote: Rest-frame near-UV emission was detected from DLA1201g1, DLA1054g1, DLA1443g1, and DLA0834g1, in addition to the emission from DLA0817g1 Neeleman20 .

In the case of DLA1201g1, at z = 3.7978 , our limit on the CO(1--0) line luminosity L'_ CO(1-0) can be directly used to infer an upper limit on the object's molecular gas mass, via the relation = L'_ CO(1-0) , where is the (assumed) CO-to-H _2 conversion factor e.g.

This is particularly evident for both the cold disk galaxy DLA0817g1 Neeleman20 and DLA1201g1, a pair of interacting galaxies discussed in Prochaska19 . For the latter system, the two galaxies and the \ emission ``bridge'' between the galaxies can also be seen clearly in the HST-WFC3 image.

gmw_00002714.name: DLA1054g1
gmw_00002714.overview: DLA1054g1 is a high-redshift galaxy with two near NUV-emitting galaxies located close to its line emission [arXiv:2107.05108]. The fainter of these two galaxies is tentatively identified as the counterpart of DLA1054g1 because its NUV star formation rate is consistent with the far-infrared estimate [arXiv:2107.05108].

sources.summary: For DLA1054g1, the paper reports NUV-emitting galaxies near the line emission and identifies the fainter candidate as likely because its NUV SFR matches the FIR estimate.

quotes.quote: We note that there are two NUV-emitting galaxies lying close to the \ emission of DLA1054g1 in Fig. fig:HST .

Of the two possible NUV counterparts of DLA1054g1, we tentatively identify the fainter of the two galaxies as likely to be associated with the \ emitter. This is because its NUV SFR is 11.7 \ yr ^ 1 , slightly lower than the total SFR of the galaxy estimated from the FIR continuum ( 21 \ yr ^ 1 ), and thus consistent with dust obscuration of the NUV emission. The brighter of the two NUV galaxies would have a dust-unobscured SFR of 54 \ yr ^ 1 if located at z = 4.1344 , i.e. higher than the total SFR estimate, and is thus unlikely to be the counterpart of DLA1054g1.

Finally, the and FIR 160 m contours in Figs. fig:HST and fig:HSTdust for DLA1054g1 fall between two galaxies in the HST-WFC3 image. We have identified the fainter NUV-emitting galaxy as the counterpart as its NUV SFR is more consistent with the FIR SFR. If the associated galaxy is the brighter galaxy, then its NUV SFR would be 54 \ yr ^ 1 , significantly higher than that derived from the ALMA dust 160 m continuum.

gmw_00002715.name: M108
gmw_00002715.overview: M108 is an edge-on spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0503070, arXiv:1803.00574] featuring a star-forming disk [arXiv:1803.00574, arXiv:2201.07773] and substantial molecular gas [arXiv:astro-ph/0503070]. It exhibits a radio halo [arXiv:astro-ph/0503070, arXiv:1508.05153] driven by a violent disk-halo interaction [arXiv:astro-ph/0503070] and an accelerating galactic wind [arXiv:1811.04015].

sources.summary: CHANG-ES combines NGC 3556 radio data with LOFAR observations, deriving spectral index, disk-to-halo magnetic-field strengths, and advective cosmic-ray transport.

quotes.quote: Total power results from CHANG-ES observations of NGC 3556 were combined with results from 144 MHz observations carried out using the Low Frequency Array (LOFAR) . These data were taken as part of the LOFAR Two-metre Sky Survey (LoTSS) . The combined results were used to calculate the spectral index between L-band and 144 MHz and the magnetic field strength in the galaxy using the equipartition approach by . This showed that the disk of the galaxy has an average magnetic field strength of 9  G, decreasing to around 5  G towards the halo.

The total power values and the magnetic field strength were used to model the cosmic ray electron propagation using SPINNAKER hee16 and its interactive wrapper SPINTERACTIVE https://github.com/vheesen/Spinnaker . Multiple models of diffusive and advective propagation were tested. The best fitting result was an advective transport of the CREs with a linearly increasing wind speed.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3556 as an edge-on spiral with violent disk-halo interaction, substantial molecular gas, and a newly detected weak H2O kilomaser.

quotes.quote: NGC 3556 is an edge-on spiral galaxy located at a distance of 12 Mpc. Its FIR luminosity, L_ FIR 10 ^ 10 L _ , is similar to that of the Milky Way. Radio continuum, \ and X-ray data indicate a violent disk halo interaction, including a prominent radio halo (e.g. de Bruyn Hummel 1979), large \ extensions possibly delineating expanding supershells (King Irwin 1997), compact radio continuum sources, likely representing supernova remnants (Irwin et al. 2000), and extraplanar diffuse X-ray emission (Wang et al. 2003). ^ 12 CO and HCN observations (Gao Solomon 2004) indicate a substantial molecular gas content.
No OH maser was detected (Unger et al. 1986). With a peak flux density of 20--40 mJy, the H _2 O maser has an isotropic luminosity of 1 L _ . Only one velocity component is seen.

sources.summary: The paper identifies NGC 3556 (M108) as a nearby star-forming spiral hosting SPIRITS 16tn in an inclined dust lane, adopting its 8.8 Mpc distance and z = 0.002332 redshift.

quotes.quote: We present the discovery by the SPitzer InfraRed Intensive Transients Survey (SPIRITS) of a likely supernova (SN) in NGC 3556 (M108) at only 8.8 Mpc, which, despite its proximity, was not detected by optical searches. A luminous infrared (IR) transient at M_ [ 4.5 ] = -16.7  mag (Vega), SPIRITS 16tn is coincident with a dust lane in the inclined, star-forming disk of the host.

Throughout this work, we adopt the most recent value from sorce14 of =29.72 0.41  mag ( D 8.8  Mpc). This estimate is based on the mid-IR Tully-Fisher relation using the [ 3.6 ] micron flux with color and selection bias corrections. The redshift of NGC 3556 is z = 0.002332 ( v = 699  km s ^ 1 ; shostak75 ). We assume Galactic extinction along the line of sight to NGC 3556 of A_V = 0.046  mag from the schlafly11 recalibration of the schlegel98 IR-based dust map assuming a fitzpatrick99 extinction law with R_V = 3.1 .

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 3556 as near the detection limit with patchy magnetic-field structure, possibly regular at lower resolution, and potentially affected by missing single-dish flux.

quotes.quote: There is also NGC 3556 observed near the detection limit that shows a somewhat patchy pattern. This galaxy has a linear resolution of 820 pc, hence comparable to NGC 891 at 20  HPBW. Also NGC 3556 may look more regular at a lower angular resolution which in this case may also be due to a higher signal-to-noise ratio.

Only four other galaxies in our sample are larger than 4  at C-band: NGC 3556, NGC 3628, NGC 4192, and NGC 5907. We do not have single-dish observations for them. Hence, their degree of polarization may be affected by missing spacings in TP and can be regarded as upper limits.

sources.summary: The paper combines LOFAR and VLA data for edge-on spiral NGC 3556, finding halo spectral aging, radio scale heights, and advection-dominated cosmic-ray transport in an accelerating galactic wind.

quotes.quote: Observations with the Very Large Array (VLA) from Continuum Haloes in Nearby Galaxies -- an EVLA Survey (CHANG-ES) are combined with those with the LOw Frequency ARray (LOFAR) from the LOFAR Two-metre Sky Survey (LoTSS ) to identify the prevailing mode of cosmic-ray transport in the edge-on spiral galaxy NGC 3556. We mapped the radio spectral index, magnetic field strength, and orientation using VLA 1.5 and 6 GHz and LOFAR 144 MHz data, and we fit 1D cosmic-ray propagation models to these maps using Spinnaker (Spectral Index Numerical Analysis of K()osmic-ray electron radio emission) and its interactive wrapper Spinteractive . We find that the spectral index in the galactic midplane is, as expected for young CRe, 0.7 and steepens towards the halo of the galaxy as a consequence of spectral ageing. The intensity scale heights are about 1.4 and 1.9 kpc for the thin disc, and 3.3 and 5.9 kpc for the thick disc at 1.5 GHz and 144 MHz, respectively. While pure diffusion cannot explain our data, advection can, particularly if we assume a linearly accelerating wind.

sources.summary: M108, also called NGC 3556, is described as an edge-on disk galaxy hosting FXRT 2, with a highly probable association and nearby extended star-forming regions.

quotes.quote: FXRT 2/XRT 010908 (cataloged as an X-ray source by Wang2016 , Liu2011 , and Mineo2012 , although never classified as an FXRT), a local FXRT, is located in the disk of the edge-on SB(s)cd galaxy M108 also known as NGC 3556; m_ R 9.2 AB mag and 9.0 Mpc; Dalya2018,Tully2013 , at an angular (projected) distance of 0 4 ( 1.1 kpc). The probability of a chance alignment is 3.2 10 ^ 6 based on its angular offset and the space density of m_ R < 9.2 AB mag galaxies using the GLADE catalog; Dalya2018 , thus implying a highly probable association; the binomial probability that this FXRT is a background source is 8.4 10 ^ 7 , reinforcing an association with M108 (see Sect. sec:population for more details). FXRT 2 appears to lie at the edge and intersection of two extended star-forming regions (see Fig. fig:image_cutouts , sources 1 and 2 in the northeast and southwest directions, respectively), with several potential, unresolved, optical/NIR candidate counterparts in the HST F606W image inside the Chandra 3 error circle. The estimated magnitudes of sources 1 and 2 are m_ F606W = 18.4 and 18.2 AB mag (i.e., M_ F606W 11.4 and 11.6 AB mag), respectively taken from the HSCv3; Whitmore2016 . As such, FXRT 2 is likely associated with a region of enhanced high-mass star formation.

sources.summary: The paper notes that NGC 3556 has a significant radio halo and very flat C-band spectral indices across the disk, suggesting stronger thermal emission there.

quotes.quote: NGC 3556 and NGC 5775 Both of these galaxies are well-known to have significant halos, which we can also see in our data. Moreover, they both show very flat spectral indices at C-band throughout the disk, where the errors in spectral indices are the lowest. Neither data set showed any particular problems and were single pointings. Bearing in mind the summary regarding spectral index in Section sec:alpha_errors , this suggests that thermal emission may be more strongly dominant for these galaxies in their disks.

gmw_00002716.name: NGC 7465
gmw_00002716.overview: NGC 7465 is an early-type [arXiv:2101.11652, arXiv:2211.12974] or spiral galaxy [arXiv:1804.07311] hosting a low-ionization [arXiv:0901.0999] or Seyfert nucleus [arXiv:1804.07311] with an extended optical narrow-line region [arXiv:2107.00244]. Part of an interacting group [arXiv:1804.07311, arXiv:2201.07773], it is unusually gas-rich [arXiv:2101.11652] and exhibits a kinematically misaligned molecular structure [arXiv:2101.11652] alongside a possible polar ring [arXiv:1408.2602, arXiv:astro-ph/0312209].

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA molecular-line observations showing NGC 7465 is gas-rich, early-type, recently accreted, kinematically misaligned, and unusual in its dense-gas tracers.

quotes.quote: We present ALMA observations of CO isotopologues and high-density molecular tracers (HCN, , CN, etc.) in NGC 7465, an unusually gas-rich early-type galaxy that acquired its cold gas recently. In the inner 300 pc, the molecular gas kinematics are misaligned with respect to all other galaxy components; as the gas works its way inward it is torqued into polar orbits about the stellar kinematically-decoupled core (KDC), indicating that the stellar KDC is not related to the current gas accretion event. The galaxy also exhibits unusually high / \ line ratios in its nucleus but typical / \ ratios. Our calculations show that this result does not necessarily indicate an unusual [ / ] abundance ratio but rather that \ (1-0) is optically thin due to high temperatures and/or large linewidths associated with the inner decoupled, misaligned molecular structure. Line ratios of the higher-density tracers suggest that the densest phase of molecular gas in NGC 7465 has a lower density than is typical for nearby galaxies, possibly as a result of the recent gas accretion.
All of the observed molecular properties of NGC 7465 are consistent with it having acquired its molecular (and atomic) gas from a spiral galaxy.

sources.summary: The PDFchem comparison treats NGC 7465's nucleus as a low-redshift early-type system with solar metallicity, whose HCO+/CO ratio implies elevated cosmic-ray ionization in the galaxy center.

quotes.quote: Panel (b) shows the observations of Youn21 for the nucleus of NGC 7465 which is a low-redshift early-type galaxy Capp11 . This panel has the same PDF and metallicity as panel (a). The observed metallicity of this system is approximately solar.

Youn21 calculated the ^ 12 CO and HCO ^+ column densities for two representative temperatures (20 and 90 K) in the optically thin limit. These lead to a range of HCO ^+ /CO ratio of _ 10 HCO^+/CO =-3.75 to 3.73 , for which PDFchem finds a _ CR 3-4 10^ 15 s ^ 1 . These elevated _ CR values are reasonable to expect in an ISM region located in the center of galaxies such as NGC 7465.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 7465 as an S4G galaxy with a previously proposed polar ring, but adopts the view that the ring is too subtle or ambiguous to accept.

quotes.quote: Other polar rings have been found in the S ^ 4 G sample galaxies NGC 2748, NGC 6870, and NGC 7465, by buta14 . Their tentative contours are marked in the appendices of comeron14 . However, those three polar rings are exceedingly subtle and/or hard to interpret. Therefore, they may actually not be polar rings. This is the position adopted in this paper.

sources.summary: NGC 7465 is described as the probable host of FXRT 6 in its outskirts, part of the merging NGC 7448 group, with the transient near a blue spiral arm.

quotes.quote: FXRT 6/XRT 130822 (cataloged as an X-ray source by Wang2016 , although never classified as an FXRT) is situated in the outskirts of the galaxy NGC 7465 ( m_ R = 12.0 AB mag; 27 Mpc), which is part of the merging NGC 7448 group, at an angular (projected) distance of 1 2 (9.4 kpc). The probability of this occurring by chance is 1.5 10 ^ 4 based on its offset and the space density of m_ R < 12 AB mag galaxies, thus implying a probable association; the binomial probability that this FXRT is a background source is 1.5 10 ^ 2 , reinforcing an association with NGC 7465 (see Sect. sec:population for more details). The FXRT position overlaps with a blue spiral arm and lies in between two diffuse blue candidate sources in DECam images (see Fig. fig:image_cutouts , sources 1 and 2 in g and band images). These have offsets of 1 3 to the northwest and 1 5 to the northeast, respectively, which lie just slightly outside of the 3 X-ray error region, but their proximity suggests that FXRT 6 is likely associated with a region of high-mass star formation.

sources.summary: The paper describes Mrk 313 as a group member with disturbed H I: a ring around the galaxy may be a polar ring formed from NGC 7464 material.

quotes.quote: High-resolution HI observations in the Mrk 313 group of galaxies reveal that the HI morphology of almost all the member galaxies are affected by the environment and four galaxies show tidally distorted HI morphology and kinematics (Li Seaquist 1994). The HI emission of the pair NGC 7464/Mrk 313 is dominated by a ring around Mrk 313. The orientation of Mrk 313 and the ring suggest that it represents a polar ring around Mrk 313 and could be material pulled out of NGC 7464 during a close encounter with Mrk 313.

Their environment, as determined from CCD and sky survey images, gives no indication to explain the perturbed kinematics : only one galaxy (Mrk 201) appears strongly disturbed, and another one (Mrk 313) is in a group, while the others are isolated or in rather distant pairs and fairly symmetric in shape (Table devTF ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7465/Mrk 313 as a low-ionization AGN/LINER with LINER-like X-ray luminosity and newly revealed Type-1 AGN signatures in near-infrared spectra.

quotes.quote: NGC 7465 (Mrk 313) Osterbrock87 and vanDriel92 claimed for the low-ionization AGN nature of the nucleus of NGC 7465, based on optical spectroscopy.

The hard X-ray absorption-corrected luminosity of NGC 7465 is 10 ^ 42 erg s^ 1 , according to Guainazzi05 , that is within the typical LINER intrinsic luminosities in the 2-10 keV band (10 ^ 40-42 erg s ^ 1 ; Terashima00 ).

Apart from confirming NGC 7465 as a low-ionization AGN, our near-infrared emission line spectrum reveals for first time the Type-1 AGN nature of this galaxy, based on the detection of broad components of Pa and Br (with FWHM > 2000 km s ^ 1 ), together with Fe II and O I permitted lines (see Figure fi:n7465 ).

sources.summary: The paper assigns NGC7465 a high kinetic temperature, describes a triple-peaked temperature PDF, and says its highest Tkin and Jmax largely drive weak turnover trends.

quotes.quote: The kinetic temperatures obtained for the best-fit models are either low ( T_ K =10 20 K for IC1024, NGC1222, NGC1266, NGC2764, NGC3032, NGC3665, NGC4150, NGC4459, NGC4526, NGC4694, NGC4710 and NGC5866), intermediate ( 30 70 K for IC0676, NGC3607, PGC058114 and UGC09519) or high ( 120 K for NGC6014 and 150 K for NGC7465).

The apparent discrepancy between the best-fit and most likely models is then generally due to the double-peaked (or triple-peaked for NGC7465) shape of the kinetic temperature PDF (see the left panels in Fig. fig:4 ).
\ larger) statistical sample, as they are mostly driven by the modelling results of a single galaxy (NGC7465, with the highest T_ kin and J _ max ).

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 7465 as a spiral Sy2 galaxy in an interacting nine-galaxy group, with a nuclear stellar population dominated by K3 III to M3 III type stars.

quotes.quote: NGC 7465 is a spiral galaxy with a Sy2 nucleus. This source is part of a group of nine galaxies interacting Haynes81 . The dominant stellar population in the nuclear region of NGC 7465 corresponds to stars between K3 III and M3 III types, according to the relative absorption bands measurements Ramos09 .

sources.summary: The paper places NGC 7465 among faint extended mid-IR cases, notes its extended optical NLR, and finds no bright extended mid-IR emission at the current resolution.

quotes.quote: Three galaxies, NGC 6814, NGC 7314, and NGC 7465, only show evidence of faint extended emission at 3-5 _ bk levels. Finally, the PSF-subtracted images of NGC 4941 and NGC 7213 do not show evidence of extended emission.

NGC 4941, NGC 7465 and NGC 7314 have extended optical NLRs, which are detected with optical IFU observations and/or HST imaging see and Sousa et al. in preparation, respectively ErrozFerrer2019, Ferruit2000 . In NGC 6814, the NLR and the coronal line region are rather compact and there is evidence for ionized outflows on these scales 1 , see MuellerSanchez2011 . In these four galaxies we do not detect extended mid-IR emission.

gmw_00002717.name: NGC 4489
gmw_00002717.overview: NGC 4489 is a low-mass early-type galaxy [arXiv:1301.7066, arXiv:1004.4439] that has been classified as a lenticular galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0204173]. It exhibits a low central velocity dispersion [arXiv:1301.7066, arXiv:astro-ph/0204173], an asymmetric rotation curve [arXiv:astro-ph/0204173], and a blue integrated color [arXiv:astro-ph/0107357].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4489 to show how IMF choice changes inferred stellar populations: a standard IMF suggests a young elliptical, while a flatter IMF reveals an old component.

quotes.quote: Here we focus on the low-mass elliptical NGC 4489. This galaxy has been previously reported to have young mean luminosity-weighted ages from line-strength studies ( 3-5 Gyr; e.g: Gorgas1997 , Terlevich2002 , Caldwell2003 , Tantalo2004 , Y06). However, this does not necessarily imply that the galaxy is composed of genuinely young stellar populations, as the old component can be masked by the luminosity of a tiny fraction of young stars on top of it.

The mass-weighted SFH reveals the composition of NGC 4489, as presented in Figure 5. It shows, like in Fig.2, the derived SFHs with different IMF slopes. From the Salpeter panel (unimodal = 1.3) one would infer that NGC 4489 is a genuinely young object with almost no contribution from stellar populations older than 5 Gyr (less than 5 ), i.e. a `` baby elliptical galaxy ''. However, considering a slightly flatter IMF slope as claimed for an elliptical galaxy of such small velocity dispersion (e.g. Cappellari2012a , F13), an old component appears, which contributes 65 to the total mass.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4489 as a possible dS0/S0 transition object, noting dwarf-like surface brightness, prior velocity-dispersion comparisons, and an asymmetric rotation curve explained by a shifted kinematic centre.

quotes.quote: Although NGC 4489 is classified as S0 (non dwarf) in Binggeli, Sandage Tammann (1985), it exhibits a mean surface brightness within the effective radius of SB_e=22.24 mag arcsec ^ 2 (Faber et al. 1989), which, in the SB_e -absolute magnitude plane, places it closer to the locus of dwarf ellipticals than to the low-luminosity early-type galaxies At the absolute magnitude of NGC 4489, classical ellipticals and S0's attain typical values of SB_e between 18 and 21 mag arcsec ^ 2 , whereas for the dwarf galaxies of this sample 22.5<SB_e<23 mag arcsec ^ 2 . .

The central velocity dispersion of NGC 4489 has been already measured by several authors. Our result ( _0=51.1 1.8 ) agrees with some of the previous measurements, like those of Davies et al. (1983; _0=53 15 ), Faber et al. (1989; _0=49 7 ) and Gonz a lez (1993; _0=48 3 ), but it is somewhat smaller than the values given by Simien Prugniel (1997; _0=63 13 ) and, especially, Smith et al. (2000; _0=72 7 ).
An exception is the rotation curve for NGC 4489. In this case, a displacement of 1.3 arcsec in the kinematic centre can explain the asymmetry.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 4489 as a young, low-velocity-dispersion Virgo galaxy whose blue residuals match NGC 4387 but whose longer-wavelength residual pattern is not a two-burst signature.

quotes.quote: Our subsample of galaxies (NGC 4387, NGC 4473, NGC 4489 and NGC 4621) cover a range in age, metallicity, abundance ratio and mass, as these galaxies were selected alongside the Colour-Magnitue Relation of the Virgo cluster. The resolution of the Subaru spectrum for NGC 4489 is FWHM = 3.1 , whereas for the other three galaxies, observed at the William Herschel Telescope (Vazdekis et al. 2001b), the resolution is FWHM = 2.4 .

For the other galaxy with low but young age, NGC 4489, the residuals in the blue part are as small as those obtained for NGC 4387. However, we see a pattern in the residuals at longer wavelengths. If the stars of this galaxy would have formed in two bursts then a characteristic residual pattern would be seen, namely a surplus of light in the blue and a deficit in the red, which is not the case.

sources.summary: The paper includes NGC 4489 in the K-band SBF calibration and argues that its apparently anomalously bright fluctuations are consistent with its very blue integrated color.

quotes.quote: Finally, we include band data for NGC 3379 in Leo and NGC 4489 in Virgo from mei2001 .

Moreover, the discovery of the slope eliminates published concerns about anomalously bright K band SBFs for M32 1993ApJ...410...81L and NGC 4489 in Virgo 1994ApJ...433..567P, 1996ApJ...468..519J,mei2001 . Both galaxies lie on the plotted calibration. (NGC 4489 has the bluest \ color in the sample, but if we remove it, the fitted zero point and slope of change negligibly.) However, we do find a few Fornax galaxies seem to have very bright band fluctuations, more than 0.5 mag above the observed mean relation.

In addition, this finding resolves published concerns that the SBFs of NGC 4489 and M32 have anomalously bright K band SBFs: their brighter fluctuations are in accord with their bluer integrated colors.

gmw_00002718.name: NGC 4431
gmw_00002718.overview: NGC 4431 is a flattened, rotationally supported low-mass galaxy that hosts a detected bar and disc structure [arXiv:0812.3272, arXiv:1610.02326]. Its stellar population exhibits flat age and metallicity distributions out to half of its effective radius [arXiv:0812.3272].

sources.summary: VCC 1010 is a flattened, rotationally supported galaxy with detected bar and disc; its HFA data show flat age and metallicity distributions to half an effective radius.

quotes.quote: VCC 1010 = NGC 4431 (Fig figvcc1010 ). Bar and disc were detected in this flattened rotationally supported galaxy by . The data available for this object in the HFA allowed us to trace the behaviour of the stellar population parameters out to r_ e / 2 . Both, age and metallicity distribution look flat showing no significant changes along the radius.

sources.summary: The paper cites NGC 4431 as the only low-mass galaxy then having a measured bar pattern speed and suggests 3D kinematic maps could test interaction signatures.

quotes.quote: At the moment the only low mass galaxy with a measured pattern speed is NGC 4431 Corsini+07 . The authors found that the probability of the bar being fast is twice that of being slow. Their study is based on long slit data of just one galaxy, so we should take it with caution. We expect that this galaxy can be studied with 3D kinematic maps in more detail, in particular by looking for signs of interactions in the kinematics.

sources.summary: The paper reports that NGC 4431 has a lower measured central velocity dispersion than earlier values, profiles reaching the effective radius, and an increasing rotation curve giving a lower-limit Vmax.

quotes.quote: Central velocity dispersions for NGC 4431, IC 3393 and UGC 7436 have already been listed in the compilation by Bender et al. (1992). We must note that these values (68, 55 and 45 km/s respectively) are significantly larger than our measurements (42, 25 and 35 km/s).

In the case of NGC 4415, NGC 4431 and UGC 7436 the profiles extend out to the effective radius of the galaxies, whereas, for the other three galaxies, the data reach roughly to half the effective radius.
In some galaxies, like NGC 4431 and UGC 7436, the rotation curves increase to the outermost points observed, so the derived V_ max should be considered as lower limits to the actual values.

gmw_00002719.name: IC 3393
gmw_00002719.overview: IC 3393 is a strongly flattened galaxy that lacks embedded substructures [arXiv:0812.3272]. Its nuclear region is distinct in both age and metallicity, displaying a metallicity gradient [arXiv:0812.3272].

sources.summary: VCC 1122 is strongly flattened, lacks embedded substructures, has a radial-velocity peculiarity, and shows a nucleus distinct in age and metallicity plus a metallicity gradient.

quotes.quote: VCC 1122 = IC 3393 (Fig figvcc1122 ). This strongly flattened galaxy with the insufficient degree of rotation to be considered as rotationally supported does not exhibit embedded substructures according to . We have the data from both Palomar dE project and the HFA. The kinematical profiles agree quite well. There is a peculiarity seen in the radial velocity profile 2 arcsec south-east of the nucleus, however presently we do not have enough evidences to interpret it as a kinematically decoupled component.

The stellar population properties were determined out to 2 r_ e from the Palomar dE data. The nuclear region of VCC 1122 is clearly distinct in both age and metallicity profiles. We also see a remarkable metallicity gradient, which is, however still insufficient to explain the high metallicity in the nuclear region.

sources.summary: The paper compares IC 3393 with earlier kinematic compilations, stating that the previous central dispersion exceeds the authors measured value while the published Vmax/sigma remains compatible with their result.

quotes.quote: Central velocity dispersions for NGC 4431, IC 3393 and UGC 7436 have already been listed in the compilation by Bender et al. (1992). We must note that these values (68, 55 and 45 km/s respectively) are significantly larger than our measurements (42, 25 and 35 km/s).

Concerning the three galaxies in common with their sample (NGC 4431, IC 3393 and UGC 7436; see above), we must note that their V_ max / values (1.00, 0.50 and 0.44, respectively) are not incompatible with ours ( 0.71 0.16 , 0.65 0.14 , 0.47 0.11 ).

gmw_00002720.name: UGC 7436
gmw_00002720.overview: UGC 7436 is a flattened, rotationally supported dwarf galaxy [arXiv:0812.3272] with a rotation curve that increases to its outermost observed points [arXiv:astro-ph/0204173]. Its stellar population exhibits a nearly flat age profile with a mean age of 5.5 Gyr and a slight metallicity gradient [arXiv:0812.3272].

sources.summary: VCC 543 is a flattened, rotationally supported galaxy with consistent Palomar and HFA kinematic and stellar-population profiles, a flat age profile, and a slight metallicity gradient.

quotes.quote: VCC 543 = UGC 7436 (Fig figvcc0543 ). This flattened rotationally supported galaxy was a subject to the several kinematical studies referenced above. It is one of the three galaxies with the data available both in the Palomar dE project and in the HFA. The kinematical and stellar population profiles are consistent between the datasets. The age profile in this object is nearly flat with a mean value of 5.5 Gyr, while the metallicity exhibits a slight gradient.

sources.summary: The paper finds UGC 7436 has a lower central velocity dispersion than an earlier compilation, profiles extending to the effective radius, and a rotation curve still rising at the outermost measured points.

quotes.quote: Central velocity dispersions for NGC 4431, IC 3393 and UGC 7436 have already been listed in the compilation by Bender et al. (1992). We must note that these values (68, 55 and 45 km/s respectively) are significantly larger than our measurements (42, 25 and 35 km/s).

In the case of NGC 4415, NGC 4431 and UGC 7436 the profiles extend out to the effective radius of the galaxies, whereas, for the other three galaxies, the data reach roughly to half the effective radius.
In some galaxies, like NGC 4431 and UGC 7436, the rotation curves increase to the outermost points observed, so the derived V_ max should be considered as lower limits to the actual values.

gmw_00002721.name: NGC 5448
gmw_00002721.overview: NGC 5448 is an active, early-type barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0508131, arXiv:0704.2839] with a dusty central region [arXiv:0704.2839]. Its stellar kinematics reveal a regular rotating disk containing a small, fast-rotating inner stellar disk [arXiv:astro-ph/0508131, arXiv:0704.2839], while its perturbed, S-shaped ionized-gas kinematics indicate non-circular, bar-driven radial motions [arXiv:astro-ph/0508131, arXiv:0704.2839].

sources.summary: The paper analyzes NGC 5448 as an Sa barred spiral whose gas shows non-circular bar-driven motions and whose central stellar disk is embedded in the larger disk.

quotes.quote: We analyse \ kinematic maps of the inner kpc of the early-type (Sa) barred spiral galaxy NGC 5448. The observed morphology and kinematics of the emission-line gas is patchy and perturbed, indicating clear departures from circular motion. The kinematics of the stars is more regular, and displays a small inner disk-like system embedded in a large-scale rotating structure.

NGC 5448 exhibits clear signatures of the presence of other components than a single bar, which affect the observed velocity field. Inspecting the photometry and the central parts of the gaseous and stellar velocity field, we find signatures of a central stellar disk embedded in the larger disk (see Fig. fig:diskmodel ).

Our analysis shows that the non-circular gas kinematics in NGC 5448 could be driven by the large-scale bar. The central disk could then have been formed as a result of the gas accumulation at the centre.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5448 as an active barred galaxy with a dusty central region, regular stellar disc, inner fast-rotating component, S-shaped ionized-gas kinematics, and central population changes.

quotes.quote: NGC 5448 is an active barred galaxy (L2; Ho et al. 1997) with a dusty central region. The stellar kinematics display a regular disc, and signatures of an inner fast rotating component in the central 5 . The ionised-gas kinematics features an 'S-shaped' zero-velocity curve, suggestive of gas radial motions. We refer the reader to Fathi et al. (2005) for a detailed study of this galaxy using SAURON . In the absorption line maps one sees an increase in H and a decrease in \ in the central 5 .
This is exactly the region of the central disc, and the excess of dust extinction.

sources.summary: The paper maps NGC 5448's inner kiloparsec, finding patchy disturbed emission-line gas, regular stellar rotation with an embedded central disk, and gas kinematics consistent with a bar model.

quotes.quote: We analyse \ kinematic maps of the inner kpc of the early-type (Sa) barred spiral galaxy NGC 5448. The observed morphology and kinematics of the emission-line gas is patchy and perturbed, indicating clear departures from circular motion. The kinematics of the stars is more regular, and display a small inner disk-like system embedded in a large-scale rotating structure.

We focus on the \ gas, and use a harmonic decomposition formalism to analyse the gas velocity field. The higher-order harmonic terms and the main kinematic features of the observed data are consistent with an analytically constructed simple bar model. The bar model is derived using linear theory, considering an m=2 perturbation mode, and with bar parameters which are consistent with the large-scale bar detected via imaging.

sources.summary: The paper uses SAURON data for the early-type spiral NGC 5448 to map ionized-gas streaming, decompose the velocity field, and interpret the perturbations with a bar model.

quotes.quote: Here we do the same with much higher resolution optical emission line data, obtained with the Integral Field Spectrograph SAURON, for NGC 5448, an early-type spiral galaxy.

In Fig. 1 we show the observed velocity field of NGC 5448 from the [ OIII ] emission line at 5007 . Clearly visible is the streaming at the bottom right and top left. Decomposing the velocity field into Fourier harmonic components using V_ los = V_ sys + ^ k _ n=1 c_n(R) n + s_n(R) n i , where k =3, _n and s _n give us information about the nature of the perturbations. As mentioned in F05, _1 , _3 and s _3 give information about possible m = 2 perturbations. These coefficients are plotted in the figure, along with an analytic bar model, which shows how well such a model can reproduce the complex observations (for details see F05).

sources.summary: NGC 5448 is presented as an active barred galaxy with a regular stellar disc, an inner fast-rotating component, centrally concentrated emission, and S-shaped gas kinematics.

quotes.quote: NGC 5448 is an active barred galaxy ho97,eskridge02 . The stellar kinematics display a regular disc, and signatures of an inner fast rotating component in the central 5 . The \ distribution is more concentrated than the . The ionised-gas kinematics features an 'S-shaped' zero-velocity curve, suggestive of gas radial motions.

gmw_00002722.name: J1406+0102
gmw_00002722.overview: J1406+0102 is a radio-loud, dust-obscured galaxy located at a redshift of z=0.236 [arXiv:2303.05605]. It hosts an active galactic nucleus undergoing super-Eddington accretion, which drives a strong ionized gas outflow that exceeds the escape velocity of the host galaxy halo [arXiv:2303.05605].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1406+0102 as a z=0.236 radio-loud dust-obscured galaxy with super-Eddington accretion, broad Balmer lines, strong AGN feedback, and radio emission from jets or outflows.

quotes.quote: Motivated by the theoretical suggestion that the super-Eddington phase efficiently produces outflows and jets bright in radio bands, we search and find a super-Eddington radio-loud dust-obscured galaxy (DOG) J1406+0102 at z=0.236 , through cross-matching of the infrared-bright DOGs of with the VLA/FIRST 1.4 GHz radio and the SDSS optical spectral catalog. DOG J1406+0102 shows broad components in the Balmer lines. Assuming those lines are from the broad line region, it gives BH mass estimation of \ ( / )=7.30 0.25 , and AGN luminosity of (L_ bol, [ OIII ] / erg s ^ 1 ) = 45.91 0.38 estimated from the intrinsic [ O iii ] luminosity, resulting in super-Eddington accretion of 3 . We show that 1) DOG J1406+0102 is operating strong AGN feedback: the [ O iii ] outflow velocity exceeds the escape velocity of the host galaxy halo and the kinetic efficiency is obtained as 8 that can be sufficient to quench the host galaxy, 2) the expected future growth pathway of DOG J1406+0102 would join an over-massive BH trajectory and 3) radio-loud DOGs can provide a significant contribution to the high-energy ( 100 TeV) cosmic neutrino background if we assume DOG J1406+0102 as a representative of radio-loud DOGs. galaxies: SMBH growth: active --- galaxies: nuclei --- quasars: general Recent surveys of high- z quasars have revealed that supermassive black holes (SMBHs) with mass of 10^ 9 \ already exist at the redshift z 7 e.g., wan21 .

AGN outflow and feedback Our results on the optical spectral fitting show that J1406 has a strong ionized [ O iii ] outflow. This ionized gas velocity well exceeds the escaping velocity of the galaxy halo with the stellar mass of J1406 of ( / )= 9.94 0.02 e.g., mos13,che20 . This indicates that the super-Eddington phase of J1406 (and possibly the radio-loud DOGs in general) has a strong impact on the host galaxy growth by injecting a large amount of energy from its nucleus.

gmw_00002723.name: RA= 18:28:24.67, DEC= +86:25:44.16 (J2000)
aliases_external: candidate GRB host at RA=18:28:24.67 Dec=+86:25:44.16
gmw_00002723.overview: A faint galaxy detected at RA= 18:28:24.67, DEC= +86:25:44.16 (J2000) is a potential host of a gamma-ray burst [arXiv:2310.08503]. Spectral analysis and photometric modeling suggest the galaxy is located at a moderate redshift of approximately 1.88 [arXiv:2310.08503].

sources.summary: The paper detects a faint candidate GRB host at these coordinates, models it as a moderate-redshift galaxy, but notes spectroscopy and large offset make the association uncertain.

quotes.quote: AT the position RA= 18:28:24.67, and DEC= +86:25:44.16 (J2000), we detected a faint galaxy with the following magnitude values g = 25.56 0.27, = 25.26 0.27, i = 24.80 0.22, and z 24.4. The position of the candidate host galaxy is consistent with the XRT position (as shown in Fig. carta JHK ).

Nature of the potential host galaxy of Deeper observations searching the host of found a potential host with an angular separation of 1.1 '' from the NIR afterglow position (see section Host Galaxy ). The potential host is a faint ( 25.26 0.27 mag ) one with typical g-i colour measured for galaxies at moderate redshift values detected in the optical bands (see Table optical/NIR observations ). The observed spectrum of the potential host galaxy taken using GTC was very noisy, and there is no emission-like feature up to z =1.08, discarding the low-redshift possibility (the non-detection of UV emission also discards the low redshift possibility). So, we executed the SED modelling of the potential galaxy of utilising photometric data and Prospector software.

Considering the redshift 1.88 obtained from SED modelling, we calculated the physical offset 9.5 kpc between the centre of the potential host galaxy and the NIR afterglow position. The measured physical distance is very large compared to those typically observed for long GRBs, suggesting that the galaxy might not be related with .

gmw_00002724.name: J164100.10+345452.7
gmw_00002724.overview: J164100.10+345452.7 is a narrow-line Seyfert 1 galaxy [arXiv:2108.08521, arXiv:1806.02058] located in a dense large-scale environment [arXiv:1806.02058]. The host is a barred late-type galaxy with a pseudo-bulge [arXiv:2108.08521], harboring a relativistic jet that produces gamma-ray emission [arXiv:2108.08521, arXiv:2003.02654] and modest 37 GHz radio flares [arXiv:2312.02326].

sources.summary: The paper describes J1641+3454 as an NLS1 with gamma-ray evidence for jets, steep JVLA radio emission, diffuse extended structure, and a barred late-type host where star formation may contribute.

quotes.quote: J1641+3454 J1641+3454 is an NLS1 at a redshift of 0.164. It was detected in FIRST with an integrated flux density of 2.69 mJy, so the radio emission slightly dominates over the optical emission. However, 37 GHz detections at Metsähovi, and later a gamma-ray detection by Fermi 2017lahteenmaki1 confirmed the presence of most likely relativistics jets in this source. The JVLA measurements do not reflect this at all since its integrated flux densities are (2.34 0.06) mJy, (0.69 0.02) mJy, and (0.35 0.01) mJy, at 1.6, 5.2, and 9.0 GHz, respectively (Fig. fig:lcx-spectra ). Its 1.6 GHz radio map (Fig. fig:J1641-radio a) shows diffuse extended emission around the centre.

The host (Figs. fig:J1641-radio e and f) was found to be a barred late-type galaxy with a pseudo-bulge 2020olguiniglesias1 , so the presence of star formation could be expected. It is interesting that unlike J1228+5017 and J1522+3923, J1641+3454 does not show any signs of flattening even at 9.0 GHz. More observations are required before we can determine whether it is similar to peaked-spectrum sources with a semi-stationary spectrum peaking at high frequency, a source with extreme variability, or possibly a combination of the two.

sources.summary: The paper presents J1641+3454 as the only sample source with significant gamma-ray detection, modest 37 GHz flaring, prior multiwavelength monitoring, and resolved JVLA/LOFAR radio structures.

quotes.quote: SDSS J164100.10+345452.7 J1641+3454 is the only one of our sources with a statistically significant gamma-ray detection 2018lahteenmaki1 , usually considered as proof of the presence of relativistic jets. Interestingly, its detection rate at 37 GHz is the lowest in the sample at 1.5 per cent. Its 37 GHz flux densities are modest, generally around 500 mJy and below, indicating that most of its flaring activity might not exceed the MRO detection threshold. Its average 37 GHz luminosity is L_ = 9.9 10 ^ 42 erg s ^ 1 .

J1641+3454 was a target of an intense 20-month multiwavelength monitoring campaign in radio, optical, ultraviolet and, X-rays 2023romano1 . During the campaign it flared twice at 37 GHz: the first radio flare was followed by brightening in X-rays, whereas the latter flare was not accompanied by any significant changes at other frequencies. Nevertheless, this was the first detection of a 37 GHz radio flare counterpart at another frequency. J1641+3454 is detected in X, Ku, K, and Ka bands with the JVLA (Table tab:jterf ).

sources.summary: The paper finds J1641+3454 matches FIRST fluxes, uniquely remains formally radio-loud and gamma-ray detected, but has low brightness temperature and very weak radio luminosity.

quotes.quote: Two of the sources, J1522+3934 and J1641+3454, have been already observed in the FIRST survey at 1.6 GHz in B configuration, which measured flux densities of 1.88 0.14 and 2.43 0.13 mJy, respectively. The integrated flux density we measured at 1.6 GHz was 2.03 0.16 and 2.52 0.10 mJy, respectively, results well in agreement with those from the FIRST.
Only in J1641+3454 the spectral indexes remain the same, while J1232+4957 is not detected at 9.0 GHz and the spectral index is only an upper limit.

However, at least in the ray source J1641+3454, a relativistic jet must be present. The detection of rays is associated with a beamed relativistic jet which, typically, has a flat radio spectrum in the JVLA range. However, its low-frequency spectrum is clearly steep, its brightness temperature is rather low (albeit still consistent with a misaligned jet), and its radio luminosity is very low, the lowest ever measured for a ray NLS1 e.g., Berton18a, Paliya19a .

sources.summary: The paper identifies J164100.10+345452.7 as a new gamma-ray emitting NLS1 galaxy, borderline radio quiet, detected twice at 37 GHz with modest radio flux in a denser environment.

quotes.quote: We also report the discovery of gamma-ray emission from one of our sources (maximum-likelihood test statistic, TS > 25): J164100.10+345452.7. This source is located 0.10 from the radio position. The TS value of 39 confirms this source as a new gamma-ray emitting NLS1 galaxy. Its radio loudness of 13 makes it a borderline source between radio loud and radio quiet, however, it has been detected at 37 GHz twice. The 37 GHz radio flux is fairly modest with peak flux density of 0.46 Jy, positioning it at the low end of the radio versus gamma-ray emission continuum. The source resides in a large-scale environment that is on a denser side of intermediate 2017jarvela1 .

gmw_00002725.name: J090113.23+465734.7
gmw_00002725.overview: J090113.23+465734.7 is a prospective gamma-ray emitting narrow-line Seyfert 1 candidate [arXiv:1806.02058]. Although previously classified as radio-loud, it is apparently radio-silent, as its historical radio data likely originated from the nearby source MJV 09737 [arXiv:1806.02058].

sources.summary: J090113.23+465734.7 is a prospective gamma-ray NLS1 candidate whose earlier radio-loud classification likely came from nearby MJV 09737; its single 37 GHz detection may be mostly uncontaminated.

quotes.quote: Another three sources are prospective gamma-ray emitting candidates (9 < TS < 25): J090113.23+465734.7, J122844.81+501751.2, and J123220.11+495721.8.

In 2015jarvela1 J090113.23+465734.7 is listed as radio loud. A search radius of 1 arcmin was used to obtain the radio data, and while there is no detected radio source at the source coordinates (the mean epoch of the FIRST observations is 1997.2), there is a radio source in its close proximity at coordinates RA 09h01m09.225s, Dec +46d57m21.19s. This is MJV 09737, which has no listed redshift or observations at other wavelengths. Their separation is 0.72 arcmin and it is therefore probable that the radio data in 2015jarvela1 originate in this source, and J090113.23+465734.7 is apparently radio silent.

The source has been detected at 37 GHz only once, and the detection raised concern about the possible contamination by MJV 09737 since it also lies within our antenna beam (2.4 arcmin at 37 GHz). The flux density of MJV 09373 at 1.4 GHz is 1.55 mJy and it has not been detected at higher radio frequencies; thus it is likely to be faint at 37 GHz. To ensure this we ran a set of simple tests to check how another radio source lying in the antenna beam might affect our observations, by observing the sources in pairs several times. All observations of MJV 09737 were non-detections so its contribution to 37 GHz observations seems negligible, but this test is not precise enough to certainly rule out possible contamination.

gmw_00002726.overview: 0406--244 is a high-redshift radio galaxy at z=2.44 [arXiv:1309.5954, arXiv:astro-ph/0412026] that features a complex, clumpy morphology aligned with its radio axis [arXiv:1309.5954, arXiv:astro-ph/0412026]. Its host galaxy is a massive star-forming system [arXiv:1309.5954] embedded in a large Lyman-alpha halo [arXiv:1309.5954, arXiv:astro-ph/0412026].

sources.summary: The paper models a z=2.44 radio galaxy as a massive star-forming host with a smooth optical stellar disk, point-source component, clumpy morphology, and jet-aligned radio/UV structures.

quotes.quote: We present multicolour Hubble Space Telescope images of the powerful z=2.4 radio galaxy \ and model its complex morphology with several components including a host galaxy, a point source, and extended nebular and continuum emission. We suggest that the main progenitor of this radio galaxy was a normal, albeit massive (M _ 10^ 11 ), star-forming galaxy. The optical stellar disc of the host galaxy is smooth and well described by a S ersic profile, which argues against a recent major merger, however there is also a point-source component which may be the remnant of a minor merger.

We selected the radio galaxy \ at z=2.44 from the original sample of Pentericci2001 for a more detailed study. Its particularly complex morphology may reveal what triggered the AGN or be indicative of feedback processes. This galaxy consists of multiple clumpy components, and is undergoing powerful, jet-induced feedback Nesvadba2008 . A high resolution 4.71 GHz radio map of \ is displayed Fig. MRC0406_radio : a central radio core is flanked by two hot-spots at a position angle of 128^ , which are separated by 61 kpc (7.4 ) and lie beyond the rest-frame optical and ultraviolet (UV) emission. The optical and UV emission, as well as the large Ly halo are aligned with the radio hot-spots, but there is no spatial overlap.

sources.summary: The paper reproduces the 0406-244 spectrum with a weighted AGN-radiation and shock model, with its highest-velocity component represented by a shock-dominated model.

quotes.quote: The 0406-244 spectrum is satisfactorily reproduced by the weighted sum (av in column 11) of models MH4, dominated by the AGN radiation, and MH5, dominated by the shock. The relative weights were calculated phenomenologically in order to obtain the best approximation to the spectrum, even if some line ratios (e.g. [ NeV ] ) are less fitted. Also for this galaxy a SD model (MH5) corresponds to the highest (700 ).

sources.summary: The paper describes 0406--244 as a z=2.44 radio galaxy with two optical/infrared components aligned with the radio axis, embedded in an extended Ly halo and opposite-sign high-RM lobes.

quotes.quote: 0406--244 at z=2.44 consists of two main optical/infrared continuum features aligned with the radio axis (see rush97 for a detailed study). The continuum components lie embedded in a large Ly halo with a spatial extent of 8 along the radio axis. Both radio lobes have an intrinsic RM of 1000 rad m ^ 2 with opposite signs.

gmw_00002727.overview: 0828+193 is a high-redshift radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.57 [arXiv:astro-ph/0412026]. It is a large radio source spanning approximately 100 kiloparsecs, featuring a northern jet-like structure, a bent multi-hotspot northern lobe, and an unresolved southern hotspot [arXiv:astro-ph/0412026].

sources.summary: The paper describes 0828+193 as the sample's largest radio source, with a northern jet-like feature, bent multi-hotspot lobe, unresolved southern hotspot, and optical core identified among HST clumps.

quotes.quote: 0828+193 at z=2.57 is the largest radio source in our sample ( 100 kpc). It has a jet-like feature north of the radio core. The northern lobe contains several hot spots with a peculiar 90 degree bend. The southern radio hotspot is unresolved. laura99 have identified the radio core with the brightest optical component of several clumps in a 2 region seen by HST.

gmw_00002728.overview: 2036--254 is a radio galaxy located at a redshift of z=2.00 [arXiv:astro-ph/0412026]. Its radio structure features a faint, compact core near the southern lobe, a pair of northern hotspots, and a southern lobe with a high rotation measure [arXiv:astro-ph/0412026].

sources.summary: The paper describes 2036--254 as a z=2.00 radio source lacking optical/near-infrared data, with a likely southern-lobe core, northern paired hotspots, and a high-RM southern lobe.

quotes.quote: 2036--254 at z=2.00 is the only source in our sample for which no optical/near-infrared data are available. The radio core is most likely the faint, compact component close to the southern lobe. There is a pair of hot spots parallel to the radio axis in the north. The southern lobe has a RM _ int of 3500 rad m ^ 2 .

gmw_00002729.overview: 2048--272 is a radio galaxy at a redshift of z = 2.06 [arXiv:astro-ph/0610567, arXiv:astro-ph/0412026] that features an undetected core [arXiv:astro-ph/0412026]. High-resolution infrared imaging reveals three separate components, including a central host galaxy with a dynamically relaxed stellar population [arXiv:astro-ph/0610567].

sources.summary: The paper describes MRC 2048--272 as a z=2.06 radio galaxy with three HST components, a relaxed central profile, and nearby confirmed Ly-alpha emitters but no field overdensity.

quotes.quote: MRC 2048--272 was listed in the 408 MHz Molonglo Reference Catalogue lar81 and identified with a z = 2.06 object by mcc96 . High resolution imaging in the infrared with the HST revealed three separate components within 3 \ pen01 , of which the central object was identified as the radio galaxy. The surface brightness of the central object could be well fit by a de Vaucouleurs profile, indicating that a dynamically relaxed stellar population is in place in this radio galaxy pen01 .

Two of the three candidates and the radio galaxy show a line in a 4 hr spectrum (see Table 2048table and Fig. \ 2048spec ). The third emitter has most likely an emission line that is too faint ( < 3 10^ 17 ) to be confirmed in a 4 hr spectroscopic observation with FORS2 at blue wavelengths ( 3750 ). The two confirmed emitters have a redshift very close to that of the radio galaxy with relative velocities of 100 and 10 .
Comparing the volume density of \ emitters near MRC 2048--272 to that of emitters near MRC 1138--262 kur04b , the density in the 2048 field is a factor 3.4 ^ +1.8 _ 1.2 smaller.

sources.summary: The paper describes 2048--272 as a z=2.06 source with an undetected core, a presumed near-infrared host galaxy between the lobes, and a measured northern-hotspot rotation measure.

quotes.quote: 2048--272 at z=2.06 is among the 30 of sources in the sample of carilli97 for which the core is undetected. However, a bright near-infrared object seen in between the lobes is presumed to be the galaxy hosting the radio source. The northern hot spot has a RM _ int of 590 rad m ^ 2 .

gmw_00002730.name: RBPLJ1048+7143
gmw_00002730.overview: RBPLJ1048+7143 is a flat-spectrum radio quasar located at a redshift of z=1.15 [arXiv:2209.05107, arXiv:2204.13051]. The active nucleus exhibits a weak, 15-kiloparsec radio jet [arXiv:2209.05107], displays intraday variability [arXiv:2108.09054], and shows quasi-periodic oscillations during its flaring states [arXiv:2204.13051].

sources.summary: The paper treats J1048+7143 as a z=1.15 flat-spectrum radio quasar, finding gamma-ray flares, misaligned pc/kpc jets, radio lags, and possible jet precession.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: right ascension RA_ J2000 =162 1067 and declination DEC_ J2000 =71 7297

quotes.quote: J1048+7143 is a low-synchrotron-peaked flat-spectrum radio quasar (FSRQ) at redshift z=1.15 Polatidis1995 , associated with the Fermi ray source 4FGL 1048.4+7143 Fermi4fgldr12020,Fermi4fgldr22020 . In this paper, based on our analysis of Fermi-LAT ray and VLBI radio observations, we propose the multimessenger picture of J1048+7143 by assuming that the flaring activity in these regimes is due to the spin--orbit precession of a binary black hole at the heart of this FSRQ. We expand the precession model of deBruijn2020 , and predict the detection time of the next ray flare from J1048+7143, assuming that the quasi-periodicity of its ray light curve is due to the spin--orbit precession at the jet base. In Section sec:gamma , we present our analysis of Fermi-LAT ray observations, generate the likelihood light curve, and model the spectral energy distribution (SED) of 4FGL J1048.4+7143 (J1048+7143).

The 8.4 GHz image showed a qualitatively similar structure with a weak jet pointing to the western direction, extending to 15 kpc projected linear size, while the weak extended jet emission was resolved out and remained undetected at 15 GHz.

In the middle panel of Fig. fig:maps (observing date 2001 Jul 5), one can see that there is a sign of a jet pointing approximately toward east and one pointing to south. In the right panel of the same figure (observing date 2010 Mar 23), at another epoch, one can see a south-directed jet, while there is no conclusive indication of the east-directed jet. Also, the large misalignment between the arcsec-scale and mas-scale jets suggest jet precession Conway1993 .

sources.summary: The paper finds two highly significant QPO candidates in the FSRQ S5 1044+71, including a newly reported multi-year oscillation near 1130 days and a shorter flaring-state signal.

quotes.quote: S5 1044+71 We identify, for the first time, two highly significant QPO candidates in S5 1044+71, a moderately distant (z=1.15) FSRQ. The two CWTs are shown in Fig. fig:CWT1044 (A) and (C). A very evident long term oscillation of period 1134 226 d ( 1127 224 d) at a conficence level 4.9 ( 4.6 ) for the monthly (weekly) binned light curve appears in the wavelet map, emerging around MJD 56000 (March 2012). Secondly, a short month-long QPO emerged during the last flaring state of the source, between MJD 58650 and MJD 59000 (June 2019 and September 2020), with a period estimated to be 116 33 d ( 117 38 d) for the monthly (weekly) binned light curve at a significance more than 5 .

Three complete cycles of the year-long QPO can be observed also in the fitted light curve in Fig. fig:CWT1044 (B), which corresponds to the large flaring states of the FSRQ. The last maximum occurred at around MJD 58800 (November 2019), so we expect the next maximum to occur at approximately MJD 59900 (November 2022).

sources.summary: The paper reports an EVPA rotation in RBPLJ1048+7143 that was interrupted by short opposite swings but, because it was well sampled, treated as one large rotation and excluded from rotation-simulation comparisons.

quotes.quote: These are the rotation events detected in the data sets of RBPLJ1048+7143 and RBPLJ2311+3425. In both cases the rotations were interrupted by short, low amplitude, albeit significant swings in the opposite direction with respect to the overall rotation. Since both events are well sampled these small deviations do not introduce any significant difference in the overall EVPA trend. Hence both events can be considered as single, large _ max rotations.
Both events have not been used in any of our statistical analyses involving comparison between simulated and observed rotations.

sources.summary: The paper reports that blazar 1044 + 719 shows IDV in both sessions, with increased IDV strength and a higher mean flux density in the second session.

quotes.quote: 1044 + 719 : this blazar shows IDV in both sessions. The IDV strength increases from session 1 to session 2. Its mean flux density also increases from 2.581 0.035 Jy in session 1 to 2.786 0.049 Jy in session 2.

gmw_00002731.name: RBPLJ2311+3425
gmw_00002731.overview: RBPLJ2311+3425 is a blazar that exhibits a well-defined rotation in its electric vector position angle [arXiv:1505.07467]. This rotation event has an amplitude of 74 degrees and is interrupted by short, low-amplitude swings in the opposite direction [arXiv:1505.07467].

sources.summary: The paper includes RBPLJ2311+3425 as a well-defined EVPA rotation despite sparse endpoints and a 74-degree amplitude below the nominal limit, treating short opposite swings as not changing the overall trend.

quotes.quote: These are the rotation events detected in the data sets of RBPLJ1048+7143 and RBPLJ2311+3425. In both cases the rotations were interrupted by short, low amplitude, albeit significant swings in the opposite direction with respect to the overall rotation. Since both events are well sampled these small deviations do not introduce any significant difference in the overall EVPA trend. Hence both events can be considered as single, large _ max rotations.

In addition the RBPLJ2311+3425 event has an amplitude of 74 , which is less than the lower limit we accepted. However the start and end points of the rotation are not defined due to a sparse sampling. It is likely that this well defined EVPA change would meet the 90^ limit if we had a longer data set for this object. It is for this reason that we include this event in our sample of rotations.

gmw_00002732.name: NGC 4589
gmw_00002732.overview: NGC 4589 is an elliptical galaxy [arXiv:1812.01629, arXiv:2201.05080] with a LINER nucleus [arXiv:1812.01629, arXiv:0906.2811] and a minor-axis dust lane [arXiv:2201.05080, arXiv:1410.7515]. It exhibits signatures of recent star formation [arXiv:1102.3511] and features a stellar stream and a major-axis taper likely caused by a past galaxy interaction [arXiv:2103.05006].

sources.summary: The paper studies NGC 4589 as a bright X-ray-emitting LINER elliptical hosting SN2005cz, finding young central clusters, a bimodal globular-cluster population, and a 25.8 Mpc GCLF distance.

quotes.quote: One of the most popular targets to study the progenitors of Ca-rich SNe Ib has been NGC 4589 hosting SN2005cz kaw10,per11,fol15 . NGC 4589 is a bright X-ray-emitting elliptical galaxy with a LINER nucleus. It is the brightest member of a loose group of galaxies (Group No.107 in gel83 ). The red integrated color ( (V-I)=1.18 ) and the relatively faint F160W-band absolute magnitude of the surface brightness fluctuation (SBF) of NGC 4589 indicate that this galaxy is dominated by old stellar populations ( jen03 , see their Figs. 2,3 and 4).

We found a small population of young star clusters with age < 10^9 yr in the central region at R<0 5 ( <3.8 kpc) of NGC 4589. These young clusters might have provided a massive star progenitor for SN 2005cz, which supports the massive star origin scenario of Ca-rich SNe Ib. The color distribution of the globular clusters is clearly bimodal.

Adopting the calibration for the metal-poor globular clusters dic06,rej12 , we derive a distance to NGC 4589 from the turnover magnitude of the blue globular clusters: (m-M)_0 =32.06 0.18 and d=25.8 2.2 Mpc.

sources.summary: The paper uses new deep imaging of NGC 4589 to identify a likely stream and a major-axis “Taper” that may be a tidal tail or disrupted disk.

quotes.quote: On the east side, 3.6 from the center, there is a linear structure nearly perpendicular to the major photometric axis of the galaxy. It is marked as a “stream” in the right panel of Fig. fig:4589 . It does not look like a cirrus -- it is far from the group of the cirri and shows no substructure.

The most notable feature in NGC 4589 is an elongated asymmetric excess of light roughly along the major photometric axis. We call it a “Taper” (see the right panel of Fig. fig:4589 ). It is about 8 long, thicker on the west side, and piercing through the stream on the east side. Such an irregular structure could be a tidal tail or a disk disrupted by a past galaxy interaction.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4589 as the SN 2005cz host, noting marginal NUV color, strong H I and dust, and model evidence for recent star formation.

quotes.quote: On the other hand, NGC 4589 (star symbol), which is the host galaxy of another peculiar SN Ib (SN 2005cz), shows a marginal NUV color. Note that NGC 4589 is a strong 21 cm HI source Theureau07 and shows large opaque dust patches MM94, Carollo97, Lauer05 . Also note that NUV colors are more sensitive to dust than optical colors by a factor of 4, so the presence of a small amount of dust can have a proportionally greater effect in the NUV than in the optical colors. After all, the intrinsic colors of this galaxy might be substantially bluer than the observed colors.
The host galaxy of SN 2005cz (NGC4589) also seems to show signatures of recent star formation. It is located below the degeneracy region in Figure fig:tyfy , and known to be dusty as discussed above.

sources.summary: The atlas describes NGC 4589 as an elliptical with Core-halo ionized gas and a minor-axis dust lane, where complex gas and stellar kinematics are attributed to an older merger.

quotes.quote: NGC 4589 (see Fig. Figure_AB7 ): The ionised gas in this elliptical galaxy shows a Core-halo morphology with a dust lane along the minor axis also seen in previous works Moellenhoff1989,Goudfrooij1994b . performed a spectroscopic study of the object that revealed complex gas and stellar kinematics, with gas moving along several position angles. They associated these motions to a previous merger. This latter event may also have caused the minor-axis dust lane by accretion of external material. The ionised gas is co-spatial with the dust lane as it already settled down, which is consistent with being produced in an old merger Moellenhoff1989 .

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4589 as a genuine E2 elliptical with LINER activity, unusual interstellar dust, and a possible merger-remnant history consistent with recent star formation.

quotes.quote: It is apparently puzzling that SN Ib 2005cz appears in the elliptical galaxy NGC 4589 if it is the Fe core-collapse event, because elliptical galaxies generally contain only low-mass, old population stars.

NGC 4589 remains a genuine E2 galaxy. However, from the literature search related to NGC 4589, Hakobyan et al. pointed out that there is a Low Ionization Nuclear Emission-line Region (LINER) activity (Type ``L2'' ^ 38 ), and suggested the host to be a merger remnant. There is also an evidence for unusual distribution of interstellar dust from HST and AKARI studies ^ 39,40 , being consistent with the merger scenario.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4589 as a position-angle outlier with a central dust lane and smoothly rotating isophotes from the break radius to the effective radius.

quotes.quote: Also evident in Fig. FigV5 , is that despite the twists, the galaxies' orientations at their break radii are fairly aligned with the spheroid's outer orientation except for five galaxies (NGC 0741, NGC NGC 3842, NGC 4589, NGC 5061 and NGC 6849) with inner position angles which are twisted by 27^ from the outer position angle.

The remaining outlier in the P.A._ inner < P.A._ mid > diagram, NGC 4589 with a dust lane in the inner regions, R 10" , (e.g., M ollenhoff Bender 1989; Tomita et al. \ 2000), has isophotes which smoothly rotate from P.A. 112^ at R_ b 0.2 to P.A. 85^ at R_ e 71 . This dust lane is visible in the optical HST /WFPC2 F555W ( V band) image.

gmw_00002733.name: J0927+2001
gmw_00002733.overview: SDSS J0927+2001 is a quasar located at a redshift of z=5.772 [arXiv:1802.08177]. Its host galaxy exhibits strong dust continuum emission [arXiv:1105.4199] and contains CO(2-1) line emission that is marginally resolved over a scale of 10 kpc [arXiv:1105.4199].

sources.summary: The paper reports CO(2-1) emission from the quasar host J0927+2001, marginally resolved over about 10 kpc and used to estimate molecular gas mass.

quotes.quote: J0927+2001 Toward this source strong dust continuum at 850 GHz, 250 GHz, and 85 GHz, and CO (6-5) and (5-4) line emission were detected carilli07,wang10 . We have detected the CO (2-1) line and the emission distribution (averaged over a velocity range of 880 km s^ 1 ) along with a spectrum is shown in Figure 2. The line peak emission centeroid is consistent with the optical quasar position and the peaks of the high-J CO lines.

CO (2-1) line emission has been detected and marginally resolved in the host galaxy of J0927+2001 over a scale of 10 kpc. The molecular gas masses ( M_ gas ) estimated from the CO (2-1) line peak surface brightness and the total intensity on the velocity-averaged map are listed in Table 1, assuming a CO luminosity-to-gas mass conversion factor of =0.8 M_ (K km s^ 1 pc^ 2 )^ 1 appropriate for local ultraluminous infrared galaxies solomon97,downes98 .

sources.summary: The paper presents a previously unpublished January 2010 X-Shooter spectrum of quasar SDSS J0927+2001 at z=5.772 and describes its reduction.

quotes.quote: An X-Shooter spectrum was obtained in January 2010 of quasar SDSS J0927+2001 at z=5.772 and had not previously been published. The reduction procedure is the same as the one presented in e.g. Becker15 . The spectrum was extracted optimally Horne86 using 10 km s ^ 1 bins after being flat-fielded and sky-subtracted following . Custom telluric absorption routines were used as presented in Becker12 .

gmw_00002734.name: J0210 0456
gmw_00002734.overview: J0210 0456 is a high-redshift quasar located at a redshift of approximately 6.44 [arXiv:1006.1342, arXiv:1105.4199]. The galaxy hosts a supermassive black hole [arXiv:1505.05262] and exhibits carbon line emission that suggests the presence of a rotating disk [arXiv:1302.1587, arXiv:1505.05262].

sources.summary: The paper reports discovery and spectroscopy of CFHQS J021013-045620, a faint z=6.44 high-redshift quasar with strong asymmetric emission and a very small ionized near-zone.

quotes.quote: Quasar & RA and DEC (J2000.0) & i' mag & z' mag & J mag & i'-z' & z'-J & M_ 1450 CFHQS J021013-045620 & 02:10:13.19 -04:56:20.9 & >25.74^ a & 22.67 0.05 & 22.28 0.27 & >3.07 & 0.39 0.28 & 24.28 All magnitudes are on the AB system. 0.93 In previous papers, we have presented imaging and optical spectroscopy of 19 CFHQS quasars. Since these publications, we have discovered one more CFHQS quasar. In this section we present the observations and features of this quasar. The quasar was discovered by its red i'-z' color in imaging in the CFHT Legacy Survey Wide W1 field \ http://www.cfht.hawaii.edu/Science/CFHTLS . Follow-up near-IR imaging observations at the 3.6m ESO New Technology Telescope with the SOFI instrument showed that it has a relatively blue z'-J color and is therefore more likely to be a high-redshift quasar than a T dwarf (see Figure 1 of Willott et al. 2009).

Figure fig:spec presents the reduced optical spectrum of CFHQS J0210-0456. The most obvious feature in the spectrum is the strong, narrow, asymmetric \ emission line. The peak of this emission line is at 9045 \ which is equal to a redshift of z=6.44 .
The spectrum of CFHQS J0210-0456 reaches zero flux at 9005 , which corresponds to a ionized near-zone size of just 1.7 proper Mpc.

sources.summary: ALMA detects continuum and [CII] emission from J0210-0456, finding modest far-infrared luminosity, a narrow line, and a possible galaxy-wide rotating disk.

quotes.quote: CFHQS J0210-0456 is detected in the continuum with a 1.2 mm flux of 120 35 Jy, whereas CFHQS J2329-0301 is undetected at a similar noise level. J2329-0301 has a star formation rate limit of <40 M _ yr ^ 1 , considerably below the typical value at all redshifts for this bolometric luminosity. By comparison with hydro simulations, we speculate that this quasar is observed at a relatively rare phase where quasar feedback has effectively shut down star formation in the host galaxy. \ emission is also detected only in J0210-0456.

The \ line image of J0210-0456 shown in Figure fig:linemap shows elongation along an E-W direction, roughly aligned with the direction of the beam. The measured size of the source is 879 55 mas 642 75 mas compared to a beam size of 770 mas 520 mas. We used the CASA IMFIT task to fit a deconvolved model image to the data.

sources.summary: The paper compares J0210 0456 with J1554+1937, noting its [C II] velocity gradient, inferred rotating disk, and likely over-massive black hole.

quotes.quote: The one source that allows a direct comparison is CFHQS J0210 0456 willott13 , which has a velocity gradient in the [ C II ] line emission as well as a SMBH mass determined from the width of Mg II 2799; like J1554+1937, its inclination is unknown. The SMBH mass is M=8.0^ +5.5 _ 4.0 10^7 M_ willott10 . Using a velocity dispersion of 80  km s ^ 1 (as estimated from the FWHM-- relationship for a Gaussian distribution), willott13 found the SMBH in this source to be approximately an order of magnitude more massive than predicted from the local M - relation.

The [ C II ] line of J0210 0456 appears to have a half-width at 20 per cent intensity of 140  km s ^ 1 . Equation  eq:vcir suggests a bulge velocity dispersion of 66  km s ^ 1 / (i) . Thus, when assuming that the [ C II ] line in J0210 0456 originates in a rotating disc in a galaxy that also contains a bulge, we agree with the conclusion of willott13 that the SMBH in this source is likely to be over-massive compared to the local M - relation.

sources.summary: The paper treats J0210 0456 as the highest-redshift quasar in the sample and reports CO(2-1) and continuum nondetections with line-luminosity limits.

quotes.quote: J0210 0456 This object is the highest redshift quasar known to date with z=6.438 0.004 determined from the object's Mg II 2798 line emission willott10b . We searched for CO (2-1) line emission in the 128 MHz window centered at the Mg II redshift but did not detect it. Here we assume a line width of 800 km s^ 1 , which is the typical full width at zero intensity ( v_ FWZI ) value found with samples of high-z CO-detected quasars coppin08,wang10 to estimate the upper limit of the line intensity. The 1 rms noise level on the map averaged over this velocity range is _ rms =16 Jy beam^ 1 , and the 3 upper limit of the line flux is estimated as 3 _ rms v_ FWZI =0.038 Jy km s^ 1 . The corresponding 3 upper limit of the line luminosity is L' _ CO(2-1) <1.28 10^ 10 K km s^ 1 pc^2 (see equation (3) in solomon05 ).

gmw_00002735.name: J1623+3112
gmw_00002735.overview: J1623+3112 is a high-redshift quasar located at a redshift of approximately 6.2605 [arXiv:1105.4199, arXiv:1204.2838]. While the object is detected in fine-structure carbon II emission, it remains undetected in both carbon monoxide line emission and dust continuum emission [arXiv:1105.4199].

sources.summary: The paper reports that J1623+3112 has C II emission but no EVLA CO(2-1) detection, giving upper limits on line flux, luminosity, and continuum emission.

quotes.quote: J1623+3112 This object is detected in C II 158 m fine structure line emission by Bertoldi et al. (2011, in prep.), but undetected in 250 GHz dust continuum wang07 . We searched for the CO (2-1) line in the 128 MHz-bandwidth window centered at the C II redshift of z=6.2605 0.0005 and did not detect the line. The rms on the map averaged over a velocity range of 800 km s^ 1 is 26 Jy beam^ 1 . This yields a 3 upper limit of <0.062 Jy km s^ 1 for the line flux and <2.0 10^ 10 K km s^ 1 pc^2 for the line luminosity. The 3 upper limit of the continuum emission at 35 GHz measured with A/C IFs is <75 Jy .

sources.summary: The paper models J1623+3112 with a specified ionized-region grid, finds higher R_S and inferred luminosity than MH07, and recovers damping-wing lower limits.

quotes.quote: Our model grid covers a different range of R_S for each quasar: in units of comoving Mpc, for J1148+5251, R_S = 37, 39, 41, 43, 45,47 , for J1030+0524, R_S = 52, 54, 56, 58, 60, 62 , and for J1623+3112, R_S = 37, 39, 41, 43, 45,47 .
