We also find significantly higher R_S values for both J1030+0524 and J1623+3112 compared to MH07, caused by the increase in the intrinsic redshifts of these two quasars. This also leads to a factor of 2-3 larger inferred quasar luminosities, which is not surprising: the material at the same observed wavelength is now farther from the quasar, so a larger intrinsic luminosity is needed to produce similar absorption. When we use the same Q_ KS statistic as MH07, we further recover very similar lower limits on , attributable to GP-damping wings for both J1030+0524 and J1623+3112.

gmw_00002736.name: ESO 4880490
gmw_00002736.overview: ESO 4880490 is a dwarf, low surface brightness galaxy [arXiv:1407.6044]. The galaxy possesses a small exponential bulge, which distinguishes it from systems with classical bulges [arXiv:1407.6044].

sources.summary: The paper finds ESO 4880490 consistent with GR and f(R) within 1 sigma, but limited by self-screening, rotation-curve scatter, and astrophysical scatter.

quotes.quote: ESO 4880490: Both GR and f(R) theories agree with the measured values within 1 . However, this galaxy is self screened for f_ R0 < 2.34 10^ 7 and conclusions about f(R) theories are applicable only for theories with f_ R0 larger than this. The intrinsic scatter in the rotation curve, which is common to dwarf galaxies, and astrophysical scatter are the major limiting factors which prevents us from drawing a stronger conclusion. This galaxy posses a small exponential bulge Exponential bulges are different from classical bulges.

gmw_00002737.name: ESO 2340130
gmw_00002737.overview: ESO 2340130 is a dwarf, low surface brightness galaxy [arXiv:1407.6044]. Unlike similar objects in its class, it is environmentally screened and has been used to test theories of gravity [arXiv:1407.6044].

sources.summary: The paper singles out ESO 2340130 as environmentally screened and reports that it rejects f(R) at about 2 sigma while agreeing with GR.

quotes.quote: It is found that, with the exception of galaxy ESO 2340130, all other galaxies inhabit regions where the Newtonian potentials is low enough that they are not environmentally screened.
ESO 2340130: This galaxy rejects f(R) theories with with 2 and agrees with GR. This galaxy is screened by environment for f_ R0 < 1.3 10^ 6 .

gmw_00002738.name: ESO 4000370
gmw_00002738.overview: ESO 4000370 is a low surface brightness galaxy with a clumpy distribution of ionized gas [arXiv:0801.2342]. Its velocity field lacks a regular rotating disk pattern, displaying an S-shaped distortion in the nucleus and a central kinematically-decoupled component [arXiv:0801.2342].

sources.summary: The paper describes ESO-LV 4000370 as poorly centered in the IFU field, with clumpy ionized gas, non-regular rotation, and a small decoupled component.

quotes.quote: ESO-LV 4000370 The field of view covers 2.4 2.4 kpc ^2 at the galaxy distance. The center of the field does not correspond to the kinematic center, which is located in the northeastern quadrant. It is marked with a cross in Fig. fig:results . The \ and \ intensity maps show a clumpy distribution of the ionized gas. An intensity peak was observed in the southwestern quadrant at about 6'' from the galactic center, which is characterized by a low emission intensity.

The velocity field, although it is not entirely visible, is not characterized by the expected pattern for a regular rotating disk. In fact, it displays an S-shaped distortion in the nucleus and rotation around the galaxy minor axis. The central kinematically-decoupled component is seen in the residual map (Fig. fig:residuals ) obtained by subtracting the circular velocity model from the observed velocity field, after masking the central regions.

sources.summary: The paper says ESO 4000370 agrees better with f(R) than GR, is self-screened below a threshold, and resembles ESO 2060140 in its rotation-curve trends.

quotes.quote: ESO 4000370: This galaxy agrees with the f(R) prediction (within 1 ) better than the GR prediction (which is > 2 away). This galaxy is self screened for f_ R0 < 2.77 10^ 6 and therefore, the rejection is applicable only to models with f_ R0 larger than this.
It is also interesting to note that the galaxies ESO 2060140 and ESO 4000370 have similar rotation velocities and show a similar trend in the stellar and gaseous rotation curves.

gmw_00002739.name: ESO 1860550
gmw_00002739.overview: ESO 1860550 is a bulge-dominated low surface brightness galaxy [arXiv:0801.2342, arXiv:1407.6044] with an overall rotating disk structure [arXiv:0801.2342]. The galaxy exhibits non-ordered motions in its inner region [arXiv:0801.2342], alongside a rising difference between its gaseous and stellar rotation curves [arXiv:1407.6044].

sources.summary: The paper finds ESO-LV 1860550 spans a 3.8 kpc field, with an overall rotating disk, central non-ordered motions, and a likely infalling cold hydrogen cloud.

quotes.quote: ESO-LV 1860550 The field of view covers 3.8 3.8 kpc ^2 at the galaxy distance.

The velocity field exhibits the overall shape of a rotating disk (Fig. fig:results ). However, there are signatures of non-ordered motions in the inner few arcsec. They are clearly visible in the residual map (Fig. fig:residuals ) obtained by subtracting the circular velocity model from the observed velocity field. In particular, there is a structure receding with v 120 , which is characterized by the highest velocity dispersion measured in the frame ( 80 ).

In the same region, we measured a narrow ( 50 ) \ absorption line. It is likely that it is due to a (relatively cold) hydrogen cloud, which is located between the observer and the galaxy and falling towards the galactic center.

sources.summary: The paper finds ESO 1860550 unusually sensitive to inner-radius cuts, with rising gas-star rotation differences, a bulge-dominated structure, and strong f(R) rejection.

quotes.quote: ESO 1860550 shows an increase in the difference between the gaseous and stellar rotation curves between 0.5 and 1.5 times the bulge scale radius. The difference in velocity curves increases from 25 7 km/s at 0.5R_e to 60 9 km/s at 1.75R_e . This galaxy is bulge dominated (B/T= 0.68, see table tab:basics ) with a concentrated bulge component (Sersic index n > 2 ) compared with the rest of the galaxies, and therefore this increase may be associated with the large velocity dispersion in the bulge.
ESO 1860550: This galaxy rejects f(R) with more than 3 confidence. This galaxy is self screened for f_ R0 < 1.49 10^ 6 .

gmw_00002740.name: RBS 1510
gmw_00002740.overview: RBS 1510 is a blazar classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1501.01974]. It has been marginally detected in the gamma-ray band, and it exhibits ultraviolet flux discrepancies that are likely caused by variability [arXiv:1501.01974].

sources.summary: The paper reports multiple Swift observations of RBS 1510, a marginal LAT detection, discrepant UV fluxes likely due to variability, and LAT-constrained SSC modeling.

quotes.quote: For RBS 1510 (TS=23.0) and 1ES0927+500 (TS=24.5) we had marginal detections while we could confirm RBS 0723, already present in both the 1FGL and 2FGL catalogs. Then we computed fluxes in the 1-10 GeV and 10-100 GeV bands, relaxing the limit for a detection to TS > 9 for the sources that were detected in the full band; however, none of the others reached this threshold in either energy bin.
The only exception is RBS 1510 for which GALEX provides fainter fluxes than UVOT. In this case variability is likely an explanation for this difference.
For 1ES 0927+500, RBS 0723 and RBS 1510, the LAT data allow us to constrain also the level of the SSC component and to determine all the parameters. Therefore in this case we present only one model.

gmw_00002741.name: RBS 1176
gmw_00002741.overview: RBS 1176 is a blazar classified as a BL Lacertae object [arXiv:1501.01974] and identified as an extreme high-frequency-peaked BL Lac candidate [arXiv:1512.05080]. Its X-ray spectrum is characterized by a hard slope at low energies, which may indicate intrinsic curvature or excess absorption [arXiv:1501.01974, arXiv:1512.05080].

sources.summary: The paper highlights RBS 1176 as spectrally complex, with excess absorption or intrinsic curvature and a possible ultra-extreme HBL interpretation.

quotes.quote: More complex is the case of RBS 1176. A fit of the summed spectrum with a power law model returns an absorption column N_H largely in excess to the Galactic value (Table 2). Alternatively, a good fit ( ^2/d.o.f.=12.46/13 ) can be obtained assuming a broken power law model and the Galactic value of N_H , with slopes _1=1.1 0.3 , _2=2.3 0.3 and break energy E_ b =1.3 0.3 keV. The spectra of the single pointings are much less constraining. A single power law model (with photon index 1.7 ) with Galactic absorption is barely compatible with the data but a curved spectrum is clearly suggested by the shape of the residuals.

As discussed in 3.1, for RBS 1176 the observed deficit of soft X-ray photons in the XRT spectrum can be interpreted either as due to intrinsic or intervening absorption or as the evidence for an intrinsic curvature of the spectrum. In the latter case, the extremely hard soft X-ray continuum (photon index close to 1) would be readily interpreted in the SSC scheme as the synchrotron low-energy tail of the electrons with Lorentz factor _ min . In this case, RBS 1176 would thus be a source characterized by a peculiarly large _ min , for which the break in the synchrotron continuum -- which in the other sources occurs between the UV and the X-ray bands -- is located around 1 keV.

sources.summary: RBS 1176 is treated as a new EHBL candidate with dedicated Swift observations, a hard low-energy X-ray spectrum, and modeled weak VHE emission that would likely evade CTA detection.

quotes.quote: Eliminating the already known EHBL, we obtained a total of 9 new candidates, whose nature is further investigated using the SED. We assembled the SED exploiting archival data (especially by Swift and GALEX ) and asking dedicated Swift pointings for RBS 0923 and RBS 1176, for which previous observations were not available. Swift observations are crucial for characterizing these sources, since UVOT and XRT data allow us to outline the UV-X-ray continuum, a key band for the identification of EHBL. Indeed, the shape of the low-energy (i.e. synchrotron) SED component of all the sources confirm the EHBL nature of all selected candidates.

RBS 1176 (left panel), displays a peculiar X-ray spectrum, with a rather hard slope at low energies. While it cannot be excluded that the lack of soft photons is related to absorption (intervening or internal), it is tempting to interpret the spectral break as directly linked to the electron distribution. In fact, the break can be well reproduced by assuming that the electrons follow a power law in energy, with a very large minimum energy E_ min , so that the hard low-energy X-ray continuum is reproduced by the ^ 1/3 low-energy tail of the synchrotron emission of electrons with E_ min . In this case (black line in Fig. 2), the predicted SSC component would peak at energies well above 10 TeV, but the predicted flux would be vary low, due to the very small flux of the target low-energy synchrotron photons. The comparison with the CTA sensitivity curve (calculated for 50 hours of exposure and 5 significance), shows that, in this case, the detection at VHE would be unlikely, also because the relatively large redshift causes a quite pronounced drop of the observed VHE flux.

gmw_00002742.name: J151225.15 + 012950.4
gmw_00002742.overview: J151225.15 + 012950.4 is a galaxy belonging to an interacting pair [arXiv:1812.01632]. It is being fed by a blueshifted gas inflow from a filamentary structure, which has triggered star formation at a rate that matches the inflow rate [arXiv:1812.01632].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J151225.15 + 012950.4 as galaxy C, with blueshifted H I inflow feeding star formation at a rate comparable to its SFR.

quotes.quote: We discovered blueshifted 21 cm emission, corresponding to M(HI)= 2 10^ 9 M_ , associated with J151225.15 + 012950.4 - one of the galaxies in the interacting pair. The blueshifted was tracing gas flowing into the galaxy from behind and toward us. Gas at similar blueshifted velocities was seen in the QSO sight line thus suggesting the presence of a filamentary structure of the order of 100 kpc feeding the galaxy. We estimated a mass inflow rate of 2 M_ yr^ 1 into the galaxy, which matches the star formation rate estimated from H emission. It is likely that the inflow of enormous amounts of gas has triggered star formation in this galaxy.

gmw_00002743.name: NGC 2523
gmw_00002743.overview: NGC 2523 is a barred spiral galaxy [arXiv:0706.3871, arXiv:0809.2478] featuring a fast-rotating central bar [arXiv:0706.3871] and a closed inner ring [arXiv:0706.3871, arXiv:0809.2478]. Outside this ring, the galaxy exhibits a multi-armed spiral pattern [arXiv:0706.3871, arXiv:0809.2478] with regions of recent star formation [arXiv:0809.2478].

sources.summary: The paper measures NGC 2523's bar pattern speed, describes its strong inner ring and multi-armed outer spiral, and finds a fast bar implying a low-concentration dark halo.

quotes.quote: We have applied the Tremaine-Weinberg continuity equation method to derive the bar pattern speed in the SB()b galaxy NGC 2523 and the SB()0/a galaxy NGC 4245 using the Calcium Triplet absorption lines. These galaxies were selected because they have strong inner rings which can be used as independent tracers of the pattern speed. The pattern speed of NGC 2523 is 26.4 6.1 km s ^ 1 kpc ^ 1 , assuming an inclination of 49.7 ^ and a distance of 51.0 Mpc.

The ratio of the corotation radius to the bar radius of NGC 2523 and NGC 4245 is 1.4 0.3 and 1.1 0.5, respectively. These values place the bright inner rings near and slightly inside the corotation radius, as predicted by barred galaxy theory. Within the uncertainties, both galaxies are found to have fast bars that likely indicate dark halos of low central concentration.
The inner ring of this galaxy is a conspicuous closed feature, a rare true ring rather than a pseudoring. Outside the inner ring, the spiral pattern is multi-armed and no outer pseudoring is formed.

sources.summary: The paper models NGC 2523 as a barred SB()b galaxy with a closed inner ring and recent star formation, deriving a pattern speed whose simulated ring morphology matches observations.

quotes.quote: NGC 2523 is an SB()b galaxy with a closed inner ring and multiple arms (see Figure 1). A color index map (Buta, Corwin, Odewahn 2007) shows that the spiral arms and part of the inner ring are sites of recent star formation.

For NGC 2523, good agreement between the observed and simulated size of the inner ring and morphology outside this ring is obtained with R_ CR /R_ B = 1.4 0.1 (see Figures 5 and 13). This corresponds to a pattern speed of 6.2 ^ +0.5 _ 0.5 km s ^ 1 arcsec ^ 1 , or 25.0 ^ +2.0 _ 2.0 km s ^ 1 kpc ^ 1 with an assumed distance of 51.0 Mpc (Kamphuis et al. 1996).

gmw_00002744.name: NGC 4245
gmw_00002744.overview: NGC 4245 is an early-type barred lenticular galaxy [arXiv:0706.3871, arXiv:1002.1386] featuring a double-component bar structure [arXiv:astro-ph/0702434] and a chemically distinct compact core [arXiv:1002.1386]. The galaxy hosts a dust-correlated nuclear ring [arXiv:0706.3871, arXiv:astro-ph/0511607] where intense star formation is currently occurring [arXiv:astro-ph/0511607, arXiv:1002.1386].

sources.summary: The paper measures NGC 4245's bar pattern speed, identifies it as an SB0/a galaxy with inner and nuclear rings, and finds a fast bar implying a low-concentration dark halo.

quotes.quote: The pattern speed of NGC 4245 is 75.5 31.3 km s ^ 1 kpc ^ 1 , assuming an inclination of 35.4 ^ and a distance of 12.6 Mpc.

The ratio of the corotation radius to the bar radius of NGC 2523 and NGC 4245 is 1.4 0.3 and 1.1 0.5, respectively. These values place the bright inner rings near and slightly inside the corotation radius, as predicted by barred galaxy theory. Within the uncertainties, both galaxies are found to have fast bars that likely indicate dark halos of low central concentration.

NGC 4245 has an absolute B band magnitude of 18.0 (derived from BCO) and a revised de Vaucouleurs type of SB()0/a. In addition to a gaseous inner ring made of tightly wrapped spiral arms, NGC 4245 has a very regular nuclear ring, but lacks an outer ring that is often present in such a galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC 4245 as a star-forming ring case, reporting dust-correlated intense star formation, low ionized-gas dispersion, dynamically cold gas, and a small stellar dispersion decrease.

quotes.quote: In Figure 3, we present the cases of NGC 4245, NGC 4274, and NGC 4314 to illustrate the most common properties of the stars and gas in these SF regions. There is a remarkable correlation between the dust and the regions where SF is very intense (i.e., large \ flux). At the same time, the / \ ratio is very low at the same locations, confirming the presence of star formation. The velocity dispersion of the ionised gas is also low ( 50 ) along the star-forming rings. We are thus seeing stars being formed from dynamically cold gas.
NGC 4245 shows a small decrease in _ * only on the brightest \ knots along the ring.

sources.summary: NGC 4245 is presented as a representative DG-type barred galaxy with two density peaks and a structural decomposition into short inner and longer outer bar components.

quotes.quote: By definition in Buta et al. (2006), DG-type bars have a broad A_2 amplitude profile, of which category the galaxies NGC 4245, NGC 2859 and NGC 1452 are representative examples. NGC 4245 has two density peaks both in the lower ( A_2 ) and in the higher Fourier modes ( A_4 and A_6 ).

Again, the radial Q_T profile follows the A_2 amplitude profile: one can imagine that the broad maximum is actually a superposition of two partially overlapping Q_T peaks. In the structural decomposition these two components were fitted by two oval/bar components. The interpretation is that the bar has two components, namely a short inner bar, and a longer outer bar. It is highly improbable that the inner component is an oval, because the higher Fourier modes are also visible.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4245 as an early-type barred galaxy with a chemically distinct compact core and young star-forming ring tied to bar-driven secular evolution.

quotes.quote: We have studied stellar and gaseous kinematics as well as stellar population properties in the center of the early-type barred galaxy NGC 4245 by means of integral-field spectroscopy. We have found a chemically distinct compact core, more metal-rich by a factor of 2.5 than the bulge, and a ring of young stars with the radius of 300 pc. Current star formation proceeds in this ring; its location corresponds to the inner Lindblad resonance of the large-scale bar. The mean age of stars in the chemically distinct core is significantly younger than the estimate by Sarzi et al. (2005) for the very center, within R=0 25 , made with the HST spectroscopy data. We conclude that the `chemically distinct core' is in fact an ancient ultra-compact star forming ring with radius less than 100 pc which marks perhaps the past position of the inner Lindblad resonance.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4245 as a barred S0 with a well-defined blue UV ring, green-valley colors, and star-forming ring structure at the bar end.

quotes.quote: The sample includes five S0 galaxies: NGC 1533, NGC 2962, NGC 2974, NGC 4245 and NGC 5636 (interacting with NGC 5638, an elliptical galaxy). Notice that NGC 2974 is erroneously classified as elliptical both in RC3 and RSA, although an exponential disk is evident from the optical and UV luminosity profiles see also Jeong07, Jeong09, Marino10a . In addition, in NGC 2974 Krajnovic05 found, with SAURON observations deZeeuw02 , the existence of non-axisymmetric perturbations consistent with the presence of inner bars.

NGC 1533 UV image shows an incomplete, clumpy ring-like structure, while NGC 2962, NGC 4245 and NGC 5636 which have well defined and complete rings, although not uniform, in the FUV and NUV bands. Spiral arm-like structures are visible in both NGC 2962 and in NGC 2974. Both the ring and the arm-like structure appear bluer than the nucleus and the galaxy body.

S0s with rings like NGC 4245 and NGC 5636 are located in the so called green valley, where evolving objects are found, while the ring structures are located in the blue sequence. The blue, star forming rings in NGC 4245, NGC 5636 and NGC 1533 are located at the end of the bar structure. The optical and UV images, shown in Figure  fig1 , suggests that NGC 2962 has two rings, one at the edge of the bar, the other is an outer ring. Only the outer ring in NGC 2962 is visible in UV GALEX images.

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4245 as an SB()0/a galaxy with nuclear and inner resonance rings, using simulations to match its morphology and estimate a bar pattern speed.

quotes.quote: NGC 4245 is an SB()0/a galaxy with strong nuclear and inner resonance ring features that are also prominent blue features in a Buta, Corwin, Odewahn (2007) color index map. NGC 4245 is an excellent candidate for such a test due to the good statistical fit of the measured pattern speed.

For NGC 4245, agreement is obtained with R_ CR /R_ B = 1.5 0.1 (see Figures 6 and 13). This corresponds to a pattern speed of 2.7 ^ +0.3 _ 0.2 km s ^ 1 arcsec ^ 1 , or 43.5 ^ +4.9 _ 3.3 km s ^ 1 kpc ^ 1 with an assumed distance of 12.6 Mpc (Garc i a-Barreto et al. 1994).

sources.summary: NGC 4245 is described as a barred galaxy whose central dust ring, misaligned stellar velocity field, inner disc signatures, and circumnuclear emission ring point to central structure.

quotes.quote: NGC 4245 is a barred galaxy with a prominent dust ring in the centre es03 . The stellar velocity field is misaligned with respect to the bar major axis. There is also evidence, in the stellar kinematics (V, , h_3 ), for a central disc in the inner 5 . A circumnuclear ring is particularly evident in the \ flux map.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 4245 as a barred galaxy with a central dust and star-forming ring, an old central population plus a small young component, and published decompositions.

quotes.quote: NGC 4245 is a barred galaxy with a prominent dust and star formation ring in the centre (Erwin Sparke 2003). Its central STIS spectrum was analysed by Sarzi et al. (2005b), who found that the galaxy has an old stellar population together with a small fraction of stars of age 10 ^ 8.5 years old. M ollenhoff Heidt (2001) perform B/D decompositions for this galaxy in many passbands.

gmw_00002745.name: WGD2038-4008
gmw_00002745.overview: WGD2038-4008 is a quadruple lensed quasar system [arXiv:1711.03971, arXiv:2110.01575] featuring a background source at a redshift of 0.777 [arXiv:2110.01575, arXiv:2404.17124]. The foreground deflector is a red, early-type galaxy [arXiv:2110.01575, arXiv:2012.10051] at a redshift of approximately 0.23 [arXiv:2110.01575, arXiv:2404.17124] with a compact bulge and a bright, extended halo [arXiv:1711.03971, arXiv:2110.01575].

sources.summary: The paper reports WGD2038-4008 as a newly discovered four-image lensed quasar whose deflector galaxy has measured structure, stellar mass, and lens-model parameters from DES imaging.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we report the discovery of two previously unknown lensed quasars, WGD2038-4008 (.a.=20:38:02.65, dec.=-40:08:14.64) and WGD2021-4115 (.a.=20:21:39.45, dec.=--41:15:57.11), from the joint use of WISE colour selection, Gaia multiplet detection, and DES image inspection.

WGD2038-4008: system configuration and gravitational lens models WGD2038-4008 has a quite symmetric configuration: two saddle-point images aligned along the major axis of the deflector, and the two minima on a nearly perpendicular line. These components are all blended together by the DES pipeline, but can be disentangled by modeling the DES cutouts as a superposition of four point-sources and a galaxy, for which we choose a single S e rsic profile ser68 .

The deflector has a compact bulge and an extended and luminous `halo', which contributes most of the light and is fit by a S e rsic profile with R_ eff =(3.45 0.12)^ , n_ s =1.90 0.05, axis ratio q=0.77 0.01 and p.a. _ l =(41.6 1.6) deg (E of N). Faint features in blue bands are visible, with isophotes twisting towards 30deg East of North. Contamination by the deflector affects the position and fluxes of image D, whose uncertainties are systematics-dominated. Properties of the deflector galaxy From the magnitudes of G as measured from the cutouts, we can infer its stellar mass.

sources.summary: The paper identifies WGD2038-4008 as a quadruple lensed quasar with an extended host, then derives spectra, black-hole masses, Eddington ratios, and accretion-disk sizes for its lensed images.

quotes.quote: This system is a quadruple lensed quasar discovered in 2017 using a combination of Wide-field Infrared Survey Explorer ( WISE , 2010Wright ) and Gaia ( 2016Gaiacollab ) over the Dark Energy Survey ( DES , 2016DES ) footprint with a source and deflector redshift of z_s=0.777 0.001 and z_l=0.230 0.002 respectively ( Agnello2018 ). The deflector is a red galaxy with a compact bulge and a bright halo while the source has an extended quasar host galaxy ( Agnello2018 ).

We have obtained high S/N observations for the quadruple lensed system WGD2038-4008 using X-shooter instrument in VLT. We use Gaussian fitting to obtain uncontaminated spectra for the A and B lensed quasar images and the lens galaxy. The most prominent emission lines are detected (Mg II , H and H ) as well as absorption lines in the lensing galaxy.

The M _ BH was measured with the luminosity and the FWHM from the broad emission lines, obtaining a consistent mass for both images in the same BEL and a mean mass of log_ 10 (M_ BH /M_ ) = 8.37 0.40 for this quadruple lensed quasar. When combined with the quasar's monochromatic luminosities, we find Eddington ratios similar to those measured in the literature for unlensed low-luminosity quasars.

sources.summary: The paper includes WGD 2038 as a narrow-line quad and widens its macromodel power-law-slope prior because the default posterior is biased high, peaking near 2.25.

quotes.quote: The narrow-line systems The quads in our sample have image fluxes measured using the narrow-line emission from the background quasar. Six of these (WGD 2038, WFI 2033, RX J0911, PS J1606, WGD J0405, and WFI 2026) have flux and astrometry presented by Nierenberg++19 , while the data for B1422 and HE0435 are taken from Nierenberg++14 and Nierenberg++17 , respectively. The flux uncertainties for the narrow-line lenses are estimated from the forward-modeling method used to fit the narrow-line spectra. For additional details regarding the measurement methodology for the narrow-line flux ratios, we refer to Nierenberg++17,Nierenberg++19 .

Lens-specific modeling for RX J0911+0551 and WGD 2038-4008 For system RX J0911, we alter the modeling strategy slightly to increase computational efficiency by allowing the external shear strength _ ext to vary freely while solving for macromodel parameters that fit the observed image positions. For the system WGD 2038, we widen the prior on the power-law slope of the macromodel as the posterior using the default range for _ macro between 1.95 - 2.2 is biased towards higher values of _ macro . For WGD 2038, the posterior peaks at _ macro 2.25 .

sources.summary: The paper models the WGD 2038 4008 central deflector/lens galaxy for time-delay cosmography, comparing software predictions, light profiles, mass profiles, and stellar kinematics.

quotes.quote: In this study, we perform a check for systematic biases in the lens modelling procedure of time-delay cosmography by comparing independent and blind time-delay predictions of the system WGD 2038 4008 from two teams using two different software programs: glee and lenstronomy . The predicted time delays from both teams incorporate the stellar kinematics of the deflector and the external convergence from line-of-sight structures.

Stellar velocity dispersion Buckley-Geer20 measure the stellar velocity dispersion of the deflector from spectroscopic observation using the Gemini Multi-Object Spectrograph (GMOS-S) on the Gemini South Telescope. The measured velocity dispersion is _ los = 296 19 km s ^ 1 from a 0 75 1 rectangular aperture , which is in agreement with a more recent measurement from the XShooter on the Very Large Telescope Melo21 .

Composite Model Our composite model consists of a baryonic component linked to the light profile of the lens galaxy, plus a dark matter component. The composite model assumes the triple Chameleon light profile for the lens galaxy in the IR/F160W band scaled by an overall mass-to-light (M/L) ratio.

sources.summary: The paper measures the main lensing galaxy G using Gemini South spectroscopy, extracts it from quasar-image blends, and fits its stellar velocity dispersion with ULySS.

quotes.quote: The main lensing galaxy G in was measured from one spectrum observed using Gemini South GMOS-S, specifically on mask (14) listed in Table  tab:spectro_observations : ``DESJ2038-4008 A'' (B600 grating), hereafter denoted ``Gemini A'' for simplicity. One slit on this mask targeted galaxy G together with the quasar images C and D the naming convention for the lensing galaxy and the lensed quasar images are shown in Figure 1 of Agnello2018 . To extract the 1D spectrum of G, we used the same method described above in sec:veldisp_0408 . Specifically, we fit the spatial profiles of G, C, and D with three Moffat profiles, subtracted off the best-fit profiles of C and D, and then summed the remaining flux over extraction windows of 1 or 2 along the length of the slit. The velocity dispersion of G was then measured with ULySS, using the same rest wavelength range and the same stellar population models as above in sec:veldisp_0408 .

sources.summary: The paper discusses GraL J203802.7 400814 as WGD2038-4008, a DES/GraL lensed quasar whose Keck spectrum reveals the reddened source and a z = 0.228 lensing galaxy.

quotes.quote: GraL J203802.7 400814 --- This lens, also known as WGD2038-4008, was previously reported in Agnello:18 from a combined search in \ and \ DR1 over the Dark Energy Survey (DES) footprint, and was independently co-discovered from the GraL ERT analysis and reported in KroneMartins:18 and Delchambre:19 . The latter GraL paper reports it as having four detected lensed images, an ERT probability of P_ ERT = 1.00 , and a maximum separation of 2 5. The Keck observation, obtained at a suboptimal PA=90 shows two spatially distinct spectra of the reddened lensed quasar, as well as the lensing host galaxy. The \ emission line from the lensed quasar is slightly physically extended, and the lensing galaxy at z = 0.228 is clearly identified with \ emission superposed on an early-type galaxy with absorption due to Ca HK, the G-band, and Mg Ib (all indicated with vertical solid marks in Figure 4). Owing to it's location in the Microscopium constellation, we have named this source the ``Microscope Lens''.

sources.summary: The paper uses WGD2038-4008 as a reference quad lens system with extended arcs, fitting HST imaging to set realistic mock lens parameters.

quotes.quote: To simulate a realistic HST-quality image of quads, we chose a reference quad system WGD2038-4008 because it has clear extended arcs shajib2019every . This system has a source redshift of z_ source =0.777 , z_ lens =0.230 agnello_2018,krone2018gaia . We selected the model parameters for our mock observations by fitting the original HST F814W image of this system with our model including the lensed quasar point sources and host galaxy as well as the base mass model. This ensures that in addition to realistic signal to noise, our mock lenses will have typical properties of gravitational lenses including potential contamination from the deflector light and realistic extended quasar host galaxy brightness. The values of the light model parameters are given in Table table:light_params , and the base mass parameters values are given in Table table:lens_mass_params .

gmw_00002746.name: ID1390
gmw_00002746.overview: ID1390 is a real submillimetre-bright, near-infrared-selected galaxy [arXiv:0708.0845]. The galaxy is detected at both 24 micrometers and radio wavelengths, and its colors place it close to the BzK selection boundary [arXiv:0708.0845].

sources.summary: ID1390 is treated as a real submillimetre-bright NIR-selected galaxy: it corresponds to preliminary SHADES source SXDF850.62, is detected at 24 m and radio wavelengths, and lies close to the BzK selection boundary.

quotes.quote: All of the submm-detected NIRGs except fo ID1390 have corresponding SHADES catalogue sources. Although a submm source for ID1390 (SXDF850.62) was included in a preliminary SHADES source catalogue, this source did not satisfy the final significance limit adopted for the SHADES catalogue presented by Coppin et al. (2006). However, we found that ID1390 is detected both at 24 m and in the radio, and therefore we conclude that this is a real submm galaxy (see also Appendix A).
Additionally, we find ID1390 as an optical-NIR counterpart of SXDF850.62 (a non-SHADES source), since this galaxy is detected at 24 m and in the radio as noted above.
These submm-bright NIRGs are all BzK selected galaxies, except for ID1390 whose BzK colour is however close to the selection boundary.

gmw_00002747.name: ID300
gmw_00002747.overview: ID300 is a submillimetre-bright near-infrared galaxy that satisfies the color selection criteria for extremely red BzK-selected galaxies [arXiv:0708.0845]. It is identified as an optical counterpart to a SHADES source and exhibits a clear flux excess at 24 microns [arXiv:0708.0845].

sources.summary: ID300 is identified as an optical counterpart of a SHADES source, satisfies the BzK, ERO, and DRG colour criteria, and is flagged as a possible MIR-excess submillimetre-bright NIR galaxy.

quotes.quote: Three (ID300, 445, 912) out of six submm-detected NIRGs are identified as optical counterparts of corresponding SHADES sources in Ivison et al. (2007).

Two galaxies, ID300 and ID445, satisfy all the colour selection criteria we adopted, i.e. BzK 0.2 , R-K > 3.35 and J-K > 1.32 . Hereafter, we refer to this BzK DRG-ERO overlapping population as extremely red BzK selected galaxies.
Submm-bright NIRGs showing a clear excess of flux at 24 m (ID300 and 1390), might be such MIR-excess galaxies.

gmw_00002748.name: ID445
gmw_00002748.overview: ID445 is classified as an extremely red BzK-selected galaxy [arXiv:0708.0845]. It is a starburst galaxy whose observed submillimeter and radio fluxes suggest a complex, multi-component starburst structure with heavily obscured molecular clouds [arXiv:0708.0845].

sources.summary: ID445 is one of two extremely red BzK-selected galaxies; its SED model underpredicts submillimetre and radio fluxes, suggesting a more complex multi-component starburst model may be needed.

quotes.quote: Two galaxies, ID300 and ID445, satisfy all the colour selection criteria we adopted, i.e. BzK 0.2 , R-K > 3.35 and J-K > 1.32 . Hereafter, we refer to this BzK DRG-ERO overlapping population as extremely red BzK selected galaxies.

We find a significant underestimate of 850 m flux for ID445 which has a very red z'-K_ s colour, although the resulting ^2 value indicates that the best-fitting model is statistically acceptable. Since the predicted radio flux is also well below the observed flux, the best-fitting model might be rejected with more accurate measurements in the submm. For this source, we may particularly need a more complicated multi-component model of starbursts, in which young heavily obscured molecular clouds are treated separately e.g. 1998ApJ...509..103S .

gmw_00002749.name: ID912
gmw_00002749.overview: ID912 is a submillimetre-bright infrared galaxy [arXiv:0708.0845] that exhibits very red near-infrared colors [arXiv:0708.0845]. It is a complex major merger consisting of two components separated by 20 kiloparsecs, featuring a northern active galactic nucleus and a southern star-forming disk [arXiv:1205.5452].

sources.summary: ID912 is a submillimetre-bright NIR galaxy whose SED fit improves when H-band data are excluded; the paper flags possible H-band systematics, very red NIR colours, and nearby-object contamination.

quotes.quote: For ID912, we found that the value of minimum ^2 reduced drastically when we excluded H band data from the fitting analysis. This may indicate that the H band photometry could have a large systematic error. The upper limit in the J band suggests that this galaxy could be very red in the observed NIR colours. The noise level in the H band may be too high to detect this galaxy. We checked the images of ID912 and found that photometry of this galaxy could be affected by a nearby object.

sources.summary: The paper describes SMMJ0217 0503 as a complex major merger containing AGN and star-forming components, with separated galaxies and far-infrared luminosity.

quotes.quote: SMMJ0217 0503 is a particularly complex system. It is clear in Fig. fig:maps_sinf that SMMJ0217 0503 consists of two spatially separated merging galaxies, with integrated spectra for the individual components shown in Fig. fig:spectra . The northern source (SMMJ0217 0503a) reveals a broad H line indicating a dominant AGN. The southern source (SMMJ0217 0503b) appears to be star formation dominated (low [ NII ] /H ) with a disk-like velocity field and a double peaked H line profile in the integrated spectrum (Fig. fig:spectra ). However, both components in this 20 kpc-separated major merger appear to be far-infrared-luminous (e.g., Bothwell et al. \ in prep.; Alaghband-Zadeh et al. \ in prep).

gmw_00002750.name: 3C 469.1
gmw_00002750.overview: 3C 469.1 is a Fanaroff-Riley II radio galaxy located at a redshift of 1.336 [arXiv:0807.3966, arXiv:0909.4123]. It is classified as a low-excitation radio galaxy that exhibits broad silicate absorption [arXiv:0807.3966] and extended X-ray emission from its radio lobes [arXiv:0909.4123].

sources.summary: 3C 469.1 is a z=1.336 radio galaxy with IRS-detected broad silicate absorption, blue mid-infrared colors, and a high 8 m-to-178 MHz luminosity ratio despite its low-excitation classification.

quotes.quote: One of these sources (3C 469.1, z=1.336 ) has an IRS spectrum available. It shows a broad silicate absorption with optical depth _ 9.7 0.55 corresponding to A_ V 10 , consistent with its position in Fig. fig_nir_mir_cc . This supports the view that the SED declines in the other radio galaxies are due to silicate absorption, too. The photometric silicate absorption sources show a wide range of colors (Fig. fig_nir_mir_cc ), but only one galaxy (3C 469.1) is on the blue (right) side, where low-extinction sources reside.

Also, the two LERGs (3C 68.2, 3C 469.1) in our sample show a high luminosity ratio L _ 8 m / L _ 178MHz > 10 comparable to that of quasars (Fig. fig_ir_radio_lum ). From this, our data do not support the existence of an accretion-inefficient population among the powerful 3CR sources at z>1 . A possible explanation for the deficit of optical high-excitation line luminosity (for instance [ O 3 ]   5007 ) in our two LERGs may be extinction of the narrow-line region.

sources.summary: The paper presents XMM-Newton and radio observations of 3C 469.1, detecting likely ICCMB X-rays and estimating the lobe magnetic field.

quotes.quote: We present XMM-Newton observations of the FR II radio galaxy 3C 469.1, where we have detected extended X-ray emission, which is likely ICCMB.

3C 469.1, MRC 2216-206 and MRC 0947-249 are located at redshifts of 1.336, 1.148 and 0.854 respectively, corresponding to linear scales of 8.6 kpc/ , 8.4 kpc/ and 7.8 kpc/ \ in our adopted cosmology of H_0=70 \ km \ s ^ 1 Mpc ^ 1 and _ =0.73 and assuming a flat universe. X-ray and radio imaging Our XMM-Newton observations of 3C 469.1 were taken on 2008 September 29 and consist of 17.4 ks of EPIC-PN and 21.3 ks of EPIC-MOS data.

Taking the radio emitting lobes of 3C 469.1 to be a cylindrical region 640 kpc long and 140 kpc in diameter and assuming a minimum Lorentz factor of the electron population, _ min = 10^3 gives an equipartition magnetic field of 9.0 G.

gmw_00002751.name: HSC J091843.27--022007.5
gmw_00002751.overview: HSC J091843.27--022007.5 is a strongly lensed quasar system [arXiv:2006.16584]. It features a red lens galaxy at a redshift of 0.459 that produces two blue point-source images of a background quasar at a redshift of 0.804 [arXiv:2006.16584].

sources.summary: HSC J091843.27--022007.5 is confirmed as a strongly lensed quasar: HSC images show two blue point sources around a red lens galaxy, and spectra give source and lens redshifts of 0.804 and 0.459.

quotes.quote: Images of HSC J091843.27--022007.5 show a red galaxy with two blue point sources at either side, separated by 2.26 arcsec. This system has a source and a lens redshifts =0.804 and =0.459 , respectively, as obtained by our follow-up spectroscopic data.

In this paper, we present the spectroscopic confirmation of three new strongly lensed quasars compiled from previous catalogs applied to the HSC-SSP footprint: HSC J091843.27--022007.5, CXCO J100201.50+020330.0, and SDSS J135944.21+012809.8 (hereafter J0918, J1002, and J1359) at (RA Dec.) = (139.6803, 2.3354), (150.5063, +2.0582), and (209.9342, +1.4693), respectively.

J0918 system was first discovered by Sonnenfeld+20 as part of the citizen science project SpaceWarps Marshall+16,More+16 using over 442 deg ^2 of data from the HSC survey. An expert volunteer, Claude Cornen, identified two blue point-like feature and a putative galaxy in between. A spectrum was obtained with the Inamori Magellan Areal Camera and Spectrograph IMACS; Dressler+11 on the Baade 6.5-m telescope on 2019 April 1 and 2 (Director's Discretionary Time, PI: P. Schechter) for a total exposure time 2 1800 s using f/2 camera, 0.9 arcsec wide-slit, and 200 mm ^ 1 grism. The 1D spectra of the two point sources correspond to a nearly identical quasar spectrum at =0.804 0.001 , based on Mg ii , O ii , He i , H , H , H , O iii lines. The blended absorption lines of the lens galaxy with the fainter quasar B can be seen clearly based on Ca K, Ca H, H , Mg b, and Na D, yielding a lens galaxy redshift of =0.459 (see panel a in fig:figure2 ).

gmw_00002752.name: CXCO J100201.50+020330.0
gmw_00002752.overview: CXCO J100201.50+020330.0 is a gravitationally lensed quasar at a redshift of z=2.016 [arXiv:2006.16584]. It is resolved into two point sources separated by 0.85 arcseconds on either side of a foreground early-type lens galaxy at z=0.439 [arXiv:2006.16584].

sources.summary: CXCO J100201.50+020330.0 is confirmed as a two-image lensed quasar: HSC resolves the pair, spectra place both quasar images at z=2.016, and the early-type lens galaxy is at z=0.439.

quotes.quote: CXCO J100201.50 + 020330.0 shows two point sources separated by 0.85 arcsec on either side of an early-type galaxy. The follow-up spectroscopic data confirm the fainter quasar has the same redshift with the brighter quasar from the SDSS fiber spectrum at =2.016 . The deflecting foreground galaxy is a typical early-type galaxy at a redshift of =0.439 .

This system was first detected in the Sloan Digital Sky Survey (SDSS) as SDSS J100201.51+020329.4 as a quasar with spectroscopic redshift of 2.016. Furthermore, this system also has CFHTLS optical and near-infrared imaging, and the COSMOS-Very Large Array (1.4 GHz) imaging in the radio shows a detection. However, the spatial resolution and sensitivity of these data are not enough to resolve this system. The HSC images of J1002, however, unambiguously reveal the presence of two sources with image separation 0.8 arcsec. This is an excellent example of HSC images being ideal for finding small separation lenses.

gmw_00002753.name: SDSS J135944.21+012809.8
gmw_00002753.overview: SDSS J135944.21+012809.8 is a gravitationally lensed system featuring two quasar images at a redshift of 1.096 [arXiv:2006.16584]. These two point sources have a small separation of 1.05 arcseconds, with a lensing galaxy positioned between them [arXiv:2006.16584].

sources.summary: SDSS J135944.21+012809.8 is confirmed as a small-separation lensed quasar candidate: HSC shows a galaxy between similar-colour point sources, and spectra place both quasar images at z=1.096.

quotes.quote: SDSS J135944.21+012809.8 has two point sources with quasar spectra at the same redshift =1.096 , separated by 1.05 arcsec, and fits to the HSC images confirm the presence of a galaxy between these.

J1359 system was listed as a likely lens candidate by the Sloan Digital Sky Survey Quasar Lens Search SQLS, e.g., Oguri+06,Inada+12 , however follow-up observations had not been performed due to the small separation ( 1 arcsec). We reinspected the SQLS candidate lists using HSC images and re-graded this system as a highly likely candidate. We can clearly observe an extended object (galaxy) between two point sources with quite similar colours (see fig:figure1 ). The quasar was first detected in the SDSS as SDSS J135944.21+012809.8 and has a spectroscopic redshift of 1.096. This system was including as a part of program to confirm dual quasars i.e., physical pairs Silverman+20 .

gmw_00002754.name: NGC 5806
gmw_00002754.overview: NGC 5806 is a nearby spiral galaxy [arXiv:1606.03074] featuring a bulge, a circumnuclear stellar bar [arXiv:0705.4162], and two spiral arms defined by young stars [arXiv:2011.13667]. The galaxy exhibits concentrated nuclear star formation mixed with dust [arXiv:0705.4162], regular stellar kinematics [arXiv:0705.4162], and irregular, asymmetric outer isophotes [arXiv:0709.3505].

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5806 as the nearby shared spiral host of PTF12os and iPTF13bvn, gives its redshift and distance, and maps its H II-region metallicity.

quotes.quote: We investigate two stripped-envelope supernovae (SNe) discovered in the nearby galaxy NGC 5806 by the (intermediate) Palomar Transient Factory [ (i)PTF ] . These SNe, designated PTF12os/SN 2012P and iPTF13bvn, exploded within 520 days of one another at a similar distance from the host-galaxy center.

NGC 5806 is a nearby spiral galaxy having SAB(s)b morphology. The spectroscopic redshift of the galaxy from the SDSS is z=0.00449 . We adopt the distance modulus =32.14 0.20 mag 2013AJ....146...86T , corresponding to a distance of 26.8_ 2.4 ^ +2.6 Mpc When the uncertainties are taken into account, this value is consistent with the kinematic distance of 23.9 1.7 Mpc.

We have mapped the metallicity of NGC 5806 via a spectroscopic programme conducted at the NOT Proposal ID 48-408, PI C. Fremling, conducted in service mode. within which we performed spectroscopy of H II regions in the galaxy using ALFOSC in long-slit mode. This dataset was also supplemented by two WHT spectra (see Fig. fig:galaxy ).

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 5806 as iPTF13bvn's host, summarizing uncertain host-extinction estimates and adopting E(B-V)_host=0.08 plus a 25.8 Mpc distance.

quotes.quote: Host Galaxy Properties iPTF13bvn was first discovered by the intermediate Palomar Transient Factory in June 2013, in the galaxy NGC 5806. There is a wide scatter in the estimated host galaxy properties among various groups. For the extinction, cao13 suggest a host galaxy colour excess of E(B-V) _ host =0.0278 mag using Na i D absorption lines from their high resolution spectroscopy data. ber14 derived a higher reddening value of E(B-V) _ host =0.17 0.03 mag, assuming an intrinsic colour law based on observational samples by the Carnegie Supernova Project.

The distance to the galaxy also holds a large uncertainty. Many works in the literature use 22.5_ 3.4 ^ +4.0 Mpc, =31.76 0.36 for the distance and distance modulus which are taken from tul09 . More recent works use the updated distance of 26.8^ +2.6 _ 2.4 Mpc, =32.14 0.20 by tul13 , or the mean value of all estimates 25.8 2.3 Mpc, =32.05 0.20 provided by the NASA/IPAC Extragalactic Database (NED). In this paper, we adopt E(B-V) _ host =0.08^ +0.07 _ 0.04 mag for the extinction value, and 25.8 2.3 Mpc for the distance to the host galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper presents NGC 5806 as a non-active control galaxy with a prominent bulge, circumnuclear stellar bar, concentrated nuclear star formation, and regular stellar kinematics.

quotes.quote: This non-active galaxy is the control for NGC 3627. observed this galaxy with the HST/NICMOS2 camera in the near-infrared. They detect a prominent bulge and a stellar bar structure in the circumnuclear regions. They classified NGC 5806 as a galaxy with a concentrated nuclear star formation mixed with dust.

The stellar morphology and kinematics are quite regular. The stellar continuum within the \ field of view is elongated along the outer photometric major-axis of the galaxy (PA  =170 ). The stellar velocity field shows a regular rotation pattern with the kinematic-major axis aligned with the photometric major-axis. The velocity dispersion is rising toward the nucleus reaching about 120 km s ^ 1 in the centre.

sources.summary: The paper describes NGC 5806 as a nearby spiral host whose young-star spiral arms contain the two supernova sites and a 300 pc star-forming complex, with MUSE gas kinematics showing disk rotation.

quotes.quote: Here we look at the cases of SNe 2004dg and 2012P, which occurred in the nearby spiral galaxy NGC 5806.

Figure im1.fig shows the HST images of NGC 5806, the host galaxy of SNe 2004dg/2012P. Two spiral arms are clearly revealed by the spatial distribution of young stars; in particular, SNe 2004dg/2012P occurred in one of the spiral arms, where a star-forming complex is apparent with a size of 300 pc. The complex hosts a large number of bright and blue sources, which could be individual stars or compact star clusters.

The central velocity has a clear gradient from north to south, arising from the disk rotation of NGC 5806. At the position of SNe 2004dg/2012P, the ionised gas is moving toward the observer at a velocity of 25--30 km/s.

sources.summary: The paper classifies NGC 5806 as Type III-d based on irregular asymmetric isophotes, persistent high ellipticity, and agreement with prior inner-scale-length and break-radius measurements.

quotes.quote: We find evidence for irregular and asymmetric isophotes beyond the break radius in our INT-WFC image; in addition, the isophotes retain a high ellipticity out to at least 170 . Consequently, we consider this a III-d profile. Our classification is otherwise similar to that of pohlen-trujillo , including very similar values for the inner scale length and the break radius.

sources.summary: The paper produces a single NGC 5806 Swift spectrum, finds an absorbed power-law fit, and notes no prior X-ray spectral studies of this optical AGN.

quotes.quote: NGC 5806 Only a single summed spectrum of NGC 5806 could be produced from the available Swift data. We find that the Swift data for NGC 5806 are best-fitted by an absorbed power law ( ^2_R = 1.16 ), with a fairly typical photon index of = 1.65 . Although NGC 5806 has been classified as an AGN optically Seth2008 , there have been no X-ray spectral studies of this object to date; the X-ray data used in this study are taken from observations intended to look at the iPTF13bvn supernova which occurred in NGC 5806 in 2013 (the supernova occured near the edge of the galaxy and could therefore not have contaminated the spectrum of the nucleus) Cao2013 .

gmw_00002755.name: F563-V2
gmw_00002755.overview: F563-V2 is a low surface brightness galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0107366]. Its rotation curve is fit significantly better by a pseudo-isothermal dark matter halo than by a Navarro-Frenk-White model [arXiv:astro-ph/0107366].

sources.summary: The paper's version of the SMT curve for F563-V2 fits NFW much worse than a pseudo-isothermal halo and shows the usual over/under/over velocity residual pattern.

quotes.quote: F563-V2 This is our version of one of the SMT curves. This curve does significantly worse at fitting NFW than a pseudo-isothermal halo. The systematics seen here are typical for many of the NFW fits: the inner parts are overestimated; the model then underestimates the middle parts, and shoots up again in the outer parts. For this galaxy no R band photometry is available and we have used B band photometry from mcg_opt94 . Assuming B-R=0.9 , which is the typical color for an LSB, this yields a value for the constant case of (B)=1.1 .

gmw_00002756.name: F568-1
gmw_00002756.overview: F568-1 is a bulgeless low surface brightness disk galaxy [arXiv:1707.08085] that displays a regular two-armed spiral pattern [arXiv:0810.3503]. It features a stable disk [arXiv:1707.08085], a central neutral hydrogen hole [arXiv:1707.08085], and a higher asymptotic rotation velocity than similar low surface brightness galaxies [arXiv:1707.08085].

sources.summary: The paper identifies F568-1 as a bulgeless LSB in the sample, noting its higher asymptotic rotation velocity, central H I hole, low H I density, and stable disc.

quotes.quote: Our LSB sample consist of F563-1, F568-1, F568-3, F568-VI and F579-VI. They have a B-band central surface surface brightness _B (0) ranging between 23-24 mg arcsec ^ 2 , spanning exponential disc scalelength R_D between 2.8 - 5.3 kpc and comparable dynamical masses as given by the asymptotic rotational velocity V _ max 105 - 118 kms ^ 1 , except for F568-1 which has a slightly higher V _ max 142 kms ^ 1 . All our galaxies are bulgeless. Our sample LSBs have a central HI surface density a few M _ pc ^ 2 . The HI surface density profile of only F568-1 clearly indicates the presence of a hole at its centre; for the rest of the galaxies, the HI surface density remains almost constant before falling off with radius.

We find that the median value of Q _ RW ^ min , the minimum of Q _ RW over R , lies between 2.6 and 3.1 for our sample LSBs, which is higher than the median value of 1.8 0.3 for Q _ RW ^ min for a sample of high surface brightness galaxies (HSBs) as obtained in earlier studies.

sources.summary: The paper uses F568-1 as a low-surface-brightness disk example, noting its regular two-armed spiral image and rotation curve as inputs to dynamical diagnostics of disk mass.

quotes.quote: In Fuchs (2002, 2003) I have applied similar diagnostics as in the previous section to a number of low surface brightness galaxies and describe here a typical result for the example of F568-1. The image of the galaxy, reproduced from in Fig. fig11 , shows a regular two-armed spiral pattern. The rotation curve of F568-1 has been observed by .

sources.summary: The paper identifies F568-1 as an SMT rotation curve whose NFW fits repeat the systematic inner, middle, and outer velocity residual pattern, with maximum disk preferred among NFW models.

quotes.quote: F568-1 This is another of the curves presented in SMT. The systematics of overestimating the inner part, underestimating the middle, and overestimating the outer velocities again are also present here. Again maximum disk is the best of the NFW models, which is another way of saying that the shapes of the (inner) rotation curves are more like that expected for the stars (albeit with the wrong ).

gmw_00002757.name: F568-3
gmw_00002757.overview: F568-3 is a bulgeless low surface brightness galaxy [arXiv:1707.08085]. It is characterized by a low central H I surface density, high disc stability [arXiv:1707.08085], and a well-determined rotation curve [arXiv:astro-ph/0107366].

sources.summary: The paper includes F568-3 in the bulgeless LSB sample, assigning shared surface-brightness, scale-length, dynamical-mass, low H I-density, and high disc-stability properties.

quotes.quote: Our LSB sample consist of F563-1, F568-1, F568-3, F568-VI and F579-VI. They have a B-band central surface surface brightness _B (0) ranging between 23-24 mg arcsec ^ 2 , spanning exponential disc scalelength R_D between 2.8 - 5.3 kpc and comparable dynamical masses as given by the asymptotic rotational velocity V _ max 105 - 118 kms ^ 1 , except for F568-1 which has a slightly higher V _ max 142 kms ^ 1 . All our galaxies are bulgeless. Our sample LSBs have a central HI surface density a few M _ pc ^ 2 .

We find that the median value of Q _ RW ^ min , the minimum of Q _ RW over R , lies between 2.6 and 3.1 for our sample LSBs, which is higher than the median value of 1.8 0.3 for Q _ RW ^ min for a sample of high surface brightness galaxies (HSBs) as obtained in earlier studies.

sources.summary: The paper compares independent F568-3 rotation-curve measurements, finding only a small systematic difference attributable to interpreting sparse data, and describes the curve as well determined.

quotes.quote: For F568-3 (Fig. swcomp , bottom) we find a small ( 1 ) systematic difference between both smooth curves. This galaxy has been measured independently by SMT and by us (Paper I). These raw data sets agree in detail, and the difference must therefore be due to a slightly different interpretation of the sparse raw data.
F568-3 This is a well determined curve, for which there are several consistent independent measurements (see Paper I).

gmw_00002758.name: F574-1
gmw_00002758.overview: F574-1 is a low surface brightness galaxy [arXiv:astro-ph/0107366]. Its dark matter distribution is well-modeled by a pseudo-isothermal halo, whereas Navarro-Frenk-White fits show a discrepancy with the galaxy's shallower inner cusp slope [arXiv:astro-ph/0107366].

sources.summary: The paper says F574-1 has good pseudo-isothermal halo fits, while NFW fits show the familiar mismatch and optical data still imply a shallower-than-NFW cusp slope.

quotes.quote: While the pseudo-isothermal halo fits for this galaxy are good, the NFW fits show the by now familiar discrepancy: too steep in the inner part, underestimating the middle, and rising too quickly in the outer parts. Maximum disk NFW provides a good fit, albeit with low and high . F574-1 was the worst case of beam-smearing from the sample, but the increase in the initital rate of rise of the rotation curve found optically does not really help NFW. The optical data imply a `cusp' slope ( ^ ) of =-0.49 0.26 paper3 , still well short of the NFW value =-1 .

gmw_00002759.name: BC6
gmw_00002759.overview: BC6 is a faint, blue stellar counterpart to a massive atomic gas cloud in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:2402.14909]. The system is actively forming stars and has an extremely high gas fraction relative to its low stellar mass [arXiv:2402.14909].

sources.summary: The paper identifies BC6 as the faint stellar counterpart to a massive Virgo H I cloud complex, with young stars, active star formation, high metallicity, and an extreme gas fraction.

quotes.quote: Its faint, blue stellar counterpart, BC6, was identified in a visual search of archival optical and UV imaging. Follow-up observations with the Green Bank Telescope, Hobby-Eberly Telescope, and Hubble Space Telescope demonstrate that this faint counterpart is at the same velocity as the atomic gas, is actively forming stars, and is metal-rich ( 12 + (O/H) = 8.58 0.25 ). We estimate its stellar mass to be only ( M_ / M_ ) 4.4 , making it one of the most gas-rich stellar systems known. Aside from its extraordinary gas content, the properties of BC6 are entirely consistent with those of a recently identified class of young, low-mass, isolated, and star-forming clouds in Virgo, that appear to have formed via extreme ram pressure stripping events.

gmw_00002760.name: SECCO 1
gmw_00002760.overview: SECCO 1 is a faint, star-forming stellar system located within the Virgo cluster of galaxies [arXiv:1611.02876, arXiv:2402.14909]. It has an extremely high neutral hydrogen-to-stellar mass ratio [arXiv:1611.02876] and likely formed recently from pre-enriched, stripped gas [arXiv:1611.02876, arXiv:2402.14909].

sources.summary: The paper presents MUSE spectroscopy of SECCO 1, showing associated stellar bodies, uniform high metallicity, extreme H I-to-stellar mass ratio, and unusually strong star formation.

quotes.quote: firstpage - lastpage We present the results of VLT-MUSE integral field spectroscopy of SECCO 1, a faint, star-forming stellar system recently discovered as the stellar counterpart of an Ultra Compact High Velocity Cloud (HVC274.68+74.0), very likely residing within a substructure of the Virgo cluster of galaxies. We have obtained the radial velocity of a total of 38 individual compact sources identified as H ii regions in the main and secondary body of the system, and derived the metallicity for 18 of them. We provide the first direct demonstration that the two stellar bodies of SECCO 1 are physically associated and that their velocities match the H i velocities. The metallicity is quite uniform over the whole system, with a dispersion lower than the uncertainty on individual metallicity estimates. The mean abundance, 12+ log(O/H) =8.44 , is much higher than the typical values for local dwarf galaxies of similar stellar mass.

This strongly suggests that the SECCO 1 stars were born from a pre-enriched gas cloud, possibly stripped from a larger galaxy. Using archival HST images we derive a total stellar mass of 1.6 10^5 M_ for SECCO 1, confirming that it has a very high H i to stellar mass ratio for a dwarf galaxy, M _ H i /M _ * 100 . The star formation rate, derived from the H _ flux is a factor of more than 10 higher than in typical dwarf galaxies of similar luminosity.

sources.summary: The paper describes SECCO 1, formerly AGC 226067, as an isolated Virgo star-forming object with young stars and rich metallicity, likely formed recently from stripped gas.

quotes.quote: Adams+2015 , Bellazzini+2015 , and Sand+2015 all, almost simultaneously, realized that one of the Adams+2013 objects (AGC 226067) did contain a small number of stars and was 10 farther away and 100 more massive than first thought. This object, now commonly referred to as SECCO 1, was found to be an isolated pocket of recent star formation (SF) embedded in the hot intra-cluster medium (ICM) of the Virgo cluster. Its rich metallicity Beccari+2017 and exclusively young stellar population Sand+2017 indicated that it must have formed recently from stripped gas, but there was no candidate parent galaxy within over 200 kpc.

gmw_00002761.name: NGC 4519
gmw_00002761.overview: NGC 4519 is a barred spiral galaxy featuring star-forming H II regions within its bar and spiral arms [arXiv:0909.1816]. Its neutral hydrogen distribution is extended toward the northwest, a disturbance that may be caused by ram pressure or tidal interactions with its nearby neighbor, NGC 4519A [arXiv:2402.14909].

sources.summary: The paper treats NGC 4519 as a possible BC6 progenitor with matching metallicity and extended H I toward BC6, though tides versus ram pressure cannot be distinguished.

quotes.quote: We see from the metallicity measurements that NGC 4519 and NGC 4522 are the closest matches to BC6 (which has [ M/H ] 0.1 ). However, there is no measurement for NGC 4380, and NGC 4535 could still be a match given the uncertainties. Thus, only NGC 4390 is strongly disfavored because of its metallicity. The predicted original \ mass of NGC 4390 also suggests that it never would have had enough \ to form the cloud complex associated with BC6. Furthermore, it appears NGC 4390 has not lost a significant amount of . We note that the predicted original \ mass is usually intended as a statistical quantity and has considerable uncertainty for any individual galaxy. For example, the measured \ content of NGC 4519 exceeds its predicted original \ mass by over 0.1 dex.

Both are detected in ALFALFA, but these data have an angular resolution of 4 . At this resolution NGC 4535 appears undisturbed, but the \ distribution of NGC 4519 is clearly extended to the NW (towards BC6). However, NGC 4519 has a neighbor immediately to the NW (NGC 4519A) separated by only 200 \ Binggeli+1985 . Thus the disturbance in the \ distribution of NGC 4519 may be the result of either tides or ram pressure and with the resolution of the ALFALFA data there is little chance of distinguishing the two.

sources.summary: The paper finds that NGC 4519 has H II regions in its bar and arms, an S-shaped central velocity field, and decoupled inner and outer bar or oval pattern speeds.

quotes.quote: NGC 4519 hosts many \ regions in the bar as well as the two partly broken spiral arms. The central regions exhibit significant molecular gas content Bokeretal2002 , although the star formation efficiency is constant in the inner two effective radii RowndYoung1999 . Our velocity field displays a ``S''-shaped zero-velocity curve over the central 4 kpc radius region of the galaxy. The rotation curve rises steeply in the central few arcseconds, which indicates a rapidly rotating component within 30 , and it continues to increase to 130 \ at 6 kpc radius.

gmw_00002762.name: NGC 4445
gmw_00002762.overview: NGC 4445 is an edge-on spiral galaxy, possibly of type Sab, located in the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:0810.3571, arXiv:2402.14909]. The galaxy is severely deficient in neutral hydrogen gas [arXiv:0810.3571] and exhibits a small ram pressure stripped tail [arXiv:2402.14909].

sources.summary: The paper favors NGC 4445 as the likely parent of the Virgo HI clouds, emphasizing its edge-on spiral type, low velocity, severe HI deficiency, and plausible stripping history.

quotes.quote: The most likely among those is thought to be one where the clouds were separated from NGC 4445 at a time >0.5 Gyr ago. The orbital velocity of the clouds and the putative parent galaxy would now be seen at a relatively large angle with respect to the line of sight.

NGC 4445 (also known as UGC 7587 and VCC 1086) is an edge on spiral, possibly of type Sab, located at =12^ h 28^ m 15.9^ s , =+09^ 26^ 11^ (J2000). Its heliocentric radial velocity is cz_ =354 and the HI mass 5.5 10^7 (Kent 2008). Solanes (2002) estimate its HI deficiency between 0.98 and 1.11, i.e. NGC 4445 has lost about between 90 and 93 of its HI gas. With a major axis angular diameter of 2^ .6 , its linear size is about 13 kpc. We are not aware of HI synthesis data of this object; none are found in the VLA data archive.

sources.summary: The paper disfavors NGC 4445 as BC6's origin because its estimated original gas content is nearly the cloud complex mass and its ram-pressure tail points away.

quotes.quote: Both Kent+2009 and Minchin+2019 focused on NGC 4445 and NGC 4424 as these are only 30 \ to the SW of BC6 and have similar radial velocities to the gas complex 346 \ and 437 , respectively; SDSSDR13,Haynes+2018 . NGC 4424 is in the process of being ram pressure stripped Chung+2009,Sorgho+2017 , but its \ tail points to the SE, roughly parallel to the \ complex containing BC6, rather than towards it. In the case of NGC 4445 the M_ HI -B-band diameter scaling relation of Jones+2018 indicates that its expected original \ content (before falling into the Virgo cluster) would have been M_ HI / M_ = 9.19 0.21 and it still contains M_ HI / M_ = 7.7 ; Haynes+2018 . The observed mass of the \ complex therefore represents almost its entire original \ content. Furthermore, in Sorgho+2017 , figure A1, NGC 4445 also appears to have a ram pressure tail (though much smaller than that of NGC 4424) which is pointing almost directly away from BC6 and the \ cloud complex. Thus, neither of these appear to be a viable candidate for the progenitor system.

gmw_00002763.name: NGC 4380
gmw_00002763.overview: NGC 4380 is an unbarred spiral galaxy [arXiv:2111.00937] with a regular stellar morphology [arXiv:astro-ph/0406243] and an inclination of 61 degrees [arXiv:2111.00937]. It features a truncated atomic gas disk [arXiv:2402.14909] and weak star formation [arXiv:astro-ph/0406243], likely caused by past ram pressure stripping [arXiv:2402.14909].

sources.summary: The paper uses NGC4380 as a VERTICO showcase, describing it as an unbarred spiral with an extended CO disk, measurable inclination, and asymmetric molecular-gas structure.

quotes.quote: Figure fig:NGC4380_panel_plot illustrates the high quality of the VERTICO CO( 2-1 ) moment maps for NGC4380. An archetypal unbarred-spiral, we choose this galaxy to showcase the VERTICO as it appears to be relatively unperturbed by its environment and has a bright, extended CO( 2-1 ) gas disk. The left panel shows the SDSS gri composite image with molecular gas surface brightness contours at the 10th, 50th, and 90th percentiles. The integrated intensity, velocity field, peak temperature, and observed CO( 2-1 ) line width maps are provided clockwise from the top middle panel. We calculate NGC4380's band inclination to be 61 .

The synthesized 7.5 beam of the NGC4380 data corresponds to approximately 600 pc at the distance of Virgo (16.5 Mpc). On this scale, the VERTICO observations reveal the imprint of stellar structure in the molecular gas distribution of NGC4380 in glorious detail. The Northern and North-Eastern outskirts of the gas disk have a ridge of emission that is not found on the opposing side of the galaxy.

sources.summary: The paper keeps NGC 4380 as a possible progenitor because its metallicity is unconstrained and VIVA shows a truncated H I disk from past ram pressure stripping.

quotes.quote: However, there is no measurement for NGC 4380, and NGC 4535 could still be a match given the uncertainties.

NGC 4380 was mapped in the VLA Imaging of Virgo in Atomic gas project VIVA; Chung+2009 , which found that it has a truncated \ disk. This indicates a past episode of ram pressure stripping, however, the direction of stripping is unknown.
NGC 4380 and NGC 4519 also remain candidates for the progenitor, but this may simply be because there are fewer observations of these galaxies and we could be ignorant of disqualifying information.

sources.summary: NGC 4380 is treated as a truncated/anemic Virgo spiral with a C30 value like isolated Sb/Sc galaxies, regular stellar morphology, weak disk star formation, and a sharp H ridge suggestive of ICM pressure.

quotes.quote: Four galaxies have truncated star-forming disks with anemic star formation within the truncation radius. All are classified as Sa by BST and/or by deVaucouleurs (1991; hereafter RC3), although two (NGC 4293 and NGC 4380) have C30 values similar to isolated Sb or Sc galaxies. Images of three of them are shown in Figure trunanem . NGC 4380, NGC 4419, and NGC 4450 appear to have regular stellar morphologies. NGC 4380 has weak star formation over much of the disk within the truncation radius of 0.7 r_ 24 , and a sharp ridge of H emission on the northwest side suggestive of ICM pressure.

gmw_00002764.name: NGC 4390
gmw_00002764.overview: NGC 4390 is a galaxy located within the Virgo Cluster [arXiv:2402.14909]. Its metallicity, predicted original H I mass, and apparent lack of gas loss strongly disfavor it as a progenitor for the BC6 cloud complex [arXiv:2402.14909].

sources.summary: The paper strongly disfavors NGC 4390 as a BC6 progenitor because its metallicity, original H I mass, and apparent lack of gas loss do not fit.

quotes.quote: Thus, only NGC 4390 is strongly disfavored because of its metallicity. The predicted original \ mass of NGC 4390 also suggests that it never would have had enough \ to form the cloud complex associated with BC6. Furthermore, it appears NGC 4390 has not lost a significant amount of .

gmw_00002765.name: D1
gmw_00002765.overview: D1 is the most luminous dominant galaxy in the Abell 773 cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0701833]. Located at the cluster center, this galaxy is associated with a low-velocity western subsystem [arXiv:astro-ph/0701833].

sources.summary: The paper identifies D1 as the most luminous dominant galaxy in Abell 773, places it at the cluster center, and links it to the low-velocity western subsystem.

quotes.quote: Our spectroscopic catalogue consists of 142 galaxies in a region of 18 13 centred on the position of the two close dominant galaxies (IDs 59 and 60, hereafter D1 and D2; see Table tab1 ).

Hereafter, we consider as cluster centre the position of the most luminous dominant galaxy [ D1, R.A.= 09^ h 17^ m 53 26 , Dec.= +51 43 36 5 (J2000.0) ] , which is related to the richer subsystem as shown in the following sections.
The low velocity peak (LV-W-sub) contains the D1 galaxy and resembles the mean velocity of LV-subcluster.

gmw_00002766.name: D2
gmw_00002766.overview: D2 is one of the two close dominant galaxies located in the Abell 773 cluster [arXiv:astro-ph/0701833]. The galaxy is assigned to the cluster's high-velocity peak, and its associated group is linked to an older merger event [arXiv:astro-ph/0701833].

sources.summary: The paper identifies D2 as one of Abell 773's two close dominant galaxies, assigns it to the high-velocity peak, and associates its group with an older merger event.

quotes.quote: Our spectroscopic catalogue consists of 142 galaxies in a region of 18 13 centred on the position of the two close dominant galaxies (IDs 59 and 60, hereafter D1 and D2; see Table tab1 ).

Finally, we note that the two dominant galaxies of the cluster are assigned to different groups: D1 lies very close to the low velocity peak and D2 lies very close to the peak of the high velocity peak (Fig. figvd ).

The likely scenario is that we are looking at a multiple merger where the N--S direction might indicate the direction of an older merger event concerning the cluster and the group associated with the D2 dominant galaxy.

gmw_00002767.name: ESO-LV 3520470
gmw_00002767.overview: The ionized-gas distribution of ESO-LV 3520470 is characterized by three blobs, consisting of a fainter blob in the galactic center and two brighter ones on either side [arXiv:0801.2342]. The galaxy also exhibits a misaligned velocity gradient along its minor axis, which may indicate the presence of a kinematically decoupled component [arXiv:0801.2342].

sources.summary: The paper reports ESO-LV 3520470 has three ionized-gas blobs, a minor-axis velocity gradient misaligned with the intensity maps, and a possible kinematically decoupled component.

quotes.quote: ESO-LV 3520470 The field of view covers 3.2 3.2 kpc ^2 at the galaxy distance. We excluded the \ line in measuring the kinematics and distribution of the ionized-gas in ESO-LV 3520470, since it was in blend with a night-sky emission line. The \ and \ intensity maps show that the emitting regions are aligned along the galaxy major axis (Fig. fig:results ). The ionized-gas distribution is characterized by three prominent blobs: a fainter one in the galactic center and two brighter ones at the two sides.

We measured a v 50 \ over a radial range of 16'' (corresponding to about 4 kpc) along a direction close to the galaxy minor axis ( PA=160^ ). The misaligned velocity gradient is not associated to any feature of the intensity maps, since its center of symmetry is off set by about 2'' westward with respect to the central intensity peak. These features could be interpreted as due to the presence of a kinematically-decoupled component, similar to the inner polar disks found in a number of early-type disk galaxies Cors2003,Cocc2005,Sil2006 .

gmw_00002768.name: ESO-LV 5340200
gmw_00002768.overview: ESO-LV 5340200 is a low surface brightness disk galaxy located at a distance of approximately 227 Mpc [arXiv:0801.2342]. Its ionized gas exhibits ordered rotation, making it a highly regular case for studying velocity fields and mass density profiles [arXiv:0801.2342].

sources.summary: The paper finds ESO-LV 5340200 is the sample’s most regular case, with ordered ionized-gas rotation used to estimate mass-density slope uncertainties from long-slit-like cuts.

quotes.quote: ESO-LV 5340200 This is the farthest galaxy of the sample. It was included in our list due to the wrong systemic velocity ( cz=3551 ) reported in the ESO-LV catalog. We measured v_ sys = 17320 \ and derived a distance of 227 Mpc.

The velocity field of the ionized gas exhibits the overall shape of a rotating disk (Fig. fig:results ). ESO-LV 5340200 has the most regular field of the sample galaxies. The rms is 8 \ in the central 2 3 kpc ^2 of the residual image obtained by subtracting the circular velocity model from the observed velocity field (Fig. fig:residuals ). Only kinematic sub-structures with a size smaller than 0.5 kpc remain undetected.

Since the ionized gas of ESO-LV 5340200 is characterized by ordered motions, we used its velocity field as a test case to study the typical uncertainties in estimating the mass density profile by means of long-slit spectroscopy.

gmw_00002769.name: SJ1613+5103
gmw_00002769.overview: SJ1613+5103 is an interacting system composed of two galaxies, including a younger post-starburst galaxy that displays a jellyfish-like extension and tidal features [arXiv:2106.14883]. This younger galaxy experienced an intense starburst 120 million years ago, with star formation shutting off approximately 70 million years ago [arXiv:2106.14883].

sources.summary: The paper measures S12's recent star-formation history from clusters, finding a young post-starburst galaxy with jellyfish-like structure, a recent burst, and shutoff around 70 Myr ago.

quotes.quote: In this work, we present a direct and independent measure of the recent SFH of post-starburst galaxy S12 (plate-mjd-fiber for SDSS 623-52051-207; designated EAS12 in Smercina2018 ) from its star cluster population. We detect clusters from high resolution, UBR optical images taken with the Hubble Space Telescope , and compare their luminosities and colors with stellar population models to estimate the ages and masses of the clusters. No clusters younger than 70 Myr are found, indicating star formation shut off at this time.

The cluster population indicates that S12 experienced an extremely intense but short-lived burst 120 Myr ago, with an estimated peak of 500^ +500 _ 250 M_ yr ^ 1 and duration of 50 25 Myr, one of the highest SFRs estimated for any galaxy in the modern universe. The cluster population also allows us to fill in more of the backstory of S12. Prior to the recent, intense burst, S12 was forming stars at a moderate rate of 3-5 M_ yr ^ 1 , typical of spiral galaxies, but the system experienced an earlier burst at some point, approximate 1-3 Gyr ago.

This system is composed of two interacting galaxies, one to the Northeast which shows tidal distortions but is dominated by old stellar populations, and a significantly younger galaxy to the southwest which shows a 'jelly-fish' like extension and extended tidal features, which we refer to as S12.

sources.summary: The paper describes SJ1613+5103 as an interacting E+A system where previous long-slit work found an extended tidal-plume signature, while the IFU data show a central Balmer gradient.

quotes.quote: Long slit spatially resolved observations of SJ1613+5103 (top row in Figure 3) have been performed using the FOCAS spectrograph on the Subaru telescope yagi06b,goto08b . The slit position dissects both nuclei of the interacting pair and has a position angle close to that of the long axis of our IFU observations. They report that the E+A signature is extended from the centre to at least 5 kpc out into the `tidal plume' and there is no evidence for a H gradient.
