This conflicts with the results displayed in Figure 3 where there is a clear H and Balmer line gradient; albeit the shallowest one in our sample.

There is rotation in each case although the maps for SJ1613+5103 (first row) and SJ1718+3007 (third row) are somewhat chaotic. SJ1613+5103 is one member of an interacting pair and disturbance of the kinematic field is not unexpected.

gmw_00002770.name: SJ1718+3007
gmw_00002770.overview: SJ1718+3007 is a post-starburst galaxy with a chaotic velocity field [arXiv:1305.3669]. Its kinematic center is offset from its photometric center, which may indicate a past merger event [arXiv:1305.3669].

sources.summary: The paper reports SJ1718+3007 has a chaotic, low-S/N velocity field, an offset kinematic center that may indicate a past merger, and an insecure kinematic classification.

quotes.quote: There is rotation in each case although the maps for SJ1613+5103 (first row) and SJ1718+3007 (third row) are somewhat chaotic.

In the case of SJ1718+3007 the kinematic centre appears offset from the photometric centre (compare with column 2 in Figure fig:images ). While this may represent evidence of a past merger event which could also explain the post-starburst spectroscopic signature; we note that these two galaxies are the faintest targets (see Table tab:targets ) and have significantly lower signal-to-noise ratio than the two brighter galaxies which have smooth rotation fields. The velocity dispersion measurements are shown in the right panels of Figure fig:vel plotted as a function of distance from the galaxy centre. These values have large uncertainties when measured from low signal-to-noise spectra as demonstrated by the scatter and large errors for the velocity dispersion measurements for SJ1613+5103 and SJ1718+3007.

In the case of SJ1718+3007 (object with the low signal-to-noise velocity field in the third row of Figure fig:vel ) the minimum value and the measured value are essentially the same (within 1 ), implying that the quality of the data are insufficient to classify this galaxy's kinematic state.

gmw_00002771.name: SJ0044-0853
gmw_00002771.overview: SJ0044-0853 is a cluster-member E+A galaxy classified as a robust fast rotator [arXiv:1305.3669]. It features centrally concentrated Balmer line gradients and a hint of a negative metallicity gradient with higher metallicity in its center [arXiv:1305.3669].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SJ0044-0853 as a cluster-member E+A galaxy with centrally concentrated Balmer gradients, a hint of central metallicity enhancement, and robust fast-rotator classification.

quotes.quote: There is a hint of a negative metallicity gradient in SJ0044-0853 ( fourth row ) with higher metallicity values in the galaxy centre.

In the case of SJ2114+0032 and SJ0044-0853, the measured values of _ R are 3.5 and 4.5 times larger than the minimum values and therefore the classification of these galaxies as fast rotators is robust.

Consistent with the properties of the local low luminosity E+A galaxies pracy12 , we find all four have centrally concentrated Balmer line gradients on scales of 1 kpc. This includes the cluster member galaxy SJ0044-0853. The gradients correspond to ages of 1 Gyr in the centre to a few Gyr at radii of 1 kpc. These gradients are consistent with production via galaxy mergers and the Balmer line profiles are similar to those obtained from merger models pracy05,bekki05 seen at times of 0.75 Gyr from the peak of the starburst (see Figure fig:images ).

gmw_00002772.name: WISP368
gmw_00002772.overview: WISP368 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy candidate located at a redshift of z=6.38 [arXiv:1701.05193]. The galaxy exhibits compact Lyman-alpha emission and has no detected continuum emission [arXiv:1701.05193].

sources.summary: The paper identifies WISP368 as a robust z=6.38 LAE candidate with line-dominated flux, no continuum detection, and compact Lyman-alpha emission.

quotes.quote: z>6 LAEs We find two robust candidates according to the selection criteria described in Section sec:sample , WISP368 at z=6.38 and WISP302 at z=6.44 . Table tab:cand summarizes the spectroscopic and photometric properties. For both sources, a large fraction of the flux density in is due to the emission line, and we include this fraction, f_ J110 ^ neb / f_ J110 ^ total in Table tab:cand . We calculate the rest frame EW of \ using the magnitudes for the continuum measurement, as the magnitudes are not contaminated by \ emission. In the case of WISP368 , we adopt the 3 magnitude limit.

As WISP368 is not detected in , we plot only the profile for this LAE. All profiles are normalized to the peak values for easy comparison of the profile shapes. We measure \ and continuum emission that are both equally compact, with full widths at half maximum (FWHM) of 0 4 .

gmw_00002773.name: WISP302
gmw_00002773.overview: WISP302 is a bright Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy located at a high redshift of z=6.44 [arXiv:1701.05193]. The galaxy exhibits highly efficient photon escape, with observations suggesting that almost all of its produced Lyman-alpha photons escape its interstellar medium without substantial attenuation by the surrounding intergalactic medium [arXiv:1701.05193].

sources.summary: The paper identifies WISP302 as a bright z=6.44 LAE whose Lyman-alpha output implies efficient photon escape and little attenuation by the surrounding IGM.

quotes.quote: z>6 LAEs We find two robust candidates according to the selection criteria described in Section sec:sample , WISP368 at z=6.38 and WISP302 at z=6.44 . Table tab:cand summarizes the spectroscopic and photometric properties. For both sources, a large fraction of the flux density in is due to the emission line, and we include this fraction, f_ J110 ^ neb / f_ J110 ^ total in Table tab:cand .

According to this model, and assuming the lowest metallicity covered by the BC03 models ( Z=0.0005Z_ ) and an age of 10 Myr, WISP302 can produce 5.1 10^ 54 ionizing photons per second. The ratio of observed to intrinsic \ emission is therefore 0.9 , implying that we are detecting almost all of the \ photons produced by this galaxy. More moderate parameter values result in an observed L_ Ly , obs that is greater than the intrinsic L_ Ly , intr . Only models with a very young age and low metallicity produce a ratio L_ Ly , obs /L_ Ly , intr < 1 . This exercise suggests that these photons are not only able to escape from the galaxy's ISM, but they are also not substantially attenuated by the surrounding IGM.

gmw_00002774.name: P o niu a 'ena
gmw_00002774.overview: PŁniuʹaʻena is a luminous quasar located at a high redshift of z=7.5 [arXiv:2007.14402]. It is powered by a supermassive black hole of 1.5 billion solar masses and resides in a host galaxy with a high star formation rate [arXiv:2007.14402].

sources.summary: The paper treats P o niu a 'ena/J1007+2115 as a z=7.5 luminous quasar with a 1.5e9 solar-mass SMBH, high host SFR, and possible hyper-massive-cluster or accreting-SMBH interpretation.

quotes.quote: A case in point: the 1.5 10^9 M_ SMBH in the luminous quasar P o niu a 'ena at z=7.5 How does this model fare in comparison with the discovery by of the z=7.5 ( t 700 Myr after birth of the Universe) quasar J1007+2115 which is interpreted to be powered by an 1.5 10^9 M_ heavy SMBH? The quasar is observed to be in a host with 80 SFR_ CII /(M_ / yr ) 520 (based on the [ CII ] emission line) and SFR_ TIR 700 M_ / yr (based on total infrared luminosity assuming T_ dust =47 K). Assuming the IMF is top-heavy and that the correction factor is =0.5 (Eq. eq:kappa below), 40 SFR_ true /(M_ / yr ) 350 .

The discovery of P o niu a 'ena thus appears to be consistent with the present model but clearly much more work will be needed to test the above possibilities. For example, concerning the first hypothesis above, it needs to be checked if a hyper-massive cluster can drive an outflow which does resemble the observations. It will also be important to check if the 100 kpc (Sec. sec:downs ) surroundings of this putative first hyper-massive cluster contains a gas cloud weighing a few times 10^ 12 M_ from which the spheroid will form. Concerning the second hypothesis, observations might verify if a major galaxy is in the process of assembling around the quasar.

gmw_00002775.name: Eridanus IV
gmw_00002775.overview: Eridanus IV is an ultra-faint dwarf galaxy orbiting the Milky Way [arXiv:2107.09080, arXiv:2308.08602]. It features an old, metal-poor stellar population [arXiv:2107.09080], and spectroscopic measurements confirm it is a dark-matter-dominated system [arXiv:2308.08602].

sources.summary: The paper reports Eridanus IV as a candidate ultra-faint dwarf satellite, measuring its morphology, old metal-poor population, proper motion, possible tidal disruption, and likely dwarf-galaxy classification.

quotes.quote: We present the discovery of a candidate ultra-faint Milky Way satellite, Eridanus IV (DELVE J0505 0931), detected in photometric data from the DECam Local Volume Exploration survey (DELVE). Eridanus IV is a faint ( M_V = -4.7 0.2 ), extended ( r_ 1/2 = 75^ +16 _ 13 ), and elliptical ( = 0.54 0.1 ) system at a heliocentric distance of 76.7^ +4.0 _ 6.1 , with a stellar population that is well-described by an old, metal-poor isochrone (age of 13.0 and metallicity of Fe/H 2.1 dex). These properties are consistent with the known population of ultra-faint Milky Way satellite galaxies. Eridanus IV is also prominently detected using proper motion measurements from Early Data Release 3, with a systemic proper motion of ( _ , _ ) = (+0.25 0.06, -0.10 0.05) mas yr ^ 1 measured from its horizontal branch and red giant branch member stars. We find that the spatial distribution of likely member stars hints at the possibility that the system is undergoing tidal disruption.

In this Letter , we report the discovery of a new ultra-faint stellar system in the constellation Eridanus, identified through a matched-filter search of data from the DECam Local Volume Exploration survey DELVE; Drlica-Wagner:2021 . The system's size and luminosity are consistent with the known population of ultra-faint dwarf galaxies orbiting the Milky Way. Based on the high likelihood that this system is a dwarf galaxy, rather than a star cluster, we adopt the dwarf galaxy naming convention and refer to it as Eridanus IV (Eri IV) after the constellation within which it is found.

sources.summary: The paper confirms Eri IV as a dark-matter-dominated ultra-faint dwarf galaxy, measuring 28 members, systemic velocity, velocity and metallicity dispersions, unusually low mean metallicity, and no strong tidal stripping evidence.

quotes.quote: We present a spectroscopic analysis of Eridanus IV (Eri IV) and Centaurus I (Cen I), two ultra-faint dwarf galaxies of the Milky Way. Using IMACS/Magellan spectroscopy, we identify 28 member stars of Eri IV and 34 member stars of Cen I. For Eri IV, we measure a systemic velocity of v_ sys = -31.5^ +1.3 _ 1.2 km s^ 1 and velocity dispersion _ v = 6.1^ +1.2 _ 0.9 km s^ 1 . Additionally, we measure the metallicities of 16 member stars of Eri IV. We find a metallicity of [ Fe/H ] =-2.87^ +0.08 _ 0.07 and resolve a dispersion of _ [ Fe/H ] = 0.20 0.09 .

We also find no strong evidence for tidal stripping of Cen I or Eri IV. Overall, our measurements confirm that Eri IV and Cen I are dark matter-dominated galaxies with properties largely consistent with other known ultra-faint dwarf galaxies. The low metallicity, right-skewed metallicity distribution, and high J-factor make Eri IV an especially interesting candidate for further followup.

gmw_00002776.name: J0926+4427
gmw_00002776.overview: J0926+4427 is a compact Green Pea galaxy and Lyman break analog [arXiv:1308.6578, arXiv:2203.07357] that exhibits a bright Lyman-alpha emission line [arXiv:1308.6578, arXiv:1505.07483] and contains a small southern companion with eastern and southern tidal tails [arXiv:1511.05406]. The system features strong, dust-depleted fluorescent emission [arXiv:2305.19177] and an edge-on, bi-conical galactic outflow [arXiv:2011.02549].

sources.summary: The paper uses simulations to interpret J0926+4427 as having narrow blueshifted absorption, significant residual flux, strong fluorescent emission, dust-depleted fluorescent regions, and outflow signatures near the brightest continuum source.

quotes.quote: These two galaxies show distinct line profiles: in J0926+4427, the \ line exhibits a relatively narrow, blue-shifted absorption with significant residual flux ( v_ cen 300 km s ^ 1 and R_f 0.5). On the other hand, in J0938+5428, the line profile is broader, centered around the systemic velocity, and shows little residual flux ( R_f 0.1). Additionally, the EW of the fluorescent emission in J0926+4427 is typically two times larger than in J0938+5428.

Additionally, in the J0926 direction, the absorption line in the brightest-region spectrum is significantly blue-shifted ( v_ cen is 150 km s ^ 1 ), suggesting the presence of strong outflows. Fluorescent emission: \ The fluorescent flux emerges from a larger region than the continuum in both directions, and the 50 contours either enclose regions with outflowing gas (the median v_ cen values within the fluorescent contours is 150 and 30 km s ^ 1 in the J0926 and J0938 direction, respectively), or with optically thin gas (i.e. the black pixels in the v_ cen images, where no absorption lines are detected). Additionally, the fluorescent emission region is largely dust-depleted in the J0926 direction, but not in the J0934 direction.

Residual flux: \ We observe different spatial origins for the residual flux in the J0926 and J0938 directions. For J0926, it emerges from a relatively small and compact region surrounding the brightest stellar cluster.

sources.summary: The paper finds ScUVLG092600 has weak regular velocity structure, a possible outflow filament, a mild gradient not clearly due to rotation, and dispersion-dominated gas kinematics.

quotes.quote: ScUVLG092600 The bright component in the NW of this system has weak, but regular velocity structure across its disk. HST H imaging reveals a filamentary component extending into the south-west, potentially suggesting an outflow. Since this feature is undetected in optical or UV morphologies, it is unlikely to be a tidal feature or spiral arm. The mild velocity gradient may arise from an outflow in the south-western region of the disk, rather than from smooth rotation.

Qualitatively, the PV diagram shows that velocity dispersion dominates the velocity structure across the disk. Quantitatively, the integrated spectrum gives = 84 3 km s ^ 1 , and v / for this component is 0.37 0.05 .

sources.summary: The Green Pea 0926+4427 shows double Si absorption, an edge-on bi-conical outflow, median Ly escape, and possible weak LyC leakage.

quotes.quote: We note that for 0926+4427 there appears to be a double absorption feature at the 1260 \ transition, and we suspect that a more sophisticated model may be required to better interpret this object.

Finally, 0926+4427 has an almost spherical outflow but oriented edge-on with respect to the observer (i.e., ). For all galaxies we find small values for the dust opacity associated with the Si ^+ gas, which suggests that the CGM of these Green Peas is not very dusty. This is in agreement with the separate observations of who reached the same conclusion through more traditional techniques. The terminal velocities of the outflows range from 271 +13 12 \ for galaxy 1244 + 0216 to 1570 +414 425 \ for galaxy 1137 + 3524. We note that the MCMC sampler was unable to converge in terminal velocity for galaxy 1137+3524 and its large value likely represents this fact. (The MCMC sampler was also unable to converge in terminal velocity for this galaxy using the 1260.42 \ \ transition lines where we obtained a value of 2000 .) Aside from galaxy 1137 + 3524, galaxy 1249 + 1234 shows the terminal velocity at 971 +512 139 . Analysis of Si ^ 2+ gas We were able to fit the 1206 \ absorption line profiles ( 1200 1212 ) for all galaxies (see Figure f6 ). For 0926+4427 , the double absorption feature seen in the 1260 \ transition, is visible also in the 1206 \ line.

Galaxies 0911+1831 , 1054+5238 , 1137+3524 , 1244+0216 , 1249+1234 , and 1424+4217 have spherical outflows in Si II, while galaxies 1133+6514 and 1219+1526 have perpendicular bi-conical outflows. Galaxy 0926+4427 is our only galaxy with a bi-conical outflow observed edge-on. (Even though > in galaxy 0926+4427 we have chosen to include 0926+4427 in this group since within our uncertainties.) Lastly, galaxy 0303-0759 is our only galaxy with a bi-conical outflow oriented parallel to the line of sight. Members of these groups are circled in blue, red, violet, and green, respectively, in Figures f8 and f9 . Because \ photons scatter multiple times in the \ medium the geometry of the gas plays a crucial role. Specifically, \ photons will escape toward directions with the minimum \ column density. In the case of bi-conical geometry, photons will encounter the lowest \ column density in the direction perpendicular to the outflow. The orientation of the outflow with respect to the line of sight, then, will determine the visibility of \ photons by an observer. This seems to be confirmed with the data: galaxies 1133+6514 and 1219+1526 , with outflows oriented perpendicular to the line of sight, have the first and third largest values of the escape fraction out of our 10 Green Pea galaxies, while galaxy 0303-0759 , our only galaxy with a bi-conical outflow oriented along the line of sight, has the lowest \ with respect to all galaxies. For a similar reason, we also expect galaxies with outflows observed edge--on with respect to the observer to still show some . However, since in this geometry roughly half the source is blocked, we expect this group to have lower values of \ in comparison to the galaxies with perpendicular bi-conical outflows. This ordering appears to agree with 0926+4427 's value of f_ esc = 0.2 which places it near the median of the f_ esc ^ Ly values from our Green Peas.

sources.summary: LARS 14 is described as UV-luminous, very low metallicity, high in specific SFR, extremely compact, and green-pea-like, with bright central emission and a symmetric surrounding halo.

quotes.quote: LARS 14 LARS 14 is among the most UV luminous galaxies in the sample but has a remarkably low metallicity ( 12+ ( O/H ) = 7.8 from the electron temperature method). It also has a moderately high SFR ( 15 ), but the current episode of star formation is also rather low-mass, making LARS 14 by far the highest specific SFR galaxy in the sample. Its extremely compact morphology and strong oxygen lines classify it as a green pea galaxy Cardamone2009 . Diagnostics of / \ vs. / \ and / \ place it right on the delimiting line between starburst and Seyfert systems, however, and inferred gas pressures in the central regions are found to be several orders of magnitude higher than in `ordinary' starbursts Overzier2008,Overzier2009 . In the \ images it shows a very bright emission, centered upon the brightest star-forming core, and a featureless, symmetric halo of surrounding .

sources.summary: The review presents LARS 14 as a bright Ly-alpha emitter with a blue peak, incomplete absorption, fluorescent emission, and profiles implying low or partial neutral-gas absorption.

quotes.quote: A similar spectrum is that of LARS 14, illustrated in the lower-right panel of fig:cosuvlya Rivera-Thorsen2015 , which shows a bright \ line with a blue peak, incomplete \ absorption, and a fluorescent emission line (seen at relative velocity of +1200 ). Further information may be inferred from the profiles of . While \ and 1260 have cross sections of the same order of magnitude and become optically think at similar column density, the metal abundances imply that \ may be absorbed by gas that is not visible to metal absorption. Particularly in the galaxies of Jaskot2014 and LARS 14, the \ lines do not resemble the strongly asymmetric absorption+emission of \ profiles that occur at high column densities of completely covered gas. Instead they show double-peaked profiles with narrow absorption at v=0 . This indicates that there must be absorbing \ that is not Doppler shifted, but that is also not of sufficient column density and/or metallicity to be seen in metal absorption lines.

sources.summary: The paper describes LARS 14 as the sample’s most luminous LAE, a green-pea galaxy with a southern companion, tidal tails, shear, and broad-wing outflow signatures.

quotes.quote: LARS 14 ( SDSS J092600.41+442736.1 ) LARS 14 is the most luminous LAE in the sample ( L_ Ly = 4.2 10^ 42 erg s ^ 1 ). It is also classified as a green pea galaxy see also their Fig. 7 Cardamone2009 . Green pea galaxies show commonly strong Ly emission and are also thought to be Lyman continuum leaking galaxies Jaskot2014,Henry2015,Yang2015 . Visible in the HST images is a small companion in the south and tidal tails extending to the east and south Cardamone2009 . Our seeing limited data does not provide enough resolution to disentangle this structure photometrically from the main component within the datacube.

sources.summary: The paper finds GP0926 + 4428 has more extended blue-part emission than red-part emission, weak blue-peak behavior, residual near-zero-velocity emission, and an LAE-like LARS14 identity.

quotes.quote: But in four cases (GP1137 + 3524, GP1249 + 1234, GP0911 + 1831, and GP0926 + 4428), the blue-part \ emission are more extended than the red-part \ emission. In the other four cases (GP1244 + 0216, GP1133 + 6514, GP1429 + 0643, and GP1219 + 1526), the blue-part and red-part \ emission are very similar. We also noticed that the \ spatial profiles show a relation with the \ velocity profiles -- the objects with weaker blue peak in \ velocity profile (i.e. small flux ratio of blue-part to red-part \ emission), such as GP1137 + 3524, GP0911 + 1831, and GP0926 + 4428, also have broader blue-part spatial profiles.

In four Green Peas (GP1219 + 1526, GP1133 + 6514, GP0926 + 4428, and GP1429 + 0643), the \ velocity profiles show large residual emission at velocity near zero. From their 2D spectra (figure 1), we find that the \ emission at velocity near zero seems to have more extended \ emission than the \ emission at other velocities.
The other 5 galaxies (LARS01, 02, 05, 07, and 14, LARS14 is the same galaxy GP0926 + 4428 in our sample) are LAEs with relatively high \ EW and are comparable to most of the Green Peas in our sample.

sources.summary: LARS14 has reddening outskirts, central regions dominated by a single or well-mixed population, and color models requiring a large young stellar mass fraction.

quotes.quote: LARS14 All optical data are HST images resampled to K resolution. The radial color profiles monotonously redden in the outskirts, suggesting a progressively older population. The RGB image and the U-B color map suggest the centeral regions are dominated either by a populaiton of a single age, or by a well-mixed distribution of several populations. This is supported by the clustering of the radial color profiles in the SEM diagrams. These points are consistent with the two populations mixture, with a young mass fraction higher than 10^ 2 , with the center reaching 10^ 1 , which is the highest in the sample.

sources.summary: The paper says this UVLG may contain merging diffuse galaxies, with a compact bright component, a faint eastern extension lacking associated emission, and missing imaging in one band.

quotes.quote: SDSS J092600.41+442736.1 . The faint contours of the diffuse emission in \ suggest that at least two diffuse galaxies may be merging. A bright, compact component dominates in \ and . The faint extension about 1 \ East of the nucleus in \ has little associated \ emission. The \ image failed to execute due to a guide star problem.

sources.summary: The CLASSY notes identify J0926+4427 as LARS14, classify it as a Lyman break analog and Green Pea galaxy, and describe its coadded spectral setup.

quotes.quote: J0926+4427 : J0926+4427 is part of the LARS and is identified as LARS14. LARS14 is classified as a Lyman break analog heckman11 , and also a Green Pea galaxy henry15 . The CLASSY coadded spectrum combines data taken at position angles that are offset by 82 ^ .

gmw_00002777.name: Arp 102A
gmw_00002777.overview: Arp 102A is a gas-rich spiral galaxy [arXiv:1308.1891]. It is a companion to Arp 102B, with which it is interacting via a connecting tidal arm [arXiv:1308.1891].

sources.summary: The paper identifies Arp 102A as Arp 102B's companion, a gas-rich spiral whose tidal arm and interaction may have supplied gas to Arp 102B.

quotes.quote: We propose a scenario in which gas has been recently captured by Arp 102B in an interaction with Arp 102A, settling in a disk rotating around the nucleus of Arp 102B and triggering its nuclear activity.
The origin of the nuclear spiral has been speculated to be the interaction with the companion galaxy Arp 102A stauffer83 and could also be tracing inflows triggered by this interaction.

A better display of the tidal arms can be seen in http://www.spacebanter.com/attachment.php?attachmentid =4120 stc=1 does indeed show a tidal arm originating in the gas rich spiral galaxy Arp102A which connects with Arp102B from the north-east.

gmw_00002778.name: SDSS J1048+0055
gmw_00002778.overview: SDSS J1048+0055 is a quasar that was previously identified as a candidate supermassive black hole binary [arXiv:1901.07243]. However, multi-epoch VLBI observations indicate that its radio properties are better explained by a core-jet active galactic nucleus structure [arXiv:1901.07243].

sources.summary: The paper uses multi-epoch VLBI to show SDSS J1048+0055 is better explained as a core-jet AGN with jet-driven narrow-line outflows than a radio SMBH binary.

quotes.quote: The quasar SDSS J1048+0055 was identified as a SMBH binary candidate based on the observed double-peaked 4959,5007 emission lines and two distinct radio components separated by 20 pc 2004ApJ...604L..33Z . To ascertain the binary nature of this source, we analyzed multi-frequency, multi-epoch very long baseline interferometry (VLBI) data to investigate its pc-scale radio properties. The source shows double components with the western feature being brighter than the eastern one.

This brighter component has a brightness temperature of 10^ 10 K, spectral index of = -0.09 0.09 (flat) and is indicative of mildly relativistic beaming. In contrast, the faint component has a lower brightness temperature of 10^ 8-9 K and steep spectrum. These clues are consistent with a core--jet structure, moreover, the apparent separation speed between the two components is much higher than the expected orbital motion in a binary SMBH. Thus the present study excludes the association of the two VLBI components with the cores of a SMBH binary, although the SMBH binary possibility (e.g., a pair of radio-loud and radio-quiet AGNs) is not fully ruled out. In the single active galactic nucleus (AGN) scenario, the double-peaked optical emission lines can originate from the jet interacting with the narrow-line region as indicated by a change in the jet direction at 140 pc. galaxies: active --- galaxies: individual (SDSS J1048+0055) --- quasars: emission lines --- radio continuum: galaxies --- black hole physics A central supermassive black hole (SMBH, M _ = 10^6-10^ 10 M _ ) is putatively hosted by every massive galaxy .

gmw_00002779.name: DCBH-1
gmw_00002779.overview: DCBH-1 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate identified in the PEARLS/NEP field [arXiv:2308.07260]. Photometric observations of the source reveal an extremely red continuum slope through the NIRCam bands [arXiv:2308.07260].

sources.summary: The paper identifies DCBH-1 as one of three PEARLS/NEP objects whose NIRCam SEDs fit Pacucci16 DCBH models, with red continua and brightness suitable for spectroscopy.

quotes.quote: Consequently, three sources with IDs 21567, 22802, and 14830 revealed acceptable fits with ^ 2 _ < 3 --from now on, we refer to the three DCBH candidates as DCBH-1, DCBH-2, and DCBH-3, designated in arbitrary order. Based on the minimum and maximum degrees of freedom (2 and 6), the corresponding limits of the P-values for the selected ^ 2 _ range are 0.049 and 0.006. Therefore, considering a maximum ^ 2 _ of 3 is quite generous for this analysis and ensures that the upper limits on the comoving density of DCBH host halos in Section sec:limits are conservative. The fit parameters and the source information of all detections are listed in Table tab:fit-params .

These three candidates are deemed suitable for follow-up spectroscopy due to their high brightness and relatively high signal-to-noise ratios. Thumbnail images and SED fits of these three candidates are shown in Figure fig:candidates-SEDs . As expected, the photometric SEDs exhibit extremely red continuum slopes through the NIRCam bands.

gmw_00002780.overview: DCBH-2 is a high-redshift galaxy candidate identified as a potential direct collapse black hole host [arXiv:2308.07260]. Photometric observations of the source reveal an extremely red continuum slope through the NIRCam bands [arXiv:2308.07260].

gmw_00002782.name: J0749+2255
gmw_00002782.overview: J0749+2255 is a dual quasar system at redshift z=2.17 hosted by an ongoing galaxy merger [arXiv:2209.11249, arXiv:2403.04002]. The host galaxies are massive, disk-dominated systems [arXiv:2209.11249] experiencing intense starburst activity [arXiv:2403.04002] and displaying low surface brightness tidal features [arXiv:2209.11249].

sources.summary: The paper confirms SDSS J0749+2255 as a z=2.17 kiloparsec-scale dual quasar in an ongoing galaxy merger with tidal features and massive compact disk-dominated hosts.

quotes.quote: Here we report multi-wavelength observations of \ ( \ for short) as the first kiloparsec (kpc)-scale dual quasar confirmed to be hosted by a galaxy merger at cosmic noon. Hubble Space Telescope (HST) near-infrared (NIR) imaging reveals extended host galaxies underlying the much brighter compact quasar nuclei (with a separation of 0 46 corresponding to 3.8 kpc at z=2.17 ) and low surface brightness tidal features as conclusive evidence for ongoing galactic interactions. The detection of both extended host galaxies and tidal features unambiguously establishes \ as a pair of quasars hosted by an ongoing galaxy merger. We also present Keck NIR imaging assisted by adaptive optics that better resolves the host galaxy structure, spatially resolved HST optical and Gemini optical and NIR spectroscopy, Chandra X-ray imaging spectroscopy, and NSF's Karl Jansky Very Large Array radio dual-band imaging in its most extended configuration, all lending support to the dual quasar hypothesis.

Unlike their low-redshift low-luminosity counterparts, the high-redshift dual quasar is hosted by two massive (with stellar masses of 10^ 11.5 M_ ) compact (with effective radii of 1.8 kpc and 1.1 kpc) disk-dominated galaxies. The disk dominance may be critical for efficient gas fueling onto the central SMBHs producing two luminous quasars in the early-stage merger. The apparent lack of stellar bulges and that \ already follows the local SMBH mass--host stellar mass relation are at odds with the canonical SMBH-host co-evolution picture, suggesting that at least some SMBHs may have formed before their host stellar bulges. While still at kpc-scale separations where the host-galaxy gravitational potential dominates, the SMBHs may evolve into a gravitationally bound binary system in 0.22 Gyr.

sources.summary: The paper studies J0749+2255 as a z=2.17 dual quasar in a starbursting host, finding PAH-based star formation, weak molecular-gas content, tidal tails, and no quasar-driven outflow.

quotes.quote: We present /MIRI mid-infrared integral field spectroscopy of J074922.96+225511.7, a dual quasar with a projected separation of 3.8 kilo-parsec at a redshift z of 2.17. We detect spatially extended \ and polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon (PAH) 3.3 \ emissions from the star formation activity in its host galaxy. We derive the SFR of 10 ^ 3.0 0.2 M _ yr ^ 1 using PAH 3.3 , which is five times higher than that derived from the cutoff luminosity of the infrared luminosity function for galaxies at z 2 .

While the SFR of J0749+2255 agrees with that of star-forming galaxies of comparable stellar mass at the same redshifts, its molecular gas content falls short of expectations based on the molecular Kennicutt-Schmidt law. This discrepancy may result from molecular gas depletion due to the longer elevated stage of star formation, even after the molecular gas reservoir is depleted. We do not observe any quasar-driven outflow that impacts PAH and \ in the host galaxy based on the spatially resolved maps. From the expected flux in PAH-based star formation, the \ line likely originates from the star-forming regions in the host galaxy. Our study highlights the stardust nature of J0749+2255, indicating a potential connection between the dual quasar phase and intense star formation activities.

sources.summary: The paper presents J0749+2255 as a z=2.17, 3.8 kpc dual quasar whose similar massive black holes sit in ionized gas with disk-like kinematics.

quotes.quote: Here we present the first detailed study of a 0.46'' , 3.8 kpc separation, VODKA-selected dual quasar, J0749+2255, at z=2.17 using JWST/NIRSpec integral field unit spectroscopy. This is one of the most distant, small separation dual quasars identified today. Dual quasars at cosmic noon are not well characterized. We detect the faint ionized gas of the host galaxy, best traced by the narrow \ emission.

Line ratio diagnostics show a mix of ionization from the two quasars and intense star formation. The spatially-resolved spectra of the two quasars suggest that they have very similar black hole properties (two M_ BH 10^9 \ M _ with large Eddington ratio reaching L/L_ Edd 0.2 ) hinting at the possible synchronized growth and accretion from the same gas supply. Surprisingly, the ionized gas kinematics suggest an extended, rotating disk rather than a disturbed system that would be expected in a major gas-rich galaxy merger.

sources.summary: The paper describes J0749 as one of two HST-resolved double-quasar candidates, with point-like cores of comparable flux and VLBA confirmation of its double-quasar nature.

quotes.quote: Two of them (J0749 and J0841) reveal two point-like cores separated by 0 5 with comparable fluxes and similar colors, with no obvious detection of extended features. In addition, the optical spectra from SDSS (Figure 2 and Extended Data Figure 3) enclosing all light within a 2-3 \ diameter fiber rule out an obvious quasar-star superposition or (unrelated) projected quasar pairs. Therefore these two objects are strongly favored for physical quasar pairs or lensed quasars.

For J0749, while we have not obtained spatially-resolved spectroscopy, a 15 GHz (2 cm wavelength) image obtained with the Very Long Baseline Array (VLBA) shows both cores (Extended Data Figure 4), confirming its double-quasar nature. These two quasars must be at the same redshift given the lack of multiple emission-line systems in the SDSS spectrum.

sources.summary: The paper identifies J0749+2255 as a known 0.46-arcsec dual quasar with a 33 pc VLBA-Gaia offset and possible additional pc-scale SMBH component.

quotes.quote: J0749+2255 shows a 4.0 0.6 mas (corresponding to 33 pc) VLBA-Gaia offset. The radio component has a inverted spectral index of 0.24, indicating a self-absorbed core. J0749+2255 was already identified as a 0 46 dual quasar ChenYC2022 , with both nuclei detected in our VLBA images and reported in Shen2021 . Gaia only reports one detection for J0749+2255, so the Gaia optical position may be affected by the blended second nucleus, leading to the offset. Nonetheless, the possibility of a third SMBH at a pc scale centered at the optical component cannot be ruled out.

sources.summary: The paper treats J0749+2255 as a confirmed high-redshift dual quasar whose dual interpretation is favored, with preliminary VLBA observations supporting that scenario.

quotes.quote: In addition to the classifications defined above, we also add ``dual quasar'', which include the two confirmed dual quasars (J0749+2255 and J0841+4825) from Shen2021 and a dual quasar candidate (J0459 0714) showing a tidal feature, ``triple quasar'', which includes a triple quasar candidate (J0455 4456) showing a tidal feature, and ``quad lens", which includes a quadruply lensed quasar (J2218 3322) with the Einstein ring and the central lens galaxy.

J0749+2255 and J0841+4825 have been reported in Shen2021 , and favor the dual quasar scenario (although the lensed quasar scenario cannot be entirely ruled out). Based on statistical arguments, Shen2021 argued that the abundance of high-redshift sub-arcsec gravitational lens is insufficient to account for most of the resolved pairs in our systematic search.
Shen2021 reported preliminary VLBA observations of J0749+2255, supporting the dual quasar scenario.

gmw_00002783.name: SDSSJ022556.0+003026
gmw_00002783.overview: SDSSJ022556.0+003026 is an active galactic nucleus and quasar [arXiv:2206.10056]. Over a period of approximately four years, the galaxy exhibited a gradual rise in its Balmer decrement ratio [arXiv:2206.10056].

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSSJ022556.0+003026 as a Balmer-decrement example, finding similar equivalent-width trends, possible late-epoch saturation in one line, and a gradual ratio rise over about four years.

quotes.quote: To assess the change in the behaviour of the / ratio as a function of time, in Figure fig:balmer_decre we show the trends of EW( ), EW( ), and EW( )/EW( ), versus the observed date (in MJD) for two exemplary sources - SDSSJ022556.0+003026 macleod2016 ; and SDSSJ141324.27+530527.0 Wang2018ApJ...858...49W . For the first source (SDSSJ022556.0+003026), we see that the overall trend between and are similar which is thus reflected in the trend with / ratio as a function of time. There is an indication of saturation in the EW( ) closer to the latest epoch - we see a downturn in the EW( ) at and after MJD=56544. Although, the EW( ) shows no such indication for saturation. This may be attributed to the longer response time for relative to emission see e.g., 2020ApJ...903...51W .

We compare with the fiducial value (= 3.1) for the / ratio from previous studies in these plots and notice a gradual rise in the / ratio as the time progresses for both these sources. For the first source the overall time between the start and the end is 1417 days ( 4 years), while for the second source, this duration is 1892 days ( 5 years).

gmw_00002784.name: SDSSJ074819.0+335311.0
gmw_00002784.overview: SDSSJ074819.0+335311.0 is an active galactic nucleus observed across two epochs [arXiv:2206.10056]. During these observations, the source transitioned from a Population A to a Population B classification, exhibiting changes in its full width at half maximum and line-strength parameters [arXiv:2206.10056].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSSJ074819.0+335311.0 as a robust two-epoch candidate transitioning from Population A to Population B, with significant, well-constrained changes in FWHM and line-strength parameters.

quotes.quote: Another source, SDSSJ074819.0+335311.0 graham2020 has 2 epochs where the source goes from A2 (FWHM( ) = 3308.01 58.11 , = 0.82 0.04) to B1 (FWHM( ) = 4466.68 104.47 , = 0.42 0.01). Here, we have a larger change both in FWHM and and the estimates are robust given the small uncertainties associated with them. This can be considered as a true candidate for the transition from Population A to Population B class.

gmw_00002785.name: SDSSJ093017.7+470721.7
gmw_00002785.overview: SDSSJ093017.7+470721.7 is an active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2206.10056]. Observations across two epochs show the source transitioned from a B1 to an A1 state, with its line-strength parameter remaining unchanged [arXiv:2206.10056].

sources.summary: The paper describes SDSSJ093017.7+470721.7 as a two-epoch source moving only slightly along EV1, from B1 to A1, with the line-strength parameter remaining unchanged.

quotes.quote: Another source from graham2020 (SDSSJ093017.7+470721.7) has 2 epochs where the source transitions from a B1 (FWHM( ) = 4395.03 70.15 , = 0.21 0.01) to A1 (FWHM( ) = 3974.48 21.64 , = 0.21 0.01). Similar to the first source (SDSSJ104705.16+544405.8) it doesn't move much along the EV1. Additionally, the remained unchanged for this source even with the small transition from B1 to A1.

gmw_00002786.name: SDSSJ110349.2+312416.7
gmw_00002786.overview: SDSSJ110349.2+312416.7 is an active galactic nucleus that exhibits variations in its luminosity and Eddington ratio [arXiv:2206.10056]. Over multiple epochs, the source transitioned from spectral class Population A1 to Population B and back [arXiv:2206.10056].

sources.summary: The paper follows SDSSJ110349.2+312416.7 across multiple epochs, finding large swings in inferred black-hole mass, luminosity, Eddington ratio, and an EV1 transition from A1 to Population B and back.

quotes.quote: For the second source, i.e., SDSSJ110349.2+312416.7, we have 4 epochs of spectral information from which we estimate the and per epoch. The source starts (MJD = 53472) with a relatively low BH mass (for this source), log = 7.93 0.03, and the highest AGN luminosity for this source among all available epochs, log = 44.03 0.01. The corresponding Eddington ratio is log = -1.07 0.04, which is the maximum estimated for this source. Then, in the next epoch (MJD = 56367, MJD = 2895), the BH mass goes up by more than an order, log = 9.1 0.1, while the AGN luminosity goes down, = 43.86 0.01. The corresponding Eddington ratio goes down as well to attain its lowest value, log = -2.41 0.1.

For the final epoch (MJD = 58514, MJD = 5042), the FWHM( ) drops close to its initial value at 3471.89 343.9 , and the falls down a bit to 0.44 0.06. The spectral type returns to its initial class of A1. Hence, this source, unlike the previous one, makes a transition from Population A to Population B and back with its significant variations in the FWHM( ) and .

gmw_00002787.name: SDSSJ141324.27+530527.0
gmw_00002787.overview: SDSSJ141324.27+530527.0 is a changing-look, hypervariable quasar located at a redshift of z=0.456 [arXiv:1906.10138]. Its optical luminosity increased tenfold between 2014 and 2017 due to intrinsic accretion-power changes [arXiv:1906.10138], while its late-time Balmer-decrement ratio gradually rose over approximately five years [arXiv:2206.10056].

sources.summary: The paper highlights SDSSJ141324.27+530527.0 as the most observed sample source, regularly monitored by SDSS RM, with late-time Balmer-decrement behavior changing over about five years.

quotes.quote: The most observed one is SDSSJ141324.27+530527.0 with 72 epochs, which we plot in Figure fig:spectra-compare-Wang2018 . This source is included in the sample for the SDSS RM campaign, which is why the source is observed regularly (onward 56660 MJD) in comparison to most of the sources in our sample.

On the other hand, for SDSSJ141324.27+530527.0, especially close to the end, the variation shown in is much larger than (by a factor 2.5) although the trends for and are similar. This is then reflected in the late rise in the / ratio. We compare with the fiducial value (= 3.1) for the / ratio from previous studies in these plots and notice a gradual rise in the / ratio as the time progresses for both these sources. For the first source the overall time between the start and the end is 1417 days ( 4 years), while for the second source, this duration is 1892 days ( 5 years).

sources.summary: The paper presents RMID 017 as a z=0.456 changing-look hypervariable quasar whose line and continuum behavior indicates intrinsic accretion-power changes rather than obscuration or a discrete flare.

quotes.quote: We analyze extensive spectroscopic and photometric data of the hypervariable quasar (RMID 017) at z=0.456 , an optical ``changing look" quasar from the Sloan Digital Sky Survey Reverberation Mapping project that increased in optical luminosity by a factor 10 between 2014 and 2017. The observed broad emission lines all respond in luminosity and width to the changing optical continuum, as expected for photoionization in a stratified, virialized broad emission line region. The luminosity changes therefore result from intrinsic changes in accretion power rather than variable obscuration. The variability is continuous and apparently stochastic, disfavoring an origin as a discrete event such as a tidal disruption flare or microlensing event. It is coordinated on day timescales with blue leading red, consistent with reprocessing powering the entire optical SED.

gmw_00002788.name: SDSSJ225240.3+010958
gmw_00002788.overview: SDSSJ225240.3+010958 is an active galactic nucleus that exhibits very broad-line spectral states [arXiv:2206.10056]. Despite experiencing variations in its spectral line widths, the source consistently remains within the Population B class [arXiv:2206.10056].

sources.summary: The paper uses SDSSJ225240.3+010958 as an EV1 example, finding very broad-line spectral states; despite significant FWHM changes, it remains within Population B throughout the transition.

quotes.quote: For the first epoch of the former source (SDSSJ225240.3+010958, MJD = 52178), the FWHM( ) = 14015.57 614.05 , and the = 0.69 0.12. Thus, this epoch belongs to the B2++ spectral type.

The subsequent epoch (MJD = 57687, MJD = 5509), the FWHM( ) rises again to 19283.97 1615.78 while the falls to its lowest value, 0.25 0.09. This epoch belongs to the B1+++ spectral type. For the final epoch (MJD = 58068, MJD = 5890), the FWHM( ) attains its highest value at 19425.04 924.37 , and the remains almost unchanged at 0.27 0.05. The spectral type of the source basically remains unchanged relative to the previous epoch. Hence, this source, even with the significant variations in the FWHM( ) and , stayed in the Population B class throughout the transition.

gmw_00002789.name: NGC 4478
gmw_00002789.overview: NGC 4478 is an elliptical galaxy that hosts a nuclear stellar disk [arXiv:1004.2190]. This nuclear disk is highly inclined at 78 degrees, with a scale length of 37 parsecs and a position angle of 153 degrees [arXiv:1004.2190].

sources.summary: The paper decomposes NGC 4478's nuclear stellar disk, measuring a 37 pc scale length, 78 degree inclination, and 153 degree position angle, and describing it as large and luminous.

quotes.quote: The scale length, inclination, and position angle of the nuclear disk in the ACS/F475W and WFPC2/F555W passbands are consistent within the errors with the WFPC2/F814W values by Fig. fig:parameters moreetal04 . The mean values of the parameters are h = 37 pc, i = 78^ , and PA = 153^ , respectively. The nuclear disk contributes a maximum D/T=0.2 in the radial range between 0 2 and 0 6 from the center (Fig. fig:plot4478 ). It is one of the largest and more luminous NSDs ( L_ F814W = 2 10^7 ) known to date.

gmw_00002790.name: IRAS F10214+4724
gmw_00002790.overview: IRAS F10214+4724 is a gravitationally lensed, ultraluminous infrared galaxy at redshift z=2.3 [arXiv:0905.3512, arXiv:1307.6564]. It hosts both an obscured active galactic nucleus and a starbursting host galaxy [arXiv:1005.1511, arXiv:1203.0627], with its narrow-line region preferentially magnified by the gravitational lens [arXiv:1307.6564].

sources.summary: The paper reports XMM-Newton detection of the lensed luminous infrared galaxy IRAS F10214+4724, discusses its amplified infrared output, obscured active nucleus, molecular gas, and secure X-ray association.

quotes.quote: We report on a short XMM-Newton observation of the gravitationally-lensed, luminous infrared galaxy IRAS F10214+4724 at z=2.3 . A faint X-ray source is detected at 4 . The observed 0.5-2 keV (1.7-6.6 keV in the rest-frame) flux is 1.3 10^ 15 and the spectral slope in the rest-frame 1-10 keV band is 2 .

IRAS F10214+4724 is an IRAS source identified with an emission-line galaxy at z=2.286 (e.g., Rowan-Robinson et al 1991; Soifer et al 1991). The extremely large infrared luminosity 10^ 14 L_ is partly due to the amplification of gravitational lensing by an intervening galaxy or a group of galaxies at z 0.9 (Broadhurst Lehar 1995; Goodrich et al 1996; Lacy, Rawlings Serjeant 1998). The amplification factor appears to have wavelength dependency and estimated to be in the range of 10-100 (Broadhurst Lehar 1995; Downes et al 1995; Eisenhardt et al 1996; Evans et al 1999; Graham Liu 1995; Serjeant et al 1995; Trentham 1995; ). The presence of an active nucleus in this galaxy was already clear from the optical emission-line spectrum of Seyfert 2 (Elston et al 1994; Soifer et al 1995) and the detection of polarised broad emission (Goodrich et al 1996) suggested that the nucleus is obscured from the direct view. The Spitzer IRS spectrum, however, shows a silicate feature in emission, in contrast to other obscured objects, of which the mid-infrared spectra generally show the silicate features in absorption (Teplitz et al 2006).

sources.summary: The paper treats F10214 as a lensed z=2.2855 ULIRG with both AGN and starburst activity, reporting tentative [O III] detection and weak high-ionization lines.

quotes.quote: We present spectra of two high-redshift galaxies (MIPS J142824.0+352619 and IRAS F10214+4724) that were observed in our initial Science Demonstration Phase observations from two PACS GT Key Projects: SHINING (PI E. Sturm) and the "Dusty Young Universe" (PI K. Meisenheimer). IRAS F10214+4724 (hereafter F10214) is a ULIRG at redshift z=2.2855. It is known to harbor a coeval AGN, with a starbursting host galaxy.

F10214: we did not detect the targeted lines of [ S III ] 33 m and [ O IV ] 26 m, but [ O III ] 52 m (which is observed in the 1st order PACS band in parallel with the 2nd order [ O IV ] data) is serendipitously detected at a 3 level. We resolve the tentative line and measure a FWHM of 300 100km/s (corrected for the instrumental profile, which has a FWHM 220km/s at 170 m), similar to the average CO line width (246 10 km/s, Ao et al. Figure Fig2 , in any case, shows that the upper limit on the [ O IV ] /FIR ratio in F10214 is lower than in Seyfert galaxies, and more in the regime of ULIRGs and starbursts.

sources.summary: The paper models IRAS FSC10214+47 as a lensed z=2.3 ULIRG/starburst-AGN, finding preferential magnification of its NLR/active nucleus and lower continuum magnifications than canonical estimates.

quotes.quote: This is the first paper in a series that present a multi-wavelength analysis of the archetype Ultra-Luminous InfraRed Galaxy (ULIRG) IRAS FSC10214+47, a gravitationally lensed, starburst/AGN at z=2.3 . Here we present a new lens model and spatially-resolved radio data, as well as a deep F160W map. The lens modelling employs a Bayesian Markov Chain Monte Carlo algorithm with extended-source, forward ray-tracing.

Our lens model finds the narrow line region (NLR), and by proxy the active nucleus, is preferentially magnified. This supports previous claims that preferential magnification could mask the expected polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon spectral features in the Spitzer mid-infrared spectrum which roughly trace the star-forming regions. Furthermore, we show the arc-to-counter-image flux ratio is not a good estimate of the magnification in this system, despite its common use in the IRAS FSC10214+47 literature. Our lens modelling suggests magnifications of 15-20 2 for the F814W, MERLIN 1.7 GHz and VLA 8 GHz maps, significantly lower than the canonical values of = 50-100 often used for this system.

sources.summary: The paper models IRAS F10214+4724 as a high-redshift hyperluminous infrared galaxy whose SED requires narrow-line-region clouds, an obscured starburst, and differential gravitational lensing.

quotes.quote: We present a new model for the infrared emission of the high redshift hyperluminous infrared galaxy IRAS F10214+4724 which takes into account recent photometric data from Spitzer and Herschel that sample the peak of its spectral energy distribution. We first demonstrate that the combination of the AGN tapered disc and starburst models of Efstathiou and coworkers, while able to give an excellent fit to the average spectrum of type 2 AGN measured by Spitzer , fails to match the spectral energy distribution of IRAS F10214+4724. This is mainly due to the fact that the S_ distribution of the galaxy falls very steeply with increasing frequency (a characteristic of heavy absorption by dust) but shows a silicate feature in emission. We propose a model that assumes two components of emission: clouds that are associated with the narrow-line region and a highly obscured starburst. The emission from the clouds must suffer significantly stronger gravitational lensing compared to the emission from the torus to explain the observed spectral energy distribution. galaxies: evolution, galaxies: formation, galaxies: high-redshift, infrared: galaxies, cosmology: observations The title of the discovery paper of IRAS F10214+4724 at a redshift of 2.285 ( ) was 'An IRAS galaxy with huge luminosity - hidden quasar or protogalaxy?'.

sources.summary: The paper fits IRAS F10214+4724 as a lensed hyperluminous infrared galaxy whose SED can be matched by pure AGN torus models and AGN+starburst variants.

quotes.quote: We discuss a fit of the SED of the hyperluminous infrared galaxy IRAS F10214+4724 which has an apparent luminosity that exceeds 10^ 14 RR91 . It is generally accepted that the enormous luminosity of this galaxy at redshift z=2.224 is due to gravitational lensing by a foreground galaxy with magnification between 50 and 100 ( Broadhurst,Serjeant ) or 15 - 20 Deane . Following the detection of a silicate emission feature T06 in this narrow-lined object, and proposed that the emission feature is due to emission from narrow-line region clouds at a temperature of 210 K. Here we find that the SED of IRAS F10214+4724 can also be fit by a pure AGN model without clouds when a small inner radius or in a 'Disk+Clouds' configuration when larger inner radii are assumed (Table para.tab ). In the AGN+SB models we find the ratio of starburst to AGN luminosity is 20 (Table paragnsb.tab ).

sources.summary: The paper treats IRAS F10214+4724 as a lensed high-redshift ULIRG and obscured quasar with extensive multiwavelength coverage, infrared luminosity, and cusp-caustic lensing.

quotes.quote: This paper is the third in a series on the gravitationally lensed, obscured quasar IRAS F10214+4724 (IRAS 10214 hereafter). IRAS 10214 is a well-studied galaxy that is often used as an archetype high-redshift ULIRG, largely because of its extensive multi-wavelength coverage and its early discovery in 1991 Rowan-Robinson1991 . It radiates 95 percent of its bolometric luminosity in the infrared, the intrinsic source of which is poorly constrained. IRAS 10214 is classified as a cusp-caustic lens that results in a large arc to the south of the lens galaxy, and a small counter-image to the north of the lens see Eisenhardt1996 .

sources.summary: The paper reports new JCMT CI J=2--1 upper limits for the strongly lensed z=2.3 ULIRG, finding no confirmation of earlier extended emission and compatibility with CO-derived molecular gas mass.

quotes.quote: Sensitive new observations of the fine structure line ^3 P _2 ^3 P _1 (J=2--1) of the neutral atomic carbon CI ( _ rest 809  GHz) in the strongly lensed Ultra Luminous Infrared Galaxy (ULIRG) IRAS F10214+4724 at z=2.3 with the mm/sub-mm telescope James Clerk Maxwel (JCMT) are presented. These do not confirm the presence of emission from this line at the flux levels or angular extent previously reported in the literature. The new 2 upper limits are: S_ CI 7 \ Jy \ km \ s^ 1 (central position), and S_ CI 8.5 \ Jy \ km \ s^ 1 (average over the two (RA), (Dec) = [ 0'', 10'' ] positions). A CI emission assumed fully concomitant with the bulk of H _2 and confined entirely within the strongly lensed object yields an upper limit of M_ CI (H_2) 1.5 10^ 10 \ M_ , compatible with the reported CO-derived H _2 gas mass, within the uncertainties of the two methods.

gmw_00002791.name: 5C4.114
gmw_00002791.overview: 5C4.114 is a Fanaroff-Riley class I radio galaxy with an unknown redshift greater than 0.023 [arXiv:1002.0594]. The source features asymmetric lobes [arXiv:1002.0594] that exhibit transverse Faraday rotation gradients, suggesting a helical or toroidal magnetic field associated with its jets [arXiv:1511.08730].

sources.summary: The paper reports statistically significant transverse Faraday-rotation gradients across 5C4.114, interpreting them as evidence for a helical or toroidal magnetic-field component in its kiloparsec-scale jets.

quotes.quote: We identified an extended, monotonic transverse Faraday rotation gradient across the Northern lobe of a previously published Very Large Array (kiloparsec-scale) RM image of 5C4.114. We reanalyzed these VLA data in order to determine the significance of this RM gradient. The RM gradient across the Northern kiloparsec-scale lobe structure of 5C4.114 has a statistical significance of about 4 . There is also a somewhat less prominent monotonic transverse Faraday rotation gradient across the Southern jet/lobe (narrower range of distances from the core, significance 3 ).

Other parts of the Faraday Rotation distribution observed across the source are patchy and show no obvious order. This suggests that we are observing a random RM component associated with the foreground material in the cluster in which the radio source is located and through which it is viewed, superposed on a more ordered RM component that arises in the immediate vicinity of the AGN jets. We interpret the transverse RM gradient as reflecting the systematic variations of the line-of-sight component of a helical or toroidal B field associated with the jets of 5C4.114.

sources.summary: The paper describes 5C4.114 as a compact FRI radio source without an optical identification, giving a redshift lower limit, lobe asymmetry, and fractional polarization.

quotes.quote: 5C4.114 5C4.114 is a FRI radio source, with angular size of 15 '' . Its redshift is unknown, and no optical identification either with a Coma cluster galaxy (Miller et al. 2009) nor with a background galaxy has been found, indicating that 5C4.114 has a redshift greater than 0.023. The southern lobe appears brighter than the northern one. The source fractional polarization is 13 at 1.365 GHz and 19 at 4.935 GHz. In Fig. fig:114_pol the radio emission is shown at 1.365 and 4.935 GHz.

gmw_00002792.name: 2MASX J23453268 0449256
gmw_00002792.overview: The massive, isolated spiral galaxy 2MASX J23453268 0449256 hosts a pseudobulge [arXiv:2405.01910], a nuclear bar [arXiv:2405.01910], and a rapidly rotating molecular gas ring [arXiv:2103.12816]. It launches giant, episodic radio jets [arXiv:2006.09388, arXiv:2103.12816] while exhibiting suppressed star formation [arXiv:2405.01910, arXiv:2103.12816].

sources.summary: The paper studies the massive spiral 2MASX J23453268-0449256/J2345-0449, finding a pseudobulge, nuclear bar/ring, symmetric disc, Mpc radio jets, hot halo, and suppressed central star formation.

quotes.quote: This study delves into the bulge-disc components and stellar mass distribution in the fast-rotating, highly massive spiral galaxy 2MASX J23453268-0449256, distinguished by extraordinary radio jets extending to Mpc scales. Using high-resolution multi-wavelength Hubble Space Telescope (HST) observations and multi-parameter panchromatic spectral energy distribution (SED) fitting, we derive estimates for the star formation rate, total baryonic mass in stars, and warm dust properties. Our findings, validated at a spatial resolution of approximately 100 pc, reveal a pseudo-bulge rather than a classical bulge and a small nuclear bar and resonant ring, challenging conventional models of galaxy formation.

The focus of present paper, galaxy 2MASX J23453268-0449256 (hereafter J2345-0449) at redshift 0.0755 . J2345-0449 is known to be an extremely luminous (optical band: M() =-23.26, near-IR K band: M(K) = -26.15), isolated spiral galaxy which also hosts one of the largest and most powerful radio jet known till now, clearly powered by the AGN residing at the center of a spiral galaxy disc and not an elliptical galaxy 2014ApJ...788..174B . GMRT and VLA observations show that this giant radio source also exhibits a rarely observed `double-double' radio morphology; viz. containing a nested pair of smaller (active/young), inner radio lobes, and a second pair of extended (relic/old), diffuse outer radio lobes, with the jet axis (in projection) nearly orthogonal to the host galaxy's stellar disc (Fig.  GMRT ).

In the present work our research endeavour was directed on the bulge-disc components and stellar mass distribution in the fast rotating, highly massive spiral galaxy J2345-0449 showing unusual radio jets extending up to Mpc scale. Using the high-resolution multi wavelength HST observations and multi-parameter panchromatic SED fitting we obtained estimates of star formation rate, the total baryonic mass contained in stars and derived the warm dust properties, which were then connected to the cold molecular gas distribution based on previous ALMA and IRAM Carbon Monoxide spectral line observations.

sources.summary: The paper describes 2MASX J23453268 0449256 as a giant late-type spiral radio galaxy with FR-II double-double jets and CO-detected molecular gas in a roughly exponential disk.

quotes.quote: R1-1: 2MASX J23453268 0449256 The galaxy 2MASX J23453268 0449256 (R1-1) is one of the largest (projected linear size 1.6 Mpc at radio wavelengths) radio galaxy known to date, hosted by a massive, late-type spiral galaxy that lacks a classical bulge at the redshift of 0.0755 Bagchi2014 . It shows FR-II and double-double (two sets of lobes, indicating episodic activity) radio morphology; the jet axis is nearly orthogonal to the disc of the host galaxy. This is very unusual because such large-scale radio jets are in general hosted by ellipticals Taylor1996, McLure2004 . It shows several other unusual properties that challenge the standard paradigms for black hole growth in galaxies lacking classical bulges.

We detected the two first rotational lines of CO in one GRG, which is the giant spiral GRG-J2345 0449, and report tentative signals for two others (R2-8 and R2-9). The two-horn shape of the spectra of R1-1 (GRG-J2345-0449) allowed us to determine the concentration of the molecular gas in the giant spiral galaxy. The best-fit model is a roughly exponential disc for the molecular component, and its half-light radius is comparable to that of the stellar component.

sources.summary: The paper presents J2345-0449 as an unusually massive bulgeless spiral hosting 1.6 Mpc relativistic jets, active star formation, and a massive central black hole.

quotes.quote: Here we present the discovery of giant radio source J2345-0449 ( z=0.0755 ), a clear and extremely rare counter example where relativistic jets are ejected from a luminous and massive spiral galaxy on scale of 1.6 Mpc, the largest known so far. Extreme physical properties observed for this bulgeless spiral host, such as its high optical and infra-red luminosity, large dynamical mass, rapid disk rotation, and episodic jet activity are possibly the results of its unusual formation history, which has also assembled, via gas accretion from a disk, its central black hole of mass >2 10^ 8 M _ . The very high mid-IR luminosity of the galaxy suggests that it is actively forming stars and still building a massive disk. We argue that the launch of these powerful jets is facilitated by an advection dominated, magnetized accretion flow at low Eddington rate onto this unusually massive (for a bulgeless disk galaxy) and possibly fast-spinning central black hole. Therefore, J2345-0449 is an extremely rare, unusual galactic system whose properties challenge the standard paradigms for black hole growth and formation of relativistic jets in disk galaxies.

sources.summary: The paper presents ALMA CO observations of 2MASSX J23453269 044925, finding a massive rotating molecular ring, powerful giant radio jets, a bright X-ray halo, and suppressed star formation.

quotes.quote: We present new, arcsecond-resolution ALMA CO(1--0) interferometry, which probes the spatially resolved, cold molecular gas in the nearby ( z=0.0755 ), massive ( M_ stellar =4 10^ 11 M_ ), isolated, late-type spiral galaxy 2MASSX J23453269 044925, which is outstanding for having two pairs of powerful, giant radio jets, and a bright X-ray halo of hot circumgalactic gas. The molecular gas is in a massive ( M_ gas =2.0 10^ 10 M_ ), 24 kpc wide, rapidly rotating ring, which is associated with the inner stellar disk. Broad ( FWHM=70-180 km s ^ 1 ) emission lines with complex profiles associated with the radio source are seen over large regions in the ring, indicating gas velocities that are high enough to keep the otherwise marginally Toomre-stable gas from fragmenting into gravitationally bound, star-forming clouds.

Resolved star-formation rate surface densities derived from GALEX fall factors 50 75 short of expectations from the standard Kennicutt-Schmidt law of star-forming galaxies, and near gas-rich early-type galaxies with signatures of star formation lowered by jet feedback.
They identified two pairs of bright, giant radio jets extending over 390 kpc and 1.6 Mpc, respectively, suggesting that radio AGN activity has been on-going for long timescales.

gmw_00002793.name: CGCG 245 031
gmw_00002793.overview: CGCG 245 031, also known as GRG J1312+4450, is a young, flat-spectrum radio galaxy [arXiv:1604.05142, arXiv:2006.09388]. It is a candidate for a restarting radio source [arXiv:2006.09388], featuring narrow jets and lobes that display hot spots without diffuse extended emission [arXiv:1604.05142].

sources.summary: The merged map traces this source's narrow jets farther from the center, but both lobes lack diffuse emission and only hot spots are visible.

quotes.quote: GRG J1312+4450 A better sensitivity to extended structures of the merged map (Fig. 10, bottom) allows to trace both narrow jets to a greater distance from the central source. Both lobes, however, do not show any diffuse extended emission. Either in the NVSS or in the merged maps only the hot spots are visible.

sources.summary: The paper identifies CGCG 245 031 as a flat-spectrum radio source with a low integrated spectral index, young radio properties, and evidence favoring a restarting classification.

quotes.quote: R1-2: CGCG 245 031 CGCG 245 031 (R1-2) is a flat-spectrum radio source Healey2007 . It has a low value for its integrated spectral index and is a young radio source. It is a good candidate for a restarting class.

gmw_00002794.name: 2MASX J14504940+1006497
gmw_00002794.overview: 2MASX J14504940+1006497 is a central dominant S0-type group galaxy [arXiv:2006.09388]. Its bright radio core, diffuse jet-axis emission, and inverted core spectrum indicate the presence of a restarted active galactic nucleus [arXiv:2006.09388].

sources.summary: The paper describes 2MASX J14504940+1006497 as a central dominant S0-group galaxy whose LOFAR structure, bright core, diffuse jet-axis emission, and inverted spectrum indicate a restarted AGN.

quotes.quote: R2-3: 2MASX J14504940+1006497 Source R2-3 is hosted by a central dominant galaxy (the core of a triplet, which is also known as UGC 09555 NED03) of a low-density galaxy group (MSPM 02158), as shown in Figure. \ fig:SDSS . Based on their SED analysis, Clarke2017 concluded that the host galaxy is an S0-type galaxy with an SFR of 1.2 0.3 M yr ^ 1 . Clarke2017 also presented its LOFAR 142 MHz radio map, which reveals a large diffuse structure that is not seen in any other radio survey. The bright radio core and high diffuse emission along the jet axis strongly indicate a restarted source.

Based on its radio core spectrum, it has been classified as a flat-spectrum galaxy Healey2007 with frequencies between 1 to 10 GHz, and it has an inverted core spectrum with frequencies between 22 to 43 GHz parkkvlbi13 . This further supports the hypothesis that this is a restarted AGN.

gmw_00002795.name: Speca
gmw_00002795.overview: Speca is an extremely massive, fast-rotating, and star-forming disk galaxy [arXiv:1402.3674] that is classified as a spiral-hosted giant radio galaxy [arXiv:2006.09388]. It serves as the central galaxy of a cluster [arXiv:2006.09388] and exhibits three pairs of radio lobes that indicate restarted activity [arXiv:2006.09388].

sources.summary: The paper reports follow-up showing Speca is a massive, fast-rotating, star-forming disk galaxy in a cluster environment and frames it as a spiral-host episodic radio galaxy.

quotes.quote: Early analyses from our follow up observations with the Subaru and XMM-Newton telescopes have revealed that Speca is likely a new entry to the cluster and is a fast rotating, extremely massive, star forming disk galaxy. Speca-like massive galaxies with giant radio lobes, are possibly remnants of luminous quasars in the early Universe or of first supermassive black holes with in first masssve galaxies.

Following discovery of the spiral-host episodic giant radio galaxy `Speca' hota11 , it has been observed with the Subaru telescope in long-slit spectroscopy mode and with the XMM-Newton for investigating the kinematics and cluster-environment it belongs to, respectively. H and [ N II ] emission lines seen from the major axis of the galaxy revealed that it is an extremely fast-rotating (maximum speed 370 km s ^ 1 near the flat regime), star forming disk galaxy. Though it is the brightest member of the identified cluster, the point-source subtracted diffuse X-ray emission is offset from Speca and co-spatial with the early-type members of the cluster.

sources.summary: The paper describes Speca as one of two spiral-hosted GRGs, a central galaxy with three lobe pairs, restarted activity, relic outer lobes, and recent star formation.

quotes.quote: R2-8: Speca Only about ten RGs ledlow01,Hota2011,Bagchi2014,Singh2015,mao16,mulcahy16 have been found to be hosted by spiral galaxies up to now, and only two (2MASX J23453268 0449256 and Speca) of them belong to the GRG subclass. Along with its unusual property of being hosted by a spiral Hota2011 , Speca is classified as the central galaxy, called MaxBCG J212.45357-03.04237 cluster or group. Another peculiar property of this object is its three pairs of lobes, also called a triple-double, with clear evidence of restarting activity. The outer lobes look like relics revived by shocks coming from the interaction between the cluster and cosmic filaments. The galaxy shows very recent star formation that might be fueled by a cooling flow from merging activity 500 Myr ago Hota2011 .

gmw_00002796.name: R2-9
gmw_00002796.overview: R2-9 is a recently identified high-redshift giant radio galaxy [arXiv:2006.09388]. It hosts a dust-obscured active galactic nucleus and is actively forming stars, with a tentative molecular gas detection [arXiv:2006.09388].

sources.summary: The paper describes R2-9 as a recently identified high-redshift GRG with active star formation, strong 22 micron luminosity, a dust-obscured AGN, and a tentative molecular-gas detection.

quotes.quote: R2-9 is one of the most recently identified GRG, at relatively high redshift DabhadeSAGAN20 . It appears to be actively forming stars. R2-9 is the most luminous GRGs at 22 microns (WISE band 4) in our sample, and appears to be harbouring a dust-obscured powerful AGN based on its red colour.

The R1-1 detected spiral galaxy has a long t _ dep of 5.5 Gyr, and the tentative detection R2-9 has t _ dep of 3.15 Gyr, and the two detected GRG (NGC 315 and 3C 236) have an exceptionally short t _ dep of 120 and 280 Myr, as well as two upper limits that are more typical of starbursts.

gmw_00002797.name: NVSS J101642+592222
gmw_00002797.overview: NVSS J101642+592222 is a bright radio galaxy with the typical colors of an early-type galaxy [arXiv:0901.3390]. It is likely situated at the center of a small galaxy group that is falling into the cluster Abell 959 [arXiv:0901.3390].

sources.summary: The paper identifies NVSS J101642+592222 as a bright early-type-colored radio galaxy near diffuse X-ray emission, likely centered in a galaxy group falling into Abell 959.

quotes.quote: Closer to A959 (at 6 WSW from the cluster center), ROSAT archival data reveal a diffuse X--ray emission probably associated to a small galaxy group. Unfortunately, we have no redshift in this region, but at the center of the X--ray emission there is a brilliant galaxy (only slightly less luminous than the I and II dominant galaxies) showing the typical colors of the early--type galaxies of A959. This galaxy (known as NVSS J101642+592222) is an evident pointlike radio source in NVSS (Condon et al. ) archival data, too. We suspect that this radio galaxy is at the center of a galaxy group falling in the gravitational well of A959.

gmw_00002798.name: SDSS J0742+3341
gmw_00002798.overview: SDSS J0742+3341 is a high-redshift Lyman-alpha emitting galaxy [arXiv:1912.04033] located at a systemic redshift of z = 2.3625 [arXiv:1912.04033]. The galaxy is strongly gravitationally lensed [arXiv:1811.03627, arXiv:1912.04033], resulting in a magnified morphology with bright extended arcs [arXiv:1912.04033].

sources.summary: The paper describes BG0742+3341 as a magnified BELLS GALLERY LAE whose faint, lens-contaminated spectrum yields a fine-structure redshift and strong Ly escape properties.

quotes.quote: These are SDSS J020121.39+322829.6, SDSS J074249.68+334148.9, SDSS J075523.52+344539.5, and SDSS J091859.21+510452.5 (hereafter BG0201+3228, BG0742+3341, BG0755+3445, and BG0918+5104, where ``BG'' stands for BELLS GALLERY). The LAEs BG0201+3228, BG0742+3341, and BG0918+5104 are magnified by factors of = 15 3 , 16 3 , and 18 3 , respectively shu2016b , and show similar lensed morphologies, with bright extended arcs and relatively compact counter-images on the other side of the lens in a cusp configuration.

For BG0742+3341, due to the limited wavelength range of its spectrum, from 1180 to 1700 in the rest-frame (it was not observed with the R2500R grism), and the relatively modest S/N continuum spectrum (BG0742+3341 is the faintest LAE in our sample), we do not detect clearly any photospheric stellar absorption line or nebular emission. Nevertheless, we clearly detect three fine-structure emission lines, Si ii* 1197 , Si ii* 1264 , and Si ii* 1533 , at a redshift z = 2.3625 0.0009 . We thus assume the redshift of the fine-structure emission lines as the systemic redshift of BG0742+3341.

As an example, the two stronger LAEs in our sample, BG0742+3341 and BG1429+1202, those with larger Ly EW_ 0 's ( 45-49 ) and escape fractions ( 0.36-0.37 ), are the ones that show weaker low-ionization ISM absorption ( EW_ 0 1 ), narrower Ly lines ( FWHM 250-300 km s ^ 1 ) and smaller Ly velocity offsets ( 30-80 km s ^ 1 ).

sources.summary: The paper finds a subhalo model is weakly preferred for SDSS J0742+3341, but the signal is below threshold and lacks a matching pixellated convergence correction.

quotes.quote: SDSS J0742+3341 The parametric analysis of Section sec:clumpy , where subhaloes are described as analytical NFW mass components, shows a persistent preference for a model that includes a substructure with a mass of M_ vir ^ NFW = (3.8 0.8) 10^ 10 M_ located at (dx,dy)= (1.14 0.04, -0.80 0.03) arcsec, relative to the main lens centre. However, the detection is only at the 6- level and therefore below our 10- threshold (see Section  sec:tests ). Moreover, no significant and localized convergence correction is identified by the pixellated analysis of Section sec:potcorr at the same location, as shown in Fig.  fig:pot_panel . We, therefore, conclude that there is no evidence for a significant detection of a mass perturbation in this system and register it as another non-detection in the sample.

gmw_00002799.name: SDSS J1110+3649
gmw_00002799.overview: SDSS J1110+3649 is a galaxy that acts as a strong gravitational lens [arXiv:1811.03627]. A pixellated analysis of the system reveals a positive localized convergence correction near the main lens, which may indicate the presence of a dark matter subhalo [arXiv:1811.03627].

sources.summary: The paper reports a positive convergence correction and low-significance subhalo preference near SDSS J1110+3649, but says deeper or multi-band data are needed.

quotes.quote: SDSS J1110+3649 The pixellated analysis for SDSS J1110+3649 shows the presence of a positive localized convergence correction at about (dx,dy)= ( -0.851, 0.628) relative to the main lens (Fig.  fig:pot_panel ). Moreover, the parametrized analysis of Section sec:clumpy gives a preference at the 4- level for a model with a subhalo with mass M_ vir ^ NFW = (5.4 1.5) 10^9 M_ . This subhalo is located at (dx,dy)= ( -0.945 0.084, 0.536 0.021) relative to the main lens centre, consistent within 1 with the position of the convergence correction.

From the pixellated convergence corrections, we derived a model-independent projected mass for the substructure of M_ 2D ^ (<r_ s ) = _ pix  2    _c  _i ^2_i = 2.5 10^9 M _ . This is consistent within 2 with the parametric projected mass M_ 2D ^ NFW (<r_ s ) = (1.7 0.7) 10^9 M _ . However, given the low statistical significance and the results presented in Section  sec:tests , we conclude that multi-band data or deeper observations are required to understand the nature of this system and, at present, also in this case we do not count this as a detection.

gmw_00002800.name: COSMOS0038+4133
gmw_00002800.overview: COSMOS0038+4133 is a confirmed strong gravitational lens galaxy surrounded by a bright ring [arXiv:2010.08680, arXiv:1009.1545]. The system is located in an overdense region without being associated with any known optical or X-ray galaxy group [arXiv:2010.08680, arXiv:1009.1545].

sources.summary: The paper models COSMOS0038+4133 as a confirmed strong lens in an overdense region, finding a large strong-lensing shear discrepant with weak-lensing estimates.

quotes.quote: All three are confirmed lenses and classified as best lenses in faure . For each system we can give a photometric redshift and the number of galaxies which are located within a circle of 1 arcmin radius. This measure indicates the density of the near neighbourhood and we can estimate the bias in the strong lensing shear measurement. Especially the lens COSMOS0038+4133 is located in an over-dense region, although it is not part of a galaxy cluster faure2 .
Especially for COSMOS0038+4133, the strong lensing shear estimate is large and estimated to high accuracy, while the absolute value of the weak lensing estimates is small.

The effect of averaging over different scales however does not allow us to fully explain the discrepant estimates for the strong lens system COSMOS0038+4133, where the difference is much larger than the predicted strong-weak-lensing-covariance. This system has been analysed before by birrer-welschen , who have also found large values for the cosmic shear. It is therefore likely that in this region there is a strong nearby source of cosmic shear induced on the strong lensing estimate, but on too small scales to significantly affect the weak lensing shear estimate.
