sources.summary: The paper notes COSMOS 0038+4133 has a high neighbor-density ratio, problematic dark-matter fraction, reliable redshift/SED, and possible light-profile bias from a bright ring.

quotes.quote: On the contrary, COSMOS 0038+4133 shows a large density ratio ( _ 10 < _ 10 >_t =5.5 0.9) without being associated to any known galaxy group in X-ray or optic. The external shear due to clusters and groups We estimate the shear and convergence produced by the groups (either X-ray or optical) detected around the lens galaxies in order to characterize the contribution of the environment to the total lens potential in future lens models (   model ).

The negative DM fraction for COSMOS 0038+4133 may indicate that the stellar mass of the lens galaxy is overestimated. This is surprising as for this system the photometric and spectroscopic redshifts agree, hence making us confident that the SED, hence the stellar mass, is reliable as well. Another possible explanation is that the light profile fit is not correct and that we measure too large an effective radius. This is indeed a possible explanation as a bright ring surrounds the lens galaxy and may bias the determination of the light profile.

gmw_00002801.name: COSMOS5758+1525
gmw_00002801.overview: COSMOS5758+1525 is a confirmed gravitational lens system [arXiv:2010.08680]. Its lensed source galaxy is modeled using an elliptical Sersic profile combined with five shapelet polynomial orders [arXiv:2010.08680].

sources.summary: The paper models COSMOS5758+1525 as one of three confirmed COSMOS lenses, finding only a small strong-weak shear discrepancy likely tied to low signal-to-noise.

quotes.quote: All three are confirmed lenses and classified as best lenses in faure . For each system we can give a photometric redshift and the number of galaxies which are located within a circle of 1 arcmin radius.

For the lensed source galaxy, we use on top of an elliptical S ersic profile a set of shapelets shapelets1 , where the number of shapelet basis functions is chosen individually for each system: We use no shapelets to model the source of COSMOS5921+0638, while we use 3 (5) shapelet polynomial orders, n_ max , in order to model the sources of COSMOS0038+4133 (COSMOS5758+1525).

The second system, COSMOS5758+1525 shows only a small discrepancy between the strong and weak lensing shear measurement. The remaining difference might be explained by the poor signal to noise ratio in the data for this particular system (cf. Fig. fig:sl-results , left middle panel).

gmw_00002802.name: COSMOS5921+0638
gmw_00002802.overview: COSMOS5921+0638 is a confirmed strong gravitational lens system [arXiv:2010.08680] containing a quadruply lensed quasar [arXiv:2009.07854]. The system features four distinct point sources and a faint connecting arc [arXiv:2010.08680], which are visible as four multiple images in Hubble Space Telescope observations [arXiv:2009.07854].

sources.summary: The paper models COSMOS5921+0638 as a confirmed strong lens whose strong and weak lensing shear estimates agree, aided by clear features and low background density.

quotes.quote: All three are confirmed lenses and classified as best lenses in faure . For each system we can give a photometric redshift and the number of galaxies which are located within a circle of 1 arcmin radius.

For the lensed source galaxy, we use on top of an elliptical S ersic profile a set of shapelets shapelets1 , where the number of shapelet basis functions is chosen individually for each system: We use no shapelets to model the source of COSMOS5921+0638, while we use 3 (5) shapelet polynomial orders, n_ max , in order to model the sources of COSMOS0038+4133 (COSMOS5758+1525).

For the third system, COSMOS5921+0638 both measurements are in good agreement. The data has a excellent signal to noise ratio, shows a clear strong lensing features (i.e. four point sources and a faint arc connecting them) and could be reproduced in the modelling process. The background density in the neighbourhood of the strong lens system was fairly low (as it was also the case for the second system), and it hence yields similar shear estimates as the weak lensing measurements.

sources.summary: The paper recovers HSCJ095921+020638 as the known quadruply lensed quasar in the HSC transient field and notes HST evidence for its four multiple images.

quotes.quote: Among the eight final lensed candidates, one is the only known quadruply lensed quasar in the survey field, HSCJ095921+020638. None of the other seven lensed candidates has been previously classified as a lens nor a lensed candidate. Three of the five final candidates with available Hubble Space Telescope (HST) images, including HSCJ095921+020638, show clues of lensed feature in the HST images. A tightening of variability selection criteria might result in the loss of possible lensed quasar candidates, especially the lensed quasars with faint brightness or narrow separation, without efficiently eliminating the non-lensed objects; Chitah is therefore important as an advanced examination to improve the lens search efficiency through the object configuration. The recovery of HSCJ095921+020638 proves the feasibility of the time-variability-based lens search method, and this lens search method can be used in other cadenced imaging surveys, such as the upcoming Rubin Observatory Legacy Survey of Space and Time.

From the HST images, we can clearly see the four multiple images in HSCJ095921+020638 (top-left panel in Fig. fig:hst_imgs ), and three possible lensed images in HSCJ100129+024427 (top-right panel in Fig. fig:hst_imgs ).

gmw_00002803.name: SDSS J134856.75+263946.7
gmw_00002803.overview: SDSS J134856.75+263946.7 is a massive galaxy located at a spectroscopic redshift of approximately z = 1.51 [arXiv:2304.13795]. It is classified as a post-starburst galaxy [arXiv:2304.13795] with a large but highly uncertain star formation rate [arXiv:2304.13795, arXiv:2303.01857].

sources.summary: The paper identifies SDSS J134856.75+263946.7 as a possible FXT 22 host at z=1.5105, likely a massive post-starburst galaxy with uncertain star formation.

quotes.quote: However, this source falls close to an extended object, SDSS J134856.75+263946.7, with m_r 21.4 AB mag that lies at a distance of 4 5 from the position of the FXT ( 40 kpc in projection) with a spectroscopic redshift of z_ spec = 1.5105 Andreoni2021,Jonker2021,Eappachen2023a . The probability of a false match is P < 0.041 adopting the formalism developed by Bloom2002 for such offsets from similar or brighter objects. To summarize, we conclude that four (FXTs 16, 19, 20 and 21) of the eight FXT candidates have high probabilities of being associated with faint (FXT 20) or moderately bright (FXTs 16, 19, and 21) extended sources within the 3 positional error circle. In the case of FXT 22, it may be associated with the extended source SDSS J134856.75+263946.7 ( z_ spec = 1.5105); nevertheless, a relation with a faint background source cannot be excluded a faint extended source is in the X-ray uncertainty region; Eappachen2023a .

The hosts of FXTs 16, 19, and 22 have SFRs, stellar masses, and young stellar populations indicative of star-forming galaxies Moustakas2013 . In terms of SFRs, the FXT hosts broadly lie in the same region populated by SNe type Ib, Ic, II, SL-SNe, and GRBs. The SFR of the host of FXT 22 compares more favorably with LGRB (30 ) and SGRB (10 ) hosts over SNe ( 0 ); meanwhile, its large host stellar mass shares little overlap with the LGRB/SGRB ( 10/15 ) and partially with type-Ia/CC-SN ( 40/30 ) host populations.

Finally, FXT 22 is located near the extended z_ spec = 1.5105 galaxy SDSS J134856.75+263946. SED fitting of the photometric data at the known redshift indicates that the host is likely a massive post-starburst galaxy, with a large but highly uncertain star formation rate.

sources.summary: The paper evaluates cNE as a candidate host at z=1.5082, deriving a large luminosity distance, offset, stellar mass, and high star-formation rate.

quotes.quote: Assuming cNE is the host galaxy for If is associated with the candidate host galaxy cNE, which has a luminosity distance of 11 Gpc, the transient would have a peak X-ray luminosity of 10^ 46 . This is consistent with the suggestion of 2014ARA&A..52...43B that BNS mergers have an X-ray peak luminosity of L_ X,peak 10 ^ 44 -10 ^ 51 erg s ^ 1 (considering beamed emission for larger luminosity). The luminosity at this distance is also consistent with the luminosity of for which a distance has been determined through a host galaxy association ( Xue2019 ) . The offset of with respect to the candidate host galaxy cNE is consistent with the 90th percentile of the SGRB offsets. The parameters such as SFR and stellar mass of cNE, obtained through the SED fitting using BAGPIPES are shown in Figure fig:sGRB .

gmw_00002804.name: HE 2347-4342
gmw_00002804.overview: HE 2347-4342 is a luminous, high-redshift quasar located at a redshift of approximately 2.885 [arXiv:2101.01196, arXiv:astro-ph/0402336]. The active galactic nucleus lacks an obvious helium II proximity zone, which is likely caused by a strong associated absorption system screening its ionizing radiation [arXiv:2101.01196, arXiv:astro-ph/0402336].

sources.summary: The paper describes HE 2347 4342 as a luminous quasar lacking a He II proximity zone, with peculiar associated absorption and gas velocities that exclude it from later modelling.

quotes.quote: The luminous quasar HE 2347 4342 lacks a He ii proximity zone, which is likely explained by its peculiar associated absorption system reimers97,fechner04 . Many of its N v and O vi bearing components are consistent with being photoionized by the quasar fechner04 . Because their He ii column densities are insufficient to shield He ii ionizing photons, shull10 argued that HE 2347 4342 has recently turned on ( t_ on <1 Myr). However, the location of the absorbing gas is unconstrained.

The accurate and precise [ O iii ] redshift z_ em =2.8852 0.0006 implies large gas infall velocities of 1500 km s ^ 1 onto the circumnuclear region (Fig. fig:he2proxassoc ), and even higher velocities if the gas resides in the circumgalactic medium of the host galaxy. Redshift space distortions hui97,weinberg97 significantly affect the He ii transmission profile, possibly masking the He ii proximity effect altogether. Conclusive evidence for or against HE 2347 4342 being a young quasar requires detailed numerical modelling of gas associated with the quasar or its host galaxy, which is outside the scope of this work. Because of the extreme peculiar gas velocities that are not represented in our simulations (Section sect:models ), we exclude HE 2347 4342 from further discussion.

sources.summary: The paper uses HE 2347-4342 as a bright z=2.885 quasar sightline, describing its Hamburg/ESO discovery and FUSE/VLT observations, and reporting no He II proximity effect because absorption screens its ionizing radiation.

quotes.quote: In our first paper (KS01), we analyzed FUSE observations of the fluctuating He II absorption toward the bright (V = 16.1) quasar HE 2347-4342 at redshift z_ em = 2.885 0.005 (Reimers et al. \ 1997; Smette et al. \ 2002). This object was discovered in the Hamburg/ESO survey for bright QSOs (Reimers, K ohler, Wisotzki 1996) and is one of the brightest targets for FUV studies of He II, with specific flux F_ 3 10^ 15 erg cm ^ 2 s ^ 1 ^ 1 at 1180 .

HE 2347-4342 was observed during four nights (October 7-9 and October 19, 2000) using the UVES installed at the second VLT Unit Telescope (Kueyen). Twelve exposures were made in the dichroic mode using standard settings for central wavelengths of 3460/4370 \ in the blue and 5800/8600 \ in the red. The integration time for each wavelength range was 6 60 min.

In the case of HE 2347-4342, Smette et al. \ (2002) noted the absence of any observed proximity effect in He II absorbers near z_ em = 2.885 . They estimated that a column density N(He II) 2.4 10^ 18 cm ^ 2 was required to absorb the QSO's ionizing radiation at 4 ryd. Thus, the QSO radiation field in the He II continuum may be strongly affected by absorption from both the galactic nucleus and the IGM.

sources.summary: The paper uses HE 2347-4342 as the main quasar sight line, describing COS spectra, strong absorption beyond systemic redshift, no obvious proximity effect, and possible internal AGN absorption.

quotes.quote: In this paper, we describe properties of the \ forest toward HE 2347-4342. This sight line is considerably less complicated, compared to HS 1700+6416, which has contamination from metal lines in several strong intervening systems in (Fechner \ 2006). The COS/G130M spectrum of HE 2347-4342 showed strong \ ( 303.78 ) absorption extending 1480 \ redward of the estimated systemic redshift ( z_ sys 2.885 ) out to 1186.4 \ ( z_a = 2.905 ). Absorption at z_a z_ QSO is also seen in \ and in associated metal-line absorbers (Fechner \ 2004). Previous studies of the weak ( + ) 1302 emission-line blend quoted systemic redshifts for HE 2347-4342 of z_ sys = 2.885 0.005 (Reimers \ 1997), z_ sys = 2.885 0.003 (Dall'Aglio \ 2008a), and z_ sys = 2.886 0.003 (Dall'Aglio \ 2008b).
There is no obvious ``proximity effect" (flux transmission shortward of the \ edge) despite the large ionizing photon luminosity of the QSO in the \ and \ continua.
This more accurate systemic redshift is important for understanding the ionizing radiation field near the QSO, and opens the possibility that some ionizing photons are being absorbed within the AGN.

sources.summary: The paper treats HE 2347 4342 as a He II quasar sightline, excluding geocoronal contamination and recalibrating, restricting, and FUSE-aligning its extreme-UV COS spectrum.

quotes.quote: Over the short range with useful G140L segment B data for HE 2347 4342 and HS 1700 + 6416 ( 2250 pixels, 1000--1180 ) spectral variations of the dark current are insignificant.
For HE 2347 4342 and HS 1700 + 6416 we also excluded geocoronal H 1 Ly emission contaminating the He 2 Ly forest at z 2.37 .

The extreme UV ( <1150 ) spectra of HS 1700 + 6416 and HE 2347 4342 recorded on COS detector segment B in the G140L 1230 \ and 1280 \ setups required wavelength recalibration e.g. shull10,syphers13 . Due to the combination of flux calibration uncertainties, low signal, and He 2 Ly absorption we restricted our analysis to >1000 . We adopted the COS G140L dispersion given by shull10 and aligned the COS spectra with their archival FUSE spectra after convolving them with the COS G140L line spread function. Considering the background subtraction problems of FUSE zheng04,fechner06 and possible source variability, the spectra were aligned by eye using several sharp features in the emerging He 2 Ly forest.

sources.summary: The paper treats HE 2347-4342 as a quasar sight line, reanalyzing its He II and H I Ly forest and reporting column-density-ratio fluctuations from the spectra.

quotes.quote: We present a reanalysis of the He ii Ly absorption towards the quasars HS 1700+6416 and HE 2347-4342 using new high S/N , optical observations.
HE 2347-4342 was observed with UVES at the second VLT unit telescope (Kueyen) during 13 nights at two epochs in 2000 and 2003.

Fitting the metal line cleaned H i Ly forest to the He ii data leads to = 1.79^ +0.71 _ 0.74 for 2.33 < z < 2.73 towards HE 2347-4342 and = 1.87^ +0.41 _ 0.37 for 2.29 < z < 2.75 towards HS 1700+6416. These results are in agreement with the median values from the Doppler profile analysis, however, slightly lower than the median for lines with N_ H i < 13.0 , which we interpret as due to the neglect of thermal broadening (see below). The mean value of the Ly forest lines with N_ H i < 13.0 in both quasars is = 1.89 0.72 , i.e. \ 80 . Strong local deviations from the average suggest that the column density ratio is fluctuating on smaller scales.

sources.summary: The paper treats HE 2347-4342 as a high-redshift quasar with a strong associated absorption system, using UV/optical spectra to test line locking and constrain the quasar spectral energy distribution.

quotes.quote: The high-redshift quasar HE 2347-4342 ( z_ em = 2.885, V = 16.1 ) was discovered in the course of the Hamburg/ESO Survey (HES; Reimers Wisotzki ). A strong associated system with absorption components up to 1500 km s ^ 1 redwards from the QSO emission line redshift is observed in highly ionized metal lines. In this paper we present a detailed analysis of the absorption characteristics and the inferences on the spectral energy distribution of the QSO.

With our analysis of the C iv and N v complex associated system of HE 2347-4342 we shall perform a statistical test in order to clarify whether the apparent line locking has a physical basis or the line coincidences are within the expectation in a random distribution of a rich associated system with up to 16 components. The strong associated system of HE 2347-4342 also seems to be responsible for the observed absence of a He ii proximity effect in this QSO (Reimers et al. ) which would imply that at least one of the components of the associated system is optically thick to radiation shortward of 228 .

sources.summary: The paper states that the He II Ly forest was first resolved toward quasar HE 2347-4342 with FUSE, with analyses finding highly variable UV background ratios and larger values in voids.

quotes.quote: The He ii Ly forest has been resolved for the first time towards the quasar HE 2347-4342 with FUSE krissetal2001, shulletal2004, zhengetal2004 . Different analysis methods have been applied to derive the column density ratio (line profile fitting was used by krissetal2001 and zhengetal2004 , while shulletal2004 applied an apparent optical depth method) and lead to the same results: The UV background is highly variable on small scales with a fluctuation of the column density ratio between 1 1000 or even more. Additionally, shulletal2004 find an apparent correlation between the value and the strength of the H i absorption in the sense, that appears to be larger in voids.

gmw_00002805.name: GAMA J113727.46-005047.6
gmw_00002805.overview: GAMA J113727.46-005047.6 is an elliptical galaxy that serves as the optical counterpart to the Cloverleaf Odd Radio Circle [arXiv:2403.09808]. It is a massive central group galaxy hosting an active galactic nucleus at the center of a diffuse X-ray and radio emission peak [arXiv:2403.09808].

sources.summary: The paper identifies GAMA J113727.46-005047.6 as the Cloverleaf ORC's elliptical optical counterpart and BCG1, a massive central group galaxy whose X-ray/radio peak also contains an AGN.

quotes.quote: The optical counterpart of the ORC is an elliptical galaxy GAMA J113727.46-005047.6 at (RA: 174.3644^ , DEC: 0.8467^ ), BCG1 hereafter, with a stellar mass of 1.4 0.3 10^ 11 M_ with a spectroscopic redshift of 0.046399 1.2 10^ 5 Driver2022 . This galaxy is also classified as the central galaxy of a low-mass galaxy group with eight total members using the friends-of-friends method in the complete Galaxy and Mass Assembly I (GAMA-I) survey galaxy group catalog (G3Cv10) Robotham2011 , with ID 200792.

The X-ray morphology of diffuse gas shows two clear peaks, i.e., the central galaxy is located at the center of the southern peak, which also marks the location of the brightest radio emission. In contrast, the X-ray peak in the northeast direction is 1.8 (98 kpc) apart from the southern peak and is associated with no galaxy. The centroid of the X-ray emission, i.e., the center of the two X-ray peaks, is 37 kpc away from the center of the two radio peaks and 80 kpc away from the ellipse's center. The southern peak also shows clear X-ray detection of an AGN in the center.

gmw_00002806.name: SDSS J122518.86+144547.7
gmw_00002806.overview: SDSS J122518.86+144547.7 is a galaxy located at a redshift of approximately 0.044 [arXiv:1004.5379, arXiv:1509.00329]. It hosts a faint nuclear X-ray source [arXiv:1509.00329] and a highly luminous off-nuclear X-ray source located 3.2 kiloparsecs from its center [arXiv:1004.5379, arXiv:1509.00329].

sources.summary: The paper identifies this SDSS DR7 z=0.0447 galaxy as hosting a bright 3.2 kpc off-nuclear X-ray source with an HST optical counterpart and several possible interpretations.
sources.paper_coordinate_text: Right Ascencion (R.A.; J2000)=12:25:18.860 (186.328583 in decimal degrees) and the Declination (Dec; J2000)=14:45:47.704 (14.763251 in decimal degrees)

quotes.quote: In this Paper we report the discovery of ; a peculiar X-ray source with a position 3.6 0.2 off-nuclear from an SDSS DR7 z=0.0447 galaxy. The 3.6 offset corresponds to 3.2 kpc at the distance of the galaxy. The 0.3--8 keV X-ray flux of this source is 5 10^ 14 and its 0.3--8 keV luminosity is 2.2 10^ 41 (2.7 10^ 41 ; 0.5--10 keV) assuming the source belongs to the associated galaxy.

We find a candidate optical counterpart in archival HST/ACS g ^ -band observations of the field containing the galaxy obtained on June 16, 2003. The observed magnitude of g ^ =26.4 0.1 corresponds to an absolute magnitude of 10.1 . We discuss the possible nature of the X-ray source and its associated candidate optical counterpart and conclude that the source is either a very blue type IIn supernova, a ULX with a very bright optical counterpart or a recoiling super-massive black hole. galaxies:individual: SDSS J122518.86+144547.7

This source has a position 3.6 off-nuclear from a galaxy identified in the SDSS DR7 as having a redshift z =0.0447 (see Fig. ima and hstima ). The SDSS DR7 measured centre of this galaxy is at Right Ascencion (R.A.; J2000)=12:25:18.860 (186.328583 in decimal degrees) and the Declination (Dec; J2000)=14:45:47.704 (14.763251 in decimal degrees).

sources.summary: The paper treats SDSS J1225 as the likely host galaxy of CXO J122518.6+144545, using its redshift, distance, and faint nuclear X-ray source to assess HLX and recoiling-BH scenarios.

quotes.quote: jonker10 reported the discovery of a luminous off-nuclear X-ray source (CXO J122518.6+144545; hereafter ) in the galaxy SDSS J122518.86+144547.7 (hereafter SDSS J1225).

Assuming is at the same distance as the galaxy ( z = 0.0445 0.0001 , 182 Mpc, sdssdr5 ), its 0.3 -- 8 keV luminosity in this observation was 2.2 10^ 41 , making it one of the most luminous ULXs known to date.

The detection of an X-ray source that is positionally consistent with the nucleus of SDSS J1225 casts further doubts on the recoiling BH scenario, as it seems to imply the presence of an SMBH in the centre of the galaxy. However, the luminosity of the X-ray source is low enough to be consistent with a low mass X-ray binary (LMXB) in the nuclear region of SDSS J1225.

gmw_00002807.name: HE 1029 1401
gmw_00002807.overview: HE 1029 1401 is a luminous, radio-quiet quasar at a redshift of z=0.086 [arXiv:1005.2959, arXiv:2405.13479]. It is hosted by a massive elliptical galaxy that contains a highly structured distribution of ionized gas, likely originating from past minor mergers [arXiv:1005.2959].

sources.summary: The paper maps ionised gas around the radio-quiet QSO HE 1029 1401, finding an elliptical host, low-metallicity gas, rotating and disturbed components, and likely minor-merger origin.

quotes.quote: We present VIMOS integral field spectroscopy of the brightest radio-quiet QSO on the southern sky HE 1029 1401 at a redshift of z=0.086 . Standard decomposition techniques for broad-band imaging are extended to integral field data in order to deblend the QSO and host emission. We perform a tentative analysis of the stellar continuum finding a young stellar population ( <100 Myr) or a featureless continuum embedded in an old stellar population (10 Gyr) typical for a massive elliptical galaxy.

For the first time we map the two-dimensional ionised gas distribution and the gas velocity field around HE 1029 1401. While the stellar host morphology is purely elliptical we find a highly structured distribution of ionised gas out to 16 kpc from the QSO. The gas is highly ionised solely by the QSO radiation and has a significantly lower metallicity than would be expected for the stellar mass of the host, indicating an external origin of the gas most likely due to minor mergers. We find a rotating gas disc around the QSO and a dispersion-dominated non-rotating gas component within the central 3 kpc. At larger distances the velocity field is heavily disturbed, which could be interpreted as another signature of past minor merger events.

sources.summary: The paper studies the luminous quasar HE 1029-1401, finding Fe K emission and modeling its soft excess with warm-corona Comptonization, consistent with a radially extended corona of a few tens of gravitational radii.

quotes.quote: This study focuses on the quasar HE 1029-1401 ( z=0.086 , (L_ bol / [ erg s^ 1 ] )= 46.0 0.2 ), with a particular emphasis on investigating the properties of the hot corona and the physical origin of the soft excess. In this study, we present the results of a joint XMM-Newton / NuSTAR monitoring campaign of this quasar conducted in May 2022. The source exhibits a cold and narrow Fe K emission line at 6.4 keV, in addition to the detection of a broad component.

Our findings suggest that the soft excess observed in HE 1029-1401 can be adequately explained by Comptonized emission originating from a warm corona. Specifically, fitting the spectra with two component we found that the warm corona is characterized by a photon index ( ^ w ) of 2.75 0.05 and by an electron temperature ( kT_ e ^ w ) of 0.39^ +0.06 _ 0.04 keV, while the optical depth ( ^ w ) is found to be 23 3 .

We also test more physical models for the warm corona, corresponding to two scenarios: pure Comptonization and Comptonization plus reflection. Both models provide a good fit to the data, and are in agreement with a radially extended warm corona having a size of a few tens of gravitational radii.

gmw_00002808.name: J0205-55:S7
gmw_00002808.overview: J0205-55:S7 is a strong tidal dwarf galaxy candidate located in the HIPASS J0205-55 Choir group [arXiv:1401.0955]. This group appears to consist of two merging systems, and the dwarf galaxy itself exhibits a symmetric, edge-on disk morphology [arXiv:1401.0955].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J0205-55:S7 as a strong tidal-dwarf candidate in a merging Choir group, near S2, with an edge-on disk morphology and 0.85 kpc half-light radius.

quotes.quote: Based on metallicity, we identify three (16 of dwarfs) strong TDG candidates (12+log(O/H) > 8.6), which have metallicities consistent with other TDG candidates in the literature and our simulations, and significantly above the SDSS control sample at 12 + log(O/H) = 8.28 0.10. These galaxies are J0205-55:S7, J0400-52:S8, J0400-52:S9, and are discussed very briefly in Appendix A.1.

HIPASS J0205-55:S7 This dwarf is located in Choir group HIPASS J0205-55, which appears to be comprised of two merging systems Sweet2013 . The nearest bright neighbour to S7 is S2, approximately 50 kpc away in projection. S7 has the morphology of a symmetric, edge-on disk galaxy with a half-light radius of 0.85 kpc.

gmw_00002809.name: J0400-52:S8
gmw_00002809.overview: J0400-52:S8 is a compact dwarf galaxy with a half-light radius of 1.5 kpc [arXiv:1401.0955]. It is classified as a strong tidal dwarf galaxy candidate and is located only 20 kpc in projection from its nearest giant galaxy companion [arXiv:1401.0955].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J0400-52:S8 as a strong tidal-dwarf candidate in HIPASS J0400-52, describing it as compact and a close companion to a nearby giant galaxy.

quotes.quote: Based on metallicity, we identify three (16 of dwarfs) strong TDG candidates (12+log(O/H) > 8.6), which have metallicities consistent with other TDG candidates in the literature and our simulations, and significantly above the SDSS control sample at 12 + log(O/H) = 8.28 0.10. These galaxies are J0205-55:S7, J0400-52:S8, J0400-52:S9, and are discussed very briefly in Appendix A.1.

HIPASS J0400-52:S8 and S9 These dwarfs are very compact with half-light radii of 1.5 and 2.5 kpc respectively. They are very close companions to S4 and S6 at only 20 kpc away in projection from their respective nearest giant galaxies.

gmw_00002810.name: J0400-52:S9
gmw_00002810.overview: J0400-52:S9 is a compact dwarf galaxy with a half-light radius of 2.5 kpc [arXiv:1401.0955]. It is classified as a strong tidal dwarf candidate and is located only 20 kpc in projection from its nearest giant galaxy companion [arXiv:1401.0955].

sources.summary: The paper classifies J0400-52:S9 as a strong tidal-dwarf candidate in HIPASS J0400-52, describing it as compact and a close companion to a nearby giant galaxy.

quotes.quote: Based on metallicity, we identify three (16 of dwarfs) strong TDG candidates (12+log(O/H) > 8.6), which have metallicities consistent with other TDG candidates in the literature and our simulations, and significantly above the SDSS control sample at 12 + log(O/H) = 8.28 0.10. These galaxies are J0205-55:S7, J0400-52:S8, J0400-52:S9, and are discussed very briefly in Appendix A.1.

HIPASS J0400-52:S8 and S9 These dwarfs are very compact with half-light radii of 1.5 and 2.5 kpc respectively. They are very close companions to S4 and S6 at only 20 kpc away in projection from their respective nearest giant galaxies.

gmw_00002811.name: J1051-17:S4
gmw_00002811.overview: J1051-17:S4 is a very metal-poor dwarf galaxy located approximately 100 kpc from the giant galaxy S1 [arXiv:1401.0955]. It features two faint H II regions within a low surface brightness host, and its star formation may have been reignited by gas gained during a recent encounter with S1 [arXiv:1401.0955].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1051-17:S4 as a very metal-poor dwarf about 100 kpc from S1, possibly rejuvenated by gas from a recent encounter, with two faint H II regions.

quotes.quote: We also identify four (21 ) very metal-poor galaxies (12+log(O/H) < 8.0), consistent with gas-rich cluster dwarfs whose star formation has been ignited due to interactions with nearby giant galaxies. These galaxies are J0400-52:S2, J1051-17:S4, J1403-06:S3, J1403-06:S4, and are discussed very briefly in Appendix A.2.

HIPASS J1051-17:S4 S4 is located about 100 kpc from giant galaxy S1 in the direction of the second-brightest spiral in the group, S2. It may have gained some HI gas and had star formation reignited during a recent encounter with S1 (Kilborn et al., in prep.). It consists of two faint H ii regions in a low surface brightness host with a half-light radius of 1.4 kpc.

gmw_00002812.name: J1403-06:S3
gmw_00002812.overview: J1403-06:S3 is a very metal-poor, compact dwarf galaxy that is one of the faintest members of its group [arXiv:1401.0955]. It consists of a single H II region in a low surface brightness host, located about 50 kpc from an interacting giant galaxy [arXiv:1401.0955].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1403-06:S3 as a very metal-poor, compact dwarf and one of the faintest group members, near interacting giants and consisting of a single H II region.

quotes.quote: We also identify four (21 ) very metal-poor galaxies (12+log(O/H) < 8.0), consistent with gas-rich cluster dwarfs whose star formation has been ignited due to interactions with nearby giant galaxies. These galaxies are J0400-52:S2, J1051-17:S4, J1403-06:S3, J1403-06:S4, and are discussed very briefly in Appendix A.2.

HIPASS J1403-06:S3 and S4 These two quite compact dwarfs are the two faintest members in this Choir group. Both are located about 50 kpc from the nearby giant S1, which itself is currently interacting with the other giant in the group, S2.
The two dwarfs each consist of a single H ii region in a low surface brightness host, with half-light radii of 0.7 and 1.2 kpc for S3 and S4 respectively.

gmw_00002813.name: J1403-06:S4
gmw_00002813.overview: J1403-06:S4 is a compact, very metal-poor dwarf galaxy that represents one of the faintest members of its group [arXiv:1401.0955]. It consists of a single H II region within a low surface brightness host, located near interacting giant galaxies [arXiv:1401.0955].

sources.summary: The paper identifies J1403-06:S4 as a very metal-poor, compact dwarf and one of the faintest group members, near interacting giants and consisting of a single H II region.
